papers.title: 21st International Workshop on Data Management on New Hardware (DaMoN).
papers.abstract: New hardware, such as multi-core CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, new mem- ory and storage technologies, and low-power devices, bring new challenges and opportunities in optimizing database systems per- formance. Consequently, exploiting the characteristics of modern hardware has become an important topic of database systems re- search. In the last two decades, the DaMoN Workshop has estab- lished itself as the primary database venue to present ideas on how to exploit new hardware for data management, in particular, how to improve the performance or scalability of databases, how new hardware unlocks new database application scenarios, and how data management could benefit from future hardware.
papers.abstract_zh: 新硬件，如多核CPU、GPU、FPGA、新内存和存储技术以及低功耗设备，为优化数据库系统性能带来了新的挑战和机遇。因此，利用现代硬件的特性已成为数据库系统研究的一个重要课题。在过去的二十年中，DaMoN Workshop 已成为主要的数据库场所，提出如何利用新硬件进行数据管理的想法，特别是如何提高数据库的性能或可扩展性、新硬件如何解锁新的数据库应用场景以及数据管理如何从未来的硬件中受益。

papers.title: A Fast Line Density Visualization Plugin for Geographic Information Systems.
papers.abstract: Line Density Visualization (LDV) has been widely used in different domains, e.g., transportation science, urban planning, and criminology. Therefore, various geographic information systems, e.g., ArcGIS and QGIS, can also support this tool. However, all these GIS platforms mainly adopt the naïve algorithm for generating LDV, which cannot be scalable to high resolution sizes and large-scale line-segment datasets. To tackle this issue, we have developed a new QGIS plugin, called Fast Line Density Analysis, for efficiently supporting two types of accurate approximation, namely (1) generating LDV with an ε-relative error guarantee (εLDV) and (2) generating LDV with multiple thresholds (τLDV). In this demonstration, we have prepared three large-scale datasets (with up to 15.7 million line segments) for participants to compare our plugin with QGIS and ArcGIS in terms of accuracy and efficiency. In particular, participants can also tune different parameters in our plugin for understanding how they can affect the visualization quality and efficiency. The demonstration video is available in the YouTube and Bilibili links, which are https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EYl3Wieb-2I and https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1aJwaeMEHr, respectively.
papers.abstract_zh: 线密度可视化（LDV）已广泛应用于不同领域，例如交通科学、城市规划和犯罪学。因此，各种地理信息系统，例如ArcGIS和QGIS，也可以支持该工具。然而，所有这些GIS平台主要采用朴素的LDV生成算法，无法扩展到高分辨率尺寸和大规模线段数据集。为了解决这个问题，我们开发了一个新的 QGIS 插件，称为快速线密度分析，用于有效支持两种类型的精确近似，即（1）生成具有 ε 相​​对误差保证的 LDV（εLDV）和（2）生成具有多个阈值的 LDV（τLDV）。在本次演示中，我们准备了三个大型数据集（多达1570万条线段）供参与者将我们的插件与QGIS和ArcGIS在准确性和效率方面进行比较。特别是，参与者还可以调整我们插件中的不同参数，以了解它们如何影响可视化质量和效率。演示视频可在 YouTube 和 Bilibili 链接中获取，分别为 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EYl3Wieb-2I 和 https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1aJwaeMEHr。

papers.title: A Modular Graph-Native Query Optimization Framework.
papers.abstract: Complex Graph Patterns (CGPs), which combine pattern matching with relational operations, are widely used in real-world applications. Existing systems rely on monolithic architectures for CGPs, which restrict their ability to integrate multiple query languages and lack certain advanced optimization techniques. Therefore, to address these issues, we introduce GOpt, a modular graph-native query optimization framework with the following features: (1) support for queries in multiple query languages, (2) decoupling execution from specific graph systems, and (3) integration of advanced optimization techniques. Specifically, GOpt offers a high-level interface, GraphIrBuilder, for converting queries from various graph query languages into a unified intermediate representation (GIR), thereby streamlining the optimization process. It also provides a low-level interface, PhysicalConverter, enabling backends integration by converting the optimized plan into a backend-compatible execution plan. Moreover, GOpt employs a graph-native optimizer that encompasses extensive heuristic rules, an automatic type inference approach, and cost-based optimization techniques tailored for CGPs. Comprehensive experiments show that integrating GOpt significantly boosts performance, with Neo4j achieving an average speedup of 9.2x (up to 48.6x), and GraphScope achieving an average speedup of 33.4x (up to 78.7x), on real-world datasets.
papers.abstract_zh: 复杂图模式（CGP）将模式匹配与关系操作相结合，广泛应用于实际应用中。现有系统依赖于 CGP 的整体架构，这限制了它们集成多种查询语言的能力，并且缺乏某些高级优化技术。因此，为了解决这些问题，我们引入了GOpt，一个模块化的图原生查询优化框架，具有以下功能：（1）支持多种查询语言的查询，（2）与特定图系统解耦执行，以及（3）集成高级优化技术。具体来说，GOpt 提供了一个高级接口 GraphIrBuilder，用于将各种图查询语言的查询转换为统一的中间表示（GIR），从而简化优化过程。它还提供了一个低级接口PhysicalConverter，通过将优化计​​划转换为后端兼容的执行计划来实现后端集成。此外，GOpt 采用了图形原生优化器，其中包含广泛的启发式规则、自动类型推断方法以及为 CGP 量身定制的基于成本的优化技术。综合实验表明，集成 GOpt 可以显着提升性能，在真实数据集上，Neo4j 平均加速 9.2 倍（最高 48.6 倍），GraphScope 平均加速 33.4 倍（最高 78.7 倍）。

papers.title: A Query-Aware Enormous Database Generator For System Performance Evaluation.
papers.abstract: In production, simulating the real application without exposing the privacy data is essential for database benchmarking or performance debugging. A rich body of query-aware database generators (QAG) are proposed for this purpose. The complex data dependencies hidden behind queries make previous work suffer from critical deficiencies in supporting complex operators with high simulation accuracy. To fill the gap between the existing QAGs and the urgent demands, we implement a data generator M irage with the attractive characteristics of reproducing applications based on the queries even with complex operators and having a theoretical zero error. Specifically,M irage leverages Query Rewriting and Set Transforming Rules to decouple the implicit dependencies from queries, which greatly simplify the generation problem; it presents a uniform representation of various join types and formulates key population as a Constraint Programming (CP) problem, which can be well solved by an off-the-shelf CP Solver . In this demonstration, users can explore the core features of M irage in generating synthetic databases, which has the widest support to operators and the best simulation fidelity compared to the related work.
papers.abstract_zh: 在生产中，在不暴露隐私数据的情况下模拟真实应用程序对于数据库基准测试或性能调试至关重要。为此目的，提出了丰富的查询感知数据库生成器（QAG）。隐藏在查询背后的复杂数据依赖关系使得之前的工作在支持高模拟精度的复杂算子方面存在严重缺陷。为了填补现有 QAG 和迫切需求之间的差距，我们实现了一种数据生成器 Mirage，它具有基于查询重现应用程序的有吸引力的特性，即使使用复杂的运算符，并且理论上为零错误。具体来说，M irage 利用查询重写和设置转换规则来解耦查询中的隐式依赖关系，从而大大简化了生成问题；它提供了各种连接类型的统一表示，并将关键群体制定为约束规划（CP）问题，可以通过现成的 CP 求解器很好地解决。在本次演示中，用户可以探索 Mirage 在生成合成数据库方面的核心功能，与相关工作相比，它对操作员的支持最广泛，模拟保真度也最好。

papers.title: ABase: the Multi-Tenant NoSQL Serverless Database for Diverse and Dynamic Workloads in Large-scale Cloud Environments.
papers.abstract: Multi-tenant architectures enhance the elasticity and resource utilization of NoSQL databases by allowing multiple tenants to co-locate and share resources. However, in large-scale cloud environments, the diverse and dynamic nature of workloads poses significant challenges for multi-tenant NoSQL databases. Based on our practical observations, we have identified three crucial challenges: (1) the impact of caching on performance isolation, as cache hits alter request execution and resource consumption, leading to inaccurate traffic control; (2) the dynamic changes in traffic, with changes in tenant traffic trends causing throttling or resource wastage, and changes in access distribution causing hot key pressure or cache hit ratio drops; and (3) the imbalanced layout of data nodes due to tenants' diverse resource requirements, leading to low resource utilization. To address these challenges, we introduce ABase, a multi-tenant NoSQL serverless database developed at ByteDance. ABase introduces a two-layer caching mechanism with a cache-aware isolation mechanism to ensure accurate resource consumption estimates. Furthermore, ABase employs a predictive autoscaling policy to dynamically adjust resources in response to tenant traffic changes and a multi-resource rescheduling algorithm to balance resource utilization across data nodes. With these innovations, ABase has successfully served ByteDance's large-scale cloud environment, supporting a total workload that has achieved a peak QPS of over 13 billion and total storage exceeding 1 EB.
papers.abstract_zh: 多租户架构允许多个租户共置和共享资源，从而增强了 NoSQL 数据库的弹性和资源利用率。然而，在大规模云环境中，工作负载的多样性和动态性给多租户 NoSQL 数据库带来了重大挑战。根据我们的实际观察，我们发现了三个关键挑战：（1）缓存对性能隔离的影响，因为缓存命中会改变请求执行和资源消耗，导致流量控制不准确； （2）流量的动态变化，租户流量趋势的变化导致节流或资源浪费，访问分布的变化导致热键压力或缓存命中率下降； (3)租户资源需求多样化导致数据节点布局不平衡，导致资源利用率低。为了应对这些挑战，我们推出了 ABase，这是字节跳动开发的多租户 NoSQL 无服务器数据库。 ABase引入了两层缓存机制和缓存感知隔离机制，以确保准确的资源消耗估算。此外，ABase还采用预测性自动扩展策略来动态调整资源以响应租户流量变化，并采用多资源重新调度算法来平衡数据节点之间的资源利用率。通过这些创新，ABase成功服务于字节跳动大规模云环境，支撑总工作负载峰值QPS超过130亿，总存储超过1EB。

papers.title: ACE-in-Action: A Smart DBMS Bufferpool for SSDs.
papers.abstract: Solid-State Drives (SSDs) have two key properties: (i) read/write asymmetry, where writes are slower than reads, and (ii) access concurrency, allowing multiple I/O operations in parallel to maximize the bandwidth. However, many applications treat reads and writes equally and do not fully utilize the device concurrency, a behavior observed in traditional database bufferpool managers. To address this, we propose an A symmetry & C oncurrency-Awar e bufferpool manager ( ACE ) that batches writes based on the device's write concurrency and amortizes the high asymmetric write cost by issuing them in parallel. ACE notably improves the application performance (e.g., PostgreSQL) and is easy to integrate since it can work as a wrapper around any existing page replacement policy. In this demonstration, we present a web simulation of the ACE bufferpool manager integrated with three popular page replacement policies (LRU, CFLRU and LRU-WSR). The conference participants can configure the simulation, view the real-time bufferpool animation and statistics, as well as run various experiments to compare the performance of different page replacement policies and their ACE counterparts. The demonstration is available at https://disc-projects.bu.edu/ACE/research.html.
papers.abstract_zh: 固态硬盘 (SSD) 具有两个关键属性：(i) 读/写不对称，其中写入速度比读取慢；(ii) 访问并发性，允许并行进行多个 I/O 操作以最大化带宽。然而，许多应用程序平等地对待读取和写入，并且没有充分利用设备并发性，这是传统数据库缓冲池管理器中观察到的行为。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种对称性和流通性感知的缓冲池管理器（ACE），它根据设备的写入并发性对写入进行批量写入，并通过并行发出它们来分摊高非对称写入成本。 ACE 显着提高了应用程序性能（例如 PostgreSQL），并且易于集成，因为它可以作为任何现有页面替换策略的包装器。在此演示中，我们展示了 ACE 缓冲池管理器的 Web 模拟，该管理器与三种流行的页面替换策略（LRU、CFLRU 和 LRU-WSR）集成。会议参与者可以配置模拟、查看实时缓冲池动画和统计数据，以及运行各种实验来比较不同页面替换策略及其 ACE 策略的性能。该演示可在 https://disc-projects.bu.edu/ACE/research.html 上获取。

papers.title: Adaptive and Efficient Log Parsing as a Cloud Service.
papers.abstract: Logs are a critical data source for cloud systems, enabling advanced features like monitoring, alerting, and root cause analysis. However, the massive scale and diverse formats of unstructured logs pose challenges for adaptable, efficient, and accurate parsing methods. This paper introduces ByteBrain-LogParser , an innovative log parsing framework designed specifically for cloud environments. ByteBrain-LogParser employs a hierarchical clustering algorithm to allow real-time precision adjustments, coupled with optimizations such as positional similarity distance, deduplication, and hash encoding to enhance performance. Experiments on large-scale datasets show that it processes 229,000 logs per second on average, achieving an 840% speedup over the fastest baseline while maintaining accuracy comparable to state-of-the-art methods. Real-world evaluations further validate its efficiency and adaptability, demonstrating its potential as a robust cloud-based log parsing solution.
papers.abstract_zh: 日志是云系统的关键数据源，可实现监控、警报和根本原因分析等高级功能。然而，规模庞大、格式多样的非结构化日志对适应性、高效、准确的解析方法提出了挑战。本文介绍了 ByteBrain-LogParser ，一个专为云环境设计的创新日志解析框架。 ByteBrain-LogParser采用分层聚类算法来实现实时精度调整，并结合位置相似距离、重复数据删除和哈希编码等优化来增强性能。对大规模数据集的实验表明，它平均每秒处理 229,000 个日志，比最快的基线实现了 840% 的加速，同时保持了与最先进方法相当的准确性。现实世界的评估进一步验证了其效率和适应性，展示了其作为强大的基于云的日志解析解决方案的潜力。

papers.title: Advances in Designing Scalable Graph Neural Networks: The Perspective of Graph Data Management.
papers.abstract:
Graph Neural Network (GNN) is a successful marriage of graph data management and deep learning, leading to notable improvements in learning quality over graphs. This advancement highly impacts graph-based applications in many areas, including computer vision, natural language processing, biology, medication, and social science. Despite the success, scaling up GNN models poses a formidable and long-lasting challenge, hindering the application to industrial-level graphs featuring millions or billions of nodes and edges. The rapid update of tasks and models requires continuous efforts in developing scalable GNN architectures. In specific, the scalability bottleneck of GNNs typically stem from graph-related computations, entailing more proficient processing and utilization of the unstructured graph data.

There has been a marked trend of incorporation between GNN and data management to tackle newly-emerged scalability challenges. This includes the utilization of graph algorithms such as Personalized PageRank (PPR) and subgraph discovery in GNN models, as well as exploring topics in graph domain including multi-scale representation and graph spectrum. This primer tutorial (3 hours) aims to provide a comprehensive overview of scalable GNN designs, highlighting the most recent and prominent models that focus on the scalability issue. We will also summarize the technical challenges and suggest potential future directions regarding the rapid developments in this field. We believe that this work can be used as one important reference for researchers looking to develop scalable GNN models.
papers.abstract_zh:
图神经网络（GNN）是图数据管理和深度学习的成功结合，显着提高了图学习质量。这一进步极大地影响了许多领域基于图的应用，包括计算机视觉、自然语言处理、生物学、医学和社会科学。尽管取得了成功，但扩展 GNN 模型仍面临着巨大而持久的挑战，阻碍了其在具有数百万或数十亿节点和边的工业级图上的应用。任务和模型的快速更新需要不断努力开发可扩展的 GNN 架构。具体来说，GNN 的可扩展性瓶颈通常源于与图相关的计算，需要更熟练地处理和利用非结构化图数据。

GNN 和数据管理的结合已成为解决新出现的可扩展性挑战的明显趋势。这包括使用图算法，例如个性化 PageRank (PPR) 和 GNN 模型中的子图发现，以及探索图领域的主题，包括多尺度表示和图谱。本入门教程（3 小时）旨在提供可扩展 GNN 设计的全面概述，重点介绍关注可扩展性问题的最新且最突出的模型。我们还将总结技术挑战，并就该领域的快速发展提出潜在的未来方向。我们相信这项工作可以作为研究人员开发可扩展 GNN 模型的重要参考。

papers.title: AlayaDB: The Data Foundation for Efficient and Effective Long-context LLM Inference.
papers.abstract: AlayaDB is a cutting-edge vector database system natively architected for efficient and effective long-context inference for Large Language Models (LLMs) at AlayaDB AI. Specifically, it decouples the KV cache and attention computation from the LLM inference systems, and encapsulates them into a novel vector database system. For the Model as a Service providers (MaaS), AlayaDB consumes fewer hardware resources and offers higher generation quality for various workloads with different kinds of Service Level Objectives (SLOs), when compared with the existing alternative solutions (e.g., KV cache disaggregation, retrieval-based sparse attention). The crux of AlayaDB is that it abstracts the attention computation and cache management for LLM inference into a query processing procedure, and optimizes the performance via a native query optimizer. In this work, we demonstrate the effectiveness of AlayaDB via (i) two use cases from our industry partners, and (ii) extensive experimental results on LLM inference benchmarks.
papers.abstract_zh: AlayaDB 是一个尖端的矢量数据库系统，其原生架构旨在为 AlayaDB AI 的大型语言模型 (LLM) 进行高效且有效的长上下文推理。具体来说，它将 KV 缓存和注意力计算与 LLM 推理系统解耦，并将它们封装到一个新颖的向量数据库系统中。对于模型即服务提供商（MaaS）来说，与现有的替代解决方案（例如，KV缓存分解、基于检索的稀疏注意力）相比，AlayaDB消耗更少的硬件资源，并为具有不同服务级别目标（SLO）的各种工作负载提供更高的生成质量。 AlayaDB的关键在于它将LLM推理的注意力计算和缓存管理抽象为查询处理过程，并通过原生查询优化器优化性能。在这项工作中，我们通过（i）我们行业合作伙伴的两个用例，以及（ii）LLM推理基准的广泛实验结果来证明AlayaDB的有效性。

papers.title: Alpha Demo: A Hardware-Accelerated Data Model for Ad-Hoc Manipulation of Point Clouds.
papers.abstract:
A point cloud is a collection of data points in a three-dimensional space that represents the external surface of an object or environment. This data type is typically generated by spatial sensing devices, like LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) systems. While these devices have revolutionized fields such as autonomous vehicles, urban traffic monitoring, and forestry management, the manipulation of point clouds still poses challenges. Applications often involve complex data manipulation and, in some cases, require very low latency. As a result, each application tends to develop its own methods for handling point clouds based on its unique requirements. The common belief is that only through specialized solutions can an application's requirements be met.

In this paper, we demonstrate that complex point cloud manipulations can be developed using generic primitives without compromising latency. To do so, we use a timeless data management approach: We establish a data model for point clouds. The model supports ad-hoc querying and transformations simply by combining the powerful operators we introduce. Along with the model, we also present a specially designed FPGA-based hardware unit that is capable of accelerating some of its critical operators. We call our data model Damocles, the hardware that supports it Palicus, and the system built on top of them Alpha. Our system works on an actual LiDAR sensor connected to Alpha and can run even on an affordable edge-sized device with no noticeable latency.
papers.abstract_zh:
点云是三维空间中数据点的集合，表示对象或环境的外表面。这种数据类型通常由空间传感设备生成，例如 LiDAR（光探测和测距）系统。尽管这些设备彻底改变了自动驾驶汽车、城市交通监控和林业管理等领域，但点云的操纵仍然面临挑战。应用程序通常涉及复杂的数据操作，并且在某些情况下需要非常低的延迟。因此，每个应用程序都倾向于根据其独特的需求开发自己的处理点云的方法。人们普遍认为，只有通过专门的解决方案才能满足应用程序的要求。

在本文中，我们证明可以使用通用基元开发复杂的点云操作，而不会影响延迟。为此，我们使用永恒的数据管理方法：我们建立点云数据模型。该模型只需结合我们引入的强大运算符即可支持即席查询和转换。除了该模型之外，我们还展示了一个专门设计的基于 FPGA 的硬件单元，该单元能够加速其一些关键运算符。我们将我们的数据模型称为“达摩克里斯”，支持它的硬件称为“帕利克斯”，以及构建在它们之上的系统“阿尔法”。我们的系统在连接到 Alpha 的实际 LiDAR 传感器上运行，甚至可以在经济实惠的边缘尺寸设备上运行，没有明显的延迟。

papers.title: anagodb: Offering Massive Parallelism for Database Engine.
papers.abstract: Parallelism is a primary factor in exploiting the potential capability of modern computer systems having a number of processing cores and storage chips/spindles. The database community has exerted much effort to achieve good parallelism in query processing to shorten latency and/or improve throughput. One approach is out-of-order database execution (OoODE), which dynamically and recursively decomposes the query work into tasks; it divides a parent task into child tasks if the parent task is dividable, and eventually, it divides the query work into a massive number of tasks. Hence, this mechanism can exploit the parallelism of the underlying computer infrastructure, specifically leveraging the potential computational capacity and/or the potential IO bandwidth. In particular, OoODE offers significant speedups for selective analytical queries. This paper presents anagodb , a research prototype database engine that we have developed for exploring the implementation of OoODE. anagodb has the capability of processing a wide variety of analytical queries according to the OoODE mechanism. Our demonstration will present how anagodb runs analytical queries on a cloud server. The visual client tool reports the query progress and the hardware performance metrics at runtime, clarifying that anagodb exploits the potential hardware power effectively.
papers.abstract_zh: 并行性是利用具有多个处理核心和存储芯片/主轴的现代计算机系统的潜在能力的主要因素。数据库社区付出了很大的努力来在查询处理中实现良好的并行性，以缩短延迟和/或提高吞吐量。一种方法是乱序数据库执行（OoODE），它将查询工作动态地、递归地分解为任务；如果父任务可分割，则将父任务分割为子任务，最终将查询工作分割为大量任务。因此，该机制可以利用底层计算机基础设施的并行性，特别是利用潜在的计算能力和/或潜在的 IO 带宽。特别是，OoODE 为选择性分析查询提供了显着的加速。本文介绍了 anagodb，这是我们为探索 OoODE 的实现而开发的研究原型数据库引擎。 anagodb 能够根据 OoODE 机制处理各种分析查询。我们的演示将展示 anagodb 如何在云服务器上运行分析查询。可视化客户端工具报告运行时的查询进度和硬件性能指标，阐明anagodb有效地利用了潜在的硬件能力。

papers.title: Andromeda: Debugging Database Performance Issues with Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models.
papers.abstract: Debugging performance issues in a database management system (DBMS) is tedious and challenging, even for experienced database administrators (DBAs). Thus, with the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), developing an LLM-powered co-pilot to assist or even replace DBAs by automatically diagnosing issues and generating recommendations for resolution presents a promising direction. However, directly prompting LLMs for DBMS performance debugging often yields either generic or irrelevant responses, as LLMs may lack both domain knowledge in performance debugging and a deep understanding of DBMS internals. In this paper, we introduce Andromeda, a retrieval-augmented, LLM-powered system for automatic DBMS performance debugging. Andromeda enables users to pose natural language questions about various performance issues and provides context-aware and actionable recommendations for resolution. To achieve this, Andromeda first retrieves relevant evidence from multiple sources, including historical questions, troubleshooting manuals, DBMS telemetry data, and execution logs. It then leverages effective training strategies to adapt an open-source LLM, empowering it to diagnose and resolve issues based on the retrieved evidence. We have developed a web application for Andromeda and demonstrated its effectiveness in debugging real-world DBMS performance issues.
papers.abstract_zh: 即使对于经验丰富的数据库管理员 (DBA) 来说，调试数据库管理系统 (DBMS) 中的性能问题也是乏味且具有挑战性的。因此，随着大语言模型（LLM）的快速发展，开发一个由 LLM 驱动的副驾驶，通过自动诊断问题并生成解决建议来协助甚至取代 DBA，这是一个有前途的方向。然而，直接提示 LLM 进行 DBMS 性能调试通常会产生通用或不相关的响应，因为 LLM 可能缺乏性能调试方面的领域知识和对 DBMS 内部结构的深入理解。在本文中，我们介绍了 Andromeda，这是一个由 LLM 驱动的检索增强系统，用于自动 DBMS 性能调试。 Andromeda 使用户能够提出有关各种性能问题的自然语言问题，并提供上下文感知和可操作的解决建议。为了实现这一目标，Andromeda 首先从多个来源检索相关证据，包括历史问题、故障排除手册、DBMS 遥测数据和执行日志。然后，它利用有效的培训策略来调整开源法学硕士，使其能够根据检索到的证据诊断和解决问题。我们为 Andromeda 开发了一个 Web 应用程序，并证明了其在调试实际 DBMS 性能问题方面的有效性。

papers.title: Apache Wayang in Action: Enabling Data Systems Integration via a Unified Data Analytics Framework.
papers.abstract: Apache Wayang is an open-source framework, which provides a systematic and efficient solution for unifying data analytics over disparate data sources and via integrating multiple heterogeneous data systems. It achieves that by decoupling applications from the underlying systems. In addition, it provides an optimizer so that users do not have to specify the platforms on which their pipeline should run but the optimizer can determine the best way given a cost metric. In this demonstration, we showcase how the flexible architecture of Wayang enables seamless integration with multiple heterogeneous data systems and how the query optimizer can lead to better performance.
papers.abstract_zh: Apache Wayang 是一个开源框架，它提供了系统且高效的解决方案，用于通过集成多个异构数据系统来统一不同数据源的数据分析。它通过将应用程序与底层系统解耦来实现这一点。此外，它还提供了一个优化器，以便用户不必指定其管道应运行的平台，但优化器可以根据给定的成本指标确定最佳方式。在本次演示中，我们展示了 Wayang 的灵活架构如何实现与多个异构数据系统的无缝集成，以及查询优化器如何带来更好的性能。

papers.title: Asynchronous Replication Strategies for a Real-Time DBMS.
papers.abstract:
Real-time mission-critical services that handle large-scale consumer facing applications typically use a real-time DBMS that can provide both high performance as well as high availability. The only sure way to ensure that such services can survive catastrophic infrastructure failures is to deploy the DBMS as well as the applications themselves on multiple sites that are hundreds (or even thousands) of miles apart from each other.

Although synchronous replication across sites can be used for some consumer services, most high-performance consumer applications cannot tolerate the high write latencies required for synchronizing writes across far-apart sites. Therefore, the use of asynchronous replication between geographically distributed sites becomes the only practical option for these applications. This means that any changes in the data must be replicated quickly across sites to keep the exposure window as low as possible in the case of a major site failure event.

In this paper, we describe a resource-optimized, asynchronous replication strategy called XDR that we developed for use with a real-time DBMS (Aerospike) that supports very high read and write throughput. XDR is resource optimized because it is co-designed within the same process as the core database. This enables replication between sites to proceed at a high throughput despite the high latency between sites that are thousands of miles apart, while not impacting the latency and throughput of the source transactions. Furthermore, this co-design allows for efficient implementation of several other features within XDR such as i) filtering data during replication and ii) allowing multiple sites to update the same record simultaneously while ensuring the sites converge on the content of the records quickly. XDR has been used to deploy more than 100 large-scale customer applications worldwide.
papers.abstract_zh:
处理面向消费者的大规模应用程序的实时关键任务服务通常使用可以提供高性能和高可用性的实时 DBMS。确保此类服务能够在灾难性基础设施故障中幸存下来的唯一可靠方法是将 DBMS 以及应用程序本身部署在彼此相距数百（甚至数千）英里的多个站点上。

尽管跨站点同步复制可用于某些消费者服务，但大多数高性能消费者应用程序无法容忍跨相距较远的站点同步写入所需的高写入延迟。因此，在地理上分布的站点之间使用异步复制成为这些应用程序的唯一实用选择。这意味着数据中的任何更改都必须跨站点快速复制，以在发生重大站点故障事件时保持尽可能低的暴露窗口。

在本文中，我们描述了一种名为 XDR 的资源优化异步复制策略，该策略是我们开发的，用于支持极高读写吞吐量的实时 DBMS (Aerospike)。 XDR 是资源优化型的，因为它是在与核心数据库相同的流程中共同设计的。这使得站点之间的复制能够以高吞吐量进行，尽管相距数千英里的站点之间存在高延迟，同时不会影响源事务的延迟和吞吐量。此外，这种协同设计允许有效实现 XDR 中的几个其他功能，例如 i) 在复制期间过滤数据，以及 ii) 允许多个站点同时更新同一记录，同时确保站点快速收敛于记录的内容。 XDR 已用于在全球范围内部署 100 多个大型客户应用程序。

papers.title: Authenticating Multi-Chain Queries: Verifiable Virtual Filesystem Is All You Need.
papers.abstract: This demonstration showcases a verifiable multi-chain querying system. The proliferation of blockchain-driven applications has created a demand for on-chain data analysis across multiple blockchains. However, existing solutions face challenges in seamlessly integrating with existing blockchains, maintaining compatible with various database engines, and ensuring the integrity of query results. In response, we develop a blockchain indexing system that utilizes a novel verifiable virtual filesystem (V 2 FS) for query authentication. Our demonstration focuses on highlighting the key features of our system, including blockchain data indexing, verifiable multi-chain query processing, and the usability and performance of our solution. Additionally, we provide an interactive visualization module to enhance attendees' understanding and insights.
papers.abstract_zh: 该演示展示了一个可验证的多链查询系统。区块链驱动的应用程序的激增产生了对跨多个区块链的链上数据分析的需求。然而，现有的解决方案在与现有区块链无缝集成、保持与各种数据库引擎的兼容以及确保查询结果的完整性方面面临挑战。为此，我们开发了一种区块链索引系统，该系统利用新颖的可验证虚拟文件系统（V 2 FS）进行查询身份验证。我们的演示重点突出我们系统的关键功能，包括区块链数据索引、可验证的多链查询处理以及我们解决方案的可用性和性能。此外，我们还提供交互式可视化模块来增强与会者的理解和见解。

papers.title: AutoComp: Automated Data Compaction for Log-Structured Tables in Data Lakes.
papers.abstract: The proliferation of small files in data lakes poses significant challenges, including degraded query performance, increased storage costs, and scalability bottlenecks in distributed storage systems. Log-structured table formats (LSTs) such as Delta Lake, Apache Iceberg, and Apache Hudi exacerbate this issue due to their append-only write patterns and metadata-intensive operations. While compaction--the process of consolidating small files into fewer, larger files--is a common solution, existing automation mechanisms often lack the flexibility and scalability to adapt to diverse workloads and system requirements while balancing the trade-offs between compaction benefits and costs. In this paper, we present AutoComp, a scalable framework for automatic data compaction tailored to the needs of modern data lakes. Drawing on deployment experience at LinkedIn, we analyze the operational impact of small file proliferation, establish key requirements for effective automatic compaction, and demonstrate how AutoComp addresses these challenges. Our evaluation, conducted using synthetic benchmarks and production environments via integration with OpenHouse--a control plane for catalog management, schema governance, and data services--shows significant improvements in file count reduction and query performance. We believe AutoComp's built-in extensibility provides a robust foundation for evolving compaction systems, facilitating future integration of refined multi-objective optimization approaches, workload-aware compaction strategies, and expanded support for broader data layout optimizations.
papers.abstract_zh: 数据湖中小文件的激增带来了重大挑战，包括查询性能下降、存储成本增加以及分布式存储系统的可扩展性瓶颈。 Delta Lake、Apache Iceberg 和 Apache Hudi 等日志结构表格式 (LST) 由于其仅追加写入模式和元数据密集型操作而加剧了此问题。虽然压缩（将小文件合并为更少、更大的文件的过程）是一种常见的解决方案，但现有的自动化机制通常缺乏灵活性和可扩展性，无法适应不同的工作负载和系统要求，同时平衡压缩收益和成本之间的权衡。在本文中，我们提出了 AutoComp，这是一个可扩展的框架，用于根据现代数据湖的需求定制自动数据压缩。借鉴 LinkedIn 的部署经验，我们分析了小文件扩散对运营的影响，建立了有效自动压缩的关键要求，并演示了 AutoComp 如何应对这些挑战。我们的评估是通过与 OpenHouse（用于目录管理、模式治理和数据服务的控制平面）集成，使用综合基准和生产环境进行的，结果显示文件数量减少和查询性能有了显着改进。我们相信，AutoComp 的内置可扩展性为不断发展的压缩系统提供了坚实的基础，有助于未来集成精细的多目标优化方法、工作负载感知压缩策略以及对更广泛的数据布局优化的扩展支持。

papers.title: Automated Database Tuning vs. Human-Based Tuning in a Simulated Stressful Work Environment: A Demonstration of the Database Gym.
papers.abstract: Machine learning (ML) has gained traction in academia and industry for database management system (DBMS) automation. Although studies demonstrate that ML-based tuning agents match or exceed human expert performance in optimizing DBMSs, researchers continue to build bespoke tuning pipelines from the ground up. The lack of a reusable infrastructure leads to redundant engineering effort and increased difficulty in comparing modeling methods. This paper demonstrates the database gym framework, a standardized training environment that provides a unified API of pluggable components. The database gym simplifies ML model training and evaluation to accelerate autonomous DBMS research. In this demonstration, we showcase the effectiveness of automated tuning and the gym's ease of use by allowing a human expert to compete against an ML-based tuning agent implemented in the gym.
papers.abstract_zh: 机器学习 (ML) 在学术界和工业界的数据库管理系统 (DBMS) 自动化方面获得了广泛关注。尽管研究表明基于 ML 的调优代理在优化 DBMS 方面可达到或超过人类专家的表现，但研究人员仍在从头开始构建定制调优管道。缺乏可重用的基础设施会导致冗余的工程工作，并增加比较建模方法的难度。本文演示了数据库gym框架，这是一个标准化的训练环境，提供了可插拔组件的统一API。数据库健身房简化了 ML 模型训练和评估，以加速自主 DBMS 研究。在此演示中，我们通过允许人类专家与健身房中实施的基于 ML 的调整代理进行竞争，展示了自动调整的有效性和健身房的易用性。

papers.title: Autotuning Systems: Techniques, Challenges, and Opportunities.
papers.abstract: The rapid growth of cloud computing and systems has introduced significant complexity in managing and optimizing configurations to meet diverse workload demands across a wide array of hardware. Autotuning systems, leveraging advancements in machine learning and optimization, offer an effective solution to these challenges. By automating configuration tuning, these systems can dynamically adapt to workload changes, optimize performance in real time, and reduce the burden on system administrators. This tutorial provides both theoretical foundations and practical demonstrations of systems autotuning software, with a focus on offline and online optimization methodologies. We present a comprehensive review of state-of-the-art autotuning systems and discuss how they address key challenges, such as handling large configuration spaces, mitigating noise in real-world environments, and ensuring safe and efficient exploration during tuning. To conclude, we offer a hands-on session where participants can experiment with an open-source system and gain experience with real-world tuning scenarios.
papers.abstract_zh: 云计算和系统的快速增长给管理和优化配置带来了极大的复杂性，以满足各种硬件上的不同工作负载需求。自动调整系统利用机器学习和优化方面的进步，为这些挑战提供了有效的解决方案。通过自动化配置调整，这些系统可以动态适应工作负载变化，实时优化性能，并减轻系统管理员的负担。本教程提供系统自动调整软件的理论基础和实践演示，重点关注离线和在线优化方法。我们对最先进的自动调谐系统进行了全面的回顾，并讨论了它们如何应对关键挑战，例如处理大型配置空间、减轻现实环境中的噪声以及确保调谐过程中安全高效的探索。最后，我们提供了一个实践课程，参与者可以尝试开源系统并获得实际调整场景的经验。

papers.title: Blink Twice - Automatic Workload Pinning and Regression Detection for Versionless Apache Spark using Retries.
papers.abstract: For many users of Apache Spark, managing Spark version upgrades is a significant interruption that typically involves a time-intensive code migration. This is mainly because in Spark, there is no clear separation between the application code and the engine code, making it hard to manage them independently (dependency clashes, use of internal APIs). In Databricks' Serverless Spark offering, we introduced Versionless Spark where we leverage Spark Connect to fully decouple the client application from the Spark engine which allows us to seamlessly upgrade Spark engine versions. In this paper, we show how our infrastructure built around Spark Connect automatically upgrades and remediates failures in automated Spark workloads without any interruption. Using Versionless Spark, Databricks users' Spark workloads run indefinitely, and always on the latest version based on a fully managed experience while retaining nearly all of the programmability of Apache Spark.
papers.abstract_zh: 对于 Apache Spark 的许多用户来说，管理 Spark 版本升级是一个重大中断，通常涉及耗时的代码迁移。这主要是因为在 Spark 中，应用程序代码和引擎代码之间没有明确的分离，导致很难独立管理它们（依赖冲突、使用内部 API）。在 Databricks 的 Serverless Spark 产品中，我们引入了 Versionless Spark，其中我们利用 Spark Connect 将客户端应用程序与 Spark 引擎完全解耦，这使我们能够无缝升级 Spark 引擎版本。在本文中，我们展示了围绕 Spark Connect 构建的基础设施如何自动升级并修复自动化 Spark 工作负载中的故障，而不会造成任何中断。使用 Versionless Spark，Databricks 用户的 Spark 工作负载可以无限期运行，并且始终基于完全托管体验的最新版本，同时保留 Apache Spark 的几乎所有可编程性。

papers.title: Catching up with Disorder: Dynamic Graphs with Out-of-Order Updates.
papers.abstract:
Several real-time applications rely on dynamic graphs to model and store data arriving from multiple streams, potentially distributed. Providing both high ingestion rate and efficient analytics with transactional guarantees over such dynamic graphs is challenging, even more so when updates may be received out-of-order at the database. We propose to demonstrate HAL [2], a novel in-memory dynamic graph database system, which addresses these challenges. HAL outperforms comparable systems by a factor of up to 73× in terms of update processing throughput and up to 357× for analytics, while being the first to support out-of-order updates.

Demo video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S-Qi6Oj68oU
papers.abstract_zh:
一些实时应用程序依赖动态图来建模和存储来自多个可能分布的流的数据。在此类动态图上提供高摄取率和高效分析以及事务保证是具有挑战性的，当数据库中可能无序接收更新时更是如此。我们建议展示 HAL [2]，一种新颖的内存动态图数据库系统，它可以解决这些挑战。 HAL 在更新处理吞吐量方面比同类系统高出 73 倍，在分析方面比同类系统高出 357 倍，同时是第一个支持乱序更新的系统。

演示视频：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S-Qi6Oj68oU

papers.title: CausaLens: A System for Summarizing Causal DAGs.
papers.abstract: Causal inference aids researchers in discovering causal relationships, leading to scientific insights. Pearl's causal model uses causal DAGs to estimate causal effects, so DAG correctness is essential for reliable causal conclusions. However, for high-dimensional data, the causal DAGs are often complex beyond human verifiability. Graph summarization is a logical next step, but current methods for general-purpose graph summarization are inadequate for causal DAG summarization, as they are not designed to preserve causal information. In this demonstration, we present a system called CausaLens that summarizes a given causal DAG and balances graph simplification for better understanding and retention of essential causal information for reliable inference directly on the summary DAG. We illustrate that causal inference on the summary DAG is more robust to misspecification in the initial causal DAG compared to performing inference directly on the initial causal DAG, thereby enhancing the robustness of causal inference. We will demonstrate the utility of CausaLens for generating useful summary causal DAGs by interacting with the SIGMOD'25 participants, who will act as data analysts aiming to perform causal analysis on high dimensional datasets.
papers.abstract_zh: 因果推理帮助研究人员发现因果关系，从而获得科学见解。 Pearl 的因果模型使用因果 DAG 来估计因果效应，因此 DAG 的正确性对于可靠的因果结论至关重要。然而，对于高维数据，因果 DAG 通常非常复杂，超出了人类的可验证性。图摘要是合乎逻辑的下一步，但当前的通用图摘要方法不足以用于因果 DAG 摘要，因为它们并非旨在保留因果信息。在此演示中，我们提出了一个名为 CausaLens 的系统，该系统总结给定的因果 DAG 并平衡图简化，以便更好地理解和保留基本因果信息，以便直接在摘要 DAG 上进行可靠的推理。我们说明，与直接在初始因果 DAG 上执行推理相比，摘要 DAG 上的因果推理对于初始因果 DAG 中的错误指定更加稳健，从而增强了因果推理的稳健性。我们将通过与 SIGMOD'25 参与者交互来演示 CausaLens 在生成有用的摘要因果 DAG 方面的实用性，这些参与者将充当数据分析师，旨在对高维数据集执行因果分析。

papers.title: CausalExplain: Causal Explanations of Black-box Models with Training Data Subsets.
papers.abstract: AI models are becoming increasingly opaque, prompting significant efforts to explain their predictions. However, data-based explanations-i.e., explaining predictions using training data points-remain largely underexplored. Such explanations are essential for debugging training data and gaining deeper insights into model behavior. We introduce a demo of CausalExplain, a model-agnostic system designed to trace the predictions of popular machine learning (ML) models back to their corresponding training data points. For any given prediction, CausalExplain provides explanations based on predicates from the training data that caused the outcome. These explanations are derived from the causal DAG of the training data, with CausalExplain presenting the user with the top- k data-centric explanations for a given query row. In this demo, participants will explore how causal DAGs support the generation of such explanations across various datasets and underlying ML models.
papers.abstract_zh: 人工智能模型变得越来越不透明，促使人们付出巨大努力来解释他们的预测。然而，基于数据的解释（即使用训练数据点解释预测）在很大程度上仍未得到充分探索。这些解释对于调试训练数据和深入了解模型行为至关重要。我们介绍了 CausalExplain 的演示，这是一个与模型无关的系统，旨在将流行机器学习 (ML) 模型的预测追溯到其相应的训练数据点。对于任何给定的预测，CausalExplain 根据导致结果的训练数据中的谓词提供解释。这些解释源自训练数据的因果 DAG，CausalExplain 向用户提供给定查询行的前 k 个以数据为中心的解释。在此演示中，参与者将探索因果 DAG 如何支持跨各种数据集和底层 ML 模型生成此类解释。

papers.title: CauSumX: Summarized Causal Explanations For Group-By-Average Queries.
papers.abstract: Group-by-average SQL queries are a cornerstone of data analysis, often employed to uncover patterns and trends within datasets. However, interpreting the results of these queries can be challenging and time-intensive, particularly when working with large, high-dimensional datasets. Automating the generation of explanations for such queries can greatly enhance analysts' ability to derive meaningful insights while reducing human effort. Effective explanations must balance succinctness and depth, offering insights into different patterns across aggregate results, while crucially reflecting cause-effect relationships rather than mere correlations. This ensures that users can make informed, data-driven decisions grounded in reality. In this demonstration, we present CauSumX, a system that produces concise and causal explanations for group-by-average queries. Leveraging background causal knowledge, CauSumX identifies the key causal factors driving variations in the outcome variable across different groups. The system employs an efficient algorithm based on a recently published paper. We will demonstrate the utility of CauSumX for generating useful summarized causal explanations by interacting with the SIGMOD'25 participants, who will act as data analysts aiming to explain their query results.
papers.abstract_zh: 按平均值分组 SQL 查询是数据分析的基石，通常用于发现数据集中的模式和趋势。然而，解释这些查询的结果可能具有挑战性且耗时，特别是在处理大型高维数据集时。自动生成此类查询的解释可以极大地增强分析师获得有意义的见解的能力，同时减少人力。有效的解释必须平衡简洁性和深度，提供对总体结果中不同模式的洞察，同时关键地反映因果关系而不仅仅是相关性。这确保用户能够基于现实做出明智的、数据驱动的决策。在此演示中，我们展示了 CauSumX，这是一个为按平均值分组的查询生成简洁且因果解释的系统。利用背景因果知识，CauSumX 确定了驱动不同群体结果变量变化的关键因果因素。该系统采用基于最近发表的论文的高效算法。我们将演示 CauSumX 通过与 SIGMOD'25 参与者交互来生成有用的总结性因果解释的实用性，这些参与者将充当数据分析师，旨在解释他们的查询结果。

papers.title: ChARLES: Change-Aware Recovery of Latent Evolution Semantics in Relational Data.
papers.abstract:
Data-driven decision-making is at the core of many modern applications, and understanding the data is critical in supporting trust in these decisions. However, data is dynamic and evolving, just like the real-world entities it represents. Thus, an important component of understanding data is analyzing and drawing insights from the changes it undergoes. Existing methods for exploring data change list differences exhaustively, which are not interpretable by humans, and fail to provide salient insights into change trends.

We demonstrate ChARLES, a system that derives semantic summaries of changes between two snapshots of an evolving database, in an effective, concise, and interpretable way. Our key observation is that while datasets often evolve through point and other small-batch updates, rich data features can reveal latent semantics that can intuitively summarize the changes. Under the hood, ChARLES compares database versions, infers feasible transformations by fitting multiple regression lines over different data partitions to derive change summaries, and ranks them. ChARLES allows users to customize the ranking to obtain their preferred explanation by navigating the accuracy-interpretability tradeoff, and offers a proof of concept for reasoning about data evolution over real-world datasets.
papers.abstract_zh:
数据驱动的决策是许多现代应用程序的核心，理解数据对于支持对这些决策的信任至关重要。然而，数据是动态的、不断发展的，就像它所代表的现实世界实体一样。因此，理解数据的一个重要组成部分是分析数据所经历的变化并从中汲取见解。现有的探索数据变化的方法详尽地列出了人类无法解释的差异，并且无法提供对变化趋势的显着洞察。

我们演示了 ChARLES，这是一个以有效、简洁和可解释的方式导出不断发展的数据库的两个快照之间变化的语义摘要的系统。我们的主要观察是，虽然数据集通常通过点更新和其他小批量更新而演变，但丰富的数据特征可以揭示潜在的语义，从而可以直观地总结变化。 Under the hood, ChARLES compares database versions, infers feasible transformations by fitting multiple regression lines over different data partitions to derive change summaries, and ranks them. ChARLES 允许用户通过精确度与可解释性权衡来定制排名，以获得他们喜欢的解释，并为推理现实世界数据集上的数据演化提供概念证明。

papers.title: CloudJump II: Optimizing Cloud Databases for Shared Storage.
papers.abstract: The CloudJump framework aims to shed light on how compute-storage disaggregation can effectively enhance cloud databases' scalability, resilience, and efficiency by only relying on standard cloud storage services. To explore the evolving landscape of cloud-native databases, this study navigates through the design and implementation intricacies of the shared-storage architecture in the CloudJump framework. By focusing on the shared storage model integral to cloud database systems with a leader-follower pattern, we address the challenges of ensuring data consistency across multiple compute nodes-a cornerstone for achieving scalability in cloud environments. At its core, this study proposes Multi-Version Data (MVD), a technique designed to improve node consistency in shared storage environments. MVD enables fine-grained version management and precise reconstruction of data pages by compute nodes, facilitating streamlined recovery and robust data persistence without requiring a custom storage layer. This design simplifies operations, reduces costs, and presents a pragmatic response to the limitations of existing cloud storage approaches. The deployment of MVD in PolarDB demonstrates its practical benefits, including enhanced node decoupling and accelerated failover. The performance and reliability of the enhanced system, CloudJump II, are substantiated through comprehensive experimental evaluation and production usage.
papers.abstract_zh: CloudJump 框架旨在阐明计算存储分解如何仅依靠标准云存储服务来有效增强云数据库的可扩展性、弹性和效率。为了探索云原生数据库不断发展的前景，本研究探讨了 CloudJump 框架中共享存储架构的设计和实现的复杂性。通过专注于具有领导者-跟随者模式的云数据库系统集成的共享存储模型，我们解决了确保跨多个计算节点的数据一致性的挑战，这是在云环境中实现可扩展性的基石。本研究的核心提出了多版本数据（MVD），这是一种旨在提高共享存储环境中节点一致性的技术。 MVD 支持计算节点的细粒度版本管理和数据页的精确重建，从而促进简化的恢复和强大的数据持久性，而无需自定义存储层。这种设计简化了操作，降低了成本，并对现有云存储方法的局限性做出了务实的回应。在PolarDB中部署MVD展示了它的实际好处，包括增强的节点解耦和加速故障转移。增强型系统 CloudJump II 的性能和可靠性通过全面的实验评估和生产使用得到证实。

papers.title: CockroachDB Serverless: Sub-second Scaling from Zero with Multi-region Cluster Virtualization.
papers.abstract: The Serverless database paradigm offers the promise of letting users pay for only what they consume, and scaling quickly and transparently as workload demands change. In this paper, we present novel extensions to CockroachDB that allow for the deployment of a multi-tenant, multi-region transactional database, which can be consumed by users in a Serverless fashion. The key architectural change enabling this new deployment model is a two-tiered approach that separates the SQL and key-value (KV) layers into different processes. This separation enables independent scaling, efficient resource sharing, and tenant isolation. We describe how we have deployed this system in a production service offering, generally available since 2022, and how we achieve scaling-to-zero, sub-second cold starts, and data and performance isolation.
papers.abstract_zh: 无服务器数据库范式承诺让用户只为他们使用的内容付费，并随着工作负载需求的变化快速、透明地扩展。在本文中，我们提出了 CockroachDB 的新颖扩展，允许部署多租户、多区域事务数据库，用户可以以无服务器方式使用该数据库。支持这种新部署模型的关键架构变化是一种两层方法，它将 SQL 和键值 (KV) 层分离到不同的进程中。这种分离可以实现独立扩展、高效资源共享和租户隔离。我们描述了如何在自 2022 年起普遍提供的生产服务产品中部署该系统，以及如何实现零扩展、亚秒级冷启动以及数据和性能隔离。

papers.title: CORE+: A Complex Event Recognition Engine in C++.
papers.abstract: Complex Event Recognition (CER) refers to the activity of analyzing streams of continuously arriving event data, to recognize collections of events that satisfy user-defined patterns. CER is known to be computationally challenging, since it requires maintaining a set of partial matches, and this set quickly grows super-linearly in the number of processed events. In this paper we demonstrate CORE+, a mature COmplex event Recognition Engine that circumvents the super-linear partial match problem. CORE+ is based on a research prototype named CORE; we demonstrate the new features and capabilities that make CORE+ a release version of CORE.
papers.abstract_zh: 复杂事件识别 (CER) 是指分析连续到达的事件数据流以识别满足用户定义模式的事件集合的活动。众所周知，CER 在计算上具有挑战性，因为它需要维护一组部分匹配，并且该组在处理事件的数量上快速超线性增长。在本文中，我们演示了 CORE+，这是一个成熟的复杂事件识别引擎，可以规避超线性部分匹配问题。 CORE+基于名为CORE的研究原型；我们展示了使 CORE+ 成为 CORE 发行版本的新特性和功能。

papers.title: D-Bot: An LLM-Powered DBA Copilot.
papers.abstract: Database administrators (DBAs) play an important role in maintaining the high performance and high availability of database systems. However, database diagnosis by DBAs often takes tedious efforts and time, which is insufficient for the large amount of database instances (e.g., millions of instances on the cloud). Besides, existing diagnosis tools built with rules and small-scale learned models lack the capability for flexible reasoning and report generation, and thus can only serve as ''one small piece of a bigger puzzle''. Recently, Large language models (LLMs) have exhibited superiority in natural language understanding, reasoning and generation, positioning them as a potential comprehensive copilot to DBAs to address these limitations. Thus, we introduce D-Bot , an LLM-powered DBA copilot that can automatically acquire pertinent knowledge from diagnostic documents, interact with users for self-refinement, and generate reasonable and well-founded diagnosis reports (i.e., specifying root causes, solutions, and references). We will show that D-Bot can effectively automate database diagnosis in real scenarios, even for complex anomalies.
papers.abstract_zh: 数据库管理员（DBA）在维护数据库系统的高性能和高可用性方面发挥着重要作用。然而，DBA的数据库诊断往往需要繁琐的工作和时间，这对于大量数据库实例（例如云上数百万个实例）来说是不够的。此外，现有的基于规则和小规模学习模型构建的诊断工具缺乏灵活的推理和报告生成的能力，因此只能充当“更大拼图中的一小部分”。最近，大型语言模型 (LLM) 在自然语言理解、推理和生成方面表现出了优越性，将其定位为 DBA 解决这些局限性的潜在综合副驾驶。因此，我们引入了 D-Bot，一个由 LLM 驱动的 DBA 副驾驶，可以自动从诊断文档中获取相关知识，与用户交互进行自我完善，并生成合理且有依据的诊断报告（即指定根本原因、解决方案和参考）。我们将展示 D-Bot 可以在实际场景中有效地自动化数据库诊断，即使是复杂的异常情况。

papers.title: DANTE: Hybrid AI System for Context-Aware Interpretable Feature Engineering.
papers.abstract: Machine learning has become essential in data management, enabling accuracy and efficiency in handling complex datasets. For structured data, however, feature engineering is required to enhance predictive performance but remains a time-consuming, domain-specific task. Moreover, as ML systems are increasingly adopted, ensuring interpretability, particularly for domain experts, has become important. To address these challenges, we proposed KRAFT , an automated feature engineering (AutoFE) approach that combines a neural generator for feature transformation with a knowledge-based discriminator to ensure interpretability through symbolic reasoning. We have also empirically shown that KRAFT outperforms SOTA approaches in both accuracy and interpretability. In this demonstration, we introduce DANTE, an interactive tool based on KRAFT to facilitate feature generation and model evaluation on structured data. Its no-code interface empowers users to generate meaningful, interpretable features for real-world applications. A demonstration video of DANTE is available at https://DANTE.com.
papers.abstract_zh: 机器学习在数据管理中已变得至关重要，可以提高处理复杂数据集的准确性和效率。然而，对于结构化数据，需要特征工程来增强预测性能，但这仍然是一项耗时的、特定于领域的任务。此外，随着机器学习系统越来越多地被采用，确保可解释性（特别是对于领域专家而言）变得非常重要。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了 KRAFT，这是一种自动化特征工程 (AutoFE) 方法，它将用于特征转换的神经生成器与基于知识的鉴别器相结合，以确保通过符号推理的可解释性。我们还凭经验证明，KRAFT 在准确性和可解释性方面均优于 SOTA 方法。在本次演示中，我们介绍了 DANTE，这是一种基于 KRAFT 的交互式工具，可促进结构化数据的特征生成和模型评估。其无代码界面使用户能够为现实应用程序生成有意义的、可解释的功能。 DANTE 的演示视频可在 https://DANTE.com 上获取。

papers.title: Data Storage and Management for Image AI Pipelines.
papers.abstract:
Image AI is essential for various applications, such as self-driving cars, medical imaging, and smart farming. Data management is key for efficient image AI, from how to store images to how to manage data while processing the images. This tutorial overviews the emerging area of image AI pipelines by combining approaches from various cross-disciplinary areas such as data management, digital signal processing, computer vision, and machine learning. We specifically focus on image storage and data management.

The tutorial first gives an overview of image AI pipelines step by step, how they work and the main the challenges. We then describe the main approaches to making image AI pipelines more efficient. We first cover how image AI pipelines store images based on standard storage formats, learned formats, task-specific learned formats, and self-designed formats. Second, we cover how state-of-the-art approaches manage data within image AI pipelines. We identify and describe three main approaches to making image AI pipelines more efficient by efficiently managing data within the pipeline: (i) compressing intermediate data, (ii) materializing and re-using data objects, and (iii) parallelism for better hardware utilization. Lastly, the tutorial covers open data management and systems problems and future directions in making image AI pipelines more efficient.
papers.abstract_zh:
图像人工智能对于自动驾驶汽车、医学成像和智能农业等各种应用至关重要。数据管理是高效图像人工智能的关键，从如何存储图像到如何在处理图像时管理数据。本教程结合数据管理、数字信号处理、计算机视觉和机器学习等各种跨学科领域的方法，概述了图像 AI 管道的新兴领域。我们特别关注图像存储和数据管理。

本教程首先逐步概述图像 AI 管道、它们的工作原理以及主要挑战。然后，我们描述了提高图像 AI 管道效率的主要方法。我们首先介绍图像人工智能管道如何基于标准存储格式、学习格式、特定于任务的学习格式和自行设计的格式来存储图像。其次，我们介绍最先进的方法如何管理图像人工智能管道中的数据。我们确定并描述了通过有效管理管道内的数据来提高图像人工智能管道效率的三种主要方法：（i）压缩中间数据，（ii）具体化和重用数据对象，以及（iii）并行性以提高硬件利用率。最后，本教程涵盖了开放数据管理和系统问题以及提高图像 AI 管道效率的未来方向。

papers.title: Data+AI: LLM4Data and Data4LLM.
papers.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized traditional data management systems by their natural language processing capabilities (e.g., understanding, reasoning, generation, few-shot and zero-shot learning), while data management techniques play a vital role in optimizing AI models (e.g., data preparation, data efficient training and inference). This establishes a two-way relationship where AI enhances data management, and data boosts AI capabilities. This tutorial covers recent advancements and challenges at this intersection, focusing on LLM4Data and Data4LLM over four parts. Initially, we discuss the background and motivations for integrating data and AI, and present the challenges and principles of LLM4Data and Data4LLM. We then explore how LLMs optimize data management by offering practical applications like managing unstructured data. We also examine how data management optimizes AI, particularly in training and fine-tuning LLMs, showcasing techniques for data preparation and inference. Finally, we provide open challenges and future research directions, aiming to drive innovation in both fields.
papers.abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）凭借其自然语言处理能力（例如理解、推理、生成、小样本和零样本学习）彻底改变了传统的数据管理系统，而数据管理技术在优化人工智能模型（例如数据准备、数据高效训练和推理）方面发挥着至关重要的作用。这建立了一种双向关系，人工智能增强数据管理，数据增强人工智能能力。本教程涵盖了该交叉点的最新进展和挑战，重点关注 LLM4Data 和 Data4LLM 的四个部分。首先，我们讨论了数据与人工智能整合的背景和动机，并提出了LLM4Data和Data4LLM的挑战和原则。然后，我们探讨法学硕士如何通过提供管理非结构化数据等实际应用来优化数据管理。我们还研究数据管理如何优化人工智能，特别是在训练和微调法学硕士方面，展示数据准备和推理技术。最后，我们提供了开放的挑战和未来的研究方向，旨在推动这两个领域的创新。

papers.title: Database as Runtime: Compiling LLMs to SQL for In-database Model Serving.
papers.abstract: Deploying large language models (LLMs) often requires specialized hardware and complex frameworks, creating barriers for CPU-based environments with resource constraints. These systems, common in air-gapped or edge scenarios, lack support for maintenance due to security, budget, or technical limits. To address this, we introduce TranSQL + , a compiler that translates LLM inference into SQL queries, enabling deployment on relational databases. By converting transformer operations into relational algebra, TranSQL + generates vector-oriented SQL queries that leverage native database features (buffer management, indexing) to manage computations without hardware accelerators or deep learning frameworks. Demonstrated with the LLaMA3.1 8B model on DuckDB, results show relational databases can effectively serve LLMs, reducing deployment barriers and expanding access to advanced AI.
papers.abstract_zh: 部署大型语言模型 (LLM) 通常需要专门的硬件和复杂的框架，这为具有资源限制的基于 CPU 的环境造成了障碍。这些系统常见于气隙或边缘场景，由于安全、预算或技术限制而缺乏维护支持。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 TranSQL +，这是一个将 LLM 推理转换为 SQL 查询的编译器，从而可以在关系数据库上进行部署。通过将转换器操作转换为关系代数，TranSQL + 生成面向向量的 SQL 查询，利用本机数据库功能（缓冲区管理、索引）来管理计算，无需硬件加速器或深度学习框架。通过 DuckDB 上的 LLaMA3.1 8B 模型进行演示，结果表明关系数据库可以有效地为法学硕士提供服务，减少部署障碍并扩大对高级人工智能的访问。

papers.title: Databricks Lakeguard: Supporting Fine-grained Access Control and Multi-user Capabilities for Apache Spark Workloads.
papers.abstract: Today, upgrading Apache Spark versions typically involves significant effort, with unclear investment requirements, including trial and error. This is mainly because in Spark there is no clear separation between the application code and the engine code. Apart from changes in the public API, any Spark internal changes may affect user workloads as users may rely on Spark internals: bug fixes in the Spark engine, changes to internal APIs, library upgrades, or language upgrades may affect customer workloads. As a result, Spark users are often reluctant to upgrade. The downside is that performance improvements, bug fixes, and new features take significantly longer to adopt, preventing customers from quickly benefiting from these improvements. In addition, it increases engineering complexity to manage a large number of different Spark versions. For Databricks serverless jobs and notebooks, we fundamentally transformed and simplified the user experience when using Spark. We shifted user focus from managing Spark runtime versions to managing the stable API that they integrate with - we created client-versioned workloads with a versionless Spark server. Decoupling the client from the Spark engine using Spark Connect has enabled Databricks to automatically upgrade the Spark server, providing users faster access to the latest features while minimizing disruptions from both intentional and unintentional breaking changes, all without compromising workload compatibility and with zero code changes needed from the user. This approach also offers significant benefits to Databricks by streamlining the release process, consolidating usage onto fewer server versions, and reducing engineering overhead from needing to backport changes. In this demonstration, we will first briefly introduce the architectural foundation of versionless Spark, leveraging Databricks' multi-user Spark compute and Spark Connect, followed by describing in more detail how we manage seamless upgrades for our customers, and finally talk about what the user experience is.
papers.abstract_zh: 如今，升级 Apache Spark 版本通常需要付出巨大的努力，且投资要求不明确，包括反复试验。这主要是因为在 Spark 中，应用程序代码和引擎代码之间没有明确的分离。除了公共 API 的更改之外，任何 Spark 内部更改都可能会影响用户工作负载，因为用户可能依赖 Spark 内部结构：Spark 引擎中的错误修复、内部 API 的更改、库升级或语言升级可能会影响客户工作负载。因此，Spark 用户往往不愿意升级。缺点是性能改进、错误修复和新功能的采用需要更长的时间，从而阻止客户快速从这些改进中受益。此外，管理大量不同的 Spark 版本也增加了工程复杂性。对于 Databricks 无服务器作业和笔记本，我们从根本上改变并简化了使用 Spark 时的用户体验。我们将用户关注点从管理 Spark 运行时版本转移到管理与其集成的稳定 API - 我们使用无版本 Spark 服务器创建了客户端版本化工作负载。使用 Spark Connect 将客户端与 Spark 引擎解耦，使 Databricks 能够自动升级 Spark 服务器，使用户能够更快地访问最新功能，同时最大限度地减少有意和无意的重大更改造成的中断，所有这些都不会影响工作负载兼容性，并且用户所需的代码更改为零。这种方法还通过简化发布流程、将使用整合到更少的服务器版本以及减少因需要向后移植更改而产生的工程开销，为 Databricks 带来显着的好处。在本次演示中，我们将首先简要介绍无版本 Spark 的架构基础，利用 Databricks 的多用户 Spark 计算和 Spark Connect，然后更详细地描述我们如何为客户管理无缝升级，最后讨论用户体验是什么。

papers.title: DataDazzle: Intelligent Data Exploration through Natural Language.
papers.abstract: For over five decades, enabling non-technical users to access databases without SQL has been a major research focus. Despite recent advances in Text-to-SQL, the primary focus remains on accuracy, often overlooking real-world deployment challenges. This demo presents DataDazzle, a natural language data exploration system. It allows users to query data, review and verify answers, and benefit from recommendations, offering a comprehensive data exploration experience.
papers.abstract_zh: 五十多年来，使非技术用户无需 SQL 即可访问数据库一直是主要的研究焦点。尽管文本到 SQL 领域最近取得了进展，但主要关注点仍然是准确性，往往忽视了现实世界的部署挑战。该演示展示了 DataDazzle，一个自然语言数据探索系统。它允许用户查询数据、查看和验证答案并从建议中受益，从而提供全面的数据探索体验。

papers.title: Demo of Kishu: Time-Traveling for Computational Notebooks.
papers.abstract:
Computational notebooks (e.g., Jupyter, Google Colab) enable the interactive computing model of iteratively executing cells (i.e., a set of statements) and observing the result (e.g., model or plot), and are widely used by data scientists. However, existing notebook systems do not offer time-traveling to past states , hence certain cell executions can irreversably modify the session state (e.g., accidentally overwriting a variable, leading to a loss of work progress.

In this demo, we introduce Kishu, a new notebook system facilitating time-traveling with lightweight incremental checkpoint and restore. Kishu's frontend allows users to create manual and automatic commits for session state snapshots at various points in time, and perform undos or checkouts to any past state. Kishu's backend transparently monitors user behavior (e.g., data operations) to create efficient incremental checkpoints, which support the frontend's (also incremental) sub-second checkouts to past states. Through this demo, we will show Kishu's ability to mitigate two common pain points of notebook usage through time-traveling: undoing cell executions and facilitating path-based exploration.
papers.abstract_zh:
计算笔记本（例如 Jupyter、Google Colab）支持迭代执行单元（即一组语句）和观察结果（例如模型或绘图）的交互式计算模型，并被数据科学家广泛使用。然而，现有的笔记本系统不提供到过去状态的时间旅行，因此某些单元执行可以不可逆地修改会话状态（例如，意外覆盖变量，导致工作进度丢失。

在此演示中，我们介绍 Kishu，这是一种新的笔记本系统，通过轻量级增量检查点和恢复来促进时间旅行。 Kishu 的前端允许用户在不同时间点为会话状态快照创建手动和自动提交，并对任何过去的状态执行撤消或签出。 Kishu 的后端透明地监视用户行为（例如数据操作）以创建高效的增量检查点，这支持前端（也是增量的）对过去状态的亚秒检查。通过这个演示，我们将展示 Kishu 通过时间旅行缓解笔记本使用的两个常见痛点的能力：撤消单元执行和促进基于路径的探索。

papers.title: Demo of LearnedWMP: Workload Memory Prediction Using Deep Query Template Representations.
papers.abstract: The database management system (DBMS) relies on a limited amount of working memory for in-memory operations during query execution. Inaccurate estimations can result in suboptimal query performance and potential failures. Achieving precise working memory estimations is therefore crucial to enhance the DBMS efficiency. We present LearnedWMP, a novel approach for simultaneously estimating working memory for a set of queries (i.e., a workload), as opposed to the conventional practice of estimating resources separately for each query. In a series of comprehensive experiments, the LearnedWMP model demonstrates improved accuracy in memory estimation and faster performance during both training and inference when compared to standard methods. The findings highlight the effectiveness and potential impact of the LearnedWMP approach on optimizing query performance. The audience can explore LearnedWMP through various scenarios showcasing its estimation accuracy and internal model workings.
papers.abstract_zh: 数据库管理系统 (DBMS) 依赖于有限数量的工作内存来执行查询执行期间的内存操作。不准确的估计可能会导致查询性能不佳和潜在的失败。因此，实现精确的工作记忆估计对于提高 DBMS 效率至关重要。我们提出 LearnedWMP，这是一种同时估计一组查询（即工作负载）的工作内存的新颖方法，而不是为每个查询单独估计资源的传统做法。在一系列综合实验中，与标准方法相比，LearnedWMP 模型证明了内存估计的准确性更高，训练和推理过程中的性能更快。研究结果强调了 LearnedWMP 方法对优化查询性能的有效性和潜在影响。观众可以通过各种场景探索 LearnedWMP，展示其估计准确性和内部模型的工作原理。

papers.title: Demonstrating CatDB: LLM-based Generation of Data-centric ML Pipelines.
papers.abstract: AutoML systems automate finding Machine Learning (ML) pipelines but struggle to scale with large datasets due to time-consuming data analysis and complex hyper-parameter search spaces. LLMs (Large Language Models) offer flexibility and scalability for code generation with strong generalization across coding tasks. However, generating data-centric ML pipeline scripts is more challenging, as it requires complex reasoning to align the needs of a dataset with coding tasks, such as data cleaning or feature transformation. Thus, LLMs struggle to generate effective and efficient ML pipelines. This demo paper presents CatDB, which overcomes these challenges by dynamically generating dataset-specific instructions to guide LLMs in generating effective pipelines. CatDB profiles datasets to extract metadata, including refined data catalog information and statistics, and then uses this metadata to break down pipeline generation into instructions of tasks such as data cleaning, transformation, and model training, tailored to specifics of the dataset at hand. This process enables CatDB to leverage LLM coding capabilities more effectively. Our evaluation shows CatDB outperforms existing LLM-based and AutoML systems with up to orders of magnitude faster runtime on large datasets. The audience will experience CatDB's capabilities with commercial and open-source LLMs, using a variety of real datasets, as shown in our demo video and Colab notebook.
papers.abstract_zh: AutoML 系统可以自动查找机器学习 (ML) 管道，但由于耗时的数据分析和复杂的超参数搜索空间，难以扩展大型数据集。 LLM（大型语言模型）为代码生成提供灵活性和可扩展性，并具有跨编码任务的强大泛化能力。然而，生成以数据为中心的 ML 管道脚本更具挑战性，因为它需要复杂的推理来使数据集的需求与编码任务（例如数据清理或特征转换）保持一致。因此，法学硕士很难生成有效且高效的机器学习管道。本演示论文介绍了 CatDB，它通过动态生成特定于数据集的指令来指导法学硕士生成有效的流程，从而克服了这些挑战。 CatDB 对数据集进行分析以提取元数据，包括精炼的数据目录信息和统计信息，然后使用此元数据将管道生成分解为数据清理、转换和模型训练等任务的指令，并根据当前数据集的具体情况进行定制。此过程使 CatDB 能够更有效地利用 LLM 编码功能。我们的评估表明，CatDB 的性能优于现有的基于 LLM 和 AutoML 系统，在大型数据集上运行速度提高了几个数量级。观众将使用各种真实数据集体验 CatDB 与商业和开源 LLM 的功能，如我们的演示视频和 Colab 笔记本中所示。

papers.title: Demonstrating CEDAR: A System for Cost-Efficient Data-Driven Claim Verification.
papers.abstract: We present CEDAR, a system for cost-efficient, data-driven claim verification using large language models (LLMs). Given a collection of text documents containing claims that can be verified from relational data, CEDAR maps claims to SQL queries for automated verification. The system employs multiple verification approaches, from cost-effective zero-shot LLM invocations to more accurate but expensive iterative, agent-based methods. CEDAR dynamically selects and combines these methods, optimizing for accuracy and cost through cost-based optimization and parameter tuning. Visitors can interact with CEDAR to observe its behavior across various scenarios and data sets. They can explore how the system prioritizes verification approaches, starting with inexpensive methods and escalating to more advanced ones as needed. Users can experiment with CEDAR by modifying claims, data sets, and tuning parameters, observing how these changes influence verification accuracy and cost. Additionally, visitors can upload their own data sets to experience CEDAR's capabilities in real-world claim verification tasks. The demonstration highlights CEDAR's adaptability and its ability to balance cost and accuracy in customizable claim verification workflows.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们推出了 CEDAR，这是一种使用大型语言模型 (LLM) 进行经济高效、数据驱动的索赔验证的系统。给定包含可以从关系数据验证的声明的文本文档集合，CEDAR 将声明映射到 SQL 查询以进行自动验证。该系统采用多种验证方法，从经济高效的零样本 LLM 调用到更准确但昂贵的基于代理的迭代方法。 CEDAR 动态选择和组合这些方法，通过基于成本的优化和参数调整来优化准确性和成本。访问者可以与 CEDAR 交互，观察其在各种场景和数据集中的行为。他们可以探索系统如何优先考虑验证方法，从廉价的方法开始，根据需要升级到更先进的方法。用户可以通过修改声明、数据集和调整参数来试验 CEDAR，观察这些变化如何影响验证准确性和成本。此外，访问者还可以上传自己的数据集，以体验 CEDAR 在现实世界索赔验证任务中的功能。该演示强调了 CEDAR 的适应性及其在可定制索赔验证工作流程中平衡成本和准确性的能力。

papers.title: Demonstrating MAST: An Efficient System for Point Cloud Data Analytics.
papers.abstract: The rapid growth of point cloud (PC) data calls for specialized solutions for data management and analytics. In this paper, we introduce MAST, a prototype system designed for efficient PC data analysis. MAST enables users to perform analytical queries efficiently, incorporating semantic and spatial predicates while ensuring high query accuracy. In this demonstration, we introduce the framework of the system prototype, including the storage layer, preprocessing layer, query processing layer, visualization and user feedback layer. We introduce the user interface of MAST and its workflow, and showcase the end-to-end usage of the MAST system with 2 analytical queries executed on a real PC dataset.
papers.abstract_zh: 点云 (PC) 数据的快速增长需要专门的数据管理和分析解决方案。在本文中，我们介绍了 MAST，这是一个专为高效 PC 数据分析而设计的原型系统。 MAST 使用户能够高效地执行分析查询，结合语义和空间谓词，同时确保高查询准确性。在本次演示中，我们介绍了系统原型的框架，包括存储层、预处理层、查询处理层、可视化和用户反馈层。我们介绍了 MAST 的用户界面及其工作流程，并展示了 MAST 系统的端到端用法，以及在真实 PC 数据集上执行的 2 个分析查询。

papers.title: Demonstrating PDSP-Bench: A Benchmarking System for Parallel and Distributed Stream Processing.
papers.abstract: Parallel stream processing plays a crucial role in efficiently handling massive workloads for modern data-driven applications. However, understanding the performance implications of operator parallelism in distributed environments remains a challenging task. Existing benchmarking systems primarily analyze Stream Processing Systems (SPS) using sequential operator pipelines in homogeneous, centralized environments, limiting their applicability to real-world distributed scenarios. In this demo paper, we demonstrate PDSPBench, a benchmarking system designed to systematically evaluate and understand the performance of parallel stream processing in distributed and heterogeneous environments. PDSP-B ench enables the evaluation of how SPS, such as Apache Flink and Apache Storm, leverage operator parallelism and available resources to process diverse workloads. Our benchmark includes parallel query structures derived from 15 real-world applications and 9 synthetic queries, allowing users to explore the impact of parallelism on performance by varying query parameters, data stream characteristics, and parallelism enumeration strategies, including different configurations and resource allocations in cloud-cluster environments. Our demo allows users to experience performance benchmarking of SPS using PDSP-B ench by providing interactive visualizations and performance insights of parallelism on query execution in heterogeneous resource environments.
papers.abstract_zh: 并行流处理在有效处理现代数据驱动应用程序的大量工作负载方面发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，理解分布式环境中运算符并行性的性能影响仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。现有的基准测试系统主要在同质、集中式环境中使用顺序运算符管道来分析流处理系统（SPS），限制了它们对现实世界分布式场景的适用性。在本演示论文中，我们演示了 PDSPBench，这是一个基准测试系统，旨在系统地评估和了解分布式异构环境中并行流处理的性能。 PDSP-B ench 能够评估 SPS（例如 Apache Flink 和 Apache Storm）如何利用运算符并行性和可用资源来处理不同的工作负载。我们的基准测试包括源自 15 个实际应用程序和 9 个综合查询的并行查询结构，允许用户通过改变查询参数、数据流特征和并行性枚举策略（包括云集群环境中的不同配置和资源分配）来探索并行性对性能的影响。我们的演示通过提供异构资源环境中查询执行并行性的交互式可视化和性能洞察，让用户能够体验使用 PDSP-B ench 的 SPS 性能基准测试。

papers.title: Demonstrating SQLBarber: Leveraging Large Language Models to Generate Customized and Realistic SQL Workloads.
papers.abstract: Database research and development require a large volume of SQL queries for benchmarking. However, it is difficult to obtain real SQL queries due to privacy issues, and existing SQL generation methods are limited in customization and satisfying realistic constraints. To address this problem, we propose SQLBarber, a novel system leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate customized and realistic SQL workloads. SQLBarber (a) eliminates the need for users to manually craft SQL templates in advance, while providing the flexibility to accept high-level natural language specifications to constrain the SQL templates, (b) scales efficiently to produce a large number of queries satisfying any user-defined cost distribution (e.g., cardinality, execution plan cost, or execution time), and (c) analyzes execution statistics obtained from Amazon Redshift and Snowflake to derive both the specifications for SQL templates and the cost distribution of SQL queries, ensuring that these constraints reflect real-world workloads. This demonstration allows the audience to experience SQLBarber in action: (1) provide their customized specifications on SQL templates, (2) gain insights on the effect of SQL template and predicate values on SQL costs, and (3) explore real-world specifications of templates as well as cost distributions of queries, and constrain their SQL queries to a desired specification and distribution, with the flexibility to try out different LLM and SQL cost types. A video demonstration is available at https://youtu.be/qOuAKVXXcdM.
papers.abstract_zh: 数据库研发需要大量的SQL查询进行基准测试。然而，由于隐私问题，很难获得真实的SQL查询，并且现有的SQL生成方法在定制和满足现实约束方面受到限制。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 SQLBarber，这是一种利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 来生成定制且真实的 SQL 工作负载的新颖系统。 SQLBarber (a) 消除了用户提前手动制作 SQL 模板的需要，同时提供了接受高级自然语言规范来约束 SQL 模板的灵活性，(b) 有效扩展以生成满足任何用户定义的成本分布（例如基数、执行计划成本或执行时间）的大量查询，以及 (c) 分析从 Amazon Redshift 和 Snowflake 获得的执行统计数据，以得出 SQL 模板的规范和 SQL 查询的成本分布，确保这些约束反映现实世界的工作负载。该演示使观众能够实际体验 SQLBarber：(1) 提供 SQL 模板的自定义规范，(2) 深入了解 SQL 模板和谓词值对 SQL 成本的影响，以及 (3) 探索模板的实际规范以及查询的成本分布，并将他们的 SQL 查询限制为所需的规范和分布，并可以灵活地尝试不同的 LLM 和 SQL 成本类型。视频演示请访问 https://youtu.be/qOuAKVXXcdM。

papers.title: Demonstration of DPClustX: Differentially Private Explanations for Clusters.
papers.abstract: We present DPClustX, a framework designed to generate Differential Privacy (DP)-compliant histogram-based explanations for black-box clustering results. Interpreting clustering results is challenging even without privacy constraints, and the challenge is amplified under DP, as analysts receive noisy query responses. By addressing these challenges, DPClustX enables the selection of high-quality explaining attributes and the generation of informative histograms that balance privacy and utility. Compatible with any DP-clustering algorithm, our framework provides explanations that uncover significant patterns even under strict privacy constraints. This demonstration highlights DPClustX's capabilities using real datasets, illustrating its practical utility in sensitive data analysis and its potential for enhancing the interpretability of clustering methods.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们提出了 DPClustX，这是一个旨在为黑盒聚类结果生成符合差分隐私 (DP) 的基于直方图的解释的框架。即使没有隐私限制，解释聚类结果也充满挑战，而在 DP 下，随着分析师收到嘈杂的查询响应，这一挑战会被放大。通过解决这些挑战，DPClustX 能够选择高质量的解释属性并生成平衡隐私和实用性的信息直方图。我们的框架与任何 DP 聚类算法兼容，提供的解释即使在严格的隐私限制下也能发现重要的模式。该演示强调了 DPClustX 使用真实数据集的功能，说明了其在敏感数据分析中的实用性及其增强聚类方法可解释性的潜力。

papers.title: Doctopus: A System for Budget-aware Structural Data Extraction from Unstructured Documents.
papers.abstract: Extracting structured data from unstructured documents is essential for applications like analytical SQL queries and decision-making. Strategies such as pre-trained language models (PLMs) can be employed, but they often fall short in quality. Large language models (LLMs) have shown effectiveness in attribute extraction but are costly, making them impractical for large-scale document sets. To best trade off quality and cost, we present Doctopus, a system designed for accurate and cost-effective attribute extraction. Overall, Doctopus combines LLMs with non-LLM strategies to achieve an optimal quality-cost balance. First, the system employs an index-based approach to efficiently identify and process only relevant chunks. Afterwards, it further estimates the quality of multiple strategies for each attribute. Finally, based on the cost and estimated quality, Doctopus dynamically selects the optimal strategies through budget-aware optimization. With a real-life scenario, we demonstrate that Doctopus allows users to extract attributes accurately and affordably. The corresponding video is available at https://youtu.be/Cxl_PfvZY10?si=NYoHt2SyD9KHqd6V.
papers.abstract_zh: 从非结构化文档中提取结构化数据对于分析 SQL 查询和决策等应用程序至关重要。可以采用预训练语言模型 (PLM) 等策略，但它们的质量往往较差。大型语言模型 (LLM) 在属性提取方面已显示出有效性，但成本高昂，因此对于大规模文档集来说不切实际。为了最好地权衡质量和成本，我们推出了 Doctopus，这是一个专为准确且经济高效的属性提取而设计的系统。总体而言，Doctopus 将法学硕士与非法学硕士策略相结合，以实现最佳的质量成本平衡。首先，系统采用基于索引的方法来有效地识别和处理相关的块。然后，它进一步估计每个属性的多个策略的质量。最后，根据成本和估计质量，Doctopus 通过预算感知优化动态选择最佳策略。通过现实生活场景，我们证明 Doctopus 允许用户准确且经济地提取属性。相应的视频可在 https://youtu.be/Cxl_PfvZY10?si=NYoHt2SyD9KHqd6V 上观看。

papers.title: Dynamic Pruning for Recursive Joins.
papers.abstract: This paper studies recursive join queries, which are witnessed in recent industrial applications such as event sequence analysis and traceability analysis. The recursive join query has the possibility to invoke deeply nested joins. Known pruning techniques generating pruning filters at compile time may not be able to exploit pruning opportunities, in particular for deep joins, at full. This paper explores dynamic pruning for recursive joins. Dynamic pruning generates pruning filters during query execution; a pruning filter is made based on the result of each recursive step, and it helps to more significantly reduce the amount of relation records to be accessed at the next recursive step, hence improving the query performance. This paper presents the intensive and extensive experiments that we have conducted with a commercial prototype database engine to clarify the performance benefit of dynamic pruning with four business benchmark scenarios. Our observation demonstrates that dynamic pruning significantly speeds up recursive join queries and non-recursive join queries having deeply nested joins; it works up to 135.6 times faster than the baseline case and 3.99 times faster even than the state-of-the-art pruning approach. In addition, our concise filter design only yielded negligibly small processing overheads for ineffective queries, even though the filter was intensively manipulated at runtime.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文研究了递归连接查询，这种查询在最近的工业应用中得到了体现，例如事件序列分析和可追溯性分析。递归连接查询可以调用深度嵌套的连接。在编译时生成修剪过滤器的已知修剪技术可能无法充分利用修剪机会，特别是对于深度连接。本文探讨了递归连接的动态修剪。动态剪枝在查询执行期间生成剪枝过滤器；根据每个递归步骤的结果进行剪枝过滤器，有助于更显着地减少下一个递归步骤要访问的关系记录数量，从而提高查询性能。本文介绍了我们使用商业原型数据库引擎进行的密集和广泛的实验，以阐明动态修剪在四个业务基准场景下的性能优势。我们的观察表明，动态修剪显着加速了递归连接查询和具有深度嵌套连接的非递归连接查询；它的工作速度比基准情况快 135.6 倍，甚至比最先进的剪枝方法快 3.99 倍。此外，我们简洁的过滤器设计只为无效查询产生了可以忽略不计的小处理开销，即使过滤器在运行时被密集操作。

papers.title: Eigen+: Memory Over-Subscription for Alibaba Cloud Databases.
papers.abstract:
Memory is a critical bottleneck in cloud computing. While memory over-subscription enhances resource utilization by allowing more instances per machine, it increases the risk of Out of Memory (OOM) errors, potentially compromising service availability and violating Service Level Objectives (SLOs). Traditional methods predict memory utilization based on historical data and use bin packing algorithms for resource scheduling but often fail under high utilization due to inaccurate predictions. We observe that in cloud databases, the causes of OOM errors adhere to the Pareto Principle : the majority of OOM errors originate from a minority of instances. Leveraging this insight, we transform the complex prediction problem into a classification task by identifying and managing these problematic instances.

We present Eigen+, a cluster management system for Alibaba Cloud databases. Eigen+ profiles and classifies instances to identify transients, optimizes resource scheduling to balance efficiency and reliability. In addition, it models the impact of memory over-subscription on SLO compliance to provide a quantitative basis for determining precise bin-packing constraints and optimizing over-subscription levels. Evaluations on production clusters demonstrate that Eigen+ improves the memory allocation ratio of an online MySQL cluster by 36.21% (from 75.67% to 111.88%) on average, while maintaining SLO compliance with no OOM occurrences.
papers.abstract_zh:
内存是云计算的关键瓶颈。虽然内存超额订阅通过允许每台计算机添加更多实例来提高资源利用率，但它会增加内存不足 (OOM) 错误的风险，可能会影响服务可用性并违反服务级别目标 (SLO)。传统方法根据历史数据预测内存利用率，并使用装箱算法进行资源调度，但在高利用率下常常因预测不准确而失败。我们观察到，在云数据库中，OOM 错误的原因遵循帕累托原则：大多数 OOM 错误源自少数实例。利用这种洞察力，我们通过识别和管理这些有问题的实例，将复杂的预测问题转化为分类任务。

我们推出 Eigen+，一个阿里云数据库集群管理系统。 Eigen+ 对实例进行分析和分类以识别瞬态，优化资源调度以平衡效率和可靠性。此外，它还模拟了内存超额订阅对 SLO 合规性的影响，为确定精确的装箱约束和优化超额订阅级别提供定量基础。对生产集群的评估表明，Eigen+将在线MySQL集群的内存分配率平均提高了36.21%（从75.67%到111.88%），同时保持了SLO合规性，没有发生OOM。

papers.title: Eighth International Workshop on Exploiting Artificial Intelligence Techniques for Data Management (aiDM).
papers.abstract:
Recent advances in AI techniques, along with the development of enabling hardware and infrastructure, have facilitated the integration of AI across a wide range of domains and tasks. Specifically, AI has been employed to process various types of data, including numerical, textual, and image data, and has been adopted in large-scale distributed systems. From a data management perspective, this requires the use of cutting-edge AI solutions for data management tasks and systems.

aiDM is a full-day workshop that provides a platform for innovative interdisciplinary research on the interaction between AI and data management, and the development of new AI technologies for data-related tasks. This year, aiDM'25 will focus on the transparent application of AI techniques (e.g., utilizing Generative AI frameworks) for data management in enterprise-level workloads.
papers.abstract_zh:
人工智能技术的最新进展，以及支持硬件和基础设施的发展，促进了人工智能在广泛领域和任务中的集成。具体来说，人工智能已被用来处理各种类型的数据，包括数字、文本和图像数据，并已被应用于大规模分布式系统。从数据管理的角度来看，这需要使用尖端的人工智能解决方案来执行数据管理任务和系统。

aiDM 是一个全天的研讨会，为人工智能和数据管理之间的交互以及为数据相关任务开发新的人工智能技术的创新跨学科研究提供了一个平台。今年，aiDM'25 将重点关注人工智能技术（例如利用生成式人工智能框架）在企业级工作负载中进行数据管理的透明应用。

papers.title: Eighth Joint Workshop on Graph Data Management Experiences & Systems (GRADES) and Network Data Analytics (NDA).
papers.abstract: GRADES-NDA 2025 marks the eighth joint meeting of the GRADES and NDA workshops, which have been independently co-located with the SIGMOD/PODS conference since 2013 and 2016, respectively. The workshop provides a dedicated forum for advancing the state of the art in graph data management and network analytics, fostering cross-disciplinary collaboration on recent advances in handling complex, interconnected data at scale. GRADES-NDA brings together researchers and practitioners from diverse backgrounds to explore foundational models, scalable systems, and emerging applications of graph-structured data. The 2025 edition highlights topics such as graph query languages, processing platforms, dynamic and streaming graphs, and graph-based machine learning, with growing attention to intersections with generative AI and privacy-preserving computation.
papers.abstract_zh: GRADES-NDA 2025 是 GRADES 和 NDA 研讨会的第八次联席会议，这两个研讨会自 2013 年和 2016 年以来分别与 SIGMOD/PODS 会议独立举办。该研讨会提供了一个专门的论坛，用于推进图形数据管理和网络分析的最新技术，促进跨学科合作，以解决大规模处理复杂、互连数据的最新进展。 GRADES-NDA 汇集了来自不同背景的研究人员和从业者，共同探索图结构数据的基础模型、可扩展系统和新兴应用。 2025 年版本重点介绍了图查询语言、处理平台、动态和流图以及基于图的机器学习等主题，并且越来越关注与生成式 AI 和隐私保护计算的交叉点。

papers.title: Enterprise Application-Database Co-Innovation for Hybrid Transactional/Analytical Processing: A Virtual Data Model and Its Query Optimization Needs.
papers.abstract:
The advent of hybrid transactional/analytical processing (HTAP) systems has reshaped enterprise data management, breaking the wall between transaction processing and analytics. This paper explores the co-innovation between enterprise applications and databases to advance HTAP architecture and drive its scalable adoption, as exemplified by SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP HANA. At the core of this innovation lies the Virtual Data Model (VDM), which simplifies the formulation of analytical queries directly over transactional data, making HTAP databases more accessible to application developers.

This paper delves into the complexities of the VDM, highlighting key query optimization challenges such as expansive join views, augmentation joins, and augmentation self-joins. Our findings underscore the pivotal role of robust query optimizers in achieving efficient HTAP query execution.
