By providing actionable insights into the design and optimization of database systems, this paper may serve as motivation for further research into advancing HTAP database technology.
papers.abstract_zh:
混合事务/分析处理 (HTAP) 系统的出现重塑了企业数据管理，打破了事务处理和分析之间的壁垒。本文探讨了企业应用程序和数据库之间的联合创新，以推进 HTAP 架构并推动其可扩展的采用，例如由 SAP HANA 提供支持的 SAP S/4HANA。这项创新的核心在于虚拟数据模型 (VDM)，它简化了直接对事务数据进行分析查询的制定，使应用程序开发人员更容易访问 HTAP 数据库。

本文深入研究了 VDM 的复杂性，重点介绍了关键的查询优化挑战，例如扩展连接视图、增强连接和增强自连接。我们的研究结果强调了强大的查询优化器在实现高效 HTAP 查询执行方面的关键作用。通过提供对数据库系统的设计和优化的可行见解，本文可以作为进一步研究推进 HTAP 数据库技术的动力。

papers.title: Experimental Evaluation of Optimizing Memory Consumption in SAP HANA using PEOopt.
papers.abstract:
Materialized intermediate results dominate memory consumption in query processing, which is a challenge in the age of Big Data workloads. Modern query processors use pipelines to produce materialized intermediate results. The order by which pipelines are executed influence the lifetime of materialized intermediate results, directly impacting the overall consumed memory. By scheduling the execution of pipelines, we can therefore save memory.

Our previous work provided a proof-of-concept for pipeline execution ordering to reduce memory consumption during runtime using a simple join query workload. This paper presents the experimental evaluation of an end-to-end implementation prototype called PEOopt within SAP HANA, which generalizes our previous work. For long running queries our prototype reduces the memory footprint by up to 36% without compromising any execution time performance. Hence, we prove the effectiveness of saving memory in real world database systems like SAP HANA.
papers.abstract_zh:
物化的中间结果主导查询处理中的内存消耗，这在大数据工作负载时代是一个挑战。现代查询处理器使用管道来生成具体化的中间结果。管道的执行顺序会影响具体化中间结果的生命周期，从而直接影响总体消耗的内存。通过调度管道的执行，我们可以节省内存。

我们之前的工作为管道执行排序提供了概念验证，以使用简单的连接查询工作负载减少运行时的内存消耗。本文介绍了 SAP HANA 中名为 PEOopt 的端到端实施原型的实验评估，它概括了我们之前的工作。对于长时间运行的查询，我们的原型可将内存占用量减少高达 36%，而不会影响任何执行时间性能。因此，我们证明了在 SAP HANA 等现实数据库系统中节省内存的有效性。

papers.title: Fifty Years of Transaction Processing Research.
papers.abstract: In this short paper, I recount some early history of transaction research, explain why transaction research continues to this day, and speculate about its future.
papers.abstract_zh: 在这篇简短的论文中，我回顾了交易研究的一些早期历史，解释了为什么交易研究持续至今，并推测了它的未来。

papers.title: Finding What You're Looking For: A Distribution-Aware Dataset Search Engine in Action.
papers.abstract: The growing volume of academic, commercial, and governmental datasets distributed across countless independent repositories calls for dataset search engines that can answer queries only using publicly shared metadata instead of relying on raw data access. However, the keyword search interfaces of existing metadata-based search engines fail to capture complex user needs, such as distributional requirements, thereby limiting their effectiveness. In this demonstration, we present the first end-to-end system for distribution-aware dataset search over decentralized data repositories. Our prototype combines existing search techniques with recently proposed percentile predicates to provide more powerful query capabilities. Based on our novel Dataset Query Language and a distribution-aware index, the system enables efficient, flexible search without access to raw data. To demonstrate its utility, we curated over 150,000 profiles of tabular datasets from Kaggle and enriched them with statistical information, enabling attendees to explore distribution-aware search and the trade-offs involved in system configuration.
papers.abstract_zh: 分布在无数独立存储库中的学术、商业和政府数据集数量不断增长，需要数据集搜索引擎能够仅使用公开共享的元数据而不是依赖于原始数据访问来回答查询。然而，现有的基于元数据的搜索引擎的关键词搜索界面无法捕获复杂的用户需求，例如分布需求，从而限制了其有效性。在本次演示中，我们展示了第一个端到端系统，用于在分散的数据存储库上进行分布式数据集搜索。我们的原型将现有的搜索技术与最近提出的百分位谓词相结合，以提供更强大的查询功能。基于我们新颖的数据集查询语言和分布感知索引，该系统无需访问原始数据即可实现高效、灵活的搜索。为了展示其实用性，我们整理了超过 150,000 个来自 Kaggle 的表格数据集配置文件，并用统计信息丰富了它们，使与会者能够探索分布感知搜索以及系统配置中涉及的权衡。

papers.title: First Workshop Connecting Academia and Industry on Modern Integrated Database and AI Systems (MIDAS).
papers.abstract: The MIDAS workshop is designed to foster meaningful collaboration between researchers and industry practitioners by identifying and addressing complex challenges in the field of Generative AI (GenAI) and Data. These challenges often require a longer-term research perspective while simultaneously needing to remain grounded in real-world constraints and operational scenarios.
papers.abstract_zh: MIDAS 研讨会旨在通过识别和解决生成人工智能 (GenAI) 和数据领域的复杂挑战，促进研究人员和行业从业者之间有意义的合作。这些挑战通常需要长期的研究视角，同时需要立足于现实世界的限制和操作场景。

papers.title: First Workshop on Novel Optimizations for Visionary AI Systems (NOVAS).
papers.abstract:
The first NOVAS workshop (Novel Optimizations for Visionary AI Systems) is aimed at hosting novel work at the intersection between artificial intelligence and data management. This area has emerged with the rise of transformer-based architectures, which have revolutionized data processing across modalities. While these models benefit from massive pre-training and large-context inference, there are significant challenges related to scalability, determinism, and resource constraints. These issues-long studied in the data management community-have sparked a convergence between generative AI and traditional database research.

The workshop will be held on June 22nd, in conjunction with SIGMOD/PODS 2025. The workshop solicits regular and short papers on topics including hardware and execution optimizations, high-level programming abstractions, integration of LLMs with relational databases, and new transformer architectures for structured data. By bridging together the different communities of machine learning, data systems, and information retrieval, NOVAS aims at becoming the venue to discuss, share ideas and early results, and spark new research collaborations for the next-generation of data-driven AI systems.
papers.abstract_zh:
首届 NOVAS 研讨会（有远见的人工智能系统的新颖优化）旨在举办人工智能和数据管理交叉领域的新颖工作。随着基于变压器的架构的兴起，这一领域出现了，它彻底改变了跨模式的数据处理。虽然这些模型受益于大规模预训练和大上下文推理，但仍存在与可扩展性、确定性和资源限制相关的重大挑战。这些问题在数据管理社区中长期研究，引发了生成式人工智能和传统数据库研究之间的融合。

该研讨会将于 6 月 22 日与 SIGMOD/PODS 2025 同期举行。该研讨会征集定期论文和短论文，主题包括硬件和执行优化、高级编程抽象、法学硕士与关系数据库的集成以及结构化数据的新变压器架构。通过将机器学习、数据系统和信息检索的不同社区联系在一起，NOVAS 旨在成为讨论、分享想法和早期成果的场所，并为下一代数据驱动的人工智能系统激发新的研究合作。

papers.title: Flux: Unifying Heterogeneous Infrastructure for Alibaba AnalyticDB.
papers.abstract: The growing complexity and variety of workloads in cloud data warehouses encompass both traditional analytical workloads and emerging AI-related workloads. And this diversity has highlighted the limitations of traditional CPU-based systems in processing heterogeneous workloads. Hardware acceleration, including Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) and Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), improves computational efficiency. However, the high total cost of ownership (TCO) hinders widespread adoption of the acceleration hardware in cloud data warehouses. Addressing the challenge, we introduce Flux, a unified cluster management system on heterogeneous infrastructure for Alibaba AnalyticDB. Our work begins by defining an acceleration priority score that evaluates the cost saving and acceleration ratios for different workloads. By employing this metric, we design a priority-based workload manager and a unified resource scheduler that optimizes the performance and TCOs for both the users and cloud service providers. In the end, we present empirical evaluations conducted on real-world AnalyticDB instances, demonstrating an average 4.39x acceleration, a 21.9% reduction in user TCO, and a 47% decrease in cloud service providers' TCO.
papers.abstract_zh: 云数据仓库中工作负载的复杂性和多样性不断增加，既包括传统的分析工作负载，也包括新兴的人工智能相关工作负载。而这种多样性凸显了传统的基于CPU的系统在处理异构工作负载方面的局限性。硬件加速，包括现场可编程门阵列 (FPGA) 和图形处理单元 (GPU)，可提高计算效率。然而，高昂的总体拥有成本（TCO）阻碍了加速硬件在云数据仓库中的广泛采用。为了应对这一挑战，我们推出了 Flux，这是阿里巴巴 AnalyticDB 异构基础设施上的统一集群管理系统。我们的工作首先定义加速优先级分数，评估不同工作负载的成本节省和加速比。通过采用这个指标，我们设计了一个基于优先级的工作负载管理器和一个统一的资源调度程序，可以优化用户和云服务提供商的性能和 TCO。最后，我们对真实的 AnalyticDB 实例进行了实证评估，结果显示平均加速为 4.39 倍，用户 TCO 降低 21.9%，云服务提供商 TCO 降低 47%。

papers.title: Fourth International Workshop on Data Systems Education (DataEd'25).
papers.abstract: Interest in data systems education is increasing, especially with the rise in demand for well-trained and re-trained data scientists. The database and computing education research communities have complementary perspectives and experiences to share with each other. The DataEd workshop is organized as a dedicated venue for these communities to come together to share findings, cross-pollinate perspectives and methods, and shed light on opportunities for mutual progress in data systems education. In the DataEd 2025 workshop, we will present and discuss data management systems education experiences and research with a keynote speaker, an industry speaker, and paper presentations followed by rich discussions.
papers.abstract_zh: 人们对数据系统教育的兴趣与日俱增，尤其是随着对训练有素和再培训的数据科学家的需求不断增加。数据库和计算教育研究界具有互补的观点和经验可以相互分享。 DataEd 研讨会是为这些社区提供一个专门的场所，让他们聚集在一起分享研究成果、交叉传播观点和方法，并揭示数据系统教育方面共同进步的机会。在 DataEd 2025 研讨会上，我们将与主题演讲者、行业演讲者以及论文演示和丰富的讨论一起展示和讨论数据管理系统教育经验和研究。

papers.title: GES: High-Performance Graph Processing Engine and Service in Huawei.
papers.abstract: This paper presents the Graph Engine Service (GES), an advanced graph database management system characterized by its composable architecture and highly factorized query executor. This design enables GES to manage a substantial volume of concurrent queries with remarkable efficiency. GES currently holds the top position in the authoritative LDBC-SNB-DECLARATIVE benchmark rankings, showcasing its superior performance - exceeding the throughput of the second-place system by over three orders of magnitude, which provides its customers with exceptional cost-effectiveness and extraordinary user experiences.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文提出了图形引擎服务（GES），这是一种先进的图形数据库管理系统，其特点是可组合架构和高度分解的查询执行器。这种设计使 GES 能够以卓越的效率管理大量并发查询。目前，GES在权威的LDBC-SNB-DECLARATIVE基准排名中位居榜首，展示了其卓越的性能——超出第二名系统吞吐量三个数量级以上，为客户提供了卓越的成本效益和非凡的用户体验。

papers.title: Grafixer: Enabling User-Centric Repairs for Property Graphs.
papers.abstract: We introduce Grafixer, an innovative interactive tool that enables human-in-the-loop repair of property graphs. By leveraging user expertise, Grafixer allows collaborative identification and correction of data inconsistencies while ensuring an efficient repair process. Users can upload property graph datasets and define constraints using Cypher, a widely adopted query language. The system efficiently manages overlapping data violations, ensuring that multiple users can work simultaneously on independent repairs without conflicts. Additionally, Grafixer provides an interactive dashboard that allows administrators to monitor real-time statistics, track progress, and oversee the repair process. In our demo, we showcase the complete repair workflow from both the administrator's and users' perspectives. Administrators can configure the repair process, monitor ongoing corrections, and assess the overall status of the graph. Meanwhile, users engage in the repair process by reviewing violations, proposing corrections, and contributing to improving data quality through an intuitive and guided interface.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们推出 Grafixer，这是一种创新的交互式工具，可以实现属性图的人机交互修复。通过利用用户的专业知识，Grafixer 可以协作识别和纠正数据不一致，同时确保高效的修复过程。用户可以使用 Cypher（一种广泛采用的查询语言）上传属性图数据集并定义约束。该系统有效地管理重叠的数据违规，确保多个用户可以同时进行独立修复而不会发生冲突。此外，Grafixer 还提供了一个交互式仪表板，允许管理员监控实时统计数据、跟踪进度并监督修复过程。在我们的演示中，我们从管理员和用户的角度展示了完整的修复工作流程。管理员可以配置修复过程、监控正在进行的纠正并评估图表的整体状态。同时，用户通过直观的引导界面审查违规行为、提出纠正建议并帮助提高数据质量，从而参与修复过程。

papers.title: Graphy'our Data: Towards End-to-End Modeling, Exploring and Generating Report from Raw Data.
papers.abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) excel at single-document queries and conversational workflows, they struggle with progressively exploring, analyzing, and synthesizing large unstructured document sets, such as in literature surveys. We address this challenge -- termed Progressive Document Investigation -- by introducing Graphy, an end-to-end platform that automates data modeling, exploration and high-quality report generation in a user-friendly manner. Graphy comprises an offline Scrapper that transforms raw documents into a graph, and an online Surveyor that enables iterative exploration and LLM-driven report generation. We showcase a pre-scrapped graph of over 50,000 papers, demonstrating how Graphy facilitates the literature-survey scenario, with video available at https://youtu.be/uM4nzkAdGlM.
papers.abstract_zh: 虽然大型语言模型 (LLM) 擅长单文档查询和对话工作流程，但它们在逐步探索、分析和综合大型非结构化文档集（例如文献调查）方面遇到困难。我们通过引入 Graphy 来应对这一挑战（称为渐进式文档调查），Graphy 是一个端到端平台，可以以用户友好的方式自动进行数据建模、探索和高质量报告生成。 Graphy 包括一个离线 Scrapper（将原始文档转换为图表）和一个在线 Surveyor（支持迭代探索和 LLM 驱动的报告生成）。我们展示了超过 50,000 篇论文的预先废弃的图表，展示了 Graphy 如何促进文献调查场景，视频可在 https://youtu.be/uM4nzkAdGlM 上观看。

papers.title: How DuckDB is USING KEY to Unlock Recursive Query Performance.
papers.abstract: SQL's recursive common table expressions (CTEs) can express complex computations over tabular data. Their accumulative semantics---which collects all intermediate results in a union table---may incur substantial space and runtime overhead, however. This paper takes the recently proposed USING KEY variant of recursive CTEs as a starting point and demonstrates it as a production-ready feature in D uck DB. This CTE variant admits queries to selectively ''overwrite'' prior intermediate results, which ultimately leads to substantially smaller union tables and runtime savings. We present the changes we made to D uck DB to support this new CTE form and showcase the performance of the USING KEY variant using the LDBC graph instances. The demonstration features a fully functional implementation of USING KEY in a D uck DB instance, pre-loaded with LDBC graphs, and a large set of queries that demonstrate the benefits. The demonstration will be interactive, allowing attendees to play with sample SQL queries and data.
papers.abstract_zh: SQL 的递归公用表表达式 (CTE) 可以表达对表格数据的复杂计算。然而，它们的累积语义（将所有中间结果收集在联合表中）可能会产生大量的空间和运行时开销。本文以最近提出的递归 CTE 的 USING KEY 变体为起点，并将其演示为 Duck DB 中的生产就绪功能。此 CTE 变体允许查询有选择地“覆盖”先前的中间结果，这最终会导致联合表变得更小并节省运行时间。我们介绍了对 Duck DB 所做的更改，以支持这种新的 CTE 形式，并展示使用 LDBC 图实例的 USING KEY 变体的性能。该演示在 Duck DB 实例中实现了 USING KEY 的全功能实现，预加载了 LDBC 图，以及展示其优势的大量查询。该演示将是交互式的，允许与会者使用示例 SQL 查询和数据。

papers.title: How to Build A Brain.
papers.abstract: My previous talk at SIGMOD/PODS happened exactly three decades ago. That was the moment when the world of computation and the field of databases were transformed by the advent of the Internet. It was a change that went beyond mere paradigm. We realized that CS is not about the computer at all --- it is about computation, a sublime scientific phenomenon that permeates the universe. CS became a natural science because the Internet seemed to us as mysterious as the cell, the brain, the universe, and the market, and hence we had to approach it through experiments, falsifiable theories, and new applied math. And, because the Internet is about people in a more intimate way than the computer was, at the same time CS became a social science. A new mode of CS research emerged, often referred to as the lens of computation: since computation underlies everything, when computer scientists look at challenging problems in other sciences, unexpected progress often ensues. CS researchers thought productively about game theory and economics, the quantum universe, phase transitions, biology and evolution, social phenomena and the law, the brain. Finally, nearly two decades after that moment in the 1990s, AI happened, a new powerful intellectual tsunami and irresistible frame of mind. In my talk I will contemplate this fascinating story, connecting its twists and turns with the subject of databases. I will conclude with a snapshot of my work over the past decade on understanding how the brain begets the mind: how the activity of individual neurons and synapses results in cognition, behavior, intelligence, and ultimately language, arguably the crowning achievement of the animal brain.
papers.abstract_zh: 我上一次在 SIGMOD/PODS 上的演讲发生在三十年前。那时，互联网的出现改变了计算世界和数据库领域。这是一个超越单纯范式的改变。我们意识到计算机科学根本与计算机无关——它与计算有关，这是一种遍布宇宙的崇高科学现象。计算机科学成为一门自然科学，因为互联网对我们来说就像细胞、大脑、宇宙和市场一样神秘，因此我们必须通过实验、可证伪的理论和新的应用数学来接近它。而且，由于互联网以比计算机更亲密的方式与人相关，因此计算机科学同时也成为一门社会科学。一种新的计算机科学研究模式出现了，通常被称为计算的透镜：由于计算是一切的基础，因此当计算机科学家研究其他科学中具有挑战性的问题时，往往会出现意想不到的进展。计算机科学研究人员对博弈论和经济学、量子宇宙、相变、生物学和进化论、社会现象和法律、大脑进行了富有成效的思考。最后，在 20 世纪 90 年代那一刻过去近二十年后，人工智能出现了，这是一场新的强大的知识海啸和不可抗拒的心态。在我的演讲中，我将思考这个引人入胜的故事，将其曲折与数据库主题联系起来。最后，我将概述我过去十年来关于大脑如何产生思维的工作：单个神经元和突触的活动如何导致认知、行为、智力以及最终的语言，这可以说是动物大脑的最高成就。

papers.title: Including Bloom Filters in Bottom-up Optimization.
papers.abstract: Bloom filters are used in query processing to perform early data reduction and improve query performance. The optimal query plan may be different when Bloom filters are used, indicating the need for Bloom filter-aware query optimization. To date, Bloom filter-aware query optimization has only been incorporated in a top-down query optimizer and limited to snowflake queries. In this paper, we show how Bloom filters can be incorporated in a bottom-up cost-based query optimizer. We highlight the challenges in limiting optimizer search space expansion, and offer an efficient solution. We show that including Bloom filters in cost-based optimization can lead to better join orders with effective predicate transfer between operators. On a 100 GB instance of the TPC-H database, our approach achieved a 32.8% further reduction in latency for queries involving Bloom filters, compared to the traditional approach of adding Bloom filters in a separate post-optimization step. Our method applies to all query types, and we provide several heuristics to balance limited increases in optimization time against improved query latency.
papers.abstract_zh: 布隆过滤器用于查询处理中，以执行早期数据缩减并提高查询性能。当使用布隆过滤器时，最优查询计划可能会有所不同，这表明需要布隆过滤器感知的查询优化。迄今为止，布隆过滤器感知的查询优化仅被纳入自上而下的查询优化器中，并且仅限于雪花查询。在本文中，我们展示了如何将布隆过滤器合并到自下而上的基于成本的查询优化器中。我们强调限制优化器搜索空间扩展的挑战，并提供有效的解决方案。我们证明，在基于成本的优化中包含布隆过滤器可以通过运算符之间的有效谓词传输来实现更好的连接顺序。在 TPC-H 数据库的 100 GB 实例上，与在单独的后优化步骤中添加布隆过滤器的传统方法相比，我们的方法将涉及布隆过滤器的查询延迟进一步减少了 32.8%。我们的方法适用于所有查询类型，并且我们提供了几种启发式方法来平衡优化时间的有限增加与查询延迟的改善。

papers.title: Interactive Fairness Auditing: Leveraging AVOIR for Dynamic Evaluation and Mitigation.
papers.abstract: Ensuring fairness in high-stakes machine learning (ML) applications remains a significant challenge. We introduce an interactive, Streamlit-based User Interface (UI) for AVOIR, an automated fairness monitoring framework. This UI allows users to select fairness metrics (e.g., Demographic Parity, Equalized Odds) and explore dataset attributes through dynamic visualizations. The UI supports iterative refinement, enabling users to define fairness constraints in a domain-specific language (DSL), evaluate models, and address violations via interactive charts. Leveraging AVOIR's inference and optimization capabilities, it provides probabilistic guarantees and detects biases at runtime. We demonstrate its utility on multiple datasets, showing how fairness violations are identified and mitigated. By combining declarative fairness specifications, rigorous optimization, and intuitive visualizations, our UI empowers stakeholders to deploy ML models responsibly.
papers.abstract_zh: 确保高风险机器学习 (ML) 应用程序的公平性仍然是一项重大挑战。我们为 AVOIR 引入了一个基于 Streamlit 的交互式用户界面 (UI)，这是一个自动公平性监控框架。该 UI 允许用户选择公平性指标（例如人口统计、平等赔率）并通过动态可视化探索数据集属性。 UI 支持迭代细化，使用户能够以特定领域语言 (DSL) 定义公平性约束、评估模型并通过交互式图表解决违规问题。利用 AVOIR 的推理和优化功能，它提供概率保证并在运行时检测偏差。我们在多个数据集上展示了它的实用性，展示了如何识别和减轻公平违规行为。通过结合声明性公平规范、严格优化和直观可视化，我们的 UI 使利益相关者能够负责任地部署 ML 模型。

papers.title: Introducing RAW Hollow: An In-Memory, Co-Located, Compressed Object Store with Opt-In Strong Consistency.
papers.abstract:
In recent years, significant advances have been made to NoSQL databases, enabling them to operate at extreme scale - Cassandra has been benchmarked at over a million writes/second. However our experience at Netflix has shown that applications often deal with small-medium datasets that change less frequently. For these scenarios, while SQL databases remain the popular persistence option, we've found that it can be challenging for applications to achieve consistent performance with low latency and high availability. Supplementing the primary store with caching improves read performance, but at the cost of dealing with cache synchronization complexities. Even fully in-memory databases carry the overhead of network calls for every request.

This paper introduces RAW Hollow, a first of its kind object store that is local and in-memory with opt-in strong consistency. With RAW Hollow, the entire dataset is disseminated across the application cluster and resides in the memory of each application process. By leveraging compression, despite being in-memory, RAW Hollow allows scaling datasets up to a 100 million records per entity. Reads are eventually consistent by default, and completely local to the application, ensuring extremely low latencies and high availability. RAW Hollow allows applications to query millions of records in milliseconds. Writes are reflected throughout the cluster with single digit millisecond latencies. Users can select strong consistency at the individual request level allowing them to balance high availability with strong consistency. RAW Hollow simplifies building stateful applications that are highly available and easy to scale.

For this demo, we will load the entire public IMDb dataset into RAW Hollow and allow users to experience first hand the in-memory performance of RAW Hollow by querying, modifying and cloning the data. We will also showcase its resiliency to distributed failures.
papers.abstract_zh:
近年来，NoSQL 数据库取得了重大进展，使其能够以极大规模运行 - Cassandra 的基准测试速度超过每秒 100 万次写入。然而，我们在 Netflix 的经验表明，应用程序通常处理变化不太频繁的中小型数据集。对于这些场景，虽然 SQL 数据库仍然是流行的持久性选项，但我们发现应用程序要在低延迟和高可用性的情况下实现一致的性能可能具有挑战性。通过缓存补充主存储可以提高读取性能，但代价是处理缓存同步的复杂性。即使完全内存数据库也会承担每个请求的网络调用开销。

本文介绍了 RAW Hollow，这是同类中第一个对象存储，它位于本地且位于内存中，具有选择加入的强一致性。使用 RAW Hollow，整个数据集可以跨应用程序集群传播，并驻留在每个应用程序进程的内存中。尽管在内存中，但通过利用压缩，RAW Hollow 允许将数据集扩展至每个实体 1 亿条记录。默认情况下，读取最终是一致的，并且完全在应用程序本地，确保极低的延迟和高可用性。 RAW Hollow 允许应用程序在几毫秒内查询数百万条记录。写入会以个位数毫秒的延迟反映在整个集群中。用户可以在单个请求级别选择强一致性，从而使他们能够平衡高可用性与强一致性。 RAW Hollow 简化了构建高可用性且易于扩展的有状态应用程序。

在本次演示中，我们将把整个公开的 IMDb 数据集加载到 RAW Hollow 中，并让用户通过查询、修改和克隆数据来亲身体验 RAW Hollow 的内存性能。我们还将展示其对分布式故障的弹性。

papers.title: JSON Relational Duality: A Revolutionary Combination of Document, Object, and Relational Models.
papers.abstract:
JSON Relational Duality is a novel solution to the object-relational impedance mismatch problem. By decoupling the data access model from the data storage model, application data can be read and written in terms of application-friendly hierarchical JSON documents using Duality Views , even when the underlying data is stored in the form of normalized relational tables (that can include JSON columns for schema flexibility). When data is stored natively as JSON documents, shared data is duplicated across many documents. With duality views, different JSON documents can efficiently share the same underlying data without any data duplication, or the risk of data divergence. Duality views also use a novel value-based optimistic concurrency control protocol that enables stateless APIs such as REST to be used across multiple reads and writes with full transactional consistency. JSON Relational Duality is a transformative breakthrough for application development that preserves the best of both worlds: Developer-friendly JSON-oriented access to application objects, combined with the power (efficiency, integrity, and composability) of the relational model.

This paper describes the concepts of JSON Relational Duality and various aspects of its implementation in Oracle Database Release 23ai. It also poses some interesting questions for further research.
papers.abstract_zh:
JSON Relational Duality 是解决对象关系阻抗不匹配问题的新颖解决方案。通过将数据访问模型与数据存储模型解耦，即使底层数据以规范化关系表的形式存储（可以包括 JSON 列以实现架构灵活性），也可以使用 Duality Views 以应用程序友好的分层 JSON 文档的形式读取和写入应用程序数据。当数据本地存储为 JSON 文档时，共享数据会在许多文档中重复。通过对偶视图，不同的 JSON 文档可以有效地共享相同的底层数据，而不会出现任何数据重复或数据发散的风险。对偶视图还使用一种新颖的基于值的乐观并发控制协议，该协议使无状态 API（例如 REST）能够在多个读取和写入中使用，并具有完全事务一致性。 JSON 关系二元性是应用程序开发的革命性突破，它保留了两全其美的优点：开发人员友好的、面向 JSON 的应用程序对象访问，与关系模型的强大功能（效率、完整性和可组合性）相结合。

本文介绍了 JSON 关系对偶性的概念及其在 Oracle 数据库版本 23ai 中实现的各个方面。它还提出了一些需要进一步研究的有趣问题。

papers.title: KDSelector: A Knowledge-Enhanced and Data-Efficient Model Selector Learning Framework for Time Series Anomaly Detection.
papers.abstract: Model selection has been raised as an essential problem in the area of time series anomaly detection (TSAD), because there is no single best TSAD model for the highly heterogeneous time series in real-world applications. However, despite the success of existing model selection solutions that train a classification model (especially neural network, NN) using historical data as a selector to predict the correct TSAD model for each series, the NN-based selector learning methods used by existing solutions do not make full use of the knowledge in the historical data and require iterating over all training samples, which limits the accuracy and training speed of the selector. To address these limitations, we propose KDSelector, a novel knowledge-enhanced and data-efficient framework for learning the NN-based TSAD model selector, of which three key components are specifically designed to integrate available knowledge into the selector and dynamically prune less important and redundant samples during the learning. We develop a TSAD model selection system with KDSelector as the internal, to demonstrate how users improve the accuracy and training speed of their selectors by using KDSelector as a plug-and-play module. Our demonstration video is hosted at https://youtu.be/2uqupDWvTF0.
papers.abstract_zh: 模型选择已成为时间序列异常检测（TSAD）领域的一个重要问题，因为对于现实应用中高度异构的时间序列，没有单一的最佳 TSAD 模型。然而，尽管现有的模型选择解决方案成功地使用历史数据作为选择器来训练分类模型（尤其是神经网络，NN）来预测每个系列的正确TSAD模型，但现有解决方案所使用的基于NN的选择器学习方法并没有充分利用历史数据中的知识，并且需要迭代所有训练样本，这限制了选择器的准确性和训练速度。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了 KDSelector，一种新颖的知识增强和数据高效框架，用于学习基于神经网络的 TSAD 模型选择器，其中三个关键组件专门设计用于将可用知识集成到选择器中，并在学习过程中动态修剪不太重要和冗余的样本。我们开发了一个以 KDSelector 为内部的 TSAD 模型选择系统，来演示用户如何通过使用 KDSelector 作为即插即用模块来提高选择器的准确性和训练速度。我们的演示视频托管在 https://youtu.be/2uqupDWvTF0。

papers.title: Learned Indexes From the One-dimensional to the Multi-dimensional Spaces: Challenges, Techniques, and Opportunities.
papers.abstract: Recently, the class of learned index structures has emerged as one form of database indexes that make use of Machine Learning (ML) techniques. The learned indexes designed for the one-dimensional space have demonstrated improvements in both the query processing time and the index size. Observing the advantages of one-dimensional learned indexes, various learned indexes have been proposed for the multi-dimensional space. This class of learned indexes is termed ''Learned Multi-dimensional Indexes.'' This tutorial on learned indexes is designed based on our long survey article on the subject [4]. In this tutorial, we use a taxonomy to categorize over 100 learned one- and multi-dimensional indexes with more focus on the class of learned multi-dimensional indexes. The goal of this tutorial is to explain the fundamental techniques behind state-of-the-art learned one- and multi-dimensional indexes with emphasis on the latter, and identify the ongoing challenges and future opportunities for research in this domain.
papers.abstract_zh: 最近，学习索引结构类已经作为利用机器学习 (ML) 技术的数据库索引的一种形式出现。为一维空间设计的学习索引在查询处理时间和索引大小方面都得到了改进。观察一维学习索引的优点，针对多维空间提出了各种学习索引。此类学习索引称为“学习多维索引”。本关于学习索引的教程是根据我们关于该主题的长篇调查文章设计的 [4]。在本教程中，我们使用分类法对 100 多个学习的一维和多维索引进行分类，更关注学习的多维索引的类别。本教程的目标是解释最先进的学习一维和多维索引背后的基本技术，重点是后者，并确定该领域研究中持续的挑战和未来的机遇。

papers.title: LingoDB-CT: Understanding LingoDB's Inner Workings.
papers.abstract:
While compiling query engines can be highly efficient, especially for complex queries, they also have the reputation of being too difficult to understand, debug, and profile. However, this is not necessarily the case if supported by the right architecture and tools.

With this demonstration, we want to show this for our own compiling query engine LingoDB. For this purpose, we built an instrumentation and visualization framework called LingoDB-CT, which aggregates data from multiple angles and visualizes it to reveal the bigger picture. Through two interactive demonstration scenarios, using publicly hosted web applications, we show that LingoDB is indeed easy to understand and profile.
papers.abstract_zh:
虽然编译查询引擎可以非常高效，尤其是对于复杂的查询，但它们也因难以理解、调试和分析而闻名。然而，如果有正确的架构和工具支持，情况就不一定如此。

通过这个演示，我们希望为我们自己的编译查询引擎 LingoDB 展示这一点。为此，我们构建了一个名为 LingoDB-CT 的仪器和可视化框架，它从多个角度聚合数据并将其可视化以揭示更大的图景。通过使用公共托管的 Web 应用程序的两个交互式演示场景，我们表明 LingoDB 确实易于理解和分析。

papers.title: LLM-DPM - Workshop on Large Language Models for Data Process Management.
papers.abstract: The LLM-DPM Workshop investigates the transformative impact of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Explainable AI (XAI) on Data and Process Management. With organizations facing growing dependence on intricate data-driven workflows, there is an urgent demand for systems that prioritize not only efficiency but also transparency, reliability, and equity. This workshop serves as a dedicated platform to explore the convergence of LLMs, process mining, and database technologies, tackling critical issues such as enhancing data integrity, refining query understanding, forecasting process outcomes, and optimizing system performance. Featuring technical discussions, keynote speeches, and collaborative panels, LLM-DPM seeks to bridge the gap between academic research and industry applications, inspiring innovative approaches and advancing the creation of accountable, AI-powered solutions for process management challenges.
papers.abstract_zh: LLM-DPM 研讨会研究大型语言模型 (LLM) 和可解释人工智能 (XAI) 对数据和流程管理的变革性影响。随着组织越来越依赖复杂的数据驱动工作流程，迫切需要不仅优先考虑效率，而且优先考虑透明度、可靠性和公平性的系统。该研讨会作为一个专用平台，探索法学硕士、流程挖掘和数据库技术的融合，解决关键问题，例如增强数据完整性、完善查询理解、预测流程结果和优化系统性能。 LLM-DPM 以技术讨论、主题演讲和协作小组为特色，旨在弥合学术研究和行业应用之间的差距，激发创新方法，并推动创建负责任的、人工智能驱动的解决方案，以应对流程管理挑战。

papers.title: LLM-Matcher: A Name-Based Schema Matching Tool using Large Language Models.
papers.abstract: We present LLM-Matcher, an interactive name-based schema matching system that utilizes large language models (LLMs) to identify correspondences between source and target schema elements relying solely on their names and descriptions. This tool is specifically designed for restricted environments where instance-based schema matching is not possible, such as the healthcare domain where instance access is often prohibited. LLM-Matcher is based on an extensive experimental study, showing the capabilities of LLMs in the schema matching task. Our system is specifically tailored towards users with sufficient domain knowledge and offers an interpretable initial mapping that can be further refined by providing textual feedback. This feedback allows to rectify model misconceptions as well as improve the quality of schema element descriptions. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of LLM-Matcher, explores its application in the healthcare domain, allows users to gain additional insight into our experimental study, and outlines different steps showcased in the demonstration.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们提出了 LLM-Matcher，这是一种基于名称的交互式模式匹配系统，它利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 仅依赖于源模式元素和目标模式元素的名称和描述来识别源模式元素和目标模式元素之间的对应关系。该工具专为无法进行基于实例的架构匹配的受限环境而设计，例如经常禁止实例访问的医疗保健领域。 LLM-Matcher 基于广泛的实验研究，展示了 LLM 在模式匹配任务中的能力。我们的系统专门针对具有足够领域知识的用户量身定制，并提供可解释的初始映射，可以通过提供文本反馈进一步完善该映射。此反馈可以纠正模型误解并提高模式元素描述的质量。本文提供了 LLM-Matcher 的全面概述，探讨了其在医疗保健领域的应用，使用户能够进一步了解我们的实验研究，并概述了演示中展示的不同步骤。

papers.title: Locator: Local Stability for Rankings.
papers.abstract: Rankings play a crucial role in decision-making. However, if minor changes to items significantly alter their rankings, the quality of the decisions being made can be compromised. The stability of ranking is a measure used to assess how modifications to the ranking algorithm or data affect results. We introduce a novel measure we refer to as local stability, indicating the effect of minor changes to the values of an item in the ranking on its rank. Our proposed definition takes into account the presence of multiple items with similar qualities in the ranking, called dense regions, permitting minor modifications to swap the positions of items within the region. We present Locator, a system utilizing local stability to provide ranking stakeholders--producers, participants, and consumers--with insights into the ranking. Ranking producers can use Locator to adjust their ranking functions, enhancing item stability. They can then share the local stability information with ranking stakeholders to provide a meaningful understanding of how uncertainty in an item's attributes affects its rank without exposing the ranking methodology.
papers.abstract_zh: 排名在决策中起着至关重要的作用。然而，如果项目的微小变化会显着改变其排名，则决策的质量可能会受到影响。排名的稳定性是用于评估排名算法或数据的修改如何影响结果的衡量标准。我们引入了一种称为局部稳定性的新颖度量，表明排名中某个项目的值的微小变化对其排名的影响。我们提出的定义考虑到排名中存在多个具有相似质量的项目，称为密集区域，允许进行较小的修改以交换区域内项目的位置。我们推出了 Locator，这是一个利用本地稳定性为排名利益相关者（生产者、参与者和消费者）提供排名见解的系统。排名制作者可以使用Locator来调整排名功能，增强项目稳定性。然后，他们可以与排名利益相关者共享本地稳定性信息，以有意义地了解项目属性的不确定性如何影响其排名，而无需公开排名方法。

papers.title: LpBound in Action: Cardinality Estimation with One-Sided Guarantees.
papers.abstract: We demonstrate LpBound , a cardinality estimator that computes guaranteed upper bounds on the output size of a given query. Among the wealth of traditional, learned, and pessimistic estimators, LpBound 's uniqueness lies in its use of two key ingredients: (1) data statistics based on ℓ p -norms of degree sequences of the join columns, and (2) a linear program formulation of the cardinality estimation problem, whose constraints are the Shannon inequalities and new information inequalities derived from data statistics. LpBound comes with a visual interface accessible in the browser. The users can interact with the interface by choosing a query from the JOB, STATS, and Subgraph Matching benchmarks and the range of ℓ p -norms available to LpBound . Within a few milliseconds, LpBound computes an upper bound on the query output size. This bound is explained by a closed-form formula using the available ℓ p -norms. The users can also inspect the estimation errors of LpBound and a variety of other estimators.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们演示了 LpBound，一个基数估计器，用于计算给定查询的输出大小的保证上限。在众多传统的、学习的和悲观的估计器中，LpBound 的独特之处在于它使用了两个关键成分：（1）基于连接列的度序列的 ℓ p 范数的数据统计，以及（2）基数估计问题的线性程序公式，其约束是香农不等式和从数据统计导出的新信息不等式。 LpBound 带有可在浏览器中访问的可视化界面。用户可以通过从 JOB、STATS 和子图匹配基准以及 LpBound 可用的 ℓ p 范数范围中选择查询来与界面进行交互。在几毫秒内，LpBound 计算查询输出大小的上限。该界限由使用可用 ℓ p 范数的封闭式公式来解释。用户还可以检查LpBound和各种其他估计器的估计误差。

papers.title: MaLT: A Framework for Managing Large Transactions in OceanBase.
papers.abstract:
Large transactions challenge the designs of the modern relational database systems, as they necessitate the management of uncommitted changes. OceanBase, a distributed relational database system, has implemented an LSM-tree based storage engine to achieve high read and write performance. However, the support for large transactions of existing LSM-tree based systems remains a problem under the append-only principle of LSM-tree, making them suffer from memory constraints and low efficiency.

In this paper, we present MaLT, a framework designed to efficiently Ma nage L arge T ransactions within OceanBase system. We introduce Transaction Context Table (TCT) and Transaction Data Table (TDT) to manage transaction states in the LSM-tree based storage engine. Based on them, we further devise an efficient recovery mechanism to provide high availability of the databases after unexpected system failures. Unlike existing LSM-tree based RDBMSs that abstract LSM-trees as key-value stores, MaLT directly implements transactions into the LSM-tree and leverages its unique features. The backfill (i.e., in-row version number update upon commit) and undo operations for transactions are seamlessly integrated into the compaction stage of the LSM-tree. This enables efficient commit and abort, and in the meantime helps avoid the instant latency of recovering the system from uncommitted large transactions. Moreover, MaLT also embeds transaction information directly within the LSM-tree, facilitating various optimizations to improve both read and write performance. Finally, the experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and the scalability of our approach implemented in OceanBase.
papers.abstract_zh:
大型事务对现代关系数据库系统的设计提出了挑战，因为它们需要管理未提交的更改。 OceanBase是一个分布式关系数据库系统，实现了基于LSM-tree的存储引擎，以实现较高的读写性能。然而，在LSM-tree的append-only原则下，现有的基于LSM-tree的系统对大事务的支持仍然是一个问题，这使得它们受到内存限制和低效率的困扰。

在本文中，我们提出了 MaLT，一个旨在高效管理 OceanBase 系统内的大额交易的框架。我们引入事务上下文表（TCT）和事务数据表（TDT）来管理基于LSM树的存储引擎中的事务状态。在此基础上，我们进一步设计了一种有效的恢复机制，以在意外系统故障后提供数据库的高可用性。与现有的基于 LSM 树的 RDBMS 将 LSM 树抽象为键值存储不同，MaLT 直接将事务实现到 LSM 树中并利用其独特的功能。事务的回填（即提交时行内版本号更新）和撤消操作无缝集成到 LSM 树的压缩阶段。这可以实现高效的提交和中止，同时有助于避免从未提交的大型事务中恢复系统的即时延迟。此外，MaLT还将事务信息直接嵌入LSM树中，促进各种优化以提高读写性能。最后，实验结果证明了我们在OceanBase中实现的方法的有效性和可扩展性。

papers.title: Managed Resource Scaling in Amazon EMR.
papers.abstract: Compute elasticity is a primary benefit of using cloud-based data processing platforms such as Amazon EMR, where clusters can be scaled both horizontally and vertically. For example, a query scanning petabytes of data can run faster in a cluster with thousands of nodes compared to one with only a few hundred. However, not all workloads require the same computational power or have the same resource utilization patterns throughout their lifetime. Optimizing solely for performance by over-provisioning clusters can result in extra costs for customers stemming from unused capacity. Under-provisioning clusters, on the other hand, will keep costs low but can delay job completion time and cause SLAs to be missed. To further complicate the problem, a single cluster may run concurrent workloads, each with different resource needs. Typical solutions to managing time-varying resource needs involve frequent manual cluster rescaling or using different-sized clusters for different types of queries, which increases the cluster administrator's workload. This paper presents Amazon EMR Managed Scaling, a feature that continuously and automatically resizes EMR clusters with a goal to optimize the cost/performance ratio, with minimal user input. We describe how we iteratively built our engine-agnostic scaling approach that uses a cluster's telemetry, topology and past behavior to adjust its resource utilization in response to changing resource requirements. We show how each of the individual techniques presented herein solve specific workload execution patterns, and how EMR combines them into a single, unified algorithm that can make correct scaling decisions within seconds.
papers.abstract_zh: 计算弹性是使用基于云的数据处理平台（例如 Amazon EMR）的主要优势，其中集群可以水平和垂直扩展。例如，与只有几百个节点的集群相比，扫描 PB 数据的查询在具有数千个节点的集群中运行得更快。然而，并非所有工作负载在其整个生命周期中都需要相同的计算能力或具有相同的资源利用模式。仅通过过度配置集群来优化性能可能会因未使用的容量而给客户带来额外成本。另一方面，配置不足的集群可以保持较低的成本，但会延迟作业完成时间并导致错过 SLA。使问题进一步复杂化的是，单个集群可能运行并发工作负载，每个工作负载都有不同的资源需求。管理随时间变化的资源需求的典型解决方案涉及频繁的手动集群重新扩展或针对不同类型的查询使用不同大小的集群，这会增加集群管理员的工作量。本文介绍了 Amazon EMR Managed Scaling，这是一项能够连续自动调整 EMR 集群大小的功能，旨在以最少的用户输入优化成本/性能比。我们描述了如何迭代地构建与引擎无关的扩展方法，该方法使用集群的遥测、拓扑和过去的行为来调整其资源利用率，以响应不断变化的资源需求。我们展示了本文中介绍的每种单独技术如何解决特定的工作负载执行模式，以及 EMR 如何将它们组合成一个统一的算法，以便在几秒钟内做出正确的扩展决策。

papers.title: MicroNN: An On-device Disk-resident Updatable Vector Database.
papers.abstract: Nearest neighbour search over dense vector collections has important applications in information retrieval, retrieval augmented generation (RAG), and content ranking. Performing efficient search over large vector collections is a well studied problem with many existing approaches and open source implementations. However, most state-of-the-art systems are generally targeted towards scenarios using large servers with an abundance of memory, static vector collections that are not updatable, and nearest neighbour search in isolation of other search criteria. We present Micro Nearest Neighbour (MicroNN), an embedded nearest-neighbour vector search engine designed for scalable similarity search in low-resource environments. MicroNN addresses the problem of on-device vector search for real-world workloads containing updates and hybrid search queries that combine nearest neighbour search with structured attribute filters. In this scenario, memory is highly constrained and disk-efficient index structures and algorithms are required, as well as support for continuous inserts and deletes. MicroNN is an embeddable library that can scale to large vector collections with minimal resources. MicroNN is used in production and powers a wide range of vector search use-cases on-device. MicroNN takes less than 7 ms to retrieve the top-100 nearest neighbours with 90% recall on publicly available million-scale vector benchmark while using ~10 MB of memory.
papers.abstract_zh: 密集向量集合上的最近邻搜索在信息检索、检索增强生成 (RAG) 和内容排名中具有重要应用。对大型向量集合执行高效搜索是许多现有方法和开源实现的一个经过充分研究的问题。然而，大多数最先进的系统通常针对使用具有大量内存的大型服务器、不可更新的静态向量集合以及与其他搜索条件隔离的最近邻居搜索的场景。我们推出了微型最近邻 (MicroNN)，这是一种嵌入式最近邻向量搜索引擎，专为在资源匮乏的环境中进行可扩展的相似性搜索而设计。 MicroNN 解决了​​现实世界工作负载的设备上矢量搜索问题，其中包含更新和将最近邻搜索与结构化属性过滤器相结合的混合搜索查询。在这种情况下，内存受到高度限制，需要磁盘高效的索引结构和算法，以及对连续插入和删除的支持。 MicroNN 是一个嵌入式库，可以用最少的资源扩展到大型向量集合。 MicroNN 用于生产并为设备上的各种矢量搜索用例提供支持。 MicroNN 在使用约 10 MB 内存的情况下，只需不到 7 毫秒即可检索前 100 个最近邻，在公开的百万级向量基准测试中召回率达到 90%。

papers.title: MiniClean: A Single-Machine System for Cleaning Big Graphs.
papers.abstract: We demonstrate MiniClean, a single-machine system for cleaning large-scale graphs. MiniClean adopts a rule-based approach that unifies logic reasoning and machine learning, and supports rule discovery, error detection and error correction. To cope with repeated graph pattern matching and large intermediate results, MiniClean proposes (1) a pipelined workflow that coordinates CPU, GPU and I/O operations, (2) memory footprint reduction by bundled processing and data compression, and (3) a multi-mode parallel model that combines SIMD, pipelined, and independent parallelism to maximize CPU--GPU synergy. We demonstrate how MiniClean cleans real-life billion-scale graphs and outperforms a SOTA multi-machine system with a 32-node cluster by at least 8.09X.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们演示了 MiniClean，一个用于清理大规模图形的单机系统。 MiniClean采用基于规则的方法，将逻辑推理和机器学习相结合，支持规则发现、错误检测和纠错。为了应对重复的图形模式匹配和大量中间结果，MiniClean 提出了 (1) 协调 CPU、GPU 和 I/O 操作的流水线工作流程，(2) 通过捆绑处理和数据压缩减少内存占用，以及 (3) 结合 SIMD、流水线和独立并行性的多模式并行模型，以最大限度地提高 CPU-GPU 协同作用。我们演示了 MiniClean 如何清理现实生活中数十亿级的图形，并且性能比具有 32 节点集群的 SOTA 多机系统至少高出 8.09 倍。

papers.title: Mobility Stream Processing on NebulaStream and MEOS.
papers.abstract:
The increasing use of Internet-of-Things (IoT) sensors in moving objects has resulted in vast amounts of spatiotemporal streaming data. To analyze this data in situ, real-time spatiotemporal processing is needed. However, current stream processing systems designed for IoT environments often lack spatiotemporal processing capabilities, and existing spatiotemporal libraries primarily focus on analyzing historical data. This gap makes performing real-time spatiotemporal analytics challenging.

In this demonstration, we present NebulaMEOS, which combines MEOS (Mobility Engine Open Source), a spatiotemporal processing library, with NebulaStream, a scalable data management system for IoT applications. By integrating MEOS into NebulaStream, NebulaMEOS utilizes spatiotemporal functionalities to process and analyze streaming data in real-time. We demonstrate NebulaMEOS by querying data streamed from edge devices on trains by the Société Nationale des Chemins de fer Belges (SNCB). Visitors can experience demonstrations of geofencing and geospatial complex event processing, visualizing real-time train operations and environmental impacts.
papers.abstract_zh:
物联网 (IoT) 传感器在移动物体中的使用越来越多，产生了大量的时空流数据。为了原位分析这些数据，需要实时时空处理。然而，当前针对物联网环境设计的流处理系统往往缺乏时空处理能力，而现有的时空库主要侧重于分析历史数据。这种差距使得执行实时时空分析变得具有挑战性。

在本次演示中，我们展示了 NebulaMEOS，它将时空处理库 MEOS（开源移动引擎）与适用于物联网应用的可扩展数据管理系统 NebulaStream 相结合。通过将MEOS集成到NebulaStream中，NebulaMEOS利用时空功能来实时处理和分析流数据。我们通过查询比利时国家铁路公司 (SNCB) 从火车边缘设备传输的数据来演示 NebulaMEOS。参观者可以体验地理围栏和地理空间复杂事件处理的演示，直观地看到实时列车运行和环境影响。

papers.title: Mosaic: An Architecture for Linking Databases and Scalable Interactive Visualizations.
papers.abstract: Real-time interaction and visualization over large data volumes requires careful coordination of data queries and visual updates. Mosaic is an architecture for optimizing scalable and interoperable visualizations backed by a database, providing a platform for developing and deploying optimizations that span both visualization clients and backing databases. Mosaic applications consist of data-consuming clients that publish data needs as declarative queries, parameterized by shared filtering selections. These queries are managed and automatically optimized by a coordinator that proxies access to a scalable data store. For example, by analyzing selection predicates and client queries, the coordinator automatically constructs materialized views to perform selection updates over pre-aggregated data at interactive rates. Given only a high-level specification, Mosaic automatically enables orders-of-magnitude performance improvements over standard update queries.
papers.abstract_zh: 大数据量的实时交互和可视化需要仔细协调数据查询和可视化更新。 Mosaic 是一种用于优化由数据库支持的可扩展和可互操作可视化的架构，提供了一个用于开发和部署跨可视化客户端和支持数据库的优化的平台。 Mosaic 应用程序由数据消耗客户端组成，这些客户端将数据需求发布为声明性查询，并通过共享过滤选择进行参数化。这些查询由代理对可扩展数据存储的访问的协调器进行管理和自动优化。例如，通过分析选择谓词和客户端查询，协调器自动构建物化视图，以交互速率对预聚合数据执行选择更新。只需给出高级规范，Mosaic 就会自动实现比标准更新查询数量级的性能改进。

papers.title: Navigating Data Errors in Machine Learning Pipelines: Identify, Debug, and Learn.
papers.abstract: Addressing data errors-such as missing, incorrect, noisy, biased, or out-of-distribution values-is essential to building reliable machine learning (ML) systems. Traditional methods often focus on refining the training process to minimize error symptoms or repairing data errors indiscriminately, without addressing their root causes. These isolated approaches ignore how errors originate and propagate through the interconnected stages of ML pipelines-data preprocessing, model training, and prediction-resulting in superficial fixes and suboptimal solutions. Consequently, they miss the opportunity to understand how data errors impact downstream tasks and to implement targeted, effective interventions. In recent years, the research community has made significant progress in developing holistic approaches to identify the most harmful data errors, prioritize impactful repairs, and reason about their effects when errors cannot be fully resolved. This tutorial surveys prominent work in this area and introduces practical tools designed to address data quality issues across the ML lifecycle. By combining theoretical insights with hands-on demonstrations, attendees will gain actionable strategies to diagnose, repair, and manage data errors, enhancing the reliability, fairness, and transparency of ML systems in real-world applications.
papers.abstract_zh: 解决数据错误（例如缺失、不正确、有噪声、有偏差或超出分布值）对于构建可靠的机器学习 (ML) 系统至关重要。传统方法通常侧重于改进训练过程，以最大程度地减少错误症状或不加区别地修复数据错误，而不解决其根本原因。这些孤立的方法忽略了错误是如何在机器学习管道的互连阶段（数据预处理、模型训练和预测）中产生和传播的，从而导致肤浅的修复和次优的解决方案。因此，他们错过了了解数据错误如何影响下游任务以及实施有针对性的有效干预措施的机会。近年来，研究界在开发整体方法方面取得了重大进展，以识别最有害的数据错误，优先考虑有影响的修复，并在错误无法完全解决时推理其影响。本教程调查了该领域的杰出工作，并介绍了旨在解决整个机器学习生命周期中的数据质量问题的实用工具。通过将理论见解与实践演示相结合，与会者将获得诊断、修复和管理数据错误的可行策略，从而增强机器学习系统在实际应用中的可靠性、公平性和透明度。

papers.title: NebulaStream: An Extensible, High-Performance Streaming Engine for Multi-Modal Edge Applications.
papers.abstract:
NebulaStream is a novel, open-source data stream processing system for massively distributed, heterogeneous data streams in the cloud-edge continuum. It adheres to the design goals of ease-of-use, extensibility, and efficiency to provide a framework for users and developers to implement diverse Internet of Things (IoT) use cases. Equipped with essential built-in functionalities, NebulaStream allows users to customize the system easily while ensuring efficient execution even on low-end devices.

In this demonstration, we highlight NebulaStream's ability to integrate and process multi-modal, multi-frequency data streams. We showcase its abilities through a real-world IoT scenario where NebulaStream is used to improve the health assessment of patients in a smart intensive care unit.
papers.abstract_zh:
NebulaStream 是一种新颖的开源数据流处理系统，适用于云边缘连续体中的大规模分布式异构数据流。它秉承易用性、可扩展性和高效性的设计目标，为用户和开发人员提供实现各种物联网 (IoT) 用例的框架。 NebulaStream配备了必要的内置功能，允许用户轻松定制系统，同时确保即使在低端设备上也能高效执行。

在本次演示中，我们重点介绍 NebulaStream 集成和处理多模式、多频率数据流的能力。我们通过真实的物联网场景展示其能力，其中 NebulaStream 用于改善智能重症监护室患者的健康评估。

papers.title: NeutronRAG: Towards Understanding the Effectiveness of RAG from a Data Retrieval Perspective.
papers.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has been widely used to enhance the generation quality of large language models (LLMs), particularly in domain-specific tasks. As application requirements become increasingly diverse, various RAG methods have been proposed to optimize the retrieval and generation quality of different task scenarios, such as VectorRAG, GraphRAG, and HybridRAG. However, RAG faces two key challenges in these retrieval methods: evaluating different retrieval methods and optimizing parameter configurations. First, different retrieval methods show different performances. How to uniformly compare and analyze the advantages and disadvantages of these retrieval methods remains an open research problem. Second, the effectiveness of RAG is highly sensitive to key parameter configurations. Optimizing these parameters is challenging due to the complexity of the parameter space and the difficulty in identifying the sources of errors in the generated responses. Existing RAG tools typically use a single retrieval method, lacking analytical capabilities and multi-strategy support. To address these challenges, we introduce NeutronRAG, a demonstration of understanding the effectiveness of RAG from a data retrieval perspective. NeutronRAG supports hybrid retrieval strategies and helps researchers iteratively refine RAG configuration to improve retrieval and generation quality through systematic analysis, visual feedback, and parameter adjustment advice. It facilitates data-driven decisions to enhance LLM generation quality while exploring effective retrieval strategies in RAG systems. The web app is available at: https://github.com/iDC-NEU/NeutronRAG.
papers.abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）已被广泛用于提高大型语言模型（LLM）的生成质量，特别是在特定领域的任务中。随着应用需求变得日益多样化，人们提出了各种RAG方法来优化不同任务场景的检索和生成质量，例如VectorRAG、GraphRAG和HybridRAG。然而，RAG 在这些检索方法中面临两个关键挑战：评估不同的检索方法和优化参数配置。首先，不同的检索方法表现出不同的性能。如何统一比较和分析这些检索方法的优缺点仍然是一个开放的研究问题。其次，RAG 的有效性对关键参数配置高度敏感。由于参数空间的复杂性以及识别生成的响应中的错误来源的困难，优化这些参数具有挑战性。现有的RAG工具通常使用单一的检索方法，缺乏分析能力和多策略支持。为了应对这些挑战，我们引入了 NeutronRAG，它演示了从数据检索角度理解 RAG 的有效性。 NeutronRAG 支持混合检索策略，并帮助研究人员迭代完善 RAG 配置，通过系统分析、视觉反馈和参数调整建议来提高检索和生成质量。它有助于数据驱动的决策，以提高 LLM 生成质量，同时探索 RAG 系统中的有效检索策略。该网络应用程序位于：https://github.com/iDC-NEU/NeutronRAG。

papers.title: Ninth Workshop on Data Management for End-to-End Machine Learning (DEEM).
papers.abstract: The DEEM'25 workshop (Data Management for End-to-End Machine Learning) is held on Friday, June 27th, in conjunction with SIGMOD/PODS 2025. DEEM brings together researchers and practitioners at the intersection of applied machine learning, data management, and systems research, with the goal of discussing the arising data management issues in ML application scenarios. The workshop solicits regular research papers (8 pages) describing preliminary and ongoing research results, including industrial experience reports of end-to-end ML deployments, related to DEEM topics. In addition, DEEM 2025 has a category for short papers (4 pages) as a forum for sharing interesting use cases, problems, datasets, benchmarks, visionary ideas, system designs, preliminary results, and descriptions of system components and tools related to end-to-end ML pipelines. This year, the workshop received 18 high-quality submissions on diverse topics relevant to DEEM.
papers.abstract_zh: DEEM'25 研讨会（端到端机器学习的数据管理）于 6 月 27 日星期五与 SIGMOD/PODS 2025 同期举行。DEEM 汇集了应用机器学习、数据管理和系统研究交叉领域的研究人员和从业者，旨在讨论 ML 应用场景中出现的数据管理问题。该研讨会征集定期研究论文（8 页），描述初步和正在进行的研究成果，包括与 DEEM 主题相关的端到端 ML 部署的行业经验报告。此外，DEEM 2025 还有一个短论文类别（4 页）作为论坛，用于分享有趣的用例、问题、数据集、基准、远见卓识、系统设计、初步结果以及与端到端 ML 管道相关的系统组件和工具的描述。今年，研讨会收到了 18 份高质量的提交材料，涉及与 DEEM 相关的不同主题。

papers.title: Ninth Workshop on Human-In-the-Loop Data Analytics (HILDA).
papers.abstract: HILDA brings together researchers and practitioners to exchange ideas and results on human-data interaction. It explores how data management and analysis can be made more effective when taking into account the people who design and build these processes as well as those who are impacted by their results. Following the past few years, we plan to continue to focus on this year's workshop on early-stage research that is promising and exciting, which includes pairing each accepted paper with a mentor. The theme for this edition of the workshop is HILDA and Large Language Models. However, the workshop is not limited to this theme and other topics are also of interest. In this summary, we describe the workshop, its main focus areas and our review and mentorship plan.
papers.abstract_zh: HILDA 将研究人员和从业者聚集在一起，交流有关人类数据交互的想法和结果。它探讨了在考虑设计和构建这些流程的人员以及受其结果影响的人员的情况下，如何使数据管理和分析更加有效。继过去几年之后，我们计划继续关注今年的早期研究研讨会，这些研究前景光明且令人兴奋，其中包括将每篇被接受的论文与导师配对。本期研讨会的主题是 HILDA 和大型语言模型。不过，研讨会并不局限于这个主题，其他话题也很有趣。在本摘要中，我们描述了研讨会、其主要重点领域以及我们的审查和指导计划。

papers.title: Oceanus: Enable SLO-Aware Vertical Autoscaling for Cloud-Native Streaming Services in Tencent.
papers.abstract:
Stream services have gained immense popularity for facilitating real-time data processing, powering numerous applications within Tencent. Most of these streaming jobs are deployed at scale on the cloud to achieve higher availability and stability. Due to the dynamic nature of input data rates, cloud-native streaming services within Tencent experience both periodical and distributional workload imbalances. Vertical scaling mechanism provided on the cloud platform aids in more fine-grained automatic resource adjustments for the workload imbalances. However, current vertical autoscaling solutions on cloud platform face challenges in achieving timely and accurate scaling predictions, managing service interruptions caused by resource scaling, and efficiently handling various autoscaling abnormalities. These issues can hinder the solutions from meeting the service level objectives (SLO) of streaming jobs in Tencent.

In this paper, we introduce Oceanus, an end-to-end solution designed to facilitate precise and automated resource adjustment through vertical autoscaling for cloud-native streaming services, while ensuring that their SLO are consistently met. Oceanus incorporates proactive ML-enhanced forecasting technique that learns from historical load patterns to accurately determine optimal scaling times, cross-layer in-situ resource adjustment mechanism that enables resource adjustments without interrupting stream processing and robust resolution workflow to ensure rapid recovery from unexpected issues after resource adjustment. We evaluate Oceanus in our production environment, and the experiment results have further validated our system design.
papers.abstract_zh:
流服务因促进实时数据处理而广受欢迎，为腾讯内部的众多应用程序提供支持。这些流作业大部分都大规模部署在云端，以实现更高的可用性和稳定性。由于输入数据速率的动态特性，腾讯内部的云原生流媒体服务会遇到周期性和分布式工作负载不平衡的情况。云平台提供的垂直伸缩机制，有助于针对工作负载不平衡情况进行更细粒度的自动资源调整。然而，当前云平台上的垂直自动伸缩解决方案在实现及时准确的伸缩预测、管理资源伸缩引起的服务中断以及高效处理各种自动伸缩异常方面面临挑战。这些问题可能会阻碍解决方案满足腾讯流媒体作业的服务水平目标（SLO）。

在本文中，我们介绍了 Oceanus，这是一种端到端解决方案，旨在通过云原生流媒体服务的垂直自动扩展来促进精确和自动化的资源调整，同时确保始终满足其 SLO。 Oceanus 采用了主动式机器学习增强预测技术，可以从历史负载模式中学习，准确确定最佳扩展时间；跨层原位资源调整机制，可以在不中断流处理的情况下进行资源调整；以及强大的解决工作流程，以确保在资源调整后从意外问题中快速恢复。我们在生产环境中对Oceanus进行了评估，实验结果进一步验证了我们的系统设计。

papers.title: OIE: An Interpretable System for Outlier Explanation and Summarization.
papers.abstract:
Outlier detection is crucial for preventing financial fraud, network intrusions, and device failures. However, while existing methods excel at identifying outliers, they often fall short of providing clear and interpretable explanations. This limitation forces users to manually analyze numerous outliers, making the process both time-consuming and inefficient. Additionally, current summarization approaches often focus only on data attributes, ignoring that outliers in the same subspace may have different causes. Consequently,these methods produce broad summaries that make quick diagnosis difficult.

To address these challenges, we propose OIE, a system that generates interpretable, fine-grained rules to summarize and explain outlier detection results. OIE leverages decision trees to generate concise rules, balancing simplicity and classification accuracy. Additionally, it employs dynamic data partitioning and a boundary stabilizer to efficiently handle high-dimensional and complex datasets.Through multiple real-world scenarios, OIE demonstrates effective anomaly detection and summarization, providing actionable insights and enhancing the efficiency of outlier analysis. A demonstration video of OIE is available at:https://youtu.be/YzfDdF9f5HI.
papers.abstract_zh:
异常值检测对于防止金融欺诈、网络入侵和设备故障至关重要。然而，虽然现有方法擅长识别异常值，但它们往往无法提供清晰且可解释的解释。这种限制迫使用户手动分析大量异常值，使得该过程既耗时又低效。此外，当前的汇总方法通常只关注数据属性，而忽略了同一子空间中的异常值可能有不同的原因。因此，这些方法产生广泛的总结，导致快速诊断变得困难。

为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了 OIE，这是一个生成可解释的细粒度规则来总结和解释异常值检测结果的系统。 OIE 利用决策树生成简洁的规则，平衡简单性和分类准确性。此外，它还采用动态数据分区和边界稳定器来有效处理高维和复杂的数据集。通过多个现实场景，OIE 展示了有效的异常检测和总结，提供了可操作的见解并提高了异常值分析的效率。 OIE 的演示视频请访问：https://youtu.be/YzfDdF9f5HI。

papers.title: OLTP Engines on Modern Storage Architectures.
papers.abstract:
Online transaction processing (OLTP) engines are crucial components of database systems, facing significant challenges due to the rapid growth of data on the Internet. Memory-oriented OLTP engines struggle with the cost and capacity limitations of hosting large data volumes, while disk-oriented engines suffer performance degradation when their working set size exceeds available memory. Limitations in DRAM density and disk latency hinder scalability and service quality, complicating large-scale data management and transaction execution.

In recent years, advancements in storage architecture, such as persistent memory, NVMe SSDs, and CXL, help alleviate these memory and I/O pressures by bridging the performance gap between DRAM and traditional block storage devices or efficiently expanding memory pools. These technologies are used to enhance and accelerate OLTP engines, with emerging storage hardware and protocols offering improved scalability and remote access.

Despite these innovations, new challenges arise, requiring advanced data management strategies in dynamic environments. This tutorial provides an overview of modern OLTP engines leveraging cutting-edge storage solutions, exploring storage hierarchies, protocols, and programming models that offer insights for researchers and industry professionals. Additionally, it highlights the challenges and opportunities presented by emerging storage architectures for OLTP engines.
papers.abstract_zh:
在线事务处理（OLTP）引擎是数据库系统的重要组成部分，由于互联网上数据的快速增长而面临着巨大的挑战。面向内存的 OLTP 引擎面临托管大数据量的成本和容量限制，而面向磁盘的引擎在工作集大小超过可用内存时会遭受性能下降。 DRAM 密度和磁盘延迟的限制阻碍了可扩展性和服务质量，使大规模数据管理和事务执行变得复杂。

近年来，持久内存、NVMe SSD 和 CXL 等存储架构的进步，通过缩小 DRAM 和传统块存储设备之间的性能差距或有效扩展内存池，有助于缓解这些内存和 I/O 压力。这些技术用于增强和加速 OLTP 引擎，新兴的存储硬件和协议可提供改进的可扩展性和远程访问。

尽管有这些创新，但还是出现了新的挑战，需要在动态环境中采用先进的数据管理策略。本教程概述了利用尖端存储解决方案的现代 OLTP 引擎，探索存储层次结构、协议和编程模型，为研究人员和行业专业人士提供见解。此外，它还强调了新兴存储架构为 OLTP 引擎带来的挑战和机遇。

papers.title: OmniTune: A Universal Framework for Query Refinement via LLMs.
papers.abstract: Numerous studies have proposed solutions for SQL query refinement, where the goal is to make minimal adjustments to an input query to satisfy a given set of constraints. While effective, these approaches typically address specific query types and constraints, whereas, in practice, users may need to refine a diverse range of queries based on their requirements. To address this, we present OmniTune, a universal framework for query refinement. OmniTune features a Refinement Problem Wizard for defining refinement tasks in natural language and a flexible Refinement Engine, which employs an LLM-based multi-agent architecture to support any query refinement problem. We demonstrate OmniTune across various query refinement scenarios using real-world datasets.
papers.abstract_zh: 许多研究提出了 SQL 查询细化的解决方案，其目标是对输入查询进行最小的调整以满足一组给定的约束。虽然有效，但这些方法通常解决特定的查询类型和约束，而在实践中，用户可能需要根据自己的要求细化各种查询。为了解决这个问题，我们推出了 OmniTune，一个用于查询细化的通用框架。 OmniTune 具有用于以自然语言定义细化任务的细化问题向导和灵活的细化引擎，该引擎采用基于 LLM 的多代理架构来支持任何查询细化问题。我们使用真实数据集在各种查询细化场景中演示 OmniTune。

papers.title: OpenMLDB: A Real-Time Relational Data Feature Computation System for Online ML.
papers.abstract:
Efficient and consistent feature computation is crucial for a wide range of online ML applications. Typically, feature computation is divided into two distinct phases, i.e., offline stage for model training and online stage for model serving. These phases often rely on execution engines with different interface languages and function implementations, causing significant inconsistencies. Moreover, many online ML features involve complex time-series computations (e.g., functions over varied-length table windows) that differ from standard streaming and analytical queries. Existing data processing systems (e.g., Spark, Flink, DuckDB) often incur multi-second latencies for these computations, making them unsuitable for real-time online ML applications that demand timely feature updates.

This paper presents OpenMLDB, a feature computation system deployed in 4Paradigm's SageOne platform and over 100 real scenarios. Technically, OpenMLDB first employs a unified query plan generator for consistent computation results across the offline and online stages, significantly reducing feature deployment overhead. Second, OpenMLDB provides an online execution engine that resolves performance bottlenecks caused by long window computations (via pre-aggregation) and multi-table window unions (via data self-adjusting). It also provides a high-performance offline execution engine with window parallel optimization and time-aware data skew resolving. Third, OpenMLDB features a compact data format and stream-focused indexing to maximize memory usage and accelerate data access. Evaluations in testing and real workloads reveal significant performance improvements and resource savings compared to the baseline systems. The open community of OpenMLDB now has over 150 contributors and gained 1.6k stars on GitHub.
papers.abstract_zh:
高效且一致的特征计算对于各种在线机器学习应用程序至关重要。通常，特征计算分为两个不同的阶段，即模型训练的离线阶段和模型服务的在线阶段。这些阶段往往依赖于不同接口语言和函数实现的执行引擎，导致严重的不一致。此外，许多在线机器学习功能涉及复杂的时间序列计算（例如，不同长度表窗口上的函数），这与标准流式处理和分析查询不同。现有的数据处理系统（例如 Spark、Flink、DuckDB）在这些计算中通常会产生数秒的延迟，这使得它们不适合需要及时功能更新的实时在线 ML 应用程序。

本文介绍了 OpenMLDB，这是一个部署在第四范式 SageOne 平台上的特征计算系统，并拥有超过 100 个真实场景。从技术上讲，OpenMLDB首先采用统一的查询计划生成器，以在离线和在线阶段实现一致的计算结果，从而显着减少功能部署开销。其次，OpenMLDB提供了在线执行引擎，解决了长窗口计算（通过预聚合）和多表窗口并集（通过数据自调整）带来的性能瓶颈。它还提供了一个高性能离线执行引擎，具有窗口并行优化和时间感知数据倾斜解决功能。第三，OpenMLDB 具有紧凑的数据格式和以流为中心的索引，可最大限度地提高内存使用率并加速数据访问。测试和实际工作负载的评估表明，与基准系统相比，性能显着提高并节省了资源。 OpenMLDB 开放社区现已拥有超过 150 名贡献者，并在 GitHub 上获得了 1,600 颗星。

papers.title: PalimpChat: Declarative and Interactive AI analytics.
papers.abstract:
Thanks to the advances in generative architectures and large language models, data scientists can now code pipelines of AI operations to process large collections of unstructured data. Recent progress has seen the rise of declarative AI frameworks (e.g., P alimpzest , L otus , and D oc ETL) to build optimized and increasingly complex pipelines, but these systems often remain accessible only to expert programmers. In this demonstration, we present P alimpChat , a chat-based interface to P alimpzest that bridges this gap by letting users create and run sophisticated AI pipelines through natural language alone. By integrating A rchytas , a ReAct-based reasoning agent, and Palimpzest's suite of relational and LLM-based operators, P alimp C hat provides a practical illustration of how a chat interface can make declarative AI frameworks truly accessible to non-experts.

Our demo system is publicly available online. At SIGMOD'25, participants can explore three real-world scenarios-scientific discovery, legal discovery, and real estate search-or apply P alimp C hat to their own datasets. In this paper, we focus on how P alimp C hat , supported by the Palimpzest optimizer, simplifies complex AI workflows such as extracting and analyzing biomedical data.
papers.abstract_zh:
由于生成架构和大型语言模型的进步，数据科学家现在可以对人工智能操作的管道进行编码，以处理大量非结构化数据。最近的进展见证了声明式 AI 框架（例如 P alimpzest、Lotus 和 D oc ETL）的兴起，用于构建优化且日益复杂的管道，但这些系统通常仍然只有专家程序员才能访问。在本次演示中，我们展示了 P alimpChat，这是一个基于聊天的 P alimpzest 界面，它允许用户仅通过自然语言创建和运行复杂的 AI 管道，从而弥补这一差距。通过集成 A rchytas（基于 ReAct 的推理代理）和 Palimpzest 的关系型和基于 LLM 的运算符套件，P alimp Chat 提供了一个实用的说明，说明聊天界面如何使声明式 AI 框架真正可供非专家使用。

我们的演示系统已在线公开提供。在 SIGMOD'25 上，参与者可以探索三个现实世界场景 - 科学发现、法律发现和房地产搜索 - 或将 P alimp C hat 应用于他们自己的数据集。在本文中，我们重点关注 Palimpzest 优化器支持下的 P alimp C hat 如何简化复杂的人工智能工作流程，例如提取和分析生物医学数据。

papers.title: Panel on AI for Future Databases: A New Beginning or a Boulevard of Broken Dreams?
papers.abstract:
AI has opened new directions in database research, from learned components replacing traditional internals to large language mod- els(LLMs), enabling a new generation of database systems that allow querying data beyond tables. Yet, adoption in commercial databases has been incremental rather than a fundamental rethinking of mod- ern data system stacks. In this panel, we bring together experts from academia and industry to discuss the tension between potential and reality in how AI shapes real-world database products.

We will explore questions such as: What should an AI-ready database stack look like: incremental evolution or radical departure? What prevents AI from replacing traditional components like query optimizers, cost models, and indexes? What does it take for LLM- based innovations to move beyond impressive demos? Can we use LLMs for more than Text-to-SQL and LLM-UDFs? By tackling these questions, this panel will challenge assumptions in research, examine the role of AI in future databases, and ask the following: Is AI the key to overcoming core limitations and will thus enable a new generation of database systems, or maybe AI is just another boulevard of broken (database) dreams?
papers.abstract_zh:
人工智能开辟了数据库研究的新方向，从取代传统内部结构的学习组件到大型语言模型（LLM），使新一代数据库系统能够查询表格之外的数据。然而，商业数据库的采用是渐进式的，而不是对现代数据系统堆栈的根本性重新思考。在这个小组中，我们聚集了学术界和工业界的专家，讨论人工智能如何塑造现实世界的数据库产品中潜力与现实之间的紧张关系。

我们将探讨以下问题：人工智能就绪的数据库堆栈应该是什么样子：增量进化还是彻底背离？是什么阻止人工智能取代查询优化器、成本模型和索引等传统组件？基于法学硕士的创新如何才能超越令人印象深刻的演示？我们是否可以将 LLM 用于文本到 SQL 和 LLM-UDF 之外的其他用途？通过解决这些问题，该小组将挑战研究中的假设，审视人工智能在未来数据库中的作用，并提出以下问题：人工智能是克服核心限制的关键，从而实现新一代数据库系统，还是人工智能只是破碎的（数据库）梦想的另一条大道？

papers.title: PASCAL: A Theory-Informed Visual Interface for Property Graph Schema Visualization.
papers.abstract: Property graph schemas are essential for organizing property graph data, serving both prescriptive and descriptive roles. This has led to the recent development of property graph schema languages such as PG-Schema. While understanding of these languages requires familiarity with complex syntax, this poses usability challenges, particularly for domain experts who are not programmers. Current visual abstractions, such as the labeled schema graph (LSG), simplify representation but suffers from visual clutter and limited feature support. In this demonstration, we present a novel theory-informed visual interface called PASCAL for multi-modal visualization of property graph schemas to address these limitations.It realizes a novel visual abstraction called labeled iconized composite schema (LICS), whose design is informed by theories and principles from HCI, cognitive psychology, and visualization. Under the hood, it leverages the novel Map-Paint paradigm for creating and maintaining the visual components of LICS.
papers.abstract_zh: 属性图模式对于组织属性图数据至关重要，具有规定性和描述性的作用。这导致了属性图模式语言（例如 PG-Schema）的最新发展。虽然理解这些语言需要熟悉复杂的语法，但这带来了可用性挑战，特别是对于非程序员的领域专家而言。当前的视觉抽象，例如标记模式图（LSG），简化了表示，但存在视觉混乱和功能支持有限的问题。在本次演示中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于理论的可视化界面，称为 PASCAL，用于属性图模式的多模式可视化，以解决这些限制。它实现了一种称为标记图标化复合模式 (LICS) 的新颖视觉抽象，其设计受到 HCI、认知心理学和可视化的理论和原理的启发。在底层，它利用新颖的地图绘制范例来创建和维护 LICS 的视觉组件。

papers.title: PatternVis: A Tool for Relational Pattern Visualization.
papers.abstract:
We demonstrate PatternVis, an interactive tool that-given a SQL query and its schema-can represent the relational pattern of the query in an extended representation of Relational Diagrams. Notably, PatternVis can represent groupings and aggregation in addition to the non-disjunctive fragment of Relational Diagrams.

Our interactive demo has two main contributions: (1) To the best of our knowledge, this is the first interactive SQL visualization tool that can give an unambiguous representation of nested SQL queries with GROUP BY clauses and aggregation. (2) We present a set of tasks involving multiple-choice questions in which the goal is to identify the correct output of a given aggregation query and database instance. Users can choose to complete each task in one of three modalities (SQL only, PatternVis only, or both) and experience the varying difficulties in each modality.
papers.abstract_zh:
我们演示了 PatternVis，这是一个交互式工具，给定 SQL 查询及其模式，可以在关系图的扩展表示中表​​示查询的关系模式。值得注意的是，除了关系图的非分离片段之外，PatternVis 还可以表示分组和聚合。

我们的交互式演示有两个主要贡献：(1) 据我们所知，这是第一个交互式 SQL 可视化工具，可以通过 GROUP BY 子句和聚合给出嵌套 SQL 查询的明确表示。 (2) 我们提出了一组涉及多项选择问题的任务，其中的目标是识别给定聚合查询和数据库实例的正确输出。用户可以选择以三种模式之一（仅 SQL、仅 PatternVis 或两者）来完成每项任务，并体验每种模式中不同的困难。

papers.title: Privacy and Security in Distributed Data Markets.
papers.abstract: Data markets play a pivotal role in modern industries by facilitating the exchange of data for predictive modeling, targeted marketing, and research. However, as data becomes a valuable commodity, privacy and security concerns have grown, particularly regarding the personal information of individuals. This tutorial explores privacy and security issues when integrating different data sources in data market platforms. As motivation for the importance of enforcing privacy requirements, we discuss attacks on data markets focusing on membership inference and reconstruction attacks. We also discuss security vulnerabilities in decentralized data marketplaces, including adversarial manipulations by buyers or sellers. We provide an overview of privacy and security mechanisms designed to mitigate these risks. In order to enforce the least amount of trust for buyers and sellers, we focus on distributed protocols. Finally, we conclude with opportunities for future research on understanding and mitigating privacy and security concerns in distributed data markets.
papers.abstract_zh: 数据市场通过促进预测建模、有针对性的营销和研究的数据交换，在现代工业中发挥着关键作用。然而，随着数据成为一种有价值的商品，隐私和安全问题日益增多，尤其是个人信息。本教程探讨在数据市场平台中集成不同数据源时的隐私和安全问题。作为执行隐私要求重要性的动机，我们讨论了对数据市场的攻击，重点是成员推理和重建攻击。我们还讨论了去中心化数据市场中的安全漏洞，包括买家或卖家的对抗性操纵。我们概述了旨在减轻这些风险的隐私和安全机制。为了增强买家和卖家的最低限度的信任，我们专注于分布式协议。最后，我们总结了未来研究理解和减轻分布式数据市场中的隐私和安全问题的机会。

papers.title: Prompt Editor: A Taxonomy-driven System for Guided LLM Prompt Development in Enterprise Settings.
papers.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into enterprise platforms, requiring business users to write prompts despite the lack of prompt engineering expertise. Although these users possess domain knowledge, they struggle to translate their intentions into effective prompts and often overlook established practices such as including few-shot examples. We present Prompt Editor, a human-in-the-loop system that assists users in writing high-quality prompts by leveraging an organization's existing corpus of prompts to both initialize and refine user-created prompts. At its core, Prompt Editor leverages a taxonomy of prompt segment types learned from an organization's existing collection of prompts. This supports the segmentation of prompts and feedback generation process. We demonstrate Prompt Editor with a hypothetical LLM-based enterprise platform that extracts structured data from unstructured text. Attendees can interact with the demo to experience first-hand how Prompt Editor supports prompt writing.
papers.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 越来越多地集成到企业平台中，要求业务用户在缺乏即时工程专业知识的情况下编写提示。尽管这些用户拥有领域知识，但他们很难将自己的意图转化为有效的提示，并且经常忽视既定的做法，例如包含少量示例。我们推出了提示编辑器，这是一个人机交互系统，可利用组织现有的提示语料库来初始化和完善用户创建的提示，从而帮助用户编写高质量的提示。提示编辑器的核心是利用从组织现有提示集合中学习到的提示段类型分类。这支持提示和反馈生成过程的分段。我们使用假设的基于 LLM 的企业平台演示 Prompt Editor，该平台可从非结构化文本中提取结构化数据。与会者可以与演示互动，亲身体验提示编辑器如何支持提示写作。

papers.title: ProvenanceWeek2025.
papers.abstract: ProvenanceWeek2025 built on a successful history of provenance workshops bringing together researchers from a wide range of computer science fields including workflows, semantic web, databases,high performance computing, distributed systems, operating systems, programming languages, and software engineering, as well as researchers from other fields, such as biology and physics that have urgent provenance needs.
papers.abstract_zh: ProvenanceWeek2025 建立在起源研讨会的成功历史之上，汇集了来自广泛计算机科学领域的研究人员，包括工作流、语义网络、数据库、高性能计算、分布式系统、操作系统、编程语言和软件工程，以及来自其他领域的研究人员，例如有紧急起源需求的生物学和物理学。

papers.title: Pruning in Snowflake: Working Smarter, Not Harder.
papers.abstract:
Modern cloud-based data analytics systems must efficiently process petabytes of data residing on cloud storage. A key optimization technique in state-of-the-art systems like Snowflake is partition pruning - skipping chunks of data that do not contain relevant information for computing query results.

While partition pruning based on query predicates is a well-established technique, we present new pruning techniques that extend the scope of partition pruning to LIMIT, top-k, and JOIN operations, significantly expanding the opportunities for pruning across diverse query types. We detail the implementation of each method and examine their impact on real-world workloads.

Our analysis of Snowflake's production workloads reveals that real-world analytical queries exhibit much higher selectivity than commonly assumed, yielding effective partition pruning and highlighting the need for more realistic benchmarks. We show that we can harness high selectivity by utilizing min/max metadata available in modern data analytics systems and data lake formats like Apache Iceberg, reducing the number of processed micro-partitions by 99.4% across the Snowflake data platform.
papers.abstract_zh:
现代基于云的数据分析系统必须有效地处理云存储上的 PB 级数据。 Snowflake 等最先进系统中的一项关键优化技术是分区修剪——跳过不包含计算查询结果相关信息的数据块。

虽然基于查询谓词的分区修剪是一种成熟的技术，但我们提出了新的修剪技术，将分区修剪的范围扩展到 LIMIT、top-k 和 JOIN 操作，从而显着扩展了跨不同查询类型进行修剪的机会。我们详细介绍了每种方法的实现，并检查它们对实际工作负载的影响。

我们对 Snowflake 生产工作负载的分析表明，现实世界的分析查询表现出比通常假设的更高的选择性，从而产生有效的分区修剪，并强调需要更现实的基准。我们证明，我们可以通过利用现代数据分析系统中可用的最小/最大元数据和 Apache Iceberg 等数据湖格式来利用高选择性，从而将 Snowflake 数据平台上处理的微分区数量减少 99.4%。

papers.title: PY-SHARQ: A Holistic Python Library for Explaining Association Rules on Relational Data.
papers.abstract: Association rules are an important technique for gaining insights over large relational datasets consisting of tuples of elements (i.e., attribute-value pairs). However, it is difficult to explain the relative importance of data elements with respect to the rules in which they appear. This demo showcases SHARQ (ShApley Rules Quantification), a Shapley-based measure for quantifying an element's contribution to a set of association rules. SHARQ is implemented in a convenient Python library, PY-SHARQ, which supports various explainability scenarios for association rules-including importance analysis of individual elements, rules, and attributes.
papers.abstract_zh: 关联规则是深入了解由元素元组（即属性值对）组成的大型关系数据集的重要技术。然而，很难解释数据元素相对于它们出现的规则的相对重要性。该演示展示了 SHARQ（ShApley 规则量化），这是一种基于 Shapley 的度量，用于量化元素对一组关联规则的贡献。 SHARQ 在方便的 Python 库 PY-SHARQ 中实现，它支持关联规则的各种可解释性场景，包括单个元素、规则和属性的重要性分析。

papers.title: Query Decorrelation in the Fabric Data Warehouse.
papers.abstract: SQL subqueries allow practitioners to embed logic almost anywhere within the context of a more general query and are used both by developers and automatic query generators to express complex SQL logic. Correlated subqueries refer to columns defined by their outer queries and can be evaluated by tuple-at-a-time execution strategies. These evaluation schemes are generally slow, except when specialized index strategies are available. Query decorrelation is a technique that removes correlation from subqueries and typically results in faster set-oriented execution. Query decorrelation has been an active area of research and has received recent attention due to some distributed engines not directly supporting execution of correlated subqueries. This work extends previous approaches and describes our experience in building a new decorrelation framework for the Microsoft Fabric Data Warehouse. We explain our new decorrelation approach, how we integrate it in the query optimizer, and report an experimental evaluation of our techniques on a complex benchmark over TPC-DS data.
papers.abstract_zh: SQL 子查询允许从业者将逻辑嵌入到更通用查询上下文中的几乎任何位置，并且开发人员和自动查询生成器都可以使用 SQL 子查询来表达复杂的 SQL 逻辑。相关子查询是指由其外部查询定义的列，并且可以通过一次元组执行策略进行评估。这些评估方案通常很慢，除非有专门的索引策略可用。查询去相关是一种从子查询中消除相关性的技术，通常会导致更快的面向集合的执行。查询去相关一直是一个活跃的研究领域，并且由于一些分布式引擎不直接支持相关子查询的执行而最近受到关注。这项工作扩展了以前的方法，并描述了我们为 Microsoft Fabric 数据仓库构建新的去相关框架的经验。我们解释了新的去相关方法、如何将其集成到查询优化器中，并报告了在 TPC-DS 数据的复杂基准上对我们的技术进行的实验评估。

papers.title: Qymera: Simulating Quantum Circuits using RDBMS.
papers.abstract: Quantum circuit simulation is crucial for quantum computing such as validating quantum algorithms. We present Qymera, a system that repurposes relational database management systems (RDBMSs) for simulation by translating circuits into SQL queries, allowing quantum operations to run natively within an RDBMS. Qymera supports a wide range of quantum circuits, offering a graphical circuit builder and code-based interfaces to input circuits. With a benchmarking framework, Qymera facilitates comparison of RDBMS-based simulation against state-of-the-art simulation methods. Our demonstration showcases Qymera's end-to-end SQL-based execution, seamless integration with classical workflows, and its utility for development, benchmarking, and education in quantum computing and data management. A video demonstrating Qymera's key features can be found at https://youtu.be/j_fTKnVIV6c.
papers.abstract_zh: 量子电路模拟对于验证量子算法等量子计算至关重要。我们推出了 Qymera，这是一个通过将电路转换为 SQL 查询来重新利用关系数据库管理系统 (RDBMS) 进行模拟的系统，允许量子操作在 RDBMS 内本机运行。 Qymera 支持广泛的量子电路，提供图形电路构建器和基于代码的输入电路接口。借助基准测试框架，Qymera 有助于将基于 RDBMS 的模拟与最先进的模拟方法进行比较。我们的演示展示了 Qymera 基于 SQL 的端到端执行、与经典工作流程的无缝集成，及其在量子计算和数据管理方面的开发、基准测试和教育的实用性。演示 Qymera 主要功能的视频可以在 https://youtu.be/j_fTKnVIV6c 上找到。

papers.title: Real Time Sentinel: An LLM Based PII Detector - A Streaming Integration and Intelligence Platform.
papers.abstract: Today's data-driven AI applications need to process, analyze and act on real-time data as it arrives, not wait for it to traverse a slow and complex pipeline. The massive amount of data is continuously generated from multiple sources and they come in a streaming fashion with high volume and high velocity, which makes it hard to process and analyze in real time. We introduce novel Gen AI based enhancements to Striim, a distributed streaming platform that enables unified real-time data integration and intelligence. Striim provides high-throughput, low-latency event processing with a scale- out architecture. It can ingest streaming data from multiple sources, process data with a SQL-like continuous query language, analyze data with sophisticated machine learning and large language models, write data into a variety of targets, and visualize data to support real-time decision making. It guarantees Exactly-Once-Processing semantics and fault tolerance. In this demonstration, we showcase Striim's ability to identify sensitive data over fast-moving data streams in real time using LLMs.
