papers.abstract_zh: 当今数据驱动的人工智能应用程序需要在实时数据到达时对其进行处理、分析和采取行动，而不是等待它穿过缓慢而复杂的管道。海量数据是从多个来源连续产生的，并且以流的方式产生，数据量大、速度快，这使得实时处理和分析变得困难。我们为 Striim 引入了新颖的基于 Gen AI 的增强功能，Striim 是一个分布式流媒体平台，可实现统一的实时数据集成和智能。 Striim 通过横向扩展架构提供高吞吐量、低延迟的事件处理。它可以从多个来源摄取流数据，使用类似 SQL 的连续查询语言处理数据，使用复杂的机器学习和大型语言模型分析数据，将数据写入各种目标，以及可视化数据以支持实时决策。它保证了 Exactly-Once-Processing 语义和容错能力。在此演示中，我们展示了 Striim 使用 LLM 实时识别快速移动数据流中的敏感数据的能力。

papers.title: RedTAO: A Trillion-edge High-throughput Graph Store.
papers.abstract:
With the explosive growth of daily active users, the social graph data of Xiaohongshu has scaled to trillions of edges, imposing high pressure on our storage system. Current state-of-the-art systems struggle to address the issue, primarily due to: (1) Traditional relational databases as the back-end storage require frequent scaling, incurring high cost and stability risks. (2) Most graph databases focus on complex multi-hop queries. Redundant components in these systems make them difficult to take advantage when processing our workloads dominated by one-hop queries. (3) Using cache systems like Redis or Memcache often struggles to ensure consistency between the cache and storage.

In this paper, we propose RedTAO, which has a scalable and efficient graph cache layer optimized for social scenarios. Over 90.7% of queries are served directly by the cache, enabling us to focus on scaling it as traffic increases. RedTAO employs cross-cloud, multi-active deployment, synchronizing replicas through the storage layer. The cache layer directly accesses local storage, avoiding costly cross-region requests. Additionally, the data transmission service (DTS) component asynchronously corrects cache data, ensuring cache consistency. RedTAO has been successfully deployed in Xiaohongshu, achieving a 1.8× throughput improvement and at least 21.3% reduction in resource usage compared to the previously used MySQL architecture.
papers.abstract_zh:
随着日活跃用户的爆发式增长，小红书的社交图数据已经扩展到万亿条边，给我们的存储系统带来了巨大的压力。目前最先进的系统很难解决这个问题，主要是由于：（1）传统的关系数据库作为后端存储需要频繁扩展，带来较高的成本和稳定性风险。 (2)大多数图数据库专注于复杂的多跳查询。这些系统中的冗余组件使得它们在处理以一跳查询为主的工作负载时难以利用。 (3) 使用Redis或Memcache等缓存系统通常很难确保缓存和存储之间的一致性。

在本文中，我们提出了 RedTAO，它具有针对社交场景优化的可扩展且高效的图缓存层。超过 90.7% 的查询直接由缓存提供服务，使我们能够随着流量的增加专注于扩展缓存。 RedTAO采用跨云、多活部署，通过存储层同步副本。缓存层直接访问本地存储，避免了昂贵的跨区域请求。此外，数据传输服务（DTS）组件异步纠正缓存数据，保证缓存一致性。 RedTAO已成功部署在小红书，与之前使用的MySQL架构相比，实现了1.8倍的吞吐量提升和至少21.3%的资源占用降低。

papers.title: Rel: A Programming Language for Relational Data.
papers.abstract: Rel is a new relational language whose key design goal is to allow both database querying and programming in the large without relying on the currently dominant paradigm in which a query sublanguage is embedded in a host programming language. With the new approach we can model the semantics of entire enterprise applications relationally, which helps significantly reduce architecture complexity and avoid the well-known impedance mismatch problem. This paradigm shift is enabled by 50 years of database research, making it possible to revisit the sublanguage/host language paradigm, starting from the fundamental principles. We present the main features of Rel: those that give it the power to express traditional query language operations, and those that are designed to grow the language and allow programming in the large.
papers.abstract_zh: Rel 是一种新的关系语言，其主要设计目标是允许大规模数据库查询和编程，而不依赖于当前占主导地位的范式（其中查询子语言嵌入到主机编程语言中）。通过新方法，我们可以对整个企业应用程序的语义进行关联建模，这有助于显着降低架构复杂性并避免众所周知的阻抗不匹配问题。这种范式转变是通过 50 年的数据库研究实现的，使得从基本原则开始重新审视子语言/主语言范式成为可能。我们介绍 Rel 的主要功能：那些赋予它表达传统查询语言操作的能力的功能，以及那些旨在扩展语言并允许大规模编程的功能。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'A Lovász-Simonovits Theorem for Hypergraphs with Application to Local Clustering'.
papers.abstract: In this report, we provide an overview of the reproducibility of the ACM SIGMOD 2025 paper ''\papertitle''. The authors provided a project on GitHub and Google Colab where all the environments, code, and scripts were set up. It is easy to regenerate the results. The output aligns with the authors' presentation in the paper.
papers.abstract_zh: 在本报告中，我们概述了 ACM SIGMOD 2025 论文“\papertitle”的可重复性。作者在 GitHub 和 Google Colab 上提供了一个项目，其中设置了所有环境、代码和脚本。重新生成结果很容易。输出与作者在论文中的介绍一致。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'A Structured Study of Multivariate Time-Series Distance Measures'.
papers.abstract: This reproducibility report provides AE details for the paper ''A Structured Study of Multivariate Time-Series Distance Measures'', which is accepted at the SIGMOD 2025 conference. In all, the code provided by the authors is well-arranged, and the main results are reproduced as expected.
papers.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了论文“多元时间序列距离测量的结构化研究”的 AE 详细信息，该论文已在 SIGMOD 2025 会议上被接受。总而言之，作者提供的代码排列良好，主要结果按照预期重现。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Alsatian: Optimizing Model Search for Deep Transfer Learning'.
papers.abstract: This is a reproduction evaluation of ''Alsatian: Optimizing Model Search for Deep Transfer Learning'', which observed the overlapping of models during model searching and optimizes it with a novel solution that utilizes caches and excellent searching algorithms. The authors provided detailed descriptions for setting up the required environment through Docker containers, as well as scripts to perform experiments for each figure in paper. Thanks to the authors' help, we were able to perform the reproduction evaluation using the same environment as described in the paper. We succeeded in reproducing the results that can serve as a certificate of the result authenticity.
papers.abstract_zh: 这是对“Alsatian：优化深度迁移学习的模型搜索”的再现评估，它观察了模型搜索过程中模型的重叠，并通过利用缓存和优秀搜索算法的新颖解决方案对其进行了优化。作者在论文中提供了通过 Docker 容器设置所需环境的详细描述，以及对每个图进行实验的脚本。感谢作者的帮助，我们能够使用论文中描述的相同环境进行再现评估。我们成功地复制了结果，可以作为结果真实性的证明。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'An Elephant Under the Microscope: Analyzing the Interaction of Optimizer Components in PostgreSQL'.
papers.abstract: This document reports our experience in reproducing the experiments from the paper ''An Elephant Under the Microscope: Analyzing the Interaction of Optimizer Components in PostgreSQL''[1], published at SIGMOD 2025. The paper investigates the intricate interactions among key components of classic SQL query optimizers: the candidate plan enumerator, the table statistics, and the plan cost estimator. This investigation is based on empirical benchmarks conducted on a real-world database system, PostgreSQL. Although optimizer-centric benchmarks are often difficult to reproduce due to long execution times, high sensitivity to the execution environment, and the sometimes unpredictable behavior of production-grade optimizers, we found that the authors of [1] did an excellent job at designing reproducible experiments. While this report does not cover the full set of experiments presented in [1], we believe that the results obtained here are sufficient to validate the paper's core thesis.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文档报告了我们在 SIGMOD 2025 上发表的论文“显微镜下的大象：分析 PostgreSQL 中优化器组件的交互”[1] 中重现实验的经验。该论文研究了经典 SQL 查询优化器的关键组件之间复杂的交互：候选计划枚举器、表统计信息和计划成本估计器。这项调查基于在现实世界的数据库系统 PostgreSQL 上进行的实证基准。尽管由于执行时间长、对执行环境高度敏感以及生产级优化器有时不可预测的行为，以优化器为中心的基准测试通常很难重现，但我们发现[1]的作者在设计可重现的实验方面做得非常出色。虽然本报告没有涵盖[1]中提出的全套实验，但我们相信这里获得的结果足以验证论文的核心论文。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Approximating Opaque Top-k Queries'.
papers.abstract: This report documents the reproducibility review of the paper ''Approximating Opaque Top-k Queries'' by Jiwon Chang and Fatemeh Nargesian. We successfully reproduced the experimental results for the synthetic and tabular datasets (Figures 4, 5, and 6), which support the core claims regarding the efficiency and accuracy of the proposed bandit algorithm. The authors provided a well-structured repository and cooperated to resolve minor script issues during the review process.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告记录了 Jiwon Chang 和 Fatemeh Nargesian 的论文“近似不透明 Top-k 查询”的可重复性审查。我们成功地重现了合成数据集和表格数据集的实验结果（图 4、5 和 6），这些结果支持了有关所提出的 bandit 算法的效率和准确性的核心主张。作者提供了一个结构良好的存储库，并在审查过程中合作解决了较小的脚本问题。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Are Database System Researchers Making Correct Assumptions about Transaction Workloads?'.
papers.abstract: This reproducibility report evaluates the artifact associated with the SIGMOD 2025 paper ''Are Database System Researchers Making Correct Assumptions about Transaction Workloads?''. We successfully reproduced the core data analysis pipeline using the authors' provided VS Code extension (splinter), ORM-specific analyzers, and Jupyter notebooks. The semi-automated tool accurately categorized ORM operations across multiple open-source applications, generating JSON results that match those in the authors' data.zip archive. Visualizations produced from the provided notebook closely replicate the figures in the original paper. Our findings confirm that the experimental methodology is well-documented, executable, and yields consistent results across environments. We conclude that the paper meets SIGMOD's reproducibility standards.
papers.abstract_zh: 这份可重复性报告评估了与 SIGMOD 2025 论文“数据库系统研究人员对事务工作负载做出正确假设吗？”相关的工件。我们使用作者提供的 VS Code 扩展（splinter）、特定于 ORM 的分析器和 Jupyter Notebook 成功地重现了核心数据分析管道。该半自动化工具准确地对多个开源应用程序中的 ORM 操作进行了分类，生成与作者的 data.zip 存档中的结果相匹配的 JSON 结果。从所提供的笔记本中生成的可视化结果与原始论文中的数字非常接近。我们的研究结果证实，实验方法有详细记录、可执行，并且在不同环境中产生一致的结果。我们的结论是这篇论文符合 SIGMOD 的可重复性标准。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Auto-Test: Learning Semantic-Domain Constraints for Unsupervised Error Detection in Tables'.
papers.abstract: The main experiments in the paper ''Auto-Test: Learning Semantic-Domain Constraints for Unsupervised Error Detection in Tables'' can be reproduced successfully. The authors provided a repository with clear instructions on reproducing each experiment, the code used to run the program, and the link to the datasets used in the experiment.
papers.abstract_zh: 论文“自动测试：学习表中无监督错误检测的语义域约束”中的主要实验可以成功重现。作者提供了一个存储库，其中包含有关重现每个实验的清晰说明、用于运行程序的代码以及实验中使用的数据集的链接。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'BCviz: A Linear-Space Index for Mining and Visualizing Cohesive Bipartite Subgraphs'.
papers.abstract: This report presents the successful reproducibility assessment of the SIGMOD 2025 paper ''BCviz: A Linear-Space Index for Mining and Visualizing Cohesive Bipartite Subgraphs''. The paper introduces BCviz, a linear-space, data-driven index designed to reveal the structure of cohesive subgraphs and accelerate maximum biclique search. The evaluation successfully reproduces key results, including index visualization, construction time, search performance, and scalability analysis. The runtime results obtained on the reviewer's testbed matched (and in some cases exceeded) those reported in the paper. Suggestions for readability and code decomposition are provided.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告介绍了 SIGMOD 2025 论文“BCviz：用于挖掘和可视化内聚二分子图的线性空间索引”的成功再现性评估。本文介绍了 BCviz，这是一种线性空间、数据驱动的索引，旨在揭示内聚子图的结构并加速最大 Biclique 搜索。评估成功地重现了关键结果，包括索引可视化、构建时间、搜索性能和可扩展性分析。在审稿人的测试平台上获得的运行时结果与论文中报告的结果相匹配（在某些情况下甚至超过了）。提供了可读性和代码分解的建议。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Clementi: Efficient Load Balancing and Communication Overlap for Multi-FPGA Graph Processing'.
papers.abstract: This reproducibility study evaluates the Clementi multi-FPGA graph processing framework using the authors' preconfigured cluster environment. By following the provided reproducibility guide and execution scripts, I was able to verify the correctness of the system's core components and confirm that the produced outputs were consistent with the expected results. Due to the specialized hardware requirements, only functional correctness was reproduced. Overall, the key claims of the paper were validated within the constraints of the available environment.
papers.abstract_zh: 这项可重复性研究使用作者预先配置的集群环境评估 Clementi 多 FPGA 图形处理框架。通过遵循提供的重现性指南和执行脚本，我能够验证系统核心组件的正确性，并确认生成的输出与预期结果一致。由于特殊的硬件要求，仅再现了功能正确性。总体而言，本文的关键主张在可用环境的限制内得到了验证。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Constant Optimization Driven Database System Testing'.
papers.abstract: The authors proposed a new automated methodology for finding bugs in databases and provided scripts to run their bug-finding method against production databases. They also provided a list of bugs found through their approach which are currently filed in the respective database's bug tracking system. We were able to successfully run the scripts and verify the bug artifacts provided by the authors.
papers.abstract_zh: 作者提出了一种新的自动化方法来查找数据库中的错误，并提供了脚本来针对生产数据库运行他们的错误查找方法。他们还提供了通过他们的方法发现的错误列表，这些错误目前已归档在各自数据库的错误跟踪系统中。我们能够成功运行脚本并验证作者提供的错误工件。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'CRDV: Conflict-free Replicated Data Views'.
papers.abstract: This report presents the reproducibility review of the paper ''CRDV: Conflict-free Replicated Data Views''. The paper's artifacts were available and well-documented. The authors provided Docker deployment with all scripts and plotting routines, which made reproducibility straightforward. We successfully reproduced the core claims of the paper and documented any deviations in this report.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告介绍了论文“CRDV：无冲突复制数据视图”的可重复性审查。这篇论文的成果是可用的并且有详细的记录。作者为 Docker 部署提供了所有脚本和绘图例程，这使得可重复性变得简单。我们成功地重现了该论文的核心主张，并在本报告中记录了任何偏差。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Dangers of List Processing in Querying Property Graphs'.
papers.abstract: The core thesis of this paper could easily be reproduced in the original environment. The authors provided an almost fully automated set of scripts that automatically execute the experiments. The experimental data was automatically collected and plotted. The resulting figures have some minor deviations from the originals, but the overall trends remain the same, and hence they can be considered a good fit.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文的核心论文可以很容易地在原始环境中复制。作者提供了一组几乎完全自动化的脚本来自动执行实验。自动收集并绘制实验数据。所得数据与原始数据有一些微小偏差，但总体趋势保持不变，因此可以认为它们是很好的拟合。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Deep Overlapping Community Search via Subspace Embedding'.
papers.abstract: The paper introduces and evaluates Simplified Multi-hop Attention Networks. The experiments included in the paper are reproducible. Our reproduced figures showcase similar trends and relationships between the alternatives compared and support the major findings of the evaluation. We noted variations in the performance and quality of training and predictive measurements of some experiments, which can be attributed to hardware differences.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文介绍并评估了简化多跳注意力网络。论文中包含的实验是可重复的。我们复制的数据展示了比较的替代方案之间的相似趋势和关系，并支持了评估的主要发现。我们注意到一些实验的训练和预测测量的性能和质量存在差异，这可以归因于硬件差异。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Disco: A Compact Index for LSM-trees'.
papers.abstract: This report presents a reproducibility study of Disco, a compact multi-run index designed to improve read performance in LSM-tree-based key-value stores. Disco introduces compact key representations to enable both point and range queries with at most one I/O to underlying runs, achieving efficiency close to B+-trees. We reproduce the main experimental results reported in the original paper, including micro-benchmark evaluations and end-to-end performance comparisons against RocksDB and REMIX. Our reproduced results match closely the trends and performance gains claimed by the authors, demonstrating the robustness of the Disco design. The provided implementation and experimental scripts are well-documented, which significantly facilitates reproducibility.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告介绍了 Disco 的可重复性研究，Disco 是一种紧凑的多运行索引，旨在提高基于 LSM 树的键值存储中的读取性能。 Disco 引入了紧凑的键表示，以支持点查询和范围查询，底层运行最多具有 1 个 I/O，实现接近 B+ 树的效率。我们重现了原始论文中报告的主要实验结果，包括微基准评估以及与 RocksDB 和 REMIX 的端到端性能比较。我们重现的结果与作者声称的趋势和性能增益非常匹配，证明了 Disco 设计的稳健性。提供的实现和实验脚本都有详细记录，这极大地促进了可重复性。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Fast and Scalable Data Transfer Across Data Systems'.
papers.abstract: The submission provides detailed instructions for setting up the required system and running the experiments using the supplied scripts. While the full experimental evaluation is time-consuming due to the large number of experiments, the documentation enables a systematic execution of the workflows and the regeneration of figures that reproduce the performance trends reported in the paper "Fast and Scalable Data Transfer Across Data Systems".
papers.abstract_zh: 提交内容提供了设置所需系统并使用提供的脚本运行实验的详细说明。虽然由于大量实验，完整的实验评估非常耗时，但文档可以系统地执行工作流程和重新生成图形，从而重现“跨数据系统的快速且可扩展的数据传输”论文中报告的性能趋势。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Galley: Modern Query Optimization for Sparse Tensor Programs'.
papers.abstract: The core thesis of the paper has been successfully reproduced. The authors provided a functional artifact containing the implementation of Galley together with scripts for running the experiments and generating the corresponding figures. With minor adjustments to the environment and paths, the vast majority of results were reproduced and the generated plots closely matched those presented in the submitted paper. The artifact enabled reliable replication of the main findings and validated the performance claims of the original work.
papers.abstract_zh: 论文核心论文已成功转载。作者提供了一个功能工件，其中包含 Galley 的实现以及用于运行实验和生成相应图形的脚本。通过对环境和路径进行细微调整，可以重现绝大多数结果，并且生成的图与提交的论文中呈现的图非常匹配。该工件能够可靠地复制主要发现，并验证原始工作的性能声明。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'H-Rocks: CPU-GPU accelerated Heterogeneous RocksDB on Persistent Memory'.
papers.abstract: This reproducibility report evaluates the artifact accompanying H-Rocks, a heterogeneous key–value store that accelerates per- sistent RocksDB operations using GPU and Persistent Memory. We reproduce the key experimental results of the paper on the authors' original hardware configuration, including Intel Optane Persistent Memory and an NVIDIA Titan RTX GPU. During the initial reproduction phase, we encountered minor ambiguities and missing components in the artifact documentation. These issues were promptly resolved, resulting in clearer instructions and a more complete artifact. Following these refinements, we success- fully reproduced the reported performance trends and results. Our evaluation confirms that the H-Rocks artifact is reproducible on the specified hardware and software environment. However, H- rocks requires a strict adherence to the required hardware system configuration for consistent results
papers.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告评估了 H-Rocks 附带的工件，H-Rocks 是一种异构键值存储，可使用 GPU 和持久内存加速持久 RocksDB 操作。我们在作者的原始硬件配置上重现了论文的关键实验结果，包括英特尔傲腾持久内存和 NVIDIA Titan RTX GPU。在最初的复制阶段，我们在工件文档中遇到了一些小的歧义和缺失的组件。这些问题得到了及时解决，从而产生了更清晰的指令和更完整的工件。经过这些改进，我们成功地重现了报告的性能趋势和结果。我们的评估证实 H-Rocks 工件在指定的硬件和软件环境上是可重现的。然而，H-rocks 需要严格遵守所需的硬件系统配置才能获得一致的结果

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'HotStuff-1: Linear Consensus with One-Phase Speculation'.
papers.abstract: We reproduce the experimental results reported in the original paper. The authors provided their original implementation and scripts that (i) execute all experiments from the evaluation section and collect the relevant metrics, and (ii) generate the plots reported in the paper. Our evaluation confirms that the main experimental findings of the paper are reproducible.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们重现了原始论文中报告的实验结果。作者提供了他们的原始实现和脚本，（i）执行评估部分的所有实验并收集相关指标，以及（ii）生成论文中报告的图。我们的评估证实该论文的主要实验结果是可重复的。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'InTime: Towards Performance Predictability In Byzantine Fault Tolerant Proof-of-Stake Consensus'.
papers.abstract: We present the availability and reproducibility report of the ACM SIGMOD 2025 paper titled ''InTime: Towards Performance Predictability In Byzantine Fault Tolerant Proof-of-Stake Consensus''. Following the instructions provided the authors, we evaluated the artifacts hosted on Github. The reviewers confirmed that the codebase is functional and evaluatable. Reproducibility was straightforward as the authors provided automated scripts that simplified the execution of experiments. Experiments on single machine were reproduced while experiments requiring a large cluster of machines are constrained by limited resource capacity and instructions. Hence, the paper's core claims were partially reproduced.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们提出了 ACM SIGMOD 2025 论文的可用性和可重复性报告，题为“InTime：在拜占庭容错权益证明共识中实现性能可预测性”。按照作者提供的说明，我们评估了 Github 上托管的工件。审阅者确认代码库功能齐全且可评估。重现性很简单，因为作者提供了简化实验执行的自动化脚本。在单台机器上进行的实验是重复的，而需要大量机器的实验则受到有限的资源容量和指令的限制。因此，该论文的核心主张被部分转载。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Live Patching for Distributed In-Memory Key-Value Stores'.
papers.abstract: This paper proposes a new solution to handle the service downtime and performance degradation issues posed by traditional rolling updates when maintaining distributed in-memory key-value stores (IMKVS). The authors propose a live patching system for Redis Cluster which proactively manages software updates by modifying process memory directly without restarting nodes, while leveraging the cluster-internal gossip protocol for automatic patch distribution. The authors present a set of experiments comparing the efficacy of live patching (LP) to state-of-the-art rolling updates (CP) on a range of cluster sizes and memory states up to 200~GiB. We find that on the hardware specified (including Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC configurations) we were able to reproduce the key results and plots shown in the paper, illustrating the robust nature of these experiments and confirming that live patching achieves constant-time updates regardless of data size. Furthermore, the authors provided well-written shell scripts and easy-to-follow instructions within a QEMU virtual machine environment to reproduce this material.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新的解决方案，用于处理维护分布式内存键值存储 (IMKVS) 时传统滚动更新带来的服务停机和性能下降问题。作者提出了一种用于 Redis 集群的实时补丁系统，该系统通过直接修改进程内存而无需重新启动节点来主动管理软件更新，同时利用集群内部八卦协议进行自动补丁分发。作者提出了一组实验，比较了实时修补 (LP) 与最先进的滚动更新 (CP) 在一系列集群大小和高达 200~GiB 的内存状态上的功效。我们发现，在指定的硬件（包括 Intel Xeon 和 AMD EPYC 配置）上，我们能够重现论文中显示的关键结果和图表，说明这些实验的稳健性，并确认实时修补可以实现恒定时间更新，无论数据大小如何。此外，作者在 QEMU 虚拟机环境中提供了编写良好的 shell 脚本和易于遵循的指令来重现此材料。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'LpBound: Pessimistic Cardinality Estimation Using ?p-Norms of Degree Sequences'.
papers.abstract: We successfully reproduced the core findings and performance results of the paper ''LpBound: Pessimistic cardinality Estimation Using ℓ p -Norms of Degree Sequences''. The paper proposed an intriguing bridge between information theory and cardinality estimation and derives tight upper bounds of the intermediate cardinalities by combining Shannon inequalities, ℓ p -norms, and linear programming. The authors provided scripts to execute the experiments, the original datasets, and comprehensive, well-written documentation that allowed to reproduce of the core results.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们成功地重现了论文“LpBound：使用 ℓ p -度数序列范数进行悲观基数估计”的核心发现和性能结果。该论文提出了信息论和基数估计之间的有趣桥梁，并通过结合香农不等式、ℓ p 范数和线性规划推导了中间基数的严格上限。作者提供了执行实验的脚本、原始数据集以及全面且编写良好的文档，可以重现核心结果。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Memento Filter: A Fast, Dynamic, and Robust Range Filter'.
papers.abstract: We evaluate the reproducibility of ''Memento Filter: A Fast, Dynamic, and Robust Range Filter''. The paper proposes a dynamic range filter with robust false-positive guarantees. Using the authors' public artifact and scripts (repository: https://github.com/n3slami/Memento_Filter), we reproduced the paper's main experimental results (paper Figures 8–14). The reproduced trends closely match the original claims.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们评估了“Memento Filter：快速、动态且鲁棒的范围过滤器”的再现性。该论文提出了一种具有鲁棒误报保证的动态范围滤波器。使用作者的公共工件和脚本（存储库：https://github.com/n3slami/Memento_Filter），我们重现了论文的主要实验结果（论文图 8-14）。复制的趋势与原始声明非常吻合。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Mnemosyne: Dynamic Workload-Aware BF Tuning via Accurate Statistics in LSM trees'.
papers.abstract: This report presents a reproducibility evaluation of the Mnemosyne paper [1] from SIGMOD 2025, which proposes a framework to allocate Bloom filter memory at a per-file granularity in evolving LSM trees. The artifact enables near one-command reproduction of all the paper's experiments and figures. We successfully reproduced all key empirical findings of the paper using a different hardware platform from the original study.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告对 SIGMOD 2025 的 Mnemosyne 论文 [1] 进行了可重复性评估，该论文提出了一个框架，用于在不断发展的 LSM 树中以每个文件的粒度分配布隆过滤器内存。该工件几乎可以通过一个命令再现论文中的所有实验和数据。我们使用与原始研究不同的硬件平台成功地重现了论文的所有关键实证结果。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'On the Feasibility and Benefits of Extensive Evaluation'.
papers.abstract: We accurately reproduced the core findings of the original paper, encompassing its reported explanation quality and extensive evaluation results of existing methods. This was facilitated by the authors' release of sample datasets, well-documented scripts, and plotting tools, which allowed us to recreate the figures with near-exact precision.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们准确地再现了原始论文的核心发现，包括其报告的解释质量和现有方法的广泛评估结果。作者发布的示例数据集、记录良好的脚本和绘图工具促进了这一点，使我们能够以近乎精确的精度重新创建图形。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Parallel k-Core Decomposition: Theory and Practice'.
papers.abstract: This report evaluates the reproducibility of the paper ''Parallel k -Core Decomposition: Theory and Practice'' by Liu et al. The authors provide a public GitHub repository and an artifact README describing a single script that runs all k -core algorithms and baselines and aggregates results into one CSV. The paper's core performance claims were reproduced, with minor variations on a few datasets.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告评估了 Liu 等人的论文“并行 k 核心分解：理论与实践”的可重复性。作者提供了一个公共 GitHub 存储库和一个工件自述文件，描述了运行所有 k 核算法和基线并将结果聚合到一个 CSV 中的单个脚本。该论文的核心性能主张被复制，但在一些数据集上略有变化。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'PilotDB: Database-Agnostic Online Approximate Query Processing with A Priori Error Guarantees'.
papers.abstract: This is a reproducibility report for the paper ''PilotDB: Database-Agnostic Online Approximate Query Processing with A Priori Error Guarantees'' published in SIGMOD 2025. The paper proposes PilotDB, an online approximate query processing (AQP) system that provides a priori error guarantees without requiring database system modification or incurring maintenance overhead. The main evaluation results of the paper can be successfully reproduced. Our findings confirm that PilotDB achieves substantial query performance improvements compared to conventional database systems.
papers.abstract_zh: 这是 SIGMOD 2025 上发表的论文“PilotDB：具有先验错误保证的与数据库无关的在线近似查询处理”的可重复性报告。该论文提出了 PilotDB，一种在线近似查询处理（AQP）系统，可以提供先验错误保证，无需数据库系统修改或产生维护开销。论文的主要评价结果可以成功复现。我们的研究结果证实，与传统数据库系统相比，PilotDB 实现了显着的查询性能改进。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'PLM4NDV: Minimizing Data Access for Number of Distinct Values Estimation with Pre-trained Language Models'.
papers.abstract: We successfully reproduced the key performance results that presented in Tables~3 and~4 of the original paper. The authors provided a GitHub repository. https://github.com/bytedance/plm4ndv that includes the implementation, detailed build and execution instructions, and links to the datasets used in the experiments. We appreciate the authors' assistance with environment setup and debugging.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们成功地重现了原始论文表~3 和~4 中的关键性能结果。作者提供了一个 GitHub 存储库。 https://github.com/bytedance/plm4ndv，其中包括实现、详细的构建和执行说明以及实验中使用的数据集的链接。我们感谢作者在环境设置和调试方面提供的帮助。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'RWalks: RandomWalks as Attribute Diffusers for Filtered Vector Search'.
papers.abstract: The reproducibility evaluation successfully replicated key functionalities of RWalks: Random Walks as Attribute Diffusers for Filtered Vector Search. The authors provided documentation of the software and hardware requirements, along with well-structured scripts and datasets hosted in an organized GitHub repository. This reproducibility report compares the original results with the reproduced findings, both of which show similar trends, thereby supporting the authors' claims.
papers.abstract_zh: 再现性评估成功复制了 RWalks 的关键功能：随机游走作为过滤矢量搜索的属性扩散器。作者提供了软件和硬件要求的文档，以及托管在有组织的 GitHub 存储库中的结构良好的脚本和数据集。这份再现性报告将原始结果与再现的结果进行了比较，两者都显示出相似的趋势，从而支持了作者的主张。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'SBSC: A fast Self-tuned Bipartite proximity graph-based Spectral Clustering'.
papers.abstract: This reproducibility report evaluates the SBSC algorithm for fast self-tuned bipartite proximity graph-based spectral clustering using the artifacts released by the original authors. The provided source code, datasets, and documentation enabled successful execution of the complete experimental pipeline. By following the supplied instructions, the main experimental results reported in the paper were reproduced successfully. Overall, the core claims of the work were validated under the described experimental setup.
papers.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告使用原作者发布的工件评估了 SBSC 算法，用于快速自调整的基于二分邻近图的谱聚类。提供的源代码、数据集和文档使完整的实验流程得以成功执行。按照提供的说明，成功重现了论文中报告的主要实验结果。总的来说，这项工作的核心主张在所描述的实验设置下得到了验证。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Self-Enhancing Video Data Management System for Compositional Events with Large Language Models'.
papers.abstract: This report presents a reproducibility evaluation of the VOCAL-UDF paper [1] from SIGMOD 2025, which introduces a video data management system that answers complex compositional queries over videos. Using the author-provided software artifact and cached outputs for LLM-based components, we successfully reproduced all main figures and tables supporting the paper's core claims.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告对 SIGMOD 2025 的 VOCAL-UDF 论文 [1] 进行了可重复性评估，该论文引入了一种视频数据管理系统，可以回答视频中复杂的构图查询。使用作者提供的软件工件和基于 LLM 的组件的缓存输出，我们成功地重现了支持论文核心主张的所有主要数字和表格。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'SPARTAN: Data-Adaptive Symbolic Time-Series Approximation'.
papers.abstract: We were able to reproduce the findings presented in the original paper. Our experimental results and key performance findings align with those presented by the authors. The setup of the environment was minimal and easy to follow. The authors provided a detailed description of the data acquisition, along with instructions for the experimental setup, running the scripts, and generating the plots presented in the paper.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们能够重现原始论文中提出的发现。我们的实验结果和关键性能发现与作者提出的结果一致。环境的设置非常简单且易于遵循。作者提供了数据采集的详细描述，以及实验设置、运行脚本和生成论文中的绘图的说明。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'SuSe: Summary Selection for Regular Expression Subsequence Aggregation over Streams'.
papers.abstract: The core claims of the SuSe paper are reproducible. The authors provide an accessible implementation of the system along with clear instructions for building and executing the experiments. The repository includes all components necessary to evaluate the StateSummary structure and the summary selection strategy. The datasets referenced in the paper are also publicly available. Our reproduction confirms the reported effectiveness of SuSe in maintaining accurate aggregates under subsequence matching while using significantly fewer resources than baseline approaches.
papers.abstract_zh: SuSe 论文的核心主张是可重复的。作者提供了系统的可访问实现以及构建和执行实验的明确说明。该存储库包括评估 StateSummary 结构和摘要选择策略所需的所有组件。论文中引用的数据集也是公开的。我们的复制证实了 SuSe 在子序列匹配下保持准确聚合的有效性，同时使用比基线方法少得多的资源。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'T3: Accurate and Fast Performance Prediction for Relational Database Systems With Compiled Decision Trees'.
papers.abstract: This report evaluates the availability and reproducibility of the paper entitled, ''T3: Accurate and Fast Performance Prediction for Relational Database Systems With Compiled Decision Trees.'' The report outlines the submitted artifact and details the reproduction process. As the reproduced results were consistent with those of the original paper, the artifact is deemed eligible for both the Availability and Reproducibility badges.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告评估了题为“T3：使用编译决策树对关系数据库系统进行准确且快速的性能预测”的论文的可用性和可重复性。该报告概述了提交的工件并详细介绍了重复过程。由于再现的结果与原始论文的结果一致，因此该工件被视为有资格获得可用性和再现性徽章。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Tribase: A Vector Data Query Engine for Reliable and Lossless Pruning Compression using Triangle Inequalities'.
papers.abstract: The authors of the paper provide detailed descriptions and comprehensive facilities to setup the required software system and perform most of their experiments. We are able to reproduce and verify the results sufficiently to support the core thesis of the paper. Although running experiments with the same configurations as in the paper is straightforward, there are limitations in flexibility and portability.
papers.abstract_zh: 该论文的作者提供了详细的描述和全面的设施来设置所需的软件系统并执行大部分实验。我们能够充分重现和验证结果以支持论文的核心论文。尽管使用与论文中相同的配置运行实验很简单，但在灵活性和可移植性方面存在限制。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Zombie Hashing: Reanimating Tombstones in Graveyard'.
papers.abstract: The paper introduces Zombie hashing, a novel scheme that deamortizes tombstone redistribution across hash table operations. The authors present a set of experiments to evaluate the proposed hash table approach on space-efficiency, throughput, and latency distribution. Our reproducibility evaluation confirms that the paper's key findings can be reliably reproduced and are supported by the reproduced results in this report.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了僵尸哈希，这是一种新颖的方案，可以跨哈希表操作对墓碑重新分配进行去分摊。作者提出了一组实验来评估所提出的哈希表方法的空间效率、吞吐量和延迟分布。我们的再现性评估证实，该论文的主要发现可以可靠地再现，并得到本报告再现结果的支持。

papers.title: Reproducible Prototyping of Query Optimizer Components.
papers.abstract: Query optimization was, is, and will for the foreseeable future be a very active research field with novel concepts being introduced each year. Over the last decade, two key trends have emerged in this domain: First, many researchers apply machine learning methods to replace components of the traditional optimizer architecture with learned models. Second, PostgreSQL has risen to the standard database system -- either as a benchmarking baseline, or as a target platform for implementation. However, most novel concepts are integrated directly into specific versions of PostgreSQL. As a result, the research community has produced a diverse ''zoo'' of optimization concepts that are difficult to compare. Implementations are often incompatible, rely on different PostgreSQL versions, lack clear a documentation of changes in the base system, or depend on custom tooling. To overcome this situation, this tutorial makes the following contributions: (i) we summarize important characteristics of the PostgreSQL optimizer architecture, emphasizing opportunities for extension, (ii) we survey recent research that improves the traditional optimizer architecture with a special focus on implementation and evaluation, and (iii) in a hands-on part, we showcase how proposed concepts can be expressed in a novel optimization framework to enable a common-ground comparison and rapid prototyping. Finally, we hope to raise the participants' awareness for the importance of reproducible and openly accessible prototypes, as well as for the importance of fair and transparent benchmarks.
papers.abstract_zh: 查询优化过去、现在和在可预见的未来都将是一个非常活跃的研究领域，每年都会推出新的概念。在过去的十年中，该领域出现了两个主要趋势：首先，许多研究人员应用机器学习方法，用学习模型取代传统优化器架构的组件。其次，PostgreSQL 已经上升为标准数据库系统——要么作为基准测试基线，要么作为实施的目标平台。然而，大多数新颖的概念都直接集成到 PostgreSQL 的特定版本中。结果，研究界产生了难以比较的多样化优化概念“动物园”。实现通常不兼容，依赖于不同的 PostgreSQL 版本，缺乏基本系统中更改的清晰文档，或者依赖于自定义工具。为了克服这种情况，本教程做出了以下贡献：（i）我们总结了 PostgreSQL 优化器架构的重要特征，强调扩展的机会，（ii）我们调查了改进传统优化器架构的最新研究，特别关注实现和评估，以及（iii）在实践部分，我们展示了如何在新颖的优化框架中表达所提出的概念，以实现共同点比较和快速原型设计。最后，我们希望提高参与者对可复制和公开访问原型的重要性以及公平和透明基准的重要性的认识。

papers.title: Rockhopper: A Robust Optimizer for Spark Configuration Tuning in Production Environment.
papers.abstract: Apache Spark, renowned for its scalability and ease of use, has become the standard for big data processing. However, optimizing Spark performance in production environments poses significant challenges. Traditional machine learning-based configuration tuning methods often necessitate extensive resources, lengthy experimentation, and risk performance regressions. Observational noise in production environments further complicates the tuning process, leading to suboptimal results. This paper presents an adaptive, robust learning approach leveraging insights from benchmark workloads to improve production tuning strategies. We propose a Centroid Learning algorithm resilient to noise, minimizing regressions and prioritizing promising configurations, combined with a workload embedding technique for context-aware adaptation and transfer learning. Evaluations using benchmark and customer workloads show consistent performance gains. Released in June 2024 as part of the Microsoft Fabric Spark offering, even with dynamic and evolving workloads, the system delivers approximately a 20% performance improvement in production for customer workloads by only tuning three query-level configurations.
papers.abstract_zh: Apache Spark 以其可扩展性和易用性而闻名，已成为大数据处理的标准。然而，在生产环境中优化 Spark 性能带来了重大挑战。传统的基于机器学习的配置调整方法通常需要大量资源、漫长的实验和风险性能回归。生产环境中的观测噪声使调整过程进一步复杂化，导致结果不理想。本文提出了一种自适应、稳健的学习方法，利用基准工作负载的见解来改进生产调整策略。我们提出了一种能够适应噪声、最小化回归并优先考虑有前景的配置的质心学习算法，并结合用于上下文感知适应和迁移学习的工作负载嵌入技术。使用基准和客户工作负载进行的评估显示出一致的性能提升。该系统于 2024 年 6 月作为 Microsoft Fabric Spark 产品的一部分发布，即使面对动态和不断变化的工作负载，该系统仅通过调整三个查询级别配置即可将客户工作负载的生产性能提高约 20%。

papers.title: RTS+: Reliable Text to SQL.
papers.abstract: The reliable translation of natural language queries to SQL is piv- otal for empowering non-technical users to interact with databases. Current state-of-the-art systems fail to holistically address ambi- guities or schema-linking challenges and may generate erroneous SQL leading to faulty query generation. We present RTS+ (Reliable Text-to-SQL Plus), a novel interactive system that builds on the con- cepts of Reliable Text-to-SQL (RTS) [3 ]. RTS+ focuses on improving user experience by incorporating adaptive abstention mechanisms and interactive feedback loops during query generation. Through this demo, we illustrate RTS+'s capabilities using a rich graphical interface that guides users through schema-linking decisions, query generation, verification, and refinement. Users can explore RTS+ on real-world datasets with detailed analytics for understanding abstention-triggered interventions and their outcomes. Using this system, users can achieve perfect scores at popular benchmarks correctly answering a few questions, if required.
papers.abstract_zh: 将自然语言查询可靠地转换为 SQL 对于非技术用户与数据库交互至关重要。当前最先进的系统无法全面解决歧义或模式链接挑战，并且可能会生成错误的 SQL，从而导致错误的查询生成。我们提出了 RTS+（Reliable Text-to-SQL Plus），这是一种基于 Reliable Text-to-SQL (RTS) [3] 概念构建的新型交互系统。 RTS+ 专注于通过在查询生成过程中结合自适应弃权机制和交互式反馈循环来改善用户体验。通过此演示，我们使用丰富的图形界面演示了 RTS+ 的功能，该界面指导用户完成模式链接决策、查询生成、验证和细化。用户可以在现实数据集上探索 RTS+，并进行详细分析，以了解弃权触发的干预措施及其结果。使用该系统，用户可以根据需要正确回答一些问题，在流行的基准测试中获得满分。

papers.title: SAP HANA Cloud: Data Management for Modern Enterprise Applications.
papers.abstract: Cloud Computing environments are a fantastic foundation for unprecedented opportunities in the context of data management. Scalability and availability can be achieved by consequently disaggregating compute and storage and leveraging different data centers around the globe. Although conceptually infinitely available resources promise an easy game for database systems, leveraging those opportunities on the one side and providing enterprise-scale data management solutions for a wide range of extremely challenging business applications on the other side is by far not trivial. Within this paper we share the fundamental principles of SAP HANA Cloud---the data management backbone for all SAP applications---by providing insights into design criteria and technological centerpieces of SAP HANA Cloud as a result of a stringent evolutionary shift from a purely on-premise, in-memory database engine to an on-Cloud, memory-storage hierarchy-aware data management platform. We will motivate the transition and the decision of certain developments by sharing key characteristics of some key applications run by SAP, which require enterprise qualities like availability, cloud-capabilities like resource elasticity, while managing overall costs and the flexibility to adapt the underlying data management system to their specific needs. The overall goal of the paper is to provide insights into the ''story of SAP HANA Cloud'' by sharing challenges and lessons learned within the evolution towards a composable cloud-based data platform, thus strongly arguing for a ''one size fits all'' solution, if done right.
papers.abstract_zh: 云计算环境是数据管理领域前所未有的机遇的绝佳基础。通过分解计算和存储并利用全球不同的数据中心可以实现可扩展性和可用性。尽管从概念上讲，无限可用的资源为数据库系统带来了轻松的游戏，但一方面利用这些机会，另一方面为各种极具挑战性的业务应用程序提供企业级数据管理解决方案，这并不是小事。在本文中，我们通过深入了解 SAP HANA Cloud 的设计标准和技术核心，分享 SAP HANA Cloud（所有 SAP 应用程序的数据管理主干）的基本原则，这是从纯粹的本地内存数据库引擎到云端内存存储层次结构感知数据管理平台的严格演进转变的结果。我们将通过共享 SAP 运行的一些关键应用程序的关键特征来推动某些开发的转变和决策，这些应用程序需要可用性等企业品质、资源弹性等云功能，同时管理总体成本以及使底层数据管理系统适应其特定需求的灵活性。本文的总体目标是通过分享在向可组合的基于云的数据平台的演进过程中遇到的挑战和经验教训，提供对“SAP HANA Cloud 的故事”的见解，从而强烈主张如果做得好的话，可以提供“一刀切”的解决方案。

papers.title: Scalable Execution of Application Logic within Everest BusinessStore.
papers.abstract:
Relational databases with SQL as the querying language are still the gold standard for developing business applications. Complex logic is often expressed using application code which involves multiple database queries resulting in higher latencies and less optimization potential for the database. Moreover, while modern web development is increasingly relying on JSON-based interfaces, database queries return identically structured rows further exacerbating the long-standing impedance mismatch.

In this paper, we introduce Everest BusinessStore which aims to simplify application development by providing a JSON-based interface to relational databases as well as a calculation language that allows to express complex business logic. The main challenge is the efficient mapping of application logic into highly efficient SQL code, which is demanding due to the high complexity of business applications and thus the calculations. Based on the real-world applications developed at Everest, we demonstrate that our approach offers speedups of up to two orders of magnitude compared to a straightforward mapping of application logic to SQL.
papers.abstract_zh:
以 SQL 作为查询语言的关系数据库仍然是开发业务应用程序的黄金标准。复杂的逻辑通常使用涉及多个数据库查询的应用程序代码来表达，从而导致更高的延迟和更少的数据库优化潜力。此外，虽然现代 Web 开发越来越依赖基于 JSON 的接口，但数据库查询返回相同结构的行，进一步加剧了长期存在的阻抗不匹配。

在本文中，我们介绍了 Everest BusinessStore，它旨在通过提供基于 JSON 的关系数据库接口以及允许表达复杂业务逻辑的计算语言来简化应用程序开发。主要挑战是将应用程序逻辑有效映射为高效的 SQL 代码，由于业务应用程序和计算的复杂性很高，因此要求很高。基于在 Everest 开发的实际应用程序，我们证明了与应用程序逻辑到 SQL 的直接映射相比，我们的方法可提供高达两个数量级的加速。

papers.title: ScaleLLM: A Technique for Scalable LLM-augmented Data Systems.
papers.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) offer powerful semantic insights for data analytics, but row-by-row LLM calls quickly become prohibitively expensive in large datasets. We introduce ScaleLLM, a novel system that substantially reduces both latency and cost on text classification tasks. ScaleLLM couples LLM-generated labels on a small subset of data with a lightweight machine learning model for large-scale inference. This approach provides significant speed-ups-up to 37×-while maintaining accuracy close to that of a full LLM baseline, converging within 1% of its accuracy in several tasks. ScaleLLM also provides cost-accuracy trade-off projections, giving users fine-grained control over performance trade-offs. Our demonstration illustrates ScaleLLM's reusable embedding views, efficient inference architecture, and potential for integration with query optimization frameworks in LLM-augmented database systems.
papers.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 为数据分析提供了强大的语义洞察，但在大型数据集中，逐行 LLM 调用很快就会变得极其昂贵。我们推出了 ScaleLLM，这是一种新颖的系统，可以大大减少文本分类任务的延迟和成本。 ScaleLLM 将 LLM 在一小部分数据上生成的标签与轻量级机器学习模型结合起来，以进行大规模推理。这种方法可显着提升高达 37 倍的速度，同时保持接近完整 LLM 基线的精度，在多项任务中收敛到其精度的 1% 以内。 ScaleLLM 还提供成本准确性权衡预测，使用户能够对性能权衡进行细粒度控制。我们的演示展示了 ScaleLLM 的可重用嵌入视图、高效的推理架构以及与 LLM 增强数据库系统中的查询优化框架集成的潜力。

papers.title: Scheduling Data Processing Pipelines for Incremental Training on MLP-based Recommendation Models.
papers.abstract: Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP)-based models have been widely exploited by modern recommendation applications. In practice, industrial recommendation scenarios frequently launch continuous incremental training jobs with only one epoch to capture real-time user features. This kind of job is shorter than full training and has a larger proportion of feature processing time. To fully utilize fragmentation resources, our model engineering team at Tencent explores resource-constrained CPU clusters to perform such incremental training workloads. To improve the efficiency of such workloads, we notice scheduling optimizations by overlapping feature processing and model training at the level of data processing pipelines. In particular, we propose an intra-pipeline scheduling strategy, which prefetches feature processing operators dynamically to fill the idle time of CPUs during the communication of embedding lookup. Furthermore, we propose an inter-pipeline scheduling strategy, which balances the resource demands of different pipelines. It prioritizes the execution of critical pipelines and overlaps the communication in critical pipelines with the execution of non-critical pipelines. Based on the two scheduling strategies, we implement a novel incremental recommendation training framework called RECS on top of TensorFlow. In our experimental studies, RECS achieves a speedup of 1.36x over existing solutions on industrial workloads.
papers.abstract_zh: 基于多层感知器（MLP）的模型已被现代推荐应用广泛利用。在实践中，工业推荐场景经常启动持续增量训练作业，仅用一个 epoch 来捕获实时用户特征。这种工作比完整训练的时间更短，并且特征处理时间比例更大。为了充分利用碎片资源，腾讯模型工程团队探索了资源受限的CPU集群来执行此类增量训练工作负载。为了提高此类工作负载的效率，我们注意到通过在数据处理管道级别重叠特征处理和模型训练来进行调度优化。特别是，我们提出了一种管道内调度策略，该策略动态预取特征处理运算符以填充嵌入查找通信期间CPU的空闲时间。此外，我们提出了一种管道间调度策略，可以平衡不同管道的资源需求。它优先考虑关键管道的执行，并将关键管道中的通信与非关键管道的执行重叠。基于这两种调度策略，我们在 TensorFlow 之上实现了一种新颖的增量推荐训练框架，称为 RECS。在我们的实验研究中，RECS 在工业工作负载方面比现有解决方案实现了 1.36 倍的加速。

papers.title: Second Workshop on Quantum Computing and Quantum-Inspired Technology for Data-Intensive Systems and Applications (Q-Data).
papers.abstract:
Quantum computing has seen significant advancements with the emergence of commercial quantum hardware from major tech companies and startups. These developments, alongside new classical hardware accelerators inspired by quantum technologies, facilitate the rapid solving of complex optimization problems and recently spawned a flurry of research in various communities.

We propose to hold the Q-Data workshop for the 2nd time. The primary objective of this workshop is to explore how quantum computing and related technologies can enhance data processing, data management, data analysis systems, and techniques. It also focuses on hybrid approaches integrating quantum and classical computing methodologies to enhance such data systems and techniques. This workshop will spur new research efforts in this emerging field and pave the way for building next-generation data-intensive systems with quantum computing support.
papers.abstract_zh:
随着主要科技公司和初创公司商业量子硬件的出现，量子计算取得了显着进步。这些发展与受量子技术启发的新经典硬件加速器一起，促进了复杂优化问题的快速解决，并且最近在各个社区引发了一系列研究。

我们建议举办第二次 Q-Data 研讨会。本次研讨会的主要目标是探索量子计算和相关技术如何增强数据处理、数据管理、数据分析系统和技术。它还专注于集成量子和经典计算方法的混合方法，以增强此类数据系统和技术。本次研讨会将刺激这一新兴领域的新研究工作，并为构建具有量子计算支持的下一代数据密集型系统铺平道路。

papers.title: SecUREmatch: Integrating Clerical Review in Privacy-Preserving Record Linkage.
papers.abstract: Data integration processes that combine sensitive personal information from different institutions are usually subject to strict privacy regulations. Privacy-preserving record linkage (PPRL) methods can be used in such projects to conceal the identities. However, the quality of such linkages may be low as the parametrization mostly must be done blindly or based on estimations from previous supposedly similar linkage problems. Our framework, SecUREmatch, integrates a multi-layer clerical review system to adapt the matching algorithm to the actual linked data in order to achieve high linkage quality while following the privacy-by-design principle.
papers.abstract_zh: 结合来自不同机构的敏感个人信息的数据集成过程通常受到严格的隐私法规的约束。在此类项目中可以使用隐私保护记录链接（PPRL）方法来隐藏身份。然而，这种链接的质量可能较低，因为参数化大多必须盲目地完成或基于先前假定的类似链接问题的估计。我们的框架 SecUREmatch 集成了多层文书审查系统，使匹配算法适应实际链接数据，以实现高链接质量，同时遵循隐私设计原则。

papers.title: SeerCuts: Explainable Attribute Discretization.
papers.abstract: This demonstration showcases SeerCuts - a tool that suggests useful and semantically meaningful discretization strategies (partitions) for numerical attributes. SeerCuts is a generic, interactive framework where users specify attributes to discretize and their utility measure for a downstream task of choice. It uses GPT-4o to assess the semantic meaningfulness of candidate partitions and employs an efficient search strategy to explore the vast space of discretization options. With hierarchical clustering to group related partitions and a multi-armed bandit policy to identify useful partitions with only a few samples, SeerCuts quickly finds meaningful and useful partitions. In the demo, we will provide an overview of SeerCuts and allow the audience to explore various datasets and tasks, including data visualization and comprehensive modeling. The users will be able to evaluate how SeerCuts identifies meaningful discretization strategies and compare the tradeoff between different discretization options.
papers.abstract_zh: 该演示展示了 SeerCuts - 一种为数值属性建议有用且语义上有意义的离散化策略（分区）的工具。 SeerCuts 是一个通用的交互式框架，用户可以在其中指定要离散化的属性及其对所选下游任务的效用度量。它使用 GPT-4o 来评估候选分区的语义意义，并​​采用有效的搜索策略来探索离散化选项的广阔空间。通过分层聚类对相关分区进行分组，并使用多臂老虎机策略仅用少量样本来识别有用的分区，SeerCuts 可以快速找到有意义且有用的分区。在演示中，我们将提供 SeerCuts 的概述，并允许观众探索各种数据集和任务，包括数据可视化和综合建模。用户将能够评估 SeerCuts 如何识别有意义的离散化策略并比较不同离散化选项之间的权衡。

papers.title: SemExplorer: A User Interface for Semantic Approach to Customized Dataset Search.
papers.abstract: In response to the increasing complexity of data and the widespread availability of diverse datasets for modeling complex relationships, there is a crucial need for efficient and customizable search capabilities. Many data scientists and researchers still need to manually browse through extensive catalogs to find suitable datasets for their studies. To address this challenge, we introduce SemExplorer, a framework designed to enhance semantic analysis and information retrieval for user-defined needs. Our system processes and interprets complex queries in natural language using large language models (LLMs), converting them into vector representations and statistical filters. When presenting search results, our system uses context dependencies to highlight important information, helping users quickly locate the results they need. Overall, SemExplorer is a robust tool that not only improves the efficiency of finding needed datasets but also enhances the precision of search outcomes by leveraging semantic analysis to deeply understand and filter network data. A demo of our system is available online.
papers.abstract_zh: 为了应对日益复杂的数据以及用于建模复杂关系的各种数据集的广泛可用性，迫切需要高效且可定制的搜索功能。许多数据科学家和研究人员仍然需要手动浏览大量目录以找到适合其研究的数据集。为了应对这一挑战，我们引入了 SemExplorer，这是一个旨在增强语义分析和信息检索以满足用户定义需求的框架。我们的系统使用大型语言模型 (LLM) 处理和解释自然语言中的复杂查询，将其转换为向量表示和统计过滤器。在呈现搜索结果时，我们的系统使用上下文依赖关系来突出显示重要信息，帮助用户快速找到所需的结果。总体而言，SemExplorer 是一个强大的工具，不仅提高了查找所需数据集的效率，还通过利用语义分析深入理解和过滤网络数据来提高搜索结果的精度。我们的系统的演示可以在线获取。

papers.title: Sentence to Model: Cost-Effective Data Collection LLM Agent.
papers.abstract: We introduce Sentence-to-Model , an automated system that converts natural language queries into tabular datasets and predictive models. The system utilizes LLM agents for planning and active learning to prioritize data collection under budget constraints, such as API costs and rate limits. By integrating resources, it generates datasets and trains machine learning models with minimal human intervention. Sentence-to-Model streamlines data collection and enables the refinement of user needs and easy exploration of the data landscape. Our approach highlights the potential of combining LLMs with algorithmic techniques for efficient data science workflows.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们引入了 Sentence-to-Model ，这是一个将自然语言查询转换为表格数据集和预测模型的自动化系统。该系统利用 LLM 代理进行规划和主动学习，以在预算限制（例如 API 成本和速率限制）下确定数据收集的优先级。通过整合资源，它可以在最少的人工干预下生成数据集并训练机器学习模型。句子到模型简化了数据收集，并能够细化用户需求并轻松探索数据景观。我们的方法强调了将法学硕士与算法技术相结合以实现高效数据科学工作流程的潜力。

papers.title: ShapX Engine: A Demonstration of Shapley Value Approximations.
papers.abstract: Interpreting decisions made by machine learning models helps build trust in their predictions, ultimately facilitating their practical application. Shapley values have emerged as a popular and theoretically robust method for interpreting models by quantifying the contribution of each feature toward individual predictions. The inherent complexity associated with the computation of Shapley values as an NP-hard problem has driven the development of numerous approximation techniques, leading to a plethora of options in literature. This abundance of choices has created a substantial gap in determining the most appropriate approach for practical applications. To address this gap, we propose ShapX, a web engine that comprehensively evaluates 17 approximation methods across diverse regression and classification tasks. ShapX facilitates an interactive exploration of the strengths and limitations of various Shapley value approximations by guiding users through the suitable selections of replacement and tractable estimation strategies. Ultimately, our study reveals that strategies competent at capturing all the feature interactions leading to accurate estimations of Shapley values. ShapX also allows users to effortlessly upload their own dataset along with the corresponding machine learning model, enabling them to obtain detailed individualized explanations. A detailed walkthrough video of the demonstration is available online. Video link: https://youtu.be/5uPocjPUAA8.
papers.abstract_zh: 解释机器学习模型做出的决策有助于建立对其预测的信任，最终促进其实际应用。沙普利值已成为一种流行且理论上可靠的方法，用于通过量化每个特征对个体预测的贡献来解释模型。作为 NP 难题，与 Shapley 值计算相关的固有复杂性推动了众多近似技术的发展，导致文献中出现了大量的选择。如此丰富的选择在确定最适合实际应用的方法方面造成了巨大的差距。为了解决这一差距，我们提出了 ShapX，这是一个 Web 引擎，可以综合评估跨不同回归和分类任务的 17 种近似方法。 ShapX 通过引导用户选择合适的替换和易于处理的估计策略，促进对各种 Shapley 值近似的优点和局限性的交互式探索。最终，我们的研究表明，能够捕获所有特征交互的策略可以准确估计 Shapley 值。 ShapX还允许用户轻松上传自己的数据集以及相应的机器学习模型，使他们能够获得详细的个性化解释。在线提供了演示的详细演练视频。视频链接：https://youtu.be/5uPocjPUAA8。

papers.title: Streaming Democratized: Ease Across the Latency Spectrum with Delayed View Semantics and Snowflake Dynamic Tables.
papers.abstract:
Streaming data pipelines remain challenging and expensive to build and maintain, despite significant advancements in stronger consistency, event time semantics, and SQL support over the last decade. Persistent obstacles continue to hinder usability, such as the need for manual incrementalization, semantic discrepancies across SQL implementations, and the lack of enterprise-grade operational features (e.g. granular access control, disaster recovery). While the rise of incremental view maintenance (IVM) as a way to integrate streaming with databases has been a huge step forward, transaction isolation in the presence of IVM remains underspecified, which leaves the maintenance of application-level invariants as a painful exercise for the user. Meanwhile, most streaming systems optimize for latencies of 100 milliseconds to 3 seconds, whereas many practical use cases are well-served by latencies ranging from seconds to tens of minutes.

In this paper, we present delayed view semantics (DVS), a conceptual foundation that bridges the semantic gap between streaming and databases, and introduce Dynamic Tables, Snowflake's declarative streaming transformation primitive designed to democratize analytical stream processing. DVS formalizes the intuition that stream processing is primarily a technique to eagerly compute derived results asynchronously, while also addressing the need to reason about the resulting system end to end. Dynamic Tables then offer two key advantages: ease of use through DVS, enterprise-grade features, and simplicity; as well as scalable cost efficiency via IVM with an architecture designed for diverse latency requirements. We first develop extensions to transaction isolation that permit the preservation of invariants in streaming applications. We then detail the implementation challenges of Dynamic Tables and our experience operating it at scale. Finally, we share insights into user adoption and discuss our vision for the future of stream processing.
papers.abstract_zh:
尽管过去十年在更强的一致性、事件时间语义和 SQL 支持方面取得了显着进步，但流数据管道的构建和维护仍然具有挑战性且昂贵。持续存在的障碍继续阻碍可用性，例如需要手动增量、SQL 实现之间的语义差异以及缺乏企业级操作功能（例如粒度访问控制、灾难恢复）。虽然增量视图维护 (IVM) 作为一种将流媒体与数据库集成的方式的兴起已经向前迈出了一大步，但 IVM 存在下的事务隔离仍然没有明确规定，这使得应用程序级不变量的维护对于用户来说是一项痛苦的练习。同时，大多数流媒体系统针对 100 毫秒到 3 秒的延迟进行优化，而许多实际用例的延迟范围从几秒到几十分钟不等。

在本文中，我们提出了延迟视图语义 (DVS)，这是弥合流处理和数据库之间语义差距的概念基础，并介绍了动态表，这是 Snowflake 的声明性流处理转换原语，旨在使分析流处理民主化。 DVS 形式化了这样的直觉：流处理主要是一种急切地异步计算派生结果的技术，同时还解决了对结果系统进行端到端推理的需要。动态表提供了两个关键优势：通过 DVS 的易用性、企业级功能和简单性；以及通过 IVM 和专为不同延迟要求设计的架构实现可扩展的成本效率。我们首先开发事务隔离的扩展，允许在流应用程序中保留不变量。然后，我们详细介绍动态表的实施挑战以及我们大规模运营它的经验。最后，我们分享了对用户采用情况的见解，并讨论了我们对流处理未来的愿景。

papers.title: Supporting Human-Centric Data Exploration Through Semantics and Natural Language Interaction.
papers.abstract:
Data science plays an increasingly central role in decision-making across domains, yet the effectiveness of these decisions hinges not only on sophisticated algorithms but also on how well systems support human interpretation, exploration, and communication of data. This tutorial explores the intersection of semantics, natural language processing (NLP), and human-computer interaction in creating human-centric data exploration tools that promote accessibility, trust, and transparency. This includes techniques for generating perceptually meaningful visual encodings, using NLP for query interpretation and ambiguity resolution, and designing conversational interfaces that align with users' intent. The tutorial will highlight research from a broad set of contributors in the SIGMOD/VLDB, HCI, and visualization communities, spanning natural language interfaces for databases, multimodal interaction systems, semantic search for data repositories, and the use of AI and large language models to augment visual and textual analysis.

As part of this 1.5-hour session, we will present case studies and systems that demonstrate how human-centric design can be integrated into the data analysis pipeline, including tools that support mixed-initiative interaction, adaptive defaults, and subjective query interpretation. We will also discuss open challenges and research opportunities, such as semantic inferencing for unstructured data, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and ethical considerations around fairness, explainability, and user agency. Drawing from principles in perception, linguistics, AI, and HCI, this tutorial aims to equip attendees with both conceptual frameworks and practical techniques to build more inclusive, interpretable, and intelligent data systems. It is intended for a broad audience in the SIGMOD/VLDB community interested in designing the next generation of data exploration tools that are aligned with human needs.
papers.abstract_zh:
数据科学在跨领域的决策中发挥着越来越重要的作用，但这些决策的有效性不仅取决于复杂的算法，还取决于系统如何支持人类对数据的解释、探索和通信。本教程探讨了语义、自然语言处理 (NLP) 和人机交互的交叉点，以创建以人为中心的数据探索工具，以促进可访问性、信任和透明度。这包括生成感知上有意义的视觉编码、使用 NLP 进行查询解释和歧义解决以及设计符合用户意图的对话界面的技术。本教程将重点介绍 SIGMOD/VLDB、HCI 和可视化社区中众多贡献者的研究，涵盖数据库自然语言界面、多模式交互系统、数据存储库语义搜索，以及使用 AI 和大型语言模型来增强视觉和文本分析。

作为这个 1.5 小时会议的一部分，我们将介绍案例研究和系统，展示如何将以人为本的设计集成到数据分析管道中，包括支持混合主动交互、自适应默认值和主观查询解释的工具。我们还将讨论开放的挑战和研究机会，例如非结构化数据的语义推理、检索增强生成（RAG）以及围绕公平性、可解释性和用户代理的道德考虑。本教程借鉴感知、语言学、人工智能和人机交互的原理，旨在为参与者提供概念框架和实用技术，以构建更具包容性、可解释性和智能的数据系统。它面向 SIGMOD/VLDB 社区中对设计符合人类需求的下一代数据探索工具感兴趣的广大受众。

papers.title: SwellDB: Dynamic Query-Driven Table Generation with Large Language Models.
papers.abstract: We present SwellDB, a data system that enables analytical query processing over tables generated dynamically with Large Language Models (LLMs). Typically, traditional database systems function under the closed-world assumption, allowing query execution over already existing tables. In contrast, SwellDB dynamically generates the required tables based on input SQL queries, and user-defined table definitions. Powered by an LLM, SwellDB integrates external data sources, including diverse datasets, database systems, and search engines to extract and blend relevant information into a structured table. Given an input query and schema, SwellDB intelligently selects data sources, synthesizes structured tables adhering to the specified schema, and makes them queriable through its execution engine. During the demonstration, we will showcase how SwellDB addresses data generation and integration challenges across domains such as web search and bioinformatics. Attendees can define custom schemas and execute SQL queries, gaining hands-on experience with SwellDB's table generation capabilities. Video URL: https://youtu.be/X1VOCVYb3Sc
papers.abstract_zh: 我们推出了 SwellDB，这是一个数据系统，可以对使用大型语言模型 (LLM) 动态生成的表进行分析查询处理。通常，传统数据库系统在封闭世界假设下运行，允许对现有表执行查询。相比之下，SwellDB 根据输入 SQL 查询和用户定义的表定义动态生成所需的表。在法学硕士的支持下，SwellDB 集成了外部数据源，包括不同的数据集、数据库系统和搜索引擎，以提取相关信息并将其混合到结构化表中。给定输入查询和模式，SwellDB 智能地选择数据源，合成符合指定模式的结构化表，并使其可通过其执行引擎进行查询。在演示过程中，我们将展示 SwellDB 如何解决跨领域（例如网络搜索和生物信息学）的数据生成和集成挑战。与会者可以定义自定义模式并执行 SQL 查询，获得 SwellDB 的表生成功能的实践经验。视频网址：https://youtu.be/X1VOCVYb3Sc

papers.title: TD-Join: Leveraging Temporal Dependencies in Time Series Joins.
papers.abstract: Time series data is pervasive across domains such as healthcare, finance, and environmental monitoring, where understanding temporal relationships between events is crucial. Traditional time series analysis primarily focuses on numerical similarity measures, often overlooking qualitative temporal relationships. This demonstration introduces TD-Join, a novel tool for temporal reasoning on time series. TD-Join enhances time series subsequence joins by integrating Allen's Algebra, a widely adopted framework for temporal reasoning. Our approach enables users to efficiently identify, query, and interpret temporal relationships such as before, overlaps, meets and equal over similar time series subsequences. By leveraging such relations, our algorithm facilitates more interpretable and robust analysis, improving performance and augmenting the results set, facilitating decision-making processes. The demonstration will showcase real-world scenarios, i.e., healthcare and financial analyses. Attendees will experience interactive visualizations and real-time query capabilities allowing more explainable and insightful time series analysis.
papers.abstract_zh: 时间序列数据普遍存在于医疗保健、金融和环境监测等领域，了解事件之间的时间关系至关重要。传统的时间序列分析主要关注数值相似性度量，通常忽略定性时间关系。该演示介绍了 TD-Join，这是一种用于对时间序列进行时间推理的新颖工具。 TD-Join 通过集成艾伦代数（一种广泛采用的时间推理框架）来增强时间序列子序列连接。我们的方法使用户能够有效地识别、查询和解释时间关系，例如相似时间序列子序列上的之前、重叠、相遇和相等。通过利用这种关系，我们的算法可以促进更具可解释性和稳健性的分析，提高性能并扩大结果集，从而促进决策过程。该演示将展示现实世界的场景，即医疗保健和财务分析。与会者将体验交互式可视化和实时查询功能，从而实现更具解释性和洞察力的时间序列分析。

papers.title: TEQ: An Open and Developer-friendly Testbed for Edge-based Query Processing Algorithms.
papers.abstract: Edge computing is ideally suited for querying populations of fast data streams. However, developing and evaluating edge-based query processing algorithms is challenging due to the decentralized setting and the lack of effective tools. For example, current simulators fail to execute real queries. To rectify these shortcomings, we propose TeQ, a <u>t</u>estbed for <u>e</u>dge-based <u>q</u>uery processing algorithms, designed expressly to be open, enabling extensible simulation and algorithm deployment, and to be developer-friendly, simplifying otherwise cumbersome simulation, complex algorithm management, and tedious evaluation. TeQ encompasses novel techniques to achieve these capabilities, including (1) a streamlined framework for implementing decentralized algorithms as modular, Docker-based executables; (2) data and query playback controls to enable reproducible and consistent evaluations; and (3) mechanisms for transforming runtime metrics into system-wide metrics desired by algorithm developers.
papers.abstract_zh: 边缘计算非常适合查询快速数据流群体。然而，由于分散的设置和缺乏有效的工具，开发和评估基于边缘的查询处理算法具有挑战性。例如，当前的模拟器无法执行真实的查询。为了纠正这些缺点，我们提出了 TeQ，这是一个基于边缘查询处理算法的测试平台，其明确设计为开放的，支持可扩展的仿真和算法部署，并且对开发人员友好，简化了繁琐的仿真、复杂的算法管理和繁琐的评估。 TeQ 包含实现这些功能的新技术，包括 (1) 一个简化的框架，用于将去中心化算法实现为模块化、基于 Docker 的可执行文件； (2) 数据和查询回放控制，以实现可重复且一致的评估； (3) 将运行时指标转换为算法开发人员所需的系统范围指标的机制。

papers.title: The 19th International Symposium on Database Programming Languages (DBPL).
papers.abstract: Since 1987, DBPL is the home for research that is performed in the thrilling intersection of database query and programming languages. The 2025 edition of this workshop series continues this tradition and publishes work in which database research draws from ideas established in programming language theory, type systems, or compiler and interpreter construction. Likewise, you will find programming-language-centric contributions that bank on techniques originally conceived to design and build database query languages, their optimizers, and executors. Bringing the DB and PL communities together has repeatedly resulted in exciting and unforeseen advances in both fields. We are looking forward to see the same happening during and after DBPL 2025.
papers.abstract_zh: 自 1987 年以来，DBPL 一直是数据库查询和编程语言令人兴奋的交叉领域的研究基地。该研讨会系列的 2025 年版本延续了这一传统，并出版了数据库研究借鉴编程语言理论、类型系统或编译器和解释器构造中建立的思想的著作。同样，您会发现以编程语言为中心的贡献，这些贡献依赖于最初设计和构建数据库查询语言、其优化器和执行器的技术。将 DB 和 PL 社区结合在一起已经多次在这两个领域带来令人兴奋和意想不到的进步。我们期待在 DBPL 2025 期间和之后看到同样的情况发生。

papers.title: The Case for Collaboration.
papers.abstract:
It's 2025, and the answer to every database performance or optimization problem is ''machine learning.'' But what kinds of models are appropriate for these applications? I'm going to try to convince you that, as in good system design, ''simpler is better.'' And, in this case, simpler has many benefits: simpler models are typically more efficient in both space and time, they are frequently transparently interpretable, and in many domains, they produce accuracy and generalization equivalent to the fanciest deep learning model you can build.

At the same time, I'm going to explain what great collaboration looks like, how it can help you overcome imposter syndrome, and how it helps you find your own personal superpower.
papers.abstract_zh:
到了 2025 年，每个数据库性能或优化问题的答案都是“机器学习”。但是什么样的模型适合这些应用程序呢？我将尝试让您相信，正如优秀的系统设计一样，“越简单越好”。而且，在这种情况下，更简单有很多好处：更简单的模型通常在空间和时间上都更高效，它们通常是透明可解释的，并且在许多领域，它们产生的准确性和泛化能力相当于您可以构建的最奇特的深度学习模型。

同时，我将解释什么是伟大的合作，它如何帮助您克服冒名顶替综合症，以及它如何帮助您找到自己的个人超能力。

papers.title: TigerVector: Supporting Vector Search in Graph Databases for Advanced RAGs.
papers.abstract: In this paper, we introduce TigerVector, a system that integrates vector search and graph query within TigerGraph, a Massively Parallel Processing (MPP) native graph database. We extend the vertex attribute type with the embedding type. To support fast vector search, we devise an MPP index framework that interoperates efficiently with the graph engine. The graph query language GSQL is enhanced to support vector type expressions and enable query compositions between vector search results and graph query blocks. These advancements elevate the expressive power and analytical capabilities of graph databases, enabling seamless fusion of unstructured and structured data in ways previously unattainable. Through extensive experiments, we demonstrate TigerVector's hybrid search capability, scalability, and superior performance compared to other graph databases (including Neo4j and Amazon Neptune) and a highly optimized specialized vector database (Milvus). TigerVector was integrated into TigerGraph v4.2 , the latest release of TigerGraph, in December 2024.
papers.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了 TigerVector，这是一个在 TigerGraph（大规模并行处理（MPP）本机图形数据库）中集成矢量搜索和图形查询的系统。我们用嵌入类型扩展顶点属性类型。为了支持快速向量搜索，我们设计了一个与图引擎高效互操作的 MPP 索引框架。图查询语言GSQL得到增强，支持向量类型表达式，并支持向量搜索结果和图查询块之间的查询组合。这些进步提升了图数据库的表达能力和分析能力，能够以以前无法实现的方式无缝融合非结构化和结构化数据。通过大量的实验，我们展示了 TigerVector 的混合搜索能力、可扩展性以及与其他图数据库（包括 Neo4j 和 Amazon Neptune）和高度优化的专用向量数据库（Milvus）相比的卓越性能。 TigerVector 于 2024 年 12 月集成到 TigerGraph v4.2（TigerGraph 的最新版本）中。

papers.title: Transactional Cloud Applications: Status Quo, Challenges, and Opportunities.
papers.abstract:
Transactional cloud applications such as payment, booking, reservation systems, and complex business workflows are currently being rewritten for deployment in the cloud. This migration to the cloud is happening mainly for reasons of cost and scalability. Over the years, application developers have used different migration approaches, such as microservice frameworks, actors, and stateful dataflow systems.

The migration to the cloud has brought back data management challenges traditionally handled by database management systems. Those challenges include ensuring state consistency, maintaining durability, and managing the application lifecycle. At the same time, the shift to a distributed computing infrastructure introduced new issues, such as message delivery, task scheduling, containerization, and (auto)scaling.

Although the data management community has made progress in developing analytical and transactional database systems, transactional cloud applications have received little attention in database research. This tutorial aims to highlight recent trends in the area and discusses open research challenges for the data management community.
papers.abstract_zh:
目前正在重写支付、预订、预订系统和复杂业务工作流程等事务性云应用程序以在云中部署。这种向云的迁移主要是出于成本和可扩展性的原因。多年来，应用程序开发人员使用了不同的迁移方法，例如微服务框架、参与者和有状态数据流系统。

向云的迁移带来了传统上由数据库管理系统处理的数据管理挑战。这些挑战包括确保状态一致性、保持持久性和管理应用程序生命周期。与此同时，向分布式计算基础设施的转变带来了新的问题，例如消息传递、任务调度、容器化和（自动）扩展。

尽管数据管理社区在开发分析和事务数据库系统方面取得了进展，但事务云应用程序在数据库研究中却很少受到关注。本教程旨在强调该领域的最新趋势，并讨论数据管理社区的开放研究挑战。

papers.title: TXSQL: Lock Optimizations Towards High Contented Workloads.
papers.abstract:
Two-phase locking (2PL) is a fundamental and widely used concurrency control protocol. It regulates concurrent access to database data by following a specific sequence of acquiring and releasing locks during transaction execution, thereby ensuring transaction isolation. However, in strict 2PL, transactions must wait for conflicting transactions to commit and release their locks, which reduces concurrency and system throughput. We have observed this issue is exacerbated in high-contented workloads at Tencent, where lock contention can severely degrade system performance. While existing optimizations demonstrate some effectiveness in high-contention scenarios, their performance remains insufficient, as they suffer from lock contention and waiting in hotspot access.

This paper presents optimizations in lock management implemented in Tencent's database, TXSQL, with a particular focus on high-contention scenarios. First, we discuss our motivations and the journey toward general lock optimization, which includes lightweight lock management, a copy-free active transaction list, and queue locking mechanisms that effectively enhance concurrency. Second, we introduce a hotspot-aware approach that enables certain highly conflicting transactions to switch to a group locking method, which groups conflicting transactions at a specific hotspot, allowing them to execute serially in an uncommitted state within a conflict group without the need for locking, thereby reducing lock contention. Our evaluation shows that under high-contented workloads, TXSQL achieves performance improvements of up to 6.5x and up to 22.3x compared to state-of-the-art methods and systems, respectively.
papers.abstract_zh:
两阶段锁定（2PL）是一种基本且广泛使用的并发控制协议。它通过在事务执行过程中遵循特定的获取和释放锁的顺序来调节对数据库数据的并发访问，从而保证事务隔离。然而，在严格的2PL中，事务必须等待冲突事务提交并释放其锁，这会降低并发性和系统吞吐量。我们观察到这个问题在腾讯的高负载工作负载中更加严重，锁争用会严重降低系统性能。虽然现有的优化在高争用场景中表现出一定的有效性，但它们的性能仍然不足，因为它们会遭受锁争用和热点访问等待的问题。

本文介绍了在腾讯数据库 TXSQL 中实现的锁管理优化，特别关注高争用场景。首先，我们讨论我们的动机和一般锁优化的历程，其中包括轻量级锁管理、无副本的活动事务列表以及有效增强并发性的队列锁定机制。其次，我们引入了一种热点感知方法，使某些高度冲突的事务能够切换到组锁定方法，该方法将冲突事务分组在特定的热点处，允许它们在冲突组内以未提交状态串行执行，而不需要锁定，从而减少锁争用。我们的评估表明，在高负载工作负载下，与最先进的方法和系统相比，TXSQL 的性能分别提高了 6.5 倍和 22.3 倍。

papers.title: UDFBench: A Tool for Benchmarking UDF Queries on SQL Engines.
papers.abstract: User-defined functions (UDFs) extend SQL with functional capabilities. However, integrating UDFs efficiently into a data engine is a challenge, mainly due to the mismatch of the UDF execution and SQL processing environments. Several techniques have been proposed to deal with this challenge spanning a variety of logical and physical optimizations, and architecture designs. These however are not evident to the casual user, who would benefit from knowing the pros and cons of the available solutions. Towards this direction, we present UDFBench , a benchmarking toolkit for running seamlessly UDF queries (queries with UDFs) on a variety of SQL engines and presenting to the user execution results accompanied with highlights of the main reasons driving the query performance. UDFBench comprises a set of queries and UDFs of varying complexity based on a real-world application and data. Our current implementation has been deployed on four popular database engines with different characteristics. Our pluggable design enables extending UDFBench to additional systems and workloads.
papers.abstract_zh: 用户定义函数 (UDF) 通过函数功能扩展 SQL。然而，将 UDF 有效地集成到数据引擎中是一个挑战，这主要是由于 UDF 执行和 SQL 处理环境的不匹配。已经提出了几种技术来应对这一挑战，涵盖各种逻辑和物理优化以及架构设计。然而，这些对于临时用户来说并不明显，他们会从了解可用解决方案的优缺点中受益。朝着这个方向，我们推出了 UDFBench，这是一个基准测试工具包，用于在各种 SQL 引擎上无缝运行 UDF 查询（使用 UDF 的查询），并向用户呈现执行结果，并重点介绍驱动查询性能的主要原因。 UDFBench 包含一组基于实际应用程序和数据的不同复杂度的查询和 UDF。我们当前的实现已部署在四种具有不同特征的流行数据库引擎上。我们的可插拔设计可以将 UDFBench 扩展到其他系统和工作负载。

papers.title: Unified Lineage System: Tracking Data Provenance at Scale.
papers.abstract: As businesses increasingly rely on large-scale data analysis to build products and features, understanding how data flows through heterogeneous storage assets has become a critical challenge. To address these challenges, we present ULS, an end-to-end lineage aggregator designed to track data flows at scale. Our system features a general data model representing data flows between different asset types, a lineage data model that enables navigation across granularities, and techniques to stitch incomplete lineage traces. We also describe methods for processing the lineage graph to customize precision and recall. We implemented ULS at Meta, where its lineage graph contains billions of nodes and edges and supports over one hundred use cases, including routine operations, capacity management and privacy.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着企业越来越依赖大规模数据分析来构建产品和功能，了解数据如何流经异构存储资产已成为一项关键挑战。为了应对这些挑战，我们推出了 ULS，这是一种端到端的谱系聚合器，旨在大规模跟踪数据流。我们的系统具有表示不同资产类型之间数据流的通用数据模型、支持跨粒度导航的沿袭数据模型以及缝合不完整沿袭痕迹的技术。我们还描述了处理谱系图以定制精度和召回率的方法。我们在 Meta 实施了 ULS，其谱系图包含数十亿个节点和边，并支持一百多个用例，包括日常操作、容量管理和隐私。

papers.title: UNITQA: A Unified Automated Tabular Question Answering System with Multi-Agent Large Language Models.
papers.abstract: Automated tabular question answering (TQA) has attracted significant attention in data analysis and natural language processing communities due to its powerful capabilities. The emergence of large language models (LLMs) has initiated a paradigm shift in this field. However, existing state-of-the-art approaches cannot generally operate on multiple tables from multiple heterogeneous systems, and the answer accuracy is insufficient to meet the demands of the industrial field. This paper presents UNITQA, a unified automated tabular question-answering system through multi-agent LLMs. First, UNITQA offers a user-friendly GUI interface that enables users to use natural language questions to execute TQA tasks. Second, UNITQA consists of five agents who collaborate to complete user-specified tasks. To efficiently orchestrate different agents, UNITQA utilizes a dynamic agent scheduling algorithm based on a finite-state machine. Third, UNITQA integrates a series of data connectors that allow UNITQA to access various tables from multiple heterogeneous systems. We have implemented and deployed UNITQA in numerous production environments and have demonstrated its usability and efficiency in representative real-world scenarios.
papers.abstract_zh: 自动表格问答（TQA）因其强大的功能而引起了数据分析和自然语言处理社区的广泛关注。大型语言模型（LLM）的出现引发了该领域的范式转变。然而，现有的最先进的方法通常无法对来自多个异构系统的多个表进行操作，并且答案准确性不足以满足工业领域的需求。本文介绍了 UNITQA，这是一个通过多代理法学硕士的统一自动化表格问答系统。首先，UNITQA 提供了一个用户友好的 GUI 界面，使用户能够使用自然语言问题来执行 TQA 任务。其次，UNITQA 由五个代理组成，他们协作完成用户指定的任务。为了有效地编排不同的代理，UNITQA 使用基于有限状态机的动态代理调度算法。第三，UNITQA集成了一系列数据连接器，允许UNITQA访问来自多个异构系统的各种表。我们已经在众多生产环境中实施和部署了 UNITQA，并在代表性的现实场景中展示了其可用性和效率。

papers.title: Unity Catalog: Open and Universal Governance for the Lakehouse and Beyond.
papers.abstract: Enterprises are increasingly adopting the Lakehouse architecture to manage their data assets due to its flexibility, low cost, and high performance. While the catalog plays a central role in this architecture, it remains underexplored, and current Lakehouse catalogs exhibit key limitations, including inconsistent governance, narrow interoperability, and lack of support for data discovery. Additionally, there is growing demand to govern a broader range of assets beyond tabular data, such as unstructured data and AI models, which existing catalogs are not equipped to handle. To address these challenges, we introduce Unity Catalog (UC), an open and universal Lakehouse catalog developed at Databricks that supports a wide variety of assets and workloads, provides consistent governance, and integrates efficiently with external systems, all with strong performance guarantees. We describe the primary design challenges and how UC's architecture meets them, and share insights from usage across thousands of customer deployments that validate its design choices. UC's core APIs and both server and client implementations have been available as open source since June 2024.
papers.abstract_zh: 由于其灵活性、低成本和高性能，越来越多的企业采用 Lakehouse 架构来管理其数据资产。虽然目录在该架构中发挥着核心作用，但它仍然未被充分开发，并且当前的 Lakehouse 目录表现出关键的局限性，包括不一致的治理、狭窄的互操作性以及缺乏对数据发现的支持。此外，人们越来越需要管理表格数据之外的更广泛的资产，例如非结构化数据和人工智能模型，而现有目录无法处理这些数据。为了应对这些挑战，我们引入了 Unity Catalog (UC)，这是一个由 Databricks 开发的开放式通用 Lakehouse 目录，支持各种资产和工作负载，提供一致的治理，并与外部系统高效集成，所有这些都具有强大的性能保证。我们描述了主要的设计挑战以及 UC 的架构如何应对这些挑战，并分享了来自数千个客户部署的使用情况的见解，以验证其设计选择。自 2024 年 6 月起，UC 的核心 API 以及服务器和客户端实现均已开源。

papers.title: Unlocking the Potential of CXL for Disaggregated Memory in Cloud-Native Databases.
papers.abstract:
Memory disaggregation has become a major trend in cloud-native databases. However, most existing memory disaggregation solutions suffer from read/write amplification, limited bandwidth, inefficient recovery, and challenges in data sharing. Fortunately, the emerging CXL technology introduces new opportunities for memory disaggregation design in cloud-native databases.

To overcome these challenges, we leverage the CXL switch to design PolarCXLMem , a CXL switch-based disaggregated memory system for cloud-native databases. To the best of our knowledge, PolarCXLMem is the first CXL switch-based disaggregated memory. Building on PolarCXLMem , we propose a novel instant recovery scheme, PolarRecv, which enables instant recovery and fast buffer pool warm-up after a crash. To further support PolarCXLMem in multi-primary databases, we design a new cache coherency protocol that facilitates data sharing between database nodes based on PolarCXLMem . Finally, we evaluate PolarCXLMem with PolarDB, a widely deployed cloud-native database, under various workloads. This is the first study, to our knowledge, that investigates the performance of CXL-based disaggregated memory in a commercially deployed cloud-native database. Our evaluation shows that PolarCXLMem can improve throughput by up to 2.1× in pooling scenarios and 1.55× in sharing scenarios compared to RDMA-based systems.
papers.abstract_zh:
