内存分解已成为云原生数据库的一大趋势。然而，大多数现有的内存分解解决方案都存在读/写放大、带宽有限、恢复效率低下以及数据共享方面的挑战等问题。幸运的是，新兴的 CXL 技术为云原生数据库中的内存分解设计带来了新的机会。

为了克服这些挑战，我们利用 CXL 交换机设计了 PolarCXLMem，这是一种用于云原生数据库的基于 CXL 交换机的分类内存系统。据我们所知，PolarCXLMem 是首款基于 CXL 开关的分解内存。在 PolarCXLMem 的基础上，我们提出了一种新颖的即时恢复方案 PolarRecv，它可以在崩溃后实现即时恢复和快速缓冲池预热。为了进一步支持多主数据库中的PolarCXLMem，我们设计了一种新的缓存一致性协议，以促进基于PolarCXLMem的数据库节点之间的数据共享。最后，我们使用广泛部署的云原生数据库 PolarDB 在各种工作负载下评估 PolarCXLMem。据我们所知，这是第一项研究商业部署的云原生数据库中基于 CXL 的分类内存的性能。我们的评估表明，与基于 RDMA 的系统相比，PolarCXLMem 在池化场景中可以将吞吐量提高高达 2.1 倍，在共享场景中可以将吞吐量提高高达 1.55 倍。

papers.title: Virtualizing Cloud Data Infrastructures with BRAD.
papers.abstract: Organizations usually manage their data using multiple specialized cloud database engines (e.g., Aurora, BigQuery, etc.). However, designing and managing multi-engine infrastructures is hard; there can be many designs, each with different performance and costs. Changing the design afterwards (e.g., due to growth) is even more challenging since application code usually ends up tightly coupled to the engines. We propose data infrastructure virtualization . The key idea is to declare a set of virtual database engines (VDBEs), which specify an engine's application-facing properties (e.g., query interface, performance) and its tables, but do not prescribe a concrete engine. An automated planner then decides how to best realize the VDBEs onto physical engines based on the workload. Clients connect to VDBE endpoints and are oblivious to the underlying physical engines-allowing for seamless infrastructure changes. We implemented VDBEs and an automated planner in BRAD: the first data infrastructure virtualization runtime. Our demo will showcase VDBEs and BRAD's automated planner under different workloads.
papers.abstract_zh: 组织通常使用多个专用云数据库引擎（例如 Aurora、BigQuery 等）来管理数据。然而，设计和管理多引擎基础设施很困难；可以有多种设计，每种设计都有不同的性能和成本。之后更改设计（例如，由于增长）甚至更具挑战性，因为应用程序代码通常最终与引擎紧密耦合。我们提出数据基础设施虚拟化。关键思想是声明一组虚拟数据库引擎（VDBE），它指定引擎面向应用程序的属性（例如查询接口、性能）及其表，但不规定具体的引擎。然后，自动规划器根据工作负载决定如何在物理引擎上最好地实现 VDBE。客户端连接到 VDBE 端点，并且不关心底层物理引擎 - 允许无缝的基础设施更改。我们在 BRAD 中实现了 VDBE 和自动规划器：第一个数据基础设施虚拟化运行时。我们的演示将展示不同工作负载下的 VDBE 和 BRAD 的自动规划器。

papers.title: VQLens: A Demonstration of Vector Query Execution Analysis.
papers.abstract: With the increasing demand for high-speed similarity search over massive datasets, vector databases have become essential in modern data-intensive applications, particularly in LLMs and other AI-driven workloads. The growing complexity and scale of vector database operations demand more comprehensive evaluation methodologies. However, existing evaluation methodologies primarily rely on high-level information retrieval metrics (e.g., recall, accuracy) and system performance indicators (e.g., query latency, throughput), which fail to provide fine-grained insights. This limitation hinders the diagnosis of unexpected query results and the optimization of system performance. To bridge this gap, we present VQLens , an interactive visual analytics system for in-depth exploration of query execution traces and large-scale vector distributions. Specifically, VQLens (1) computes the overall data distribution and overlays query traces to reveal retrieval patterns, (2) visualizes query execution trace using multiple representations to enhance interpretability, and (3) enables interactive multi-view exploration, supporting both global pattern discovery and case-specific analysis. VQLens is open-source at https://github.com/DBGroup-SUSTech/VQLens.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着对海量数据集的高速相似性搜索的需求不断增加，矢量数据库在现代数据密集型应用程序中变得至关重要，特别是在法学硕士和其他人工智能驱动的工作负载中。矢量数据库操作的复杂性和规模不断增长，需要更全面的评估方法。然而，现有的评估方法主要依赖于高级信息检索指标（例如召回率、准确性）和系统性能指标（例如查询延迟、吞吐量），无法提供细粒度的洞察。这种限制阻碍了对意外查询结果的诊断和系统性能的优化。为了弥补这一差距，我们推出了 VQLens，这是一种交互式可视化分析系统，用于深入探索查询执行轨迹和大规模向量分布。具体来说，VQLens (1) 计算整体数据分布并覆盖查询跟踪以揭示检索模式，(2) 使用多种表示形式可视化查询执行跟踪以增强可解释性，(3) 实现交互式多视图探索，支持全局模式发现和特定于案例的分析。 VQLens 已开源，网址为 https://github.com/DBGroup-SUSTech/VQLens。

papers.title: Where Does Academic Database Research Go From Here?
papers.abstract: An open forum to discuss and debate the future of database research in the context of industry, other research communities, and AI.
papers.abstract_zh: 一个开放的论坛，讨论和辩论工业、其他研究社区和人工智能背景下数据库研究的未来。

papers.title: Zorro: Quantifying Uncertainty in Models & Predictions Arising from Dirty Data.
papers.abstract: Machine learning models are increasingly employed in high-stakes decision making in domains such as personalized medicine, policing,and many others. As data quality issues are prevalent and recovering a ground truth clean dataset is often impossible or prohibitively expense, heuristic cleaning techniques are employed in practice to clean training data. The net result are models whose predictions can fundamentally not be trusted as we do not know how much the model's predictions differ from a model trained on the unknown ground truth clean data. We present Zorro, a principled framework for modeling the uncertainty in model parameters and predictions arising from the multiplicity of datasets that could feasibly be the ground truth clean version of a dirty training / test dataset. Under the hood, Zorro employs a novel framework for training and prediction with linear models over uncertain data. Given training and test datasets that are subject to data quality issues, we compute a sound over-approximation of all possible models, the set of models generated by training a model on each possible clean version of the dataset, and then over-approximate all possible predictions based on these models. Using Zorro, we can certify the robustness of models, i.e., to what degree are the model parameters impacted by data quality issues, and of individual and aggregated predictions. The demonstration video is available at https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gxkvRY3pLM0ATco2qvBnM1EcR8-U9F5h/view?usp=sharing.
papers.abstract_zh: 机器学习模型越来越多地应用于个性化医疗、警务等领域的高风险决策。由于数据质量问题普遍存在，并且恢复真实的干净数据集通常是不可能的或费用高昂，因此在实践中采用启发式清理技术来清理训练数据。最终结果是模型的预测从根本上不可信，因为我们不知道模型的预测与在未知的真实干净数据上训练的模型有多大差异。我们提出了 Zorro，一个原则框架，用于对模型参数的不确定性和由多个数据集产生的预测进行建模，这些数据集可能是脏训练/测试数据集的地面真实干净版本。在幕后，佐罗采用了一种新颖的框架，通过不确定数据的线性模型进行训练和预测。给定受数据质量问题影响的训练和测试数据集，我们计算所有可能模型的合理过度近似，即通过在数据集的每个可能的干净版本上训练模型而生成的模型集，然后基于这些模型过度近似所有可能的预测。使用 Zorro，我们可以证明模型的稳健性，即模型参数受数据质量问题以及个体和聚合预测影响的程度。演示视频位于 https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gxkvRY3pLM0ATco2qvBnM1EcR8-U9F5h/view?usp=sharing。

papers.title: A Demonstration of GPTuner: A GPT-Based Manual-Reading Database Tuning System.
papers.abstract: Selecting appropriate values for the configurable knobs of Database Management Systems (DBMS) is crucial to improve performance. But because such complexity has surpassed the abilities of even the best human experts, database community turns to machine learning (ML)-based automatic tuning systems. However, these systems still incur significant tuning costs or only yield sub-optimal performance, attributable to their overly high reliance on black-box optimization and an oversight of domain knowledge. This paper demonstrates GPTuner, a manual-reading database tuning system that leverages Large Language Model (LLM) to bridge the gap between black-box optimization and white-box domain knowledge. This demonstration empowers (1) regular users with limited tuning experience to gain qualitative insights on the features of knobs, and optimize their DBMS performance automatically and efficiently, (2) database administrators and experts to further enhance GPTuner by simply contributing their invaluable tuning suggestions in natural language. Finally, we offer visitors the opportunity to explore a range of DBMS and optimization metrics, coupled with the flexibility to tailor their target workloads to their specific needs.
papers.abstract_zh: 为数据库管理系统 (DBMS) 的可配置旋钮选择适当的值对于提高性能至关重要。但由于这种复杂性甚至超出了最优秀的人类专家的能力，数据库社区转向基于机器学习 (ML) 的自动调优系统。然而，这些系统仍然会产生大量的调优成本，或者只能产生次优的性能，这归因于它们过度依赖黑盒优化和对领域知识的监督。本文演示了 GPTuner，这是一个手动读取的数据库调优系统，它利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 来弥合黑盒优化和白盒领域知识之间的差距。该演示使 (1) 调优经验有限的普通用户能够获得有关旋钮功能的定性见解，并自动高效地优化其 DBMS 性能，(2) 数据库管理员和专家只需以自然语言贡献宝贵的调优建议即可进一步增强 GPTuner。最后，我们为访问者提供探索一系列 DBMS 和优化指标的机会，以及根据其特定需求定制目标工作负载的灵活性。

papers.title: AI for Systems.
papers.abstract: In the last decade, data management has been steadily moving to-wards public clouds, turning DBMS "builders" into "operators". The combined scale and vertical integration gives us the opportunity to optimize systems holistically using a telemetry-based feedback loop. These events aligned with fast advances in the field of Data Science to create the perfect conditions for an explosion of "ML for Systems" solutions. More recently, massive improvements in Large Language Models (LLMs) has introduced another intriguing element into the mix. In this panel, we explore questions around the practical adoption and robustness of ML for Systems solutions, as well as the potential role for LLMs, spanning very concrete engineering considerations and very open-ended "future of this industry" type thinking.
papers.abstract_zh: 在过去的十年中，数据管理一直在稳步向公共云发展，将 DBMS“构建者”转变为“运营者”。规模和垂直整合的结合使我们有机会使用基于遥测的反馈循环来整体优化系统。这些事件与数据科学领域的快速发展相结合，为“系统机器学习”解决方案的爆炸式增长创造了完美的条件。最近，大型语言模型（LLM）的巨大改进引入了另一个有趣的元素。在这个小组中，我们探讨了有关系统机器学习解决方案的实际采用和稳健性的问题，以及法学硕士的潜在作用，涵盖非常具体的工程考虑因素和非常开放的“这个行业的未来”类型的思维。

papers.title: Amazon MemoryDB: A Fast and Durable Memory-First Cloud Database.
papers.abstract: Amazon MemoryDB for Redis is a database service designed for 11 9s of durability with in-memory performance. In this paper, we describe the architecture of MemoryDB and how we leverage open-source Redis, a popular data structure store, to build an enterprise-grade cloud database. MemoryDB offloads durability concerns to a separate low-latency, durable transaction log service, allowing us to scale performance, availability, and durability independently from the in-memory execution engine. We describe how, using this architecture, we are able to remain fully compatible with Redis, while providing single-digit millisecond write and microsecond-scale read latencies, strong consistency, and high availability. MemoryDB launched in 2021.
papers.abstract_zh: Amazon MemoryDB for Redis 是一项数据库服务，旨在实现 11 个 9 的持久性和内存性能。在本文中，我们描述了 MemoryDB 的架构以及如何利用开源 Redis（一种流行的数据结构存储）来构建企业级云数据库。 MemoryDB 将持久性问题转移到单独的低延迟、持久事务日志服务，使我们能够独立于内存执行引擎来扩展性能、可用​​性和持久性。我们描述了如何使用此架构保持与 Redis 的完全兼容，同时提供个位数毫秒级写入和微秒级读取延迟、强一致性和高可用性。 MemoryDB 于 2021 年推出。

papers.title: An Overview of Continuous Querying in (Modern) Data Systems.
papers.abstract: Continuous queries, also known as standing or streaming queries, are a class of queries that continuously monitor data sources over time, remaining active until explicitly terminated. This concept was introduced in 1992 by Terry and colleagues to address the need to access data that changes over time. Since then, the domain of continuous queries has seen significant development, research, and application in various data systems, including multiple Database Management Systems (DBMS). Notably, the past five years have marked the rise of Streaming Databases (SDS), and DBMSs that integrate continuous queries with traditional query responses.This growth reflects both industrial and academic interest in the field. Initially, continuous queries were primarily focused on the relational model, with their semantics expressed through extended algebras, calculi, or denotational semantics. However, more recently, there has been a shift towards applying continuous queries in the context of connected data. This includes exploration and abstraction in Graph DBMS, Knowledge Graphs, and Knowledge Evolution, expanding beyond the initial focus on relational models. This tutorial delves into a comprehensive survey of Continuous Queries in past and modern data systems. In addition to the historical perspective, the tutorial will focus on the latest developments in Streaming Databases and the continuous processing of streaming graphs.
papers.abstract_zh: 连续查询，也称为常设查询或流式查询，是一类随着时间的推移持续监视数据源的查询，在明确终止之前保持活动状态。这一概念由 Terry 及其同事于 1992 年提出，旨在满足访问随时间变化的数据的需求。从那时起，连续查询领域在各种数据系统（包括多个数据库管理系统（DBMS））中得到了显着的发展、研究和应用。值得注意的是，过去五年标志着流数据库 (SDS) 以及将连续查询与传统查询响应集成在一起的 DBMS 的兴起。这种增长反映了工业界和学术界对该领域的兴趣。最初，连续查询主要关注关系模型，其语义通过扩展代数、演算或指称语义来表达。然而，最近，人们开始转向在互联数据的背景下应用连续查询。这包括图 DBMS、知识图和知识演化的探索和抽象，超出了最初对关系模型的关注。本教程深入研究了过去和现代数据系统中连续查询的全面调查。除了历史视角之外，本教程还将重点关注流数据库的最新发展和流图的连续处理。

papers.title: Apache Arrow DataFusion: A Fast, Embeddable, Modular Analytic Query Engine.
papers.abstract:
Apache Arrow DataFusion is a fast, embeddable, and extensible query engine written in Rust that uses Apache Arrow as its memory model. In this paper we describe the technologies on which it is built, and how it fits in long-term database implementation trends. We then enumerate its features, optimizations, architecture and extension APIs to illustrate the breadth of requirements of modern OLAP engines as well as the interfaces needed by systems built with them. Finally, we demonstrate open standards and extensible design do not preclude state-of-the-art performance using a series of experimental comparisons to DuckDB.

While the individual techniques used in DataFusion have been previously described many times, it differs from other industrial strength engines by providing competitive performance and an open architecture that can be customized using more than 10 major extension APIs. This flexibility has led to use in many commercial and open source databases, machine learning pipelines, and other data-intensive systems. We anticipate that the accessibility and versatility of DataFusion, along with its competitive performance, will further the proliferation of high-performance custom data infrastructures tailored to specific needs assembled from modular components. While the individual techniques used in DataFusion have been previously described many times, it differs from other industrial strength engines by providing competitive performance and an open architecture that can be customized using more than 10 major extension APIs. This flexibility has led to use in many commercial and open source databases, machine learning pipelines, and other data-intensive systems. We anticipate that the accessibility and versatility of DataFusion, along with its competitive performance, will further the proliferation of high-performance custom data infrastructures tailored to specific needs assembled from modular components.
papers.abstract_zh:
Apache Arrow DataFusion 是一种用 Rust 编写的快速、可嵌入且可扩展的查询引擎，使用 Apache Arrow 作为其内存模型。在本文中，我们描述了它所基于的技术，以及它如何适应长期数据库实施趋势。然后，我们列举其功能、优化、架构和扩展 API，以说明现代 OLAP 引擎的需求广度以及使用它们构建的系统所需的接口。最后，我们通过与 DuckDB 的一系列实验比较来证明开放标准和可扩展设计并不排除最先进的性能。

虽然之前已多次描述过 DataFusion 中使用的各项技术，但它与其他工业强度引擎不同，它提供有竞争力的性能和开放式架构，可以使用 10 多个主要扩展 API 进行定制。这种灵活性使其在许多商业和开源数据库、机器学习管道和其他数据密集型系统中得到使用。我们预计，DataFusion 的可访问性和多功能性及其具有竞争力的性能将进一步促进由模块化组件组装而成的、根据特定需求定制的高性能定制数据基础设施的普及。虽然之前已多次描述过 DataFusion 中使用的各项技术，但它与其他工业强度引擎不同，它提供有竞争力的性能和开放式架构，可以使用 10 多个主要扩展 API 进行定制。这种灵活性使其在许多商业和开源数据库、机器学习管道和其他数据密集型系统中得到使用。我们预计，DataFusion 的可访问性和多功能性及其具有竞争力的性能将进一步促进由模块化组件组装而成的、根据特定需求定制的高性能定制数据基础设施的普及。

papers.title: Applications and Computation of the Shapley Value in Databases and Machine Learning.
papers.abstract: Recently, the Shapley value, a concept rooted in cooperative game theory, has found more and more applications in databases and machine learning. Due to its combinatoric nature, the computation of the Shapley value is #P-hard. To address this challenge, numerous studies are actively engaged in developing efficient computation methods or exploring alternative solutions in specific application contexts. Applications of the Shapley value in databases and machine learning as well as fast computation or approximation of the Shapley value in those applications are becoming a new research frontier in the database community. This tutorial presents a comprehensive and systematic overview of Shapley value applications and computation within both database and machine learning domains. We survey the existing methods from a unique perspective that diverges from the current literature. Unlike most reviews, which mainly focus on applications, our approach focuses on the underlying algorithmic mechanisms and application specific assumptions in these methods. This approach allows us to highlight the similarities and differences among the various Shapley value applications and computation techniques more effectively. Our tutorial categorizes these methods based on their intrinsic processes, cutting across different applications. The tutorial begins with an introduction to the Shapley value and its diverse applications in databases and machine learning. Subsequently, it delves into the computational challenges of the Shapley value, presents cutting-edge solutions for its efficient computation, and explores alternative solutions.
papers.abstract_zh: 近年来，沙普利值这一植根于合作博弈论的概念在数据库和机器学习中得到了越来越多的应用。由于其组合性质，Shapley 值的计算是#P-困难的。为了应对这一挑战，许多研究积极致力于开发高效的计算方法或探索特定应用环境中的替代解决方案。 Shapley值在数据库和机器学习中的应用以及这些应用中Shapley值的快速计算或逼近正在成为数据库界的一个新的研究前沿。本教程全面系统地概述了数据库和机器学习领域中的 Shapley 值应用和计算。我们从不同于当前文献的独特视角来调查现有方法。与大多数主要关注应用程序的评论不同，我们的方法侧重于这些方法中的底层算法机制和应用程序特定假设。这种方法使我们能够更有效地突出各种 Shapley 值应用和计算技术之间的异同。我们的教程根据这些方法的内在过程对它们进行分类，跨越不同的应用程序。本教程首先介绍 Shapley 值及其在数据库和机器学习中的各种应用。随后，深入研究了 Shapley 值的计算挑战，提出了高效计算的前沿解决方案，并探索了替代解决方案。

papers.title: ASM in Action: Fast and Practical Learned Cardinality Estimation.
papers.abstract: Learned cardinality estimators have shown remarkable improvements in estimation accuracy by exploiting machine learning techniques, yet suffer from inefficiency or sub-optimal query plans when deployed in query optimizers. ASM is a new learned cardinality estimator that significantly outperformed previous approaches in terms of end-to-end execution times. This demonstration illustrates the internal estimation process of that utilizes autoregressive models, sampling, and multi-dimensional statistics merging, and compares its performance with other alternatives. To do so, we visualize the detailed plan space exploration utilizing the estimation results.
papers.abstract_zh: 通过利用机器学习技术，学习基数估计器在估计准确性方面表现出了显着的改进，但在查询优化器中部署时却遇到效率低下或查询计划次优的问题。 ASM 是一种新的学习基数估计器，在端到端执行时间方面显着优于以前的方法。该演示说明了利用自回归模型、采样和多维统计合并的内部估计过程，并将其性能与其他替代方案进行了比较。为此，我们利用估计结果可视化详细的空间探索计划。

papers.title: ASQP-RL Demo: Learning Approximation Sets for Exploratory Queries.
papers.abstract: We demonstrate the Approximate Selection Query Processing (ASQP-RL) system, which uses Reinforcement Learning to select a subset of a large external dataset to process locally in a notebook during data exploration. Given a query workload over an external database and notebook memory size, the system translates the workload to select-project-join (non-aggregate) queries and finds a subset of each relation such that the data subset - called the approximation set - fits into the notebook memory and maximizes query result quality. The data subset can then be loaded into the notebook, and rapidly queried by the analyst. Our demonstration shows how ASQP-RL can be used during data exploration and achieve comparable results to external queries over the large dataset at significantly reduced query times. It also shows how ASQP-RL can be used for aggregation queries, achieving surprisingly good results compared to state-of-the-art techniques.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们演示了近似选择查询处理 (ASQP-RL) 系统，该系统使用强化学习来选择大型外部数据集的子集，以便在数据探索期间在笔记本中进行本地处理。给定外部数据库上的查询工作负载和笔记本内存大小，系统会将工作负载转换为选择项目连接（非聚合）查询，并找到每个关系的子集，以便数据子集（称为近似集）适合笔记本内存并最大化查询结果质量。然后可以将数据子集加载到笔记本中，并由分析师快速查询。我们的演示展示了如何在数据探索期间使用 ASQP-RL，并在显着减少查询时间的情况下，在大型数据集上获得与外部查询相当的结果。它还展示了如何将 ASQP-RL 用于聚合查询，与最先进的技术相比，取得了令人惊讶的良好结果。

papers.title: Automated Clustering Recommendation With Database Zone Maps.
papers.abstract: Zone maps are a data access structure provided in multiple data warehousing products [1, 3, 5, 14, 20], that reduce the cost of data access for a query by advance pruning of irrelevant data blocks. This is achieved by dividing the table into zones and storing for each zone, the minimum and maximum value of selected columns. The structures used to store these values are called zone maps. Zone maps are most effective for a query if the data is sorted (or clustered) on a column that the query has filters on, and if it also has zone maps constructed on the column. More selective filters are likely to lead to better performance. But as filter predicates vary from query to query, it is not straightforward to identify a single clustering that is effective across the entire workload. This paper describes a novel method for automatically analyzing a workload and recommending a clustering solution, as well as the zone maps to construct for improved performance. It works for both linear and interleaved (z-order) clustering, the two most commonly used clustering methodologies. In our experiments on the TPCH bench- mark and customer datasets, this approach provides significant performance improvements over the baseline. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to address the automated cluster- ing (auto-clustering) problem. The method described here has been implemented and will be released in an upcoming version of the Oracle Autonomous Database.
papers.abstract_zh: 区域映射是多种数据仓库产品[1,3,5,14,20]中提供的一种数据访问结构，它通过提前修剪不相关的数据块来降低查询的数据访问成本。这是通过将表划分为区域并为每个区域存储所选列的最小值和最大值来实现的。用于存储这些值的结构称为区域映射。如果数据在查询具有筛选器的列上排序（或聚集），并且还具有在该列上构建的区域映射，则区域映射对于查询最有效。更具选择性的过滤器可能会带来更好的性能。但由于过滤谓词因查询而异，因此识别在整个工作负载中有效的单个集群并不简单。本文描述了一种自动分析工作负载并推荐集群解决方案的新颖方法，以及为提高性能而构建的区域映射。它适用于线性和交错（z 顺序）聚类，这两种最常用的聚类方法。在我们对 TPCH 基准和客户数据集进行的实验中，这种方法比基线提供了显着的性能改进。据我们所知，这是第一个解决自动聚类（auto-clustering）问题的工作。这里描述的方法已经实现，并将在即将发布的 Oracle 自治数据库版本中发布。

papers.title: Automated Multidimensional Data Layouts in Amazon Redshift.
papers.abstract:
Analytic data systems typically use data layouts to improve the performance of scanning and filtering data. Common data layout techniques include single-column sort keys, compound sort keys, and more complex multidimensional data layouts such as the Z-order. An appropriately-selected data layout over a table, in combination with metadata such as zone maps, enables the system to skip irrelevant data blocks when scanning the table, which reduces the amount of data scanned and improves query performance.

In this paper, we introduce Multidimensional Data Layouts (MDDL), a new data layout technique which outperforms existing data layout techniques for query workloads with repetitive scan filters. Unlike existing data layout approaches, which typically sort tables based on columns, MDDL sorts tables based on a collection of predicates, which enables a much higher degree of specialization to the user's workload. We additionally introduce an algorithm for automatically learning the best MDDL for each table based on telemetry collected from the historical workload. We implemented MDDL within Amazon Redshift. Benchmarks on internal datasets and workloads show that MDDL achieves up to 85% reduction in end-to-end workload runtime compared to using traditional column-based data layout techniques. MDDL is, to the best of our knowledge, the first data layout technique in a commercial product that sorts based on predicates and automatically learns the best predicates.
papers.abstract_zh:
分析数据系统通常使用数据布局来提高扫描和过滤数据的性能。常见的数据布局技术包括单列排序键、复合排序键以及更复杂的多维数据布局（例如 Z 顺序）。适当选择表上的数据布局，结合区域映射等元数据，使系统在扫描表时跳过不相关的数据块，从而减少扫描的数据量并提高查询性能。

在本文中，我们介绍了多维数据布局（MDDL），这是一种新的数据布局技术，对于具有重复扫描过滤器的查询工作负载，其性能优于现有的数据布局技术。与通常根据列对表进行排序的现有数据布局方法不同，MDDL 根据谓词集合对表进行排序，这使得用户的工作负载能够实现更高程度的专业化。我们还引入了一种算法，用于根据从历史工作负载收集的遥测数据自动学习每个表的最佳 MDDL。我们在 Amazon Redshift 中实施了 MDDL。内部数据集和工作负载的基准测试表明，与使用传统的基于列的数据布局技术相比，MDDL 可将端到端工作负载运行时间减少高达 85%。据我们所知，MDDL 是商业产品中第一个基于谓词排序并自动学习最佳谓词的数据布局技术。

papers.title: Beyond Bloom: A Tutorial on Future Feature-Rich Filters.
papers.abstract:
Filters, such as Bloom, quotient, and cuckoo, save space by maintaining an approximate representation of a set and occasionally returning false positives. Filters play a critical role in building modern dataintensive applications and are used across various domains such as databases, storage engines, computational biology, cyber- security, and networks. There has been extensive research on filters in the past few decades resulting in filters with much improved performance and features. Yet modern data-intensive applications are still designed around the limitations of traditional filters resulting in complex designs and sub-optimal performance.

This tutorial aims to bring together researchers at the forefront of filter data structure research to help the database community learn about the recent advancements in the theory and practice of filters. The tutorial will cover real-world case studies of redesigning applications using the modern filter APIs to achieve simplicity and improved application performance. The tutorial will further help uncover the open research problems, both in theory and systems, and increase interaction among researchers to tackle those problems.
papers.abstract_zh:
过滤器（例如 Bloom、quotient 和 cuckoo）通过维护集合的近似表示并偶尔返回误报来节省空间。过滤器在构建现代数据密集型应用程序中发挥着关键作用，并广泛应用于数据库、存储引擎、计算生物学、网络安全和网络等各个领域。在过去的几十年里，人们对滤波器进行了广泛的研究，从而使滤波器的性能和功能有了很大的改进。然而，现代数据密集型应用程序仍然围绕传统过滤器的局限性进行设计，导致设计复杂且性能不佳。

本教程旨在汇集过滤器数据结构研究前沿的研究人员，帮助数据库社区了解过滤器理论和实践的最新进展。本教程将涵盖使用现代过滤器 API 重新设计应用程序的实际案例研究，以实现简单性并提高应用程序性能。该教程将进一步帮助揭示理论和系统上的开放研究问题，并增加研究人员之间的互动来解决这些问题。

papers.title: BG3: A Cost Effective and I/O Efficient Graph Database in Bytedance.
papers.abstract: ByteDance's products, including TikTok, Douyin, and Toutiao, generate massive amounts of graph data every day. Previously, we developed ByteGraph, a distributed graph database that manages the large-scale graph data with varying performance requirements. BG3 is deployed on the computation and storage decoupled architecture, which allows for high performance in-memory execution and independent scaling of computation and storage layers. Byte-Graph has demonstrated robust performance throughout its years of service in global-scale applications. However, as the business scale expands and applications evolve, the complexity and volume of graph analysis and processing have also increased. We observe that conventional database design faces issues with high operational costs when dealing with the large-scale graph workloads in social network management. To address this issue, we develop BG3 (ByteGraph 3.0), a cost-effective and high performance distributed graph database which provides three critical components. Firstly, a cost-effective yet query-efficient graph storage engine based on the BW-tree-based memory indices and affordable cloud storage. Secondly, a workload aware space reclamation mechanism, which enhances storage utilization and reduces write amplifications. Thirdly, a lightweight leader-follower synchronization mechanism ensuring strong consistency for scaling out real-time graph analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that BG3 addresses the limitations of ByteGraph, offering a cost-effective, efficient, and scalable solution for processing ByteDance's large-scale graphs.
papers.abstract_zh: 字节跳动的产品，包括 TikTok、抖音、今日头条，每天都会产生海量的图数据。此前，我们开发了ByteGraph，一个分布式图数据库，用于管理具有不同性能要求的大规模图数据。 BG3部署在计算和存储解耦架构上，可实现高性能内存执行以及计算层和存储层的独立扩展。 Byte-Graph 在全球规模的应用程序中服务多年，展示了强大的性能。然而，随着业务规模的扩大和应用的演进，图分析处理的复杂度和数量也随之增加。我们观察到，传统的数据库设计在处理社交网络管理中的大规模图形工作负载时面临着高运营成本的问题。为了解决这个问题，我们开发了BG3（ByteGraph 3.0），这是一种经济高效且高性能的分布式图数据库，它提供了三个关键组件。首先，基于 BW 树的内存索引和经济实惠的云存储，具有成本效益且查询高效的图存储引擎。其次，工作负载感知空间回收机制，可提高存储利用率并减少写入放大。第三，轻量级的leader-follower同步机制确保了横向扩展实时图分析的强一致性。实验结果表明，BG3解决了ByteGraph的局限性，为处理字节跳动的大规模图提供了一种经济有效、高效且可扩展的解决方案。

papers.title: BigLake: BigQuery's Evolution toward a Multi-Cloud Lakehouse.
papers.abstract:
BigQuery's cloud-native disaggregated architecture has allowed Google Cloud to evolve the system to meet several customer needs across the analytics and AI/ML workload spectrum. A key customer requirement for BigQuery centers around the unification of data lake and enterprise data warehousing workloads. This approach combines: (1) the need for core data management primitives, e.g., security, governance, common runtime metadata, performance acceleration, ACID transactions, provided by an enterprise data warehouses coupled with (2) harnessing the flexibility of the open source format and analytics ecosystem along with new workload types such as AI/ML over unstructured data on object storage. In addition, there is a strong requirement to support BigQuery as a multi-cloud offering given cloud customers are opting for a multi-cloud footprint by default.

This paper describes BigLake, an evolution of BigQuery toward a multi-cloud lakehouse to address these customer requirements in novel ways. We describe three main innovations in this space. We first present BigLake tables, making open-source table formats (e.g., Apache Parquet, Iceberg) first class citizens, providing fine-grained governance enforcement and performance acceleration over these formats to BigQuery and other open-source analytics engines. Next, we cover the design and implementation of BigLake Object tables that allow BigQuery to integrate AI/ML for inferencing and processing over unstructured data. Finally, we present Omni, a platform for deploying BigQuery on non-GCP clouds, focusing on the infrastructure and operational innovations we made to provide an enterprise lakehouse product regardless of the cloud provider hosting the data.
papers.abstract_zh:
BigQuery 的云原生分解架构使 Google Cloud 能够不断发展系统，以满足分析和 AI/ML 工作负载范围内的多种客户需求。 BigQuery 的一个关键客户需求集中在数据湖和企业数据仓库工作负载的统一上。这种方法结合了：(1) 对企业数据仓库提供的核心数据管理原语的需求，例如安全性、治理、通用运行时元数据、性能加速、ACID 事务，以及 (2) 利用开源格式和分析生态系统的灵活性以及新的工作负载类型，例如对象存储上非结构化数据上的 AI/ML。此外，鉴于云客户默认选择多云足迹，因此强烈要求支持 BigQuery 作为多云产品。

本文介绍了 BigLake，这是 BigQuery 向多云 Lakehouse 的演变，旨在以新颖的方式满足这些客户需求。我们描述了这个领域的三个主要创新。我们首先推出 BigLake 表，使开源表格式（例如 Apache Parquet、Iceberg）成为一等公民，为 BigQuery 和其他开源分析引擎提供针对这些格式的细粒度治理实施和性能加速。接下来，我们将介绍 BigLake 对象表的设计和实现，这些表允许 BigQuery 集成 AI/ML 以对非结构化数据进行推理和处理。最后，我们介绍 Omni，一个用于在非 GCP 云上部署 BigQuery 的平台，重点关注我们为提供企业 Lakehouse 产品而进行的基础设施和运营创新，无论托管数据的云提供商如何。

papers.title: Bouncer: Admission Control with Response Time Objectives for Low-latency Online Data Systems.
papers.abstract: Internet companies rely on low-latency online data systems to provide quick responses to users. These systems employ complementary overload management techniques to offer a continued, acceptable service throughout traffic surges, where "acceptable" partly means that serviced queries meet or track closely their response time objectives. Thus, in this paper we present Bouncer, an admission control policy aimed to keep admitted queries under or near their service level objectives (SLOs) on percentile response times. Bouncer decides to accept or reject incoming queries based on inexpensive estimates of such percentiles. It can assign separate SLOs to different classes of queries in the workload, and implements early rejections to let clients react promptly and help data systems avoid doing useless work. We propose two starvation avoidance strategies that supplement Bouncer's basic formulation and prevent query types from receiving no service. Our evaluation, in simulation and on a production-grade distributed graph database, shows that Bouncer and its starvation-avoiding variants 1) let admitted queries meet or stay close to their SLOs when other in-house policies do not, and 2) report fewer overall rejections and a small overhead, while letting the system reach high utilization. We observe that the proposed strategies can prevent query starvation, but with a modest increase in rejections and with SLO violation counts for serviced queries that may be acceptable in practice.
papers.abstract_zh: 互联网公司依靠低延迟的在线数据系统为用户提供快速响应。这些系统采用互补的过载管理技术，在流量激增期间提供持续的、可接受的服务，其中“可接受”部分意味着所服务的查询满足或密切跟踪其响应时间目标。因此，在本文中，我们提出了 Bouncer，这是一种准入控制策略，旨在使已准入的查询在百分位数响应时间上保持在或接近其服务级别目标 (SLO)。 Bouncer 根据此类百分位的廉价估计来决定接受或拒绝传入查询。它可以为工作负载中不同类别的查询分配单独的SLO，并实现早期拒绝，让客户端迅速做出反应，帮助数据系统避免做无用的工作。我们提出了两种避免饥饿的策略，以补充 Bouncer 的基本公式并防止查询类型接收不到服务。我们在模拟和生产级分布式图形数据库上的评估表明，Bouncer 及其避免饥饿的变体 1) 让承认的查询满足或保持接近其 SLO，而其他内部策略则不然；2) 报告较少的总体拒绝和较小的开销，同时让系统达到高利用率。我们观察到，所提出的策略可以防止查询饥饿，但拒绝次数会适度增加，并且服务查询的 SLO 违规计数在实践中可能是可以接受的。

papers.title: Building Reactive Large Language Model Pipelines with Motion.
papers.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) rely on prompts with detailed and informative context to produce high-quality responses at scale. One way to develop such prompts is through reactive LLM pipelines, which incorporate new information---e.g., end-user feedback and summaries of historical inputs and outputs---back into prompts to improve future response quality. We present Motion, a Python framework to build and execute reactive LLM pipelines. Motion uses a weak consistency model to maintain prompt versions, trading off freshness for end-user latency. We demonstrate Motion with an e-commerce application that suggests apparel to wear for any event, allowing attendees to indirectly influence prompts with their queries. Attendees can interact with the demo as end-users or modify the application as developers, adding new information sources for reactive prompts.
papers.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 依靠包含详细且信息丰富的上下文的提示来大规模生成高质量的响应。开发此类提示的一种方法是通过反应性 LLM 管道，该管道将新信息（例如最终用户反馈以及历史输入和输出的摘要）纳入提示中，以提高未来的响应质量。我们推出 Motion，一个用于构建和执行反应式 LLM 管道的 Python 框架。 Motion 使用弱一致性模型来维护提示版本，以新鲜度换取最终用户延迟。我们通过电子商务应用程序演示了 Motion，该应用程序建议在任何活动中穿着的服装，允许与会者通过他们的查询间接影响提示。与会者可以作为最终用户与演示进行交互，或作为开发人员修改应用程序，为反应提示添加新的信息源。

papers.title: ByteCard: Enhancing ByteDance's Data Warehouse with Learned Cardinality Estimation.
papers.abstract:
Cardinality estimation is a critical component and a longstanding challenge in modern data warehouses. ByteHouse, ByteDance's cloud-native engine for extensive data analysis in exabyte-scale environments, serves numerous internal decision-making business scenarios. With the increasing demand for ByteHouse, cardinality estimation becomes the bottleneck for efficiently processing queries. Specifically, the existing query optimizer of ByteHouse uses the traditional Selinger-like cardinality estimator, which can produce substantial estimation errors, resulting in suboptimal query plans.

To improve cardinality estimation accuracy while maintaining a practical inference overhead, we develop a framework ByteCard that enables efficient training and integration of learned cardinality estimators. Furthermore, ByteCard adapts recent advances in cardinality estimation to build models that can balance accuracy and practicality (e.g., inference latency, model size, training overhead). We observe significant query processing speed-up in ByteHouse after replacing the existing cardinality estimator with ByteCard for several optimization scenarios. Evaluations on real-world datasets show the integration of ByteCard leads to an improvement of up to 30% in the 99th quantile of latency. At last, we share our valuable experience in engineering advanced cardinality estimators. This experience can help ByteHouse integrate more learning-based solutions on the critical query execution path in the future.
papers.abstract_zh:
基数估计是现代数据仓库中的一个关键组成部分和一个长期的挑战。 ByteHouse是字节跳动的云原生引擎，用于在EB级环境中进行广泛的数据分析，服务于众多内部决策业务场景。随着 ByteHouse 需求的不断增加，基数估计成为高效处理查询的瓶颈。具体来说，ByteHouse现有的查询优化器使用传统的类似Selinger的基数估计器，这可能会产生大量的估计错误，从而导致查询计划不理想。

为了提高基数估计的准确性，同时保持实际的推理开销，我们开发了一个框架 ByteCard，该框架能够有效地训练和集成学习的基数估计器。此外，ByteCard 采用基数估计方面的最新进展来构建可以平衡准确性和实用性（例如推理延迟、模型大小、训练开销）的模型。在使用 ByteCard 替换现有的基数估计器后，我们观察到 ByteHouse 中的查询处理速度显着提高，以适应多种优化场景。对现实世界数据集的评估表明，ByteCard 的集成可将延迟的第 99 个分位数提高高达 30%。最后，我们分享了我们在设计高级基数估计器方面的宝贵经验。这种经验可以帮助ByteHouse未来在关键查询执行路径上集成更多基于学习的解决方案。

papers.title: ChatPipe: Orchestrating Data Preparation Pipelines by Optimizing Human-ChatGPT Interactions.
papers.abstract: Orchestrating a high-quality data preparation program is essential for successful machine learning (ML), but it is known to be time and effort consuming. Despite the impressive capabilities of large language models like ChatGPT in generating programs by inter- acting with users through natural language prompts, there are still limitations. Specifically, a user must provide specific prompts to iteratively guide ChatGPT in improving data preparation programs, which requires a certain level of expertise in programming, the dataset used and the ML task. Moreover, once a program has been generated, it is non-trivial to revisit a previous version or make changes to the program without starting the process over again. In this paper, we present ChatPipe, a novel system designed to facilitate seamless interaction between users and ChatGPT. Chat- Pipe provides users with effective recommendation on next data preparation operations, and guides ChatGPT to generate program for the operations. Also, ChatPipe enables users to easily roll back to previous versions of the program, which facilitates more efficient experimentation and testing. We have developed a web application for ChatPipe and prepared several real-world ML tasks from Kaggle, which can demonstrate the capabilities of ChatPipe.
papers.abstract_zh: 编排高质量的数据准备程序对于成功的机器学习 (ML) 至关重要，但众所周知，它非常耗时和费力。尽管像 ChatGPT 这样的大型语言模型在通过自然语言提示与用户交互来生成程序方面具有令人印象深刻的能力，但仍然存在局限性。具体来说，用户必须提供特定的提示来迭代指导 ChatGPT 改进数据准备程序，这需要在编程、使用的数据集和 ML 任务方面具有一定水平的专业知识。此外，一旦生成了程序，重新访问以前的版本或对程序进行更改而不重新开始该过程就很重要。在本文中，我们提出了 ChatPipe，这是一个旨在促进用户和 ChatGPT 之间无缝交互的新颖系统。 Chat-Pipe为用户下一步的数据准备操作提供有效的建议，并指导ChatGPT生成该操作的程序。此外，ChatPipe 使用户能够轻松回滚到程序的先前版本，从而有利于更高效的实验和测试。我们为 ChatPipe 开发了一个 Web 应用程序，并准备了几个来自 Kaggle 的实际 ML 任务，这可以演示 ChatPipe 的功能。

papers.title: Cognitive Psychology Meets Data Management: State of the Art and Future Directions.
papers.abstract: Data definition and data manipulation language platforms of a DBMS are oftenhuman-centric. For instance, declarative queries are often formulated by humans or their results are directly consumed by humans. Consequently, superior and effective design of these platforms needs to be informed by theories fromcognitive psychology, a branch of psychology that studies how individuals think and process information. In this tutorial, we review and summarize the state of the art in the emerging paradigm ofpsychology theory-informed design ofhuman-centric components of a DBMS. In this paradigm, the design of the human-centric components is guided by theories from cognitive psychology in addition to theories from computer science. We review techniques and frameworks that embrace this paradigm in the arena of database querying. To guide future work in this arena, the tutorial proposes open problems and new research directions.
papers.abstract_zh: DBMS 的数据定义和数据操作语言平台通常以人为中心。例如，声明性查询通常由人类制定，或者它们的结果由人类直接使用。因此，这些平台的卓越而有效的设计需要借鉴认知心理学的理论，认知心理学是研究个人如何思考和处理信息的心理学分支。在本教程中，我们回顾并总结了以心理学理论为基础的 DBMS 以人为中心的组件设计的新兴范式的最新技术。在这种范式中，以人为中心的组件的设计除了计算机科学的理论之外还受到认知心理学的理论指导。我们回顾了数据库查询领域采用这种范例的技术和框架。为了指导该领域的未来工作，本教程提出了开放问题和新的研究方向。

papers.title: Comquest: Large Scale User Comment Crawling and Integration.
papers.abstract: User-generated content like comments are valuable sources for various downstream applications. However, access to user comments data is often limited to specific platforms or outlets, which imposes a great limitation on the available data, and may not provide a representative sample of opinions from a diverse population on a particular event. This paper presents a comment crawling system that leverages the Web API of popular third-party commenting systems to collect comments from a large number of websites integrated with the commenting systems. Given a target page, the crawling system utilizes a deep learning model to extract API parameters and send HTTP requests to the API to retrieve comments. The system, Comquest, that we propose to demo is news-oriented and crawls comments regarding specific news topics/stories. Comquest can work with any website that allows commenting. Comquest provides a useful tool for collecting comments that represent a wider range of opinions, stances, and sentiments from websites on a global scale.
papers.abstract_zh: 用户生成的内容（例如评论）是各种下游应用程序的宝贵来源。然而，对用户评论数据的访问通常仅限于特定平台或渠道，这对可用数据造成了很大的限制，并且可能无法提供不同人群对特定事件的代表性意见样本。本文提出了一种评论抓取系统，该系统利用流行的第三方评论系统的Web API来收集大量与评论系统集成的网站的评论。给定一个目标页面，爬虫系统利用深度学习模型提取API参数并向API发送HTTP请求以检索评论。我们建议演示的系统 Comquest 是面向新闻的，会抓取有关特定新闻主题/故事的评论。 Comquest 可以与任何允许评论的网站一起使用。 Comquest 提供了一个有用的工具，用于从全球范围内的网站收集代表更广泛的意见、立场和情绪的评论。

papers.title: COSMO: A Large-Scale E-commerce Common Sense Knowledge Generation and Serving System at Amazon.
papers.abstract: Applications of large-scale knowledge graphs in the e-commerce platforms can improve shopping experience for their customers. While existing e-commerce knowledge graphs (KGs) integrate a large volume of concepts or product attributes, they fail to discover user intentions, leaving the gap with how people think, behave, and interact with the surrounding world. In this work, we present COSMO, a scalable system to mine user-centric commonsense knowledge from massive behaviors and construct industry-scale knowledge graphs to empower diverse online services. In particular, we describe a pipeline for collecting high-quality seed knowledge assertions that are distilled from large language models (LLMs) and further refined by critic classifiers trained over human-in-the-loop annotated data.Since those generations may not always align with human preferences and contain noises, we then describe how we adopt instruction tuning to finetune an efficient language model~(COSMO-LM) for faithful e-commerce commonsense knowledge generation at scale. COSMO-LM effectively expands our knowledge graph to 18 major categories at Amazon, producing millions of high-quality knowledge with only 30k annotated instructions. Finally COSMO has been deployed in Amazon search applications such as search navigation. Both offline and online A/B experiments demonstrate our proposed system achieves significant improvement. Furthermore, these experiments highlight the immense potential of commonsense knowledge extracted from instruction-finetuned large language models.
papers.abstract_zh: 大规模知识图谱在电子商务平台中的应用可以改善客户的购物体验。现有的电商知识图谱虽然集成了大量概念或产品属性，但无法发现用户意图，与人们的思维、行为以及与周围世界的交互方式存在差距。在这项工作中，我们提出了 COSMO，这是一个可扩展的系统，可以从海量行为中挖掘以用户为中心的常识知识，并构建行业规模的知识图谱来赋能多样化的在线服务。特别是，我们描述了一个用于收集高质量种子知识断言的管道，这些断言是从大型语言模型（LLM）中提炼出来的，并由在人机循环注释数据上训练的批评分类器进一步细化。由于这些生成可能并不总是符合人类偏好并包含噪音，因此我们随后描述了如何采用指令调整来微调有效的语言模型~（COSMO-LM），以大规模地生成忠实的电子商务常识知识。 COSMO-LM 有效地将我们的知识图谱扩展到亚马逊的 18 个主要类别，仅用 3 万条带注释的指令就产生了数百万条高质量知识。最后，COSMO 已部署在搜索导航等亚马逊搜索应用中。离线和在线 A/B 实验都表明我们提出的系统取得了显着的改进。此外，这些实验凸显了从指令微调大型语言模型中提取常识知识的巨大潜力。

papers.title: Data-Juicer: A One-Stop Data Processing System for Large Language Models.
papers.abstract:
The immense evolution in Large Language Models (LLMs) has underscored the importance of massive, heterogeneous, and high-quality data. A data recipe is a mixture of data from different sources for training LLMs, which plays a vital role in LLMs' performance. Existing open-source tools for LLM data processing are mostly tailored for specific data recipes. To continuously uncover the potential of LLMs, incorporate data from new sources, and improve LLMs' performance, we build a new system named Data-Juicer, with which we can efficiently generate diverse data recipes, explore different possibilities in forming data mixtures, and evaluate their effects on model performance. Different from traditional data-analytics pipelines, Data-Juicer faces some unique challenges. Firstly, the possible data sources for forming data recipes are truly heterogeneous and massive with various qualities. Secondly, it is extremely expensive to precisely evaluate data recipes' impact on LLMs' performance. Thirdly, the end users of Data-Juicer, model developers, need sufficient flexibility to configure and evaluate different data recipes.

Data-Juicer features a fine-grained abstraction of pipelines for constructing data recipes, with over 50 built-in operators for easy composition and extension. By incorporating visualization and auto-evaluation capabilities, Data-Juicer enables a timely feedback loop for both LLM pre-training and fine-tuning. Further, Data-Juicer is optimized and integrated with ecosystems for LLM training, evaluation, and distributed computing. The data recipes derived with Data-Juicer gain notable improvements on state-of-the-art LLMs, by up to 7.45% increase in averaged score across 16 benchmarks and 17.5% higher win rate in pair-wise GPT-4 evaluations. Our system, recipes, and tutorials are released, calling for broader data-centric research on training and understanding LLMs.
papers.abstract_zh:
大型语言模型 (LLM) 的巨大发展凸显了海量、异构和高质量数据的重要性。数据配方是来自不同来源的数据的混合，用于训练法学硕士，这对法学硕士的表现起着至关重要的作用。现有的 LLM 数据处理开源工具大多是针对特定数据配方量身定制的。为了不断发掘LLM的潜力，整合新来源的数据并提高LLM的性能，我们构建了一个名为Data-Juicer的新系统，通过它我们可以有效地生成不同的数据配方，探索形成数据混合的不同可能性，并评估它们对模型性能的影响。与传统的数据分析管道不同，Data-Juicer 面临着一些独特的挑战。首先，形成数据配方的可能数据源确实是异构的、海量的、质量各异的。其次，精确评估数据配方对法学硕士绩效的影响极其昂贵。第三，Data-Juicer的最终用户，即模型开发人员，需要足够的灵活性来配置和评估不同的数据配方。

Data-Juicer 具有用于构建数据配方的细粒度管道抽象，具有 50 多个内置运算符，可轻松组合和扩展。通过整合可视化和自动评估功能，Data-Juicer 为 LLM 预训练和微调提供了及时的反馈循环。此外，Data-Juicer 还针对 LLM 培训、评估和分布式计算进行了优化并与生态系统集成。使用 Data-Juicer 得出的数据配方在最先进的 LLM 上获得了显着改进，16 个基准测试的平均分数提高了 7.45%，成对 GPT-4 评估的获胜率提高了 17.5%。我们的系统、配方和教程已发布，呼吁对法学硕士的培训和理解进行更广泛的以数据为中心的研究。

papers.title: Demonstrating CAESURA: Language Models as Multi-Modal Query Planners.
papers.abstract: In many domains, multi-modal data takes an important role and modern question-answering systems based on LLMs allow users to query this data using simple natural language queries. Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) is a recent approach that extends Large Language Models (LLM) with database technology to enable such multi-modal QA systems. In RAG, relevant data is first retrieved from a vector database and then fed into an LLM that computes the query result. However, RAG-based approaches have severe issues, such as regarding efficiency and scalability, since LLMs have high inference costs and can only process limited amounts of data. Therefore, in this demo paper, we propose CAESURA, a database-first approach that extends databases with LLMs. The main idea is that CAESURA utilizes the reasoning capabilities of LLMs to translate natural language queries into execution plans. Using such execution plans allows CAESURA to process multi-modal data outside the LLM using query operators and optimization strategies that are footed in scalable query execution strategies of databases. Our demo allows users to experience CAESURA on two example datasets containing tables, texts, and images1.
papers.abstract_zh: 在许多领域，多模态数据发挥着重要作用，基于法学硕士的现代问答系统允许用户使用简单的自然语言查询来查询这些数据。检索增强生成 (RAG) 是一种最新方法，它利用数据库技术扩展大型语言模型 (LLM)，以实现此类多模式 QA 系统。在 RAG 中，首先从向量数据库中检索相关数据，然后将其输入到计算查询结果的 LLM 中。然而，基于 RAG 的方法存在严重的问题，例如效率和可扩展性，因为 LLM 的推理成本很高，并且只能处理有限的数据量。因此，在这篇演示论文中，我们提出了 CAESURA，这是一种利用法学硕士扩展数据库的数据库优先方法。主要思想是 CAESURA 利用 LLM 的推理能力将自然语言查询转换为执行计划。使用此类执行计划，CAESURA 可以使用基于数据库可扩展查询执行策略的查询运算符和优化策略来处理 LLM 之外的多模式数据。我们的演示允许用户在两​​个包含表格、文本和图像的示例数据集上体验 CAESURA。

papers.title: Demonstrating Nexus for Correlation Discovery over Collections of Spatio-Temporal Tabular Data.
papers.abstract: Causal analysis is crucial for understanding cause-and-effect relationships in observed data to inform better decisions. However, conducting precise causal analysis on observational data is usually impractical, so domain experts often begin their exploration by identifying correlations. In this paper, we demonstrate Nexus, a system that aligns tabular datasets across space and time, handles missing data, and identifies correlations deemed "interesting", facilitating the exploration of causal relationships.
papers.abstract_zh: 因果分析对于理解观察数据中的因果关系以提供更好的决策至关重要。然而，对观测数据进行精确的因果分析通常是不切实际的，因此领域专家通常通过识别相关性来开始探索。在本文中，我们演示了 Nexus，这是一个跨空间和时间对齐表格数据集、处理缺失数据并识别被认为“有趣”的相关性的系统，从而促进因果关系的探索。

papers.title: Demonstrating REmatch: A Novel RegEx Engine for Finding all Matches.
papers.abstract: In this demonstration we showcase REmatch, a regular expression (RegEx) engine built to find all matches of a given pattern in a document. REmatch is based on the theory of enumeration algorithms, and it extends the capability of classical regex engines with the ability to find nested and overlapping matches with a simple and intuitive syntax, and no need to use non-standard operators, while at the same time maintaining efficient performance. The algorithmic core of REmatch is an algorithm that builds a compressed representation of all matching results and enumerates them on demand in time proportional to writing them down symbol by symbol. For this demonstration, we developed a simple Web interface to access the REmatch engine, available at https://rematch.cl, and will showcase the utility of the engine in the context of analysing DNA sequences, linguistic analysis, among others. For this, we have prepared a series of examples that illustrate the expressiveness and ease of use of REmatch. Additionally, at the demo we will have a series of challenge tasks, asking attendees to capture the result set of a simple REmatch expression using their favourite RegEx engine.
papers.abstract_zh: 在此演示中，我们展示了 REmatch，这是一个正则表达式 (RegEx) 引擎，旨在查找文档中给定模式的所有匹配项。 REmatch基于枚举算法的理论，它扩展了经典正则表达式引擎的能力，能够以简单直观的语法查找嵌套和重叠匹配，并且无需使用非标准运算符，同时保持高效的性能。 REmatch 的算法核心是一种算法，该算法构建所有匹配结果的压缩表示，并按照与逐个符号写下它们成比例的时间按需枚举它们。对于本次演示，我们开发了一个简单的 Web 界面来访问 REmatch 引擎（可在 https://rematch.cl 上获取），并将展示该引擎在分析 DNA 序列、语言分析等方面的实用性。为此，我们准备了一系列示例来说明 REmatch 的表现力和易用性。此外，在演示中，我们将进行一系列挑战任务，要求与会者使用他们最喜欢的 RegEx 引擎捕获简单 REmatch 表达式的结果集。

papers.title: Demonstrating λ-Tune: Exploiting Large Language Models for Workload-Adaptive Database System Tuning.
papers.abstract: We demonstrate λ-Tune, a tool that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) for automated, workload-adaptive database system tuning. λ-Tune harnesses the ability of LLMs to process and comprehend arbitrary textual data in a zero-shot manner, employing a workload-adaptive optimization approach. Given a database system, the hardware specifications, and a set of queries, λ-Tune generates prompts to retrieve configuration recommendations for the tuning knobs and the physical design, tailored to the specific system and workload. Our framework utilizes a workload compression approach that extracts and includes in the prompt only the most insightful workload characteristics, while the prompt size can be adjusted by a user-defined token budget. Utilizing the zero-shot capabilities of LLMs, λ-Tune outperforms other LLM and machine learning-enhanced database tuning baselines, which rely on time-consuming tuning and training phases, as well as expensive hardware, such as GPUs. During demonstration, users will be able to experiment with λ-Tune to tune Postgres and MySQL, as well as explore and modify the configurations retrieved by the LLM in an interactive way through λ-Tune's user interface.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们演示了 λ-Tune，这是一种利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 进行自动化、工作负载自适应数据库系统调优的工具。 λ-Tune 利用法学硕士以零样本方式处理和理解任意文本数据的能力，采用工作负载自适应优化方法。给定数据库系统、硬件规格和一组查询，λ-Tune 会生成提示来检索针对特定系统和工作负载定制的调整旋钮和物理设计的配置建议。我们的框架采用工作负载压缩方法，仅提取最有洞察力的工作负载特征并将其包含在提示中，而提示大小可以通过用户定义的令牌预算进行调整。利用 LLM 的零样本功能，λ-Tune 的性能优于其他 LLM 和机器学习增强型数据库调优基线，这些基线依赖于耗时的调优和训练阶段以及昂贵的硬件（例如 GPU）。在演示过程中，用户将能够尝试使用 λ-Tune 来调整 Postgres 和 MySQL，并通过 λ-Tune 的用户界面以交互方式探索和修改 LLM 检索到的配置。

papers.title: Demonstration of ElasticNotebook: Migrating Live Computational Notebook States.
papers.abstract:
Computational notebooks (e.g., Jupyter, Google Colab) are widely used for interactive data science and machine learning. However, existing notebook systems lack the functionality of reliably and efficiently persisting thenotebook session state consisting of user-defined variables (e.g., processed datasets, ML models), hence the termination of a session often leads to loss of work.

In this demo, we introduce a new notebook system, ElasticNotebook, that offers live migration of session states via computational checkpointing/restoration for notebook systems (e.g., Jupyter Notebook, Colab). ElasticNotebook's frontend allows users to configure the periodic creation of session state checkpoints, which can then be restored at will through a drop-down menu. ElasticNotebook's backend utilizes novel lightweight monitoring techniques to find a reliable and efficient way (i.e., replication plan ) for replicating session states when requested. This demo will showcase ElasticNotebook's ability to preserve the user's work progress in Jupyter Servers by replicating their session state in two common use cases: live migration across machines and resumption after termination.
papers.abstract_zh:
计算笔记本（例如 Jupyter、Google Colab）广泛用于交互式数据科学和机器学习。然而，现有的笔记本系统缺乏可靠且高效地保存由用户定义的变量（例如，处理的数据集、ML 模型）组成的笔记本会话状态的功能，因此会话的终止通常会导致工作丢失。

在此演示中，我们介绍了一种新的笔记本系统 ElasticNotebook，它通过笔记本系统（例如 Jupyter Notebook、Colab）的计算检查点/恢复提供会话状态的实时迁移。 ElasticNotebook 的前端允许用户配置会话状态检查点的定期创建，然后可以通过下拉菜单随意恢复。 ElasticNotebook 的后端利用新颖的轻量级监控技术来找到可靠且有效的方法（即复制计划）来根据请求复制会话状态。该演示将展示 ElasticNotebook 通过在两个常见用例中复制会话状态来保留用户在 Jupyter 服务器中的工作进度：跨计算机的实时迁移和终止后的恢复。

papers.title: Demonstration of Udon: Line-by-line Debugging of User-Defined Functions in Data Workflows.
papers.abstract: Many big data systems are written in languages such as C, C++, Java, and Scala for high efficiency, whereas data analysts often use Python to conduct data wrangling, statistical analysis, and machine learning. User-defined functions (UDFs) are commonly used in these systems to bridge the gap between the two ecosystems. Debugging complex UDFs in data-processing systems is challenging due to the required coordination between language debuggers and the data-processing engine, as well as the debugging overhead on large volumes of data. In this paper, we showcase Udon, a novel debugger to support line-by-line debugging of UDFs in data-processing systems. Udon encapsulates modern line-by-line debugging primitives, such as those to set breakpoints, perform code inspections, and make code modifications while executing a UDF on a single tuple. In this demonstration, we use real-world scenarios to showcase the experience of using Udon for line-by-line debugging of a UDF.
papers.abstract_zh: 为了提高效率，许多大数据系统都是用 C、C++、Java 和 Scala 等语言编写的，而数据分析师经常使用 Python 来进行数据整理、统计分析和机器学习。这些系统中通常使用用户定义函数（UDF）来弥合两个生态系统之间的差距。由于语言调试器和数据处理引擎之间需要协调，以及大量数据的调试开销，在数据处理系统中调试复杂的 UDF 具有挑战性。在本文中，我们展示了 Udon，这是一种新型调试器，支持数据处理系统中 UDF 的逐行调试。 Udon 封装了现代的逐行调试原语，例如在单个元组上执行 UDF 时设置断点、执行代码检查和进行代码修改的原语。在此演示中，我们使用真实场景来展示使用 Udon 逐行调试 UDF 的体验。

papers.title: Demonstration of Ver: View Discovery in the Wild.
papers.abstract: We demonstrate Ver¹ [10], a data discovery system that identifies project-join views over large repositories of tables that do not contain join path information. Ver solves both the technical (scale and search) and human (semantic ambiguity, navigating a large number of results) problems of view discovery.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们演示了 Ver1 [10]，这是一个数据发现系统，可识别不包含连接路径信息的大型表存储库上的项目连接视图。 Ver 解决了视图发现的技术（规模和搜索）和人类（语义模糊、导航大量结果）问题。

papers.title: Demystifying Data Management for Large Language Models.
papers.abstract: Navigating the intricacies of data management in the era of Large Language Models (LLMs) presents both challenges and opportunities for database and data management communities. In this tutorial, we offer a comprehensive exploration into the vital role of data management across the development and deployment phases of advanced LLMs. We provide an in-depth survey of existing techniques of managing knowledge and parameter data during the whole LLM lifecycle, emphasizing the balance between efficiency and effectiveness. This tutorial stands to offer participants valuable insights into the best practices and contemporary challenges in data management for LLMs, equipping them with the knowledge to navigate and contribute to this rapidly evolving field.
papers.abstract_zh: 在大型语言模型 (LLM) 时代应对错综复杂的数据管理，为数据库和数据管理社区带来了挑战和机遇。在本教程中，我们全面探讨了数据管理在高级法学硕士的开发和部署阶段的重要作用。我们对整个法学硕士生命周期中管理知识和参数数据的现有技术进行了深入调查，强调效率和效果之间的平衡。本教程旨在为参与者提供有关法学硕士数据管理的最佳实践和当代挑战的宝贵见解，为他们提供驾驭这个快速发展的领域并为其做出贡献的知识。

papers.title: Distributed Transaction Processing in Untrusted Environments.
papers.abstract: Byzantine Fault-Tolerant (BFT) protocols have recently been extensively used by distributed and decentralized data management systems with non-trustworthy infrastructures to establish consensus on the order of transactions. BFT protocols cover a broad spectrum of design dimensions from infrastructure settings, such as the communication topology, to more technical features, such as commitment strategy and even fundamental social choice properties like order-fairness. The proliferation of different protocols has made it difficult to navigate the BFT landscape, let alone determine the protocol that best meets application needs. In this tutorial, we discuss BFT protocols that are used in modern large-scale data management systems, present a design space consisting of a set of design dimensions and explore several design choices that capture the trade-offs between different design space dimensions. The presented design space and its design choices will help developers analyze BFT protocols, understand how different protocols are related to each other, and find the protocol that best fits their needs.
papers.abstract_zh: 拜占庭容错（BFT）协议最近被具有不可信基础设施的分布式和去中心化数据管理系统广泛使用，以建立对交易顺序的共识。 BFT 协议涵盖了广泛的设计维度，从基础设施设置（例如通信拓扑）到更多技术功能（例如承诺策略）甚至基本的社会选择属性（例如订单公平）。不同协议的激增使得浏览 BFT 领域变得困难，更不用说确定最能满足应用程序需求的协议了。在本教程中，我们讨论现代大规模数据管理系统中使用的 BFT 协议，提出由一组设计维度组成的设计空间，并探索几种设计选择，以捕获不同设计空间维度之间的权衡。所呈现的设计空间及其设计选择将帮助开发人员分析 BFT 协议，了解不同协议之间的相互关系，并找到最适合他们需求的协议。

papers.title: Eighth Workshop on Data Management for End-to-End Machine Learning (DEEM).
papers.abstract: The DEEM'24 workshop (Data Management for End-to-End Machine Learning) is held on Sunday June 9th, in conjunction with SIGMOD/PODS 2024. DEEM brings together researchers and practitioners at the intersection of applied machine learning, data management and systems research, with the goal to discuss the arising data management issues in ML application scenarios. The workshop solicits regular research papers (8 pages) describing preliminary and ongoing research results, including industrial experience reports of end-to-end ML deployments, related to DEEM topics. In addition, DEEM 2023 has a category for short papers (4 pages) as a forum for sharing interesting use cases, problems, datasets, benchmarks, visionary ideas, system designs, preliminary results, and descriptions of system components and tools related to end-to-end ML pipelines. This year, the workshop received 16 high-quality submissions on diverse topics relevant to DEEM, of which 8 regular papers and 8 short papers.
papers.abstract_zh: DEEM'24 研讨会（端到端机器学习的数据管理）于 6 月 9 日星期日与 SIGMOD/PODS 2024 一起举行。DEEM 汇集了应用机器学习、数据管理和系统研究交叉领域的研究人员和从业者，旨在讨论 ML 应用场景中出现的数据管理问题。该研讨会征集定期研究论文（8 页），描述初步和正在进行的研究成果，包括与 DEEM 主题相关的端到端 ML 部署的行业经验报告。此外，DEEM 2023 还有一个短论文类别（4 页）作为论坛，用于分享有趣的用例、问题、数据集、基准、远见卓识、系统设计、初步结果以及与端到端 ML 管道相关的系统组件和工具的描述。今年，研讨会收到了 16 篇与 DEEM 相关的不同主题的高质量论文，其中 8 篇常规论文和 8 篇短论文。

papers.title: Eighth Workshop on Human-In-the-Loop Data Analytics (HILDA).
papers.abstract: HILDA brings together researchers and practitioners to exchange ideas and results on human-data interaction. It explores how data management and analysis can be made more effective when taking into account the people who design and build these processes as well as those who are impacted by their results. Following the past two years, we plan to continue to focus on this year's workshop on early-stage research that is promising and exciting, which includes pairing each accepted paper with a mentor. The theme for this edition of the workshop is HILDA and Large Language Models. However, the workshop is not limited to this theme and other topics are also of interest. In this summary, we describe the workshop, its main focus areas, our review and mentorship plan as well as the keynote and panel sessions.
papers.abstract_zh: HILDA 将研究人员和从业者聚集在一起，交流有关人类数据交互的想法和结果。它探讨了在考虑设计和构建这些流程的人员以及受其结果影响的人员的情况下，如何使数据管理和分析更加有效。继过去两年之后，我们计划继续关注今年的早期研究研讨会，这些研究前景光明且令人兴奋，其中包括为每篇被接受的论文与导师配对。本期研讨会的主题是 HILDA 和大型语言模型。不过，研讨会并不局限于这个主题，其他话题也很有趣。在本摘要中，我们描述了研讨会、其主要重点领域、我们的审查和指导计划以及主题演讲和小组会议。

papers.title: ESTELLE: An Efficient and Cost-effective Cloud Log Engine.
papers.abstract: With the advancement of cloud computing, more and more enterprises are adopting cloud services to build a variety of applications. Monitoring and observability are integral to the complex and fragile cloud-native architecture. As an extremely important data source for both, logs play an indispensable role in applications such as code debugging, root cause analysis, troubleshooting, and trend analysis. However, the inherent characteristic of cloud logs, with TB-level daily data production per user and continuous growth over time and with business, poses core challenges for log engines. Traditional log management systems are inadequate for handling the requirements of massive log data high-frequency writing and storage, along with low-frequency retrieval and analysis in cloud environments. Exploring a low-cost, high-performance cloud-native log engine solution is an extremely extraordinary challenging task. To tackle these challenges, we propose a cost-effective cloud-native log engine, called ESTELLE, equipped with a low-cost pluggable log index framework. This engine features a compute-storage separation and read-write separation architecture, enabling linear scalability. We designed a near-lock-free writing process for handling high-frequency writing demands of massive logs. Object storage is used to significantly reduce storage costs. We also tailored ESTELLE Log Bloom filter and approximate inverted index for this cloud-native engine, applying them flexibly to enhance query efficiency and optimize various queries. Extensive experiments on real open-source log datasets have demonstrated that the ESTELLE Log Engine achieves ultra-high single-core CPU write speeds and pretty low storage costs. Furthermore, when equipped with the complete index framework, it also maintains fairly low query latency across various log scenarios.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着云计算的进步，越来越多的企业采用云服务来构建各种应用程序。监控和可观察性是复杂而脆弱的云原生架构不可或缺的一部分。日志作为两者极其重要的数据源，在代码调试、根本原因分析、故障排除、趋势分析等应用中发挥着不可或缺的作用。然而，云日志的固有特性，即每个用户每天产生的TB级数据，以及随着时间和业务的不断增长，对日志引擎提出了核心挑战。传统的日志管理系统无法满足云环境下海量日志数据高频写入和存储以及低频检索和分析的需求。探索低成本、高性能的云原生日志引擎解决方案是一项极具挑战性的任务。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一种经济高效的云原生日志引擎，称为 ESTELLE，配备了低成本的可插拔日志索引框架。该引擎采用计算存储分离和读写分离架构，可实现线性扩展。我们设计了近乎无锁的写入流程来应对海量日志的高频写入需求。使用对象存储可以显着降低存储成本。我们还为这个云原生引擎定制了ESTELLE Log Bloom过滤器和近似倒排索引，灵活应用它们来提高查询效率并优化各种查询。对真实开源日志数据集的大量实验表明，ESTELLE日志引擎实现了超高的单核CPU写入速度和相当低的存储成本。此外，当配备完整的索引框架时，它还可以在各种日志场景中保持相当低的查询延迟。

papers.title: Extending Polaris to Support Transactions.
papers.abstract: In Polaris [1], we introduced a cloud-native distributed query processor to perform analytics at scale. In this paper, we extend the underlying Polaris distributed computation framework, which can be thought of as a read-only transaction engine, to execute general transactions (including updates, deletes, inserts and bulk loads, in addition to queries) for Tier 1 warehousing workloads in a highly performant and predictable manner. We take advantage of the immutability of data files in log-structured data stores and build on SQL Server transaction management to deliver full transactional support with Snapshot Isolation semantics, including multi-table and multi-statement transactions. With the enhancements described in this paper, Polaris supports both query processing and transactions for T-SQL in Microsoft Fabric [2].
papers.abstract_zh: 在 Polaris [1] 中，我们引入了云原生分布式查询处理器来大规模执行分析。在本文中，我们扩展了底层的 Polaris 分布式计算框架（可以将其视为只读事务引擎），以高性能和可预测的方式为第 1 层仓储工作负载执行一般事务（除了查询之外，还包括更新、删除、插入和批量加载）。我们利用日志结构数据存储中数据文件的不变性，并构建 SQL Server 事务管理，通过快照隔离语义提供完整的事务支持，包括多表和多语句事务。通过本文中描述的增强功能，Polaris 支持 Microsoft Fabric [2] 中的 T-SQL 查询处理和事务。

papers.title: FinSQL: Model-Agnostic LLMs-based Text-to-SQL Framework for Financial Analysis.
papers.abstract: Text-to-SQL, which provides zero-code interface for operating relational databases, has gained much attention in financial analysis; because financial professionals may not be well-skilled in SQL programming. However, until now, there is no practical Text-to-SQL benchmark dataset for financial analysis, and existing Text-to-SQL methods have not considered the unique characteristics of databases in financial applications, such as commonly existing wide tables. To address these issues, we collect a practical Text-to-SQL benchmark dataset and propose a model-agnostic Large Language Model (LLMs)-based Text-to-SQL framework for financial analysis. The benchmark dataset, BULL, is collected from the practical financial analysis business of Hundsun Technologies Inc., including databases for fund, stock, and macro economy. Besides, the proposed LLMs-based Text-to-SQL framework, FinSQL, provides a systematic treatment for financial Text-to-SQL from the perspectives of prompt construction, parameter-efficient fine-tuning and output calibration. Experiments on BULL demonstrate that FinSQL achieves state-of-the-art performance at low cost, and it brings up to 36.64% improvement in few-shot cross-database scenarios.
papers.abstract_zh: Text-to-SQL提供操作关系数据库的零代码接口，在金融分析领域备受关注；因为金融专业人士可能不擅长 SQL 编程。然而，到目前为止，还没有用于金融分析的实用的Text-to-SQL基准数据集，并且现有的Text-to-SQL方法没有考虑金融应用中数据库的独特特征，例如常见的宽表。为了解决这些问题，我们收集了一个实用的文本到 SQL 基准数据集，并提出了一个与模型无关的基于大型语言模型 (LLM) 的文本到 SQL 财务分析框架。基准数据集BULL来自恒生电子实战金融分析业务，包括基金、股票、宏观经济数据库。此外，提出的基于LLM的Text-to-SQL框架FinSQL从提示构建、参数高效微调和输出校准的角度为金融Text-to-SQL提供了系统的处理。 BULL 上的实验表明，FinSQL 以低成本实现了最先进的性能，在少样本跨数据库场景中带来了高达 36.64% 的提升。

papers.title: First Workshop on Governance, Understanding and Integration of Data for Effective and Responsible AI (GUIDE-AI).
papers.abstract: With the recent advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), data-driven automated systems are being deployed in numerous high-stakes applications. Central to AI's effectiveness is its foundation in data. This workshop aims to bring together researchers from academia and industry to discuss the role of data management to guide the trustworthy design of AI-based applications. We plan the first edition of the workshop to include invited talks and a panel discussion with researchers from neighboring communities like ML, FAccT, HCI and Theoretical Computer science. The workshop aims to create a collaborative platform for these diverse communities to contribute to the evolving narrative of responsible AI development.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习 (ML) 的最新进展，数据驱动的自动化系统正在众多高风险应用中部署。人工智能有效性的核心是其数据基础。本次研讨会旨在聚集学术界和工业界的研究人员，讨论数据管理在指导基于人工智能的应用程序的可信设计方面的作用。我们计划第一届研讨会包括受邀演讲以及与来自邻近社区（例如 ML、FAccT、HCI 和理论计算机科学）的研究人员进行的小组讨论。该研讨会旨在为这些不同的社区创建一个协作平台，为负责任的人工智能开发的不断发展做出贡献。

papers.title: First Workshop on Quantum Computing and Quantum-Inspired Technology for Data-Intensive Systems and Applications (Q-Data).
papers.abstract:
Quantum computing has seen significant advancements with the emergence of commercial quantum hardware from major tech companies and startups. These developments, alongside new classical hardware accelerators inspired by quantum technologies, facilitate the rapid solving of complex optimization problems and recently spawned a flurry of research in various communities.

The primary objective of the Q-Data workshop is to explore how quantum computing and related technologies can enhance data processing, data management, data analysis systems, and techniques. It also focuses on hybrid approaches integrating quantum and classical computing methodologies to enhance such data systems and techniques. This workshop will spur new research efforts in this emerging field and pave the way for building next-generation data-intensive systems with quantum computing support.
papers.abstract_zh:
随着主要科技公司和初创公司商业量子硬件的出现，量子计算取得了显着进步。这些发展与受量子技术启发的新经典硬件加速器一起，促进了复杂优化问题的快速解决，并且最近在各个社区引发了一系列研究。

Q-Data 研讨会的主要目标是探索量子计算和相关技术如何增强数据处理、数据管理、数据分析系统和技术。它还专注于集成量子和经典计算方法的混合方法，以增强此类数据系统和技术。本次研讨会将刺激这一新兴领域的新研究工作，并为构建具有量子计算支持的下一代数据密集型系统铺平道路。

papers.title: Flux: Decoupled Auto-Scaling for Heterogeneous Query Workload in Alibaba AnalyticDB.
papers.abstract: Modern cloud data warehouses are integral to processing heterogeneous query workloads, which range from quick online transactions to intensive ad-hoc queries and extract, transform, load (ETL) processes. The synchronization of heterogeneous workloads, particularly the blend of short and long-running queries, often degrades performance due to intricate concurrency controls and cooperative multi-tasking execution models. Additionally, the auto-scaling mechanisms for mixed workloads can lead to spikes in demand and underutilized resources, impacting both performance and cost-efficiency. This paper introduces the Flux, a cloud-native workload auto-scaling platform designed for Alibaba AnalyticDB, which implements a pioneering decoupled auto-scaling architecture. By separating the scaling mechanisms for short and long-running queries, Flux not only resolves performance bottlenecks but also harnesses the elasticity of serverless container instances for on-demand resource provisioning. Our extensive evaluations demonstrate Flux's superiority over traditional scaling methods, with up to a 75% reduction in query response time (RT), a 19.0% increase in resource utilization ratio, and a 77.8% decrease in cost overhead.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代云数据仓库是处理异构查询工作负载不可或缺的一部分，这些工作负载的范围从快速在线事务到密集的临时查询以及提取、转换、加载 (ETL) 流程。由于复杂的并发控制和协作多任务执行模型，异构工作负载的同步，特别是短时间和长时间运行的查询的混合，通常会降低性能。此外，混合工作负载的自动扩展机制可能会导致需求激增和资源利用不足，从而影响性能和成本效率。本文介绍了Flux，这是一个为阿里AnalyticDB设计的云原生工作负载自动伸缩平台，它实现了首创的解耦自动伸缩架构。通过分离短查询和长时间查询的扩展机制，Flux 不仅解决了性能瓶颈，还利用无服务器容器实例的弹性来按需配置资源。我们的广泛评估证明了 Flux 相对于传统扩展方法的优越性，查询响应时间 (RT) 降低了 75%，资源利用率提高了 19.0%，成本开销降低了 77.8%。

papers.title: Fourth International Workshop on Big Data in Emergent Distributed Environments (BiDEDE).
papers.abstract: Emergent distributed environments going beyond cloud computing offer new kinds of processing for massive and heterogeneous data, but also pose new challenges for data management. These challenges include among others: load balancing, dynamic data allocation on computing nodes, fault tolerance, handling low quality data, data and processes replication, resource provisioning, buffer management, query processing and optimization on different types of hardware, transaction management, and minimization of power usage. The BiDEDE workshop addresses these and other challenges related to building post-cloud distributed environments for data processing.
papers.abstract_zh: 超越云计算的新兴分布式环境为海量异构数据提供了新的处理方式，但也给数据管理带来了新的挑战。这些挑战包括：负载平衡、计算节点上的动态数据分配、容错、处理低质量数据、数据和进程复制、资源配置、缓冲区管理、不同类型硬件上的查询处理和优化、事务管理以及功耗最小化。 BiDEDE 研讨会解决了与构建后云分布式数据处理环境相关的这些挑战和其他挑战。

papers.title: GRADES-NDA'24: 7th Joint Workshop on Graph Data Management Experiences & Systems (GRADES) and Network Data Analytics (NDA).
papers.abstract: GRADES-NDA is the premier workshop series on graph data management and analytics that aims to bring together researchers from academia, industry, and governmental organizations. GRADES-NDA'24 is a forum for discussing recent advances in (large-scale) graph data management and analytics systems, as well as proposing and discussing novel methods and techniques for addressing domain-specific challenges. In 2024, GRADES-NDA is in its seventh edition.
papers.abstract_zh: GRADES-NDA 是有关图形数据管理和分析的顶级研讨会系列，旨在汇集来自学术界、工业界和政府组织的研究人员。 GRADES-NDA'24 是一个讨论（大规模）图数据管理和分析系统最新进展的论坛，以及提出和讨论解决特定领域挑战的新方法和技术。 2024 年，GRADES-NDA 已进入第七版。

papers.title: GraphScope Flex: LEGO-like Graph Computing Stack.
papers.abstract: Graph computing has become increasingly crucial in processing large-scale graph data, with numerous systems developed for this purpose. Two years ago, we introduced GraphScope as a system addressing a wide array of graph computing needs, including graph traversal, analytics, and learning in one system. Since its inception, GraphScope has achieved significant technological advancements and gained widespread adoption across various industries. However, one key lesson from this journey has been understanding the limitations of a "one-size-fits-all" approach, especially when dealing with the diversity of programming interfaces, applications, and data storage formats in graph computing. In response to these challenges, we present GraphScope Flex, the next iteration of GraphScope. GraphScope Flex is designed to be both resource-efficient and cost-effective, while also providing flexibility and user-friendliness through its LEGO-like modularity. This paper explores the architectural innovations and fundamental design principles of GraphScope Flex, all of which are direct outcomes of the lessons learned during our ongoing development process. We validate the adaptability and efficiency of GraphScope Flex with extensive evaluations on synthetic and real-world datasets. The results show that GraphScope Flex achieves 2.4× throughput and up to 55.7× speedup over other systems on the LDBC Social Network and Graphalytics benchmarks, respectively. Furthermore, GraphScope Flex accomplishes up to a 2,400× performance gain in real-world applications, demonstrating its proficiency across a wide range of graph computing scenarios with increased effectiveness.
papers.abstract_zh: 图计算在处理大规模图数据方面变得越来越重要，为此开发了许多系统。两年前，我们推出了 GraphScope 作为一个系统，可以满足广泛的图计算需求，包括在一个系统中进行图遍历、分析和学习。自成立以来，GraphScope 取得了重大的技术进步，并在各个行业得到了广泛采用。然而，这一旅程的一个重要教训是了解“一刀切”方法的局限性，特别是在处理图计算中编程接口、应用程序和数据存储格式的多样性时。为了应对这些挑战，我们推出了 GraphScope Flex，这是 GraphScope 的下一代产品。 GraphScope Flex 的设计既具有资源效率又具有成本效益，同时还通过其类似乐高的模块化提供灵活性和用户友好性。本文探讨了 GraphScope Flex 的架构创新和基本设计原则，所有这些都是我们在持续开发过程中吸取的经验教训的直接结果。我们通过对合成数据集和真实数据集的广泛评估来验证 GraphScope Flex 的适应性和效率。结果表明，与其他系统相比，GraphScope Flex 在 LDBC 社交网络和 Graphalytics 基准测试中分别实现了 2.4 倍的吞吐量和高达 55.7 倍的加速。此外，GraphScope Flex 在实际应用中实现了高达 2,400 倍的性能提升，展示了其在各种图形计算场景中的熟练程度以及更高的效率。

papers.title: IDE: A System for Iterative Mislabel Detection.
papers.abstract:
While machine learning techniques, especially deep neural networks, have shown remarkable success in various applications, their performance is adversely affected by label errors in training data. Acquiring high-quality annotated data is both costly and time-consuming in real-world scenarios, requiring extensive human annotation and verification. Consequently, many industry-applied models are trained over data containing substantial noise, significantly degrading the performance of these models.

To address this critical issue, we demonstrate IDE, a novel system that iteratively detects mislabeled instances and repairs the wrong labels. Specifically, IDE leverages the early loss observation and influence-based verification to iteratively identify mislabeled instances. When the mislabeled instances are obtained in each iteration, IDE will repair their labels to enhance detection accuracy for subsequent iterations. The framework automatically determines the termination point when the early loss is no longer effective. For uncertain instances, it generates pseudo labels to train a binary classification model, leveraging the model's generalization ability to make the final decision. With a real-life scenario, we demonstrate that IDE produces high-quality training data by effective mislabel detection and repair.
papers.abstract_zh:
虽然机器学习技术，特别是深度神经网络，在各种应用中取得了显着的成功，但它们的性能受到训练数据中标签错误的不利影响。在现实场景中，获取高质量的注释数据既昂贵又耗时，需要大量的人工注释和验证。因此，许多行业应用的模型都是在包含大量噪声的数据上进行训练的，从而显着降低了这些模型的性能。

为了解决这个关键问题，我们演示了 IDE，这是一种新颖的系统，可以迭代地检测错误标记的实例并修复错误的标签。具体来说，IDE 利用早期丢失观察和基于影响的验证来迭代识别错误标记的实例。当每次迭代中获得错误标记的实例时，IDE将修复其标签，以提高后续迭代的检测准确性。当早期损失不再有效时，框架会自动确定终止点。对于不确定的情况，它生成伪标签来训练二元分类模型，利用模型的泛化能力做出最终决策。通过现实场景，我们证明 IDE 通过有效的错误标签检测和修复来生成高质量的训练数据。

papers.title: IMBridge: Impedance Mismatch Mitigation between Database Engine and Prediction Query Execution.
papers.abstract: Prediction queries that apply machine learning (ML) models to perform analysis on data stored in the database are prevalent with the advance of research. Thanks to the prosperity of ML frameworks in Python, current database systems introduce Python UDFs into query engines for inference invocation. However, there are impedance mismatches between database engines and prediction query execution with this approach. In particular, the database engine is oblivious to the semantics within prediction functions, which incurs the repetitive inference context setup. Moreover, the evaluation of the prediction function is coupled with the operator, which results in an undesirable inference batch size with low inference throughput. To mitigate these, we propose a system called IMBridge, which leverages aprediction function rewriter to eliminate redundant inference context setup and introduces adecoupled prediction operator to ensure that the evaluation batch size matches the desirable inference batch size. In this demonstration, we will showcase how IMBridge addresses these mismatches and boosts prediction query execution.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着研究的进步，应用机器学习 (ML) 模型对数据库中存储的数据进行分析的预测查询越来越普遍。由于Python中ML框架的繁荣，当前的数据库系统将Python UDF引入查询引擎中进行推理调用。然而，使用这种方法，数据库引擎和预测查询执行之间存在阻抗不匹配。特别是，数据库引擎忽略了预测函数内的语义，这会导致重复的推理上下文设置。此外，预测函数的评估与算子耦合，这会导致推理批量大小不理想且推理吞吐量较低。为了缓解这些问题，我们提出了一个名为 IMBridge 的系统，它利用预测函数重写器来消除冗余的推理上下文设置，并引入解耦预测运算符以确保评估批量大小与所需的推理批量大小相匹配。在此演示中，我们将展示 IMBridge 如何解决这些不匹配问题并提高预测查询执行速度。

papers.title: Intelligent Scaling in Amazon Redshift.
papers.abstract: Cloud-based data warehouses are built to be easy to use, requiring minimal intervention from customers as their workloads scale. However, there are still many dimensions of a workload that they do not scale with automatically. For example, in cloud-managed clusters, large ad-hoc queries and ETL workloads must use the same cluster size provisioned for the rest of the workload, and warehouse size does not automatically grow as the underlying data grows in size, causing queries to slow down. In this paper, we describe RAIS, the latest collection of AI-powered scaling and optimization techniques in Amazon Redshift, released in preview at re:Invent 2023, which enable it to scale both vertically and horizontally to adapt to all types of workload variability. RAIS dynamically provisions compute resources to run heavy queries efficiently and automatically optimizes warehouse size for the customer's workload, even as it shifts over time. We show that, depending on the workload, RAIS improves either cost or average query execution time by up to 7.6× and 14.2×, respectively, over existing baselines.
papers.abstract_zh: 基于云的数据仓库易于使用，随着工作负载的扩展，客户只需极少的干预。然而，工作负载的许多维度仍然无法自动扩展。例如，在云管理集群中，大型即席查询和 ETL 工作负载必须使用为其余工作负载配置的相同集群大小，并且仓库大小不会随着底层数据大小的增长而自动增长，从而导致查询速度变慢。在本文中，我们介绍了 RAIS，它是 Amazon Redshift 中最新的人工智能驱动的扩展和优化技术集合，在 re:Invent 2023 上发布了预览版，使其能够垂直和水平扩展以适应所有类型的工作负载变化。 RAIS 动态配置计算资源以高效运行繁重的查询，并根据客户的工作负载自动优化仓库大小，即使它随时间变化也是如此。我们表明，根据工作负载，RAIS 比现有基准分别将成本或平均查询执行时间提高了 7.6 倍和 14.2 倍。

papers.title: International Workshop on Data Management on New Hardware (DaMoN).
papers.abstract: New hardware, such as multi-core CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, new memory and storage technologies, low-power devices, bring new challenges and opportunities in optimizing database systems performance. Consequently, exploiting the characteristics of modern hardware has become an important topic of database systems research. In the last two decades, the DaMoN Workshop has established itself as the primary database venue to present ideas on how to exploit new hardware for data management, in particular how to improve performance or scalability of databases, how new hardware unlocks new database application scenarios, and how data management could benefit from future hardware.
papers.abstract_zh: 新的硬件，如多核CPU、GPU、FPGA、新的内存和存储技术、低功耗设备，给优化数据库系统性能带来了新的挑战和机遇。因此，利用现代硬件的特性已成为数据库系统研究的一个重要课题。在过去的二十年中，DaMoN Workshop 已成为主要的数据库场所，提出如何利用新硬件进行数据管理的想法，特别是如何提高数据库的性能或可扩展性、新硬件如何解锁新的数据库应用场景以及数据管理如何从未来的硬件中受益。

papers.title: Learned Query Optimizer: What is New and What is Next.
papers.abstract: In recent times, learned query optimizer has becoming a hot research topic in learned databases. It serves as the most suitable experimental plots for utilizing numerous machine-learning techniques and exhibits its superiority with enough evidence. In this tutorial, we aim to provide a wide and deep review and analysis on this field, ranging from theory to practice.
