At first, we would categorize and introduce representative methods for each learned component in the query optimizer, as well as for the end-to-end learned query optimizer. Then, we describe some benchmark evaluations and prototype applications. Their results have exhibited the bright future of applying learned query optimizers in practice. Based on them, we describe a cutting edge system with step-by-step guidelines. It is a middleware proposed recently to reduce the difficulties of developing and deploying learned algorithms in databases. It would help researchers to iterate their work and make learned query optimizers truly applicable in production. Finally, we summarize and point out several future directions. We hope this tutorial could inspire and guide both researchers and engineers working on learned query optimizers, as well as other contexts in learned databases.
papers.abstract_zh: 近年来，学习查询优化器已成为学习数据库的研究热点。它是最适合利用多种机器学习技术的实验田，并以足够的证据展示了其优越性。在本教程中，我们的目标是从理论到实践对该领域进行广泛而深入的回顾和分析。首先，我们将对查询优化器中的每个学习组件以及端到端学习查询优化器进行分类并介绍代表性方法。然后，我们描述一些基准评估和原型应用。他们的结果展示了在实践中应用学习的查询优化器的光明前景。基于它们，我们描述了一个带有分步指南的尖端系统。它是最近提出的一个中间件，旨在减少在数据库中开发和部署学习算法的难度。它将帮助研究人员迭代他们的工作，并使学习的查询优化器真正适用于生产。最后，我们总结并指出了未来的几个方向。我们希望本教程能够启发和指导研究学习查询优化器以及学习数据库中其他上下文的研究人员和工程师。

papers.title: LETUS: A Log-Structured Efficient Trusted Universal BlockChain Storage.
papers.abstract: With the evolution of Web3.0 and decentralized applications (Dapps), increasing business logic is running on the blockchain. Blockchain storage, as the core infrastructure supporting the increasing volume of data, plays a crucial role. However, the performance and cost of blockchain storages are suffering seriously, which inspires us to re-examine the design of blockchain storage based on the blockchain data characteristics. We propose LETUS, a Log-structured Efficient Trusted Universal Storage for blockchain, providing cryptographic tamper evidence with excellent performance and resource efficiency. (1) LETUS breaks the traditional two-layered architecture and pushes down the Authenticated Data Structure (ADS) into the storage engine to enable fine-grained I/O optimizations. (2) LETUS proposes DMM-Tree which is a novel ADS combining the functionalities of Merkle tree and delta-encoding, significantly reducing storage consumption. (3) LETUS adopts a version-based indexing schema and manages the large volume of pages generated by ADS in a page store indexed by a B-tree variant. (4) LETUS provides a universal solution for different blockchains, such as public blockchains like Ethereum, BNB Smart Chain and AntChain as a representation of consortium blockchains. LETUS has been deployed in AntChain commercial applications, such as NFT and digital torch ignition for 2023 Asian Games. Experimental results also show that with LETUS, AntChain can achieve up to 15.8× improvement in throughput and 80.3% storage cost saving, Ethereum can achieve up to 10.1× improvement in throughput and 75.0% storage cost saving.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着Web3.0和去中心化应用程序（Dapps）的发展，越来越多的业务逻辑在区块链上运行。区块链存储作为支撑不断增长的数据量的核心基础设施，发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，区块链存储的性能和成本受到严重影响，这促使我们根据区块链数据的特点重新审视区块链存储的设计。我们提出 LETUS，一种用于区块链的日志结构的高效可信通用存储，以优异的性能和资源效率提供密码篡改证据。 （1）LETUS打破传统的两层架构，将认证数据结构（ADS）下推到存储引擎中，实现细粒度的I/O优化。 (2) LETUS提出DMM-Tree，这是一种结合了Merkle树和delta编码功能的新型ADS，显着减少了存储消耗。 (3)LETUS采用基于版本的索引模式，并在由B树变体索引的页面存储中管理ADS生成的大量页面。 （4）LETUS为不同区块链提供通用的解决方案，例如以太坊、BNB智能链、以蚂蚁链为代表的联盟链等公有链。 LETUS已部署在蚂蚁链商业应用中，例如NFT、2023年亚运会数字火炬点火等。实验结果还表明，通过 LETUS，蚂蚁链可以实现高达 15.8 倍的吞吐量提升和 80.3% 的存储成本节省，以太坊可以实现高达 10.1 倍的吞吐量提升和 75.0% 的存储成本节省。

papers.title: Machine Learning for Databases: Foundations, Paradigms, and Open problems.
papers.abstract: This tutorial delves into the burgeoning field of Machine Learning for Databases (ML4DB), highlighting its recent progress and the challenges impeding its integration into industrial-grade database management systems. We systematically explore three key themes: the exploration of foundations in ML4DB and their potential for diverse applications, the two paradigms in ML4DB, i.e., using machine learning as a replacement versus enhancement of traditional database components, and the critical open challenges such as improving model efficiency and addressing data shifts. Through an in-depth analysis, including a survey of recent works in major database conferences, this tutorial encapsulates the current state of ML4DB, as well as charts a roadmap for its future development and wider adoption in practical database environments.
papers.abstract_zh: 本教程深入探讨了新兴的数据库机器学习 (ML4DB) 领域，重点介绍了其最新进展以及阻碍其集成到工业级数据库管理系统中的挑战。我们系统地探讨了三个关键主题：探索 ML4DB 的基础及其在不同应用中的潜力、ML4DB 中的两种范式，即使用机器学习作为传统数据库组件的替代与增强，以及提高模型效率和解决数据转移等关键的开放挑战。通过深入分析，包括对主要数据库会议近期工作的调查，本教程概括了 ML4DB 的现状，并为其未来的发展和在实际数据库环境中更广泛采用绘制了路线图。

papers.title: Making Data Management Better with Vectorized Query Processing.
papers.abstract: Vectorized query processing is a query processing technique that 20 years ago was introduced in the Vectorwise database system, originally developed in the CWI database architectures research group. Nowadays, it is employed by most analytical data systems, from recent systems like DuckDB, Clickhouse, Velox, DataFusion and Polars to analytical cloud services such as Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks and MotherDuck. The keynote will tell the story of vectorized query processing, as it evolved in research and industry over the past two decades. It will conclude with a reflection on the purpose of our research ("making data management better").
papers.abstract_zh: 矢量化查询处理是 20 年前在 Vectorwise 数据库系统中引入的查询处理技术，最初是由 CWI 数据库架构研究小组开发的。如今，大多数分析数据系统都采用它，从 DuckDB、Clickhouse、Velox、DataFusion 和 Polars 等最新系统到 Snowflake、BigQuery、Databricks 和 MotherDuck 等分析云服务。主题演讲将讲述矢量化查询处理在过去二十年在研究和行业中的发展历程。最后将反思我们的研究目的（“让数据管理变得更好”）。

papers.title: Measures in SQL.
papers.abstract:
SQL has attained widespread adoption, but Business Intelligence tools still use their own higher level languages based upon a multidimensional paradigm. Composable calculations are what is missing from SQL, and we propose a new kind of column, called a measure, that attaches a calculation to a table. Like regular tables, tables with measures are composable and closed when used in queries.

SQL-with-measures has the power, conciseness and reusability of multidimensional languages but retains SQL semantics. Measure invocations can be expanded in place to simple, clear SQL.

To define the evaluation semantics for measures, we introduce context-sensitive expressions (a way to evaluate multidimensional expressions that is consistent with existing SQL semantics), a concept called evaluation context, and several operations for setting and modifying the evaluation context.
papers.abstract_zh:
SQL 已得到广泛采用，但商业智能工具仍然使用自己的基于多维范式的高级语言。可组合计算是 SQL 中所缺少的，我们提出了一种新的列，称为度量，它将计算附加到表中。与常规表一样，具有度量的表在查询中使用时是可组合和关闭的。

SQL-with-measures 具有多维语言的强大、简洁和可重用性，但保留了 SQL 语义。测量调用可以就地扩展为简单、清晰的 SQL。

为了定义度量的求值语义，我们引入了上下文相关表达式（一种与现有 SQL 语义一致的求值多维表达式的方法）、一个称为求值上下文的概念以及用于设置和修改求值上下文的几个操作。

papers.title: MillenniumDB: A Multi-modal, Multi-model Graph Database.
papers.abstract:
Current knowledge graphs encompass diverse data formats, including images, text, tables, audio files, and videos. Additionally, the graph database ecosystem is required to support multiple co-existing data models. Addressing these challenges is essential for promoting interoperability between data sources. This demo introduces MillenniumDB, a high-performing, open-source graph database handling this diversity of data formats and models.

MillenniumDB is a multi-modal, multi-model graph database, supporting the popular property graph paradigm, the Semantic Web format RDF, and the multi-layered graph model, which combines and extends the two. In terms of querying, its provides support for a Cypher-like language over property graphs and multilayered graphs, as well as SPARQL 1.1 support over RDF. The engine is build on a solid theoretical foundation and it leverages worst-case optimal join algorithms in combination with traditional relational query optimization. It also support a wide array of graph-specific tasks such as path finding, pattern recognition, and similarity search on multi-modal data. In this demo, we will showcase how MillenniumDB is currently being used to host three public multi-modal knowledge graphs. The first one, a multi-layered graph called TelarKG, was developed at IMFD Chile to track the information about the Chilean constitutional reform. In the second one, called BibKG, we integrate information about Computer Science publications from different sources and make them available as a property graph. Finally, for RDF, we provide a SPARQL endpoint for Wikidata, the largest knowledge graph openly available online. We remark that our endpoints have stable links, allowing the audience to post queries using their Web browser with no restrictions, and will be available during the review process and during the demo.
papers.abstract_zh:
当前的知识图谱包含多种数据格式，包括图像、文本、表格、音频文件和视频。此外，图数据库生态系统需要支持多种共存的数据模型。解决这些挑战对于促进数据源之间的互操作性至关重要。该演示介绍了 MillenniumDB，这是一个高性能的开源图形数据库，可处理多种数据格式和模型。

MillenniumDB是一个多模态、多模型的图数据库，支持流行的属性图范式、语义Web格式RDF和多层图模型，将两者结合和扩展。在查询方面，它提供了对属性图和多层图的类 Cypher 语言的支持，以及对 RDF 的 SPARQL 1.1 支持。该引擎建立在坚实的理论基础之上，它利用最坏情况的最佳连接算法与传统的关系查询优化相结合。它还支持各种特定于图的任务，例如多模态数据的路径查找、模式识别和相似性搜索。在此演示中，我们将展示 MillenniumDB 目前如何用于托管三个公共多模式知识图。第一个是名为 TelarKG 的多层图表，由国际货币基金组织智利公司开发，用于跟踪有关智利宪法改革的信息。在第二个名为 BibKG 的项目中，我们整合了来自不同来源的计算机科学出版物的信息，并将它们作为属性图提供。最后，对于 RDF，我们为 Wikidata（最大的在线公开知识图谱）提供了 SPARQL 端点。我们指出，我们的端点具有稳定的链接，允许观众使用其 Web 浏览器不受限制地发布查询，并且在审核过程和演示期间将可用。

papers.title: Multi-Backend Zonal Statistics Execution with Raven.
papers.abstract: The recent explosion in the number and size of spatial remote sensing datasets from satellite missions creates new opportunities for data-driven approaches in domains such as climate change monitoring and disaster management. These approaches typically involve a feature engineering step that summarizes remote sensing pixel data located within zones of interest defined by another spatial dataset, an operation called zonal statistics. Although several spatial systems support zonal statistics operations, they differ significantly in terms of interfaces, architectures, and algorithms, making it hard for users to select the best system for a specific workload. To address this limitation, we propose Raven, a zonal statistics framework that provides users with a unified interface across multiple execution backends, while facilitating easy benchmarking and comparisons across systems. This demonstration showcases Raven 's multi-backend execution environment, domain-specific declarative language, optimization techniques, and benchmarking capabilities.
papers.abstract_zh: 最近，卫星任务空间遥感数据集的数量和规模呈爆炸式增长，为气候变化监测和灾害管理等领域的数据驱动方法创造了新的机遇。这些方法通常涉及特征工程步骤，该步骤总结位于由另一个空间数据集定义的感兴趣区域内的遥感像素数据，这种操作称为区域统计。尽管多个空间系统支持分区统计操作，但它们在接口、架构和算法方面存在显着差异，使得用户很难为特定工作负载选择最佳系统。为了解决这个限制，我们提出了 Raven，一个区域统计框架，为用户提供跨多个执行后端的统一界面，同时促进跨系统的轻松基准测试和比较。该演示展示了 Raven 的多后端执行环境、特定领域的声明性语言、优化技术和基准测试功能。

papers.title: Native Cloud Object Storage in Db2 Warehouse: Implementing a Fast and Cost-Efficient Cloud Storage Architecture.
papers.abstract: Database systems built on traditional storage subsystems typically store their data in small blocks referred to as data pages (commonly sized in a multiple of 4KB for historical reasons). These traditional storage subsystems, for example network attached block storage, were designed for efficient random-access I/O patterns at the block level, and the block size is usually configurable by the application based on its needs. For large scale analytic databases in cloud environments, these traditional storage subsystems are not cost effective when compared to cloud object storage, and database systems that exploit them risk becoming uncompetitive. This paper describes the modernization of the storage architecture of Db2 Warehouse, a traditional full feature and high-performance database system with 3 decades of development, to exploit the new paradigm of cost-effective storage for the cloud. We discuss a solution based on the integration of LSM trees as part of the storage subsystem, that enables Db2 Warehouse to efficiently store data pages within object storage, and through the application of special techniques to minimize read and write latencies as well as all of the amplification factors (write, read, and storage), achieve not only storage cost savings, but also higher performance. Further, by retaining the traditional data page format, we are able to avoid significantly re-architecting the database kernel and thereby retain the substantial capabilities and optimizations of the existing system.
papers.abstract_zh: 基于传统存储子系统构建的数据库系统通常将数据存储在称为数据页的小块中（由于历史原因，大小通常为 4KB 的倍数）。这些传统的存储子系统，例如网络附加块存储，是为块级别的高效随机访问 I/O 模式而设计的，并且块大小通常可由应用程序根据其需要进行配置。对于云环境中的大规模分析数据库，与云对象存储相比，这些传统存储子系统的成本效益不高，并且利用它们的数据库系统面临失去竞争力的风险。本文介绍了 Db2 Warehouse（经过 3 年发展的传统全功能高性能数据库系统）存储架构的现代化，以开发经济高效的云存储新范式。我们讨论一种基于将 LSM 树集成为存储子系统一部分的解决方案，该解决方案使 Db2 Warehouse 能够在对象存储中高效存储数据页，并通过应用特殊技术来最大限度地减少读写延迟以及所有放大因素（写入、读取和存储），不仅实现存储成本节省，而且实现更高的性能。此外，通过保留传统的数据页格式，我们能够避免对数据库内核进行重大的重新架构，从而保留现有系统的大量功能和优化。

papers.title: NPA: Improving Large-scale Graph Neural Networks with Non-parametric Attention.
papers.abstract: Recent works show great interest in designing Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) that scale to large graphs. While previous work focuses on designing advanced sampling techniques for existing GNNs, the design of non-parametric GNNs, an orthogonal direction for scalable performance, has aroused lots of concerns recently. For example, nearly all top solutions in the Open Graph Benchmark leaderboard are non-parametric GNNs. Despite their high predictive performance and scalability, non-parametric GNNs still face two limitations. First, due to the propagation of over-smoothed features, they suffer from severe performance degradation along with the propagation depth. More importantly, they only consider the graph structure and ignore the feature influence during the non-parametric propagation, leading to sub-optimal propagated features. To address these limitations, we present non-parametric attention (NPA), a plug-and-play module that is compatible with non-parametric GNNs, to get scalable and deep GNNs simultaneously. We have deployed NPA in Tencent with the Angel platform, and we further evaluate NPA on both real-world datasets and large-scale industrial datasets. Experimental results on seven homophilic graphs (including the industrial Tencent Video graph) and five heterophilic graphs demonstrate NPA enjoys high performance -- achieves large performance gain over existing non-parametric GNNs, deeper architecture -- improves non-parametric GNNs with large model depth, and high scalability -- can support large-scale graphs with low time costs. Notably, it achieves state-of-the-art performance on the large ogbn-papers100M dataset.
papers.abstract_zh: 最近的工作对设计可扩展到大型图的图神经网络（GNN）表现出极大的兴趣。虽然之前的工作重点是为现有 GNN 设计先进的采样技术，但非参数 GNN 的设计（可扩展性能的正交方向）最近引起了很多关注。例如，Open Graph Benchmark 排行榜中几乎所有排名靠前的解决方案都是非参数 GNN。尽管非参数 GNN 具有很高的预测性能和可扩展性，但它仍然面临两个限制。首先，由于过度平滑特征的传播，它们的性能随着传播深度的增加而严重下降。更重要的是，他们只考虑图结构，忽略非参数传播过程中的特征影响，导致传播特征次优。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了非参数注意力（NPA），这是一种与非参数 GNN 兼容的即插即用模块，可以同时获得可扩展的深度 GNN。我们通过Angel平台在腾讯部署了NPA，并在现实数据集和大规模工业数据集上进一步评估NPA。在七个同亲图（包括工业腾讯视频图）和五个异亲图上的实验结果表明，NPA具有高性能——比现有的非参数GNN获得了很大的性能增益，更深的架构——改进了具有大模型深度的非参数GNN，并且具有高可扩展性——可以以较低的时间成本支持大规模图。值得注意的是，它在大型 ogbn-papers100M 数据集上实现了最先进的性能。

papers.title: OpenIVM: a SQL-to-SQL Compiler for Incremental Computations.
papers.abstract:
This demonstration presents a new Open Source SQL-to-SQL compiler for Incremental View Maintenance (IVM). While previous systems, such as DBToaster, implemented computational functionality for IVM in a separate system, the core principle of OpenIVM is to make use of existing SQL query processing engines and perform all IVM computations via SQL. This approach enables the integration of IVM in these systems without code duplication. Also, it eases its use in cross-system IVM, i.e. to orchestrate an HTAP system in which one (OLTP) DBMS provides insertions/updates/deletes (deltas), which are propagated using SQL into another (OLAP) DBMS, hosting materialized views. Our system compiles view definitions into SQL to eventually propagate deltas into the table that materializes the view, following the principles of DBSP.

Under the hood, OpenIVM uses the DuckDB library to compile (parse, transform, optimize) the materialized view maintenance logic. We demonstrate OpenIVM in action (i) as the core of a DuckDB extension module that adds IVM functionality to it and (ii) powering cross-system IVM for HTAP, with PostgreSQL handling updates on base tables and DuckDB hosting materialized views on these.
papers.abstract_zh:
该演示展示了一个用于增量视图维护 (IVM) 的新开源 SQL-to-SQL 编译器。虽然以前的系统（例如 DBToaster）在单独的系统中实现了 IVM 的计算功能，但 OpenIVM 的核心原理是利用现有的 SQL 查询处理引擎并通过 SQL 执行所有 IVM 计算。这种方法可以将 IVM 集成到这些系统中，而无需重复代码。此外，它还简化了其在跨系统 IVM 中的使用，即编排 HTAP 系统，其中一个 (OLTP) DBMS 提供插入/更新/删除（增量），这些插入/更新/删除（增量）使用 SQL 传播到另一个托管物化视图的 (OLAP) DBMS。我们的系统将视图定义编译为 SQL，最终将增量传播到具体化视图的表中，遵循 DBSP 的原则。

在底层，OpenIVM 使用 DuckDB 库来编译（解析、转换、优化）物化视图维护逻辑。我们演示了 OpenIVM 的实际应用：(i) 作为 DuckDB 扩展模块的核心，向其添加 IVM 功能；(ii) 为 HTAP 提供跨系统 IVM，其中 PostgreSQL 处理基表更新，DuckDB 托管这些表的物化视图。

papers.title: PG-Triggers: Triggers for Property Graphs.
papers.abstract:
Graph databases are emerging as the leading data management technology for storing large knowledge graphs; significant efforts are ongoing to produce new standards (such as the Graph Query Language, GQL), as well as enrich them with properties, types, schemas, and keys. In this article, we introduce PG-Triggers, a complete proposal for adding triggers to Property Graphs, along the direction marked by the SQL3 Standard.

We define the syntax and semantics of PG-Triggers and then illustrate how they can be implemented on top of Neo4j, one of the most popular graph databases. In particular, we introduce a syntax-directed translation from PG-Triggers into Neo4j, which makes use of the so-called APOC triggers; APOC is a community-contributed library for augmenting the Cypher query language supported by Neo4j. We also cover Memgraph, and show that our approach applies to this system in a similar way. We illustrate the use of PG-Triggers through a life science application inspired by the COVID-19 pandemic.

The main objective of this article is to introduce an active database standard for graph databases as a first-class citizen at a time when reactive graph management is in its infancy, so as to minimize the conversion efforts towards a full-fledged standard proposal.
papers.abstract_zh:
图数据库正在成为存储大型知识图的领先数据管理技术；我们正在付出巨大的努力来制定新标准（例如图形查询语言，GQL），并通过属性、类型、模式和键来丰富它们。在本文中，我们沿着 SQL3 标准标记的方向，介绍 PG-Triggers，这是一个向属性图添加触发器的完整提案。

我们定义 PG-Triggers 的语法和语义，然后说明如何在最流行的图形数据库之一 Neo4j 之上实现它们。特别是，我们引入了从 PG-Triggers 到 Neo4j 的语法定向转换，它利用了所谓的 APOC 触发器； APOC 是一个社区贡献的库，用于增强 Neo4j 支持的 Cypher 查询语言。我们还介绍了 Memgraph，并表明我们的方法以类似的方式适用于该系统。我们通过受 COVID-19 大流行启发的生命科学应用来说明 PG-Triggers 的使用。

本文的主要目标是在反应式图管理还处于起步阶段时，为图数据库引入一个主动数据库标准，作为一等公民，从而最大限度地减少向成熟标准提案的转换工作。

papers.title: PLUTUS: Understanding Data Distribution Tailoring for Machine Learning.
papers.abstract: Existing data debugging tools allow users to trace model performance problems all the way to the data by efficiently identifying slices (conjunctions of features and values) for which a trained model performs significantly worse than the entire dataset. To ensure accurate and fair models, one solution is to acquire enough data for these slices. In addition to crowdsourcing, recent data acquisition techniques design cost-effective algorithms to obtain such data from a union of external sources such as data lakes and data markets. We demonstrate PLUTUS, a tool for human-in-the-loop and model-aware data acquisition pipeline, on SystemDS, as an open source ML system for the end-to-end data science lifecycle. In PLUTUS, a user can efficiently identify problematic slices, connect to external data sources, and acquire the right amount of data for these slices in a cost-effective manner.
papers.abstract_zh: 现有的数据调试工具允许用户通过有效地识别训练模型的性能明显低于整个数据集的切片（特征和值的结合）来跟踪模型性能问题一直到数据。为了确保模型的准确性和公平性，一种解决方案是为这些切片获取足够的数据。除了众包之外，最近的数据获取技术还设计了具有成本效益的算法，以从数据湖和数据市场等外部来源的联合中获取此类数据。我们在 SystemDS 上演示了 PLUTUS，这是一种用于人机交互和模型感知数据采集管道的工具，作为端到端数据科学生命周期的开源机器学习系统。在 PLUTUS 中，用户可以有效地识别有问题的切片，连接到外部数据源，并以经济高效的方式获取这些切片的适量数据。

papers.title: PolarDB-MP: A Multi-Primary Cloud-Native Database via Disaggregated Shared Memory.
papers.abstract:
Primary-secondary databases often have limited write throughput as they rely on a single primary node. To improve this, some systems use a shared-nothing architecture for scalable multi-primary clusters. However, these face performance issues due to distributed transaction overheads. Recently, shared-storage-based multi-primary cloud-native databases have emerged to avoid these issues, but they still struggle with performance in high-conflict scenarios, often due to expensive conflict resolution and inefficient data fusion.

This paper proposes PolarDB-MP, an innovative multi-primary cloud-native database that leverages both disaggregated shared memory and storage. In PolarDB-MP, each node has equal access to all data, enabling transactions to be processed on individual nodes without the need for distributed transactions. At the core of PolarDB-MP is the Polar Multi-Primary Fusion Server (PMFS), built on disaggregated shared memory. PMFS plays a critical role in facilitating global transaction coordination and enhancing buffer fusion, seamlessly integrated with RDMA for minimal latency. Its three main functionalities include Transaction Fusion for transaction ordering and visibility, Buffer Fusion providing a distributed shared buffer, and Lock Fusion for cross-node concurrency control. Moreover, PolarDB-MP introduces an LLSN design, establishing a partial order for write-ahead logs generated across different nodes, accompanied by a tailored recovery policy. Our evaluations of PolarDB- MP demonstrate its superior performance when compared to the state-of-the-art solutions. Notably, PolarDB-MP is already in production and undergoing commercial trials at Alibaba Cloud. To our knowledge, PolarDB-MP is the first multi-primary cloud-native database that utilizes disaggregated shared memory and shared storage for transaction coordination and buffer fusion.
papers.abstract_zh:
主从数据库通常具有有限的写入吞吐量，因为它们依赖于单个主节点。为了改善这一点，一些系统使用无共享架构来实现可扩展的多主集群。然而，由于分布式事务开销，这些面临着性能问题。最近，基于共享存储的多主云原生数据库的出现可以避免这些问题，但它们在高冲突场景中的性能仍然存在问题，这通常是由于昂贵的冲突解决和低效的数据融合造成的。

本文提出了 PolarDB-MP，这是一种利用分解共享内存和存储的创新多主云原生数据库。在PolarDB-MP中，每个节点对所有数据具有平等的访问权限，使得事务可以在各个节点上处理，而不需要分布式事务。 PolarDB-MP 的核心是 Polar 多主融合服务器 (PMFS)，它构建在分解共享内存上。 PMFS 在促进全局事务协调和增强缓冲区融合方面发挥着关键作用，与 RDMA 无缝集成以实现最小延迟。它的三个主要功能包括用于事务排序和可见性的Transaction Fusion、提供分布式共享缓冲区的Buffer Fusion以及用于跨节点并发控制的Lock Fusion。此外，PolarDB-MP引入了LLSN设计，为跨节点生成的预写日志建立偏序，并辅以定制的恢复策略。我们对 PolarDB-MP 的评估表明，与最先进的解决方案相比，它具有卓越的性能。值得注意的是，PolarDB-MP已经在阿里云投入生产并进行商用试验。据我们所知，PolarDB-MP是第一个利用分解共享内存和共享存储进行事务协调和缓冲区融合的多主云原生数据库。

papers.title: Predicate Caching: Query-Driven Secondary Indexing for Cloud Data Warehouses.
papers.abstract:
Cloud data warehouses are today's standard for analytical query processing. Multiple cloud vendors offer state-of-the-art systems, such as Amazon Redshift. We have observed that customer workloads experience highly repetitive query patterns, i.e., users and systems frequently send the same queries. In order to improve query performance on these queries, most systems rely on techniques like result caches or materialized views.

However, these caches are often stale due to inserts, deletes, or updates that occur between query repetitions. We propose a novel secondary index, predicate caching, to improve query latency for repeating scans and joins. Predicate caching stores ranges of qualifying tuples of base table scans. Such an index can be built on the fly, is lightweight, and can be kept online without recomputation.

We implemented a prototype of this idea in the cloud data warehouse Amazon Redshift. Our evaluation shows that predicate caching improves query runtimes by up to 10x on selected queries with negligible build overhead.
papers.abstract_zh:
云数据仓库是当今分析查询处理的标准。多家云供应商提供最先进的系统，例如 Amazon Redshift。我们观察到客户工作负载经历高度重复的查询模式，即用户和系统频繁发送相同的查询。为了提高这些查询的查询性能，大多数系统依赖于结果缓存或物化视图等技术。

然而，由于查询重复之间发生的插入、删除或更新，这些缓存通常会过时。我们提出了一种新颖的二级索引，即谓词缓存，以改善重复扫描和连接的查询延迟。谓词缓存存储基表扫描的合格元组的范围。这样的索引可以即时构建，是轻量级的，并且可以在线保存而无需重新计算。

我们在云数据仓库 Amazon Redshift 中实现了这个想法的原型。我们的评估表明，谓词缓存可以将选定查询的查询运行时间提高多达 10 倍，而构建开销可以忽略不计。

papers.title: Proactive Resume and Pause of Resources for Microsoft Azure SQL Database Serverless.
papers.abstract: Demand-driven resource allocation for cloud databases has become a popular research direction. Recent approaches have evolved from reactive policies to proactive decision making. These approaches leverage not only the current resource demand but also the predicted demand to make more informed resource allocation decisions for each database and thus improve the quality of service and reduce the operational costs. We present an infrastructure that enables proactive resource allocation capabilities for millions of serverless Azure SQL databases. Our solution finds near-optimal middle ground between high availability of resources, low operational costs, and low computational overhead of the proactive policy. We describe the design principles we followed and the architectural decisions we made during this cross-team, multi-year journey. Given the size and scope of our solution, we believe that the relational cloud databases in other companies could benefit from the proactive resource allocation capabilities.
papers.abstract_zh: 云数据库需求驱动的资源分配已成为热门研究方向。最近的方法已经从被动政策演变为主动决策。这些方法不仅利用当前的资源需求，还利用预测的需求，为每个数据库做出更明智的资源分配决策，从而提高服务质量并降低运营成本。我们提供了一个基础架构，可为数百万个无服务器 Azure SQL 数据库提供主动资源分配功能。我们的解决方案在资源的高可用性、低运营成本和主动策略的低计算开销之间找到了近乎最佳的中间立场。我们描述了我们在这个跨团队、多年的旅程中遵循的设计原则以及我们做出的架构决策。考虑到我们解决方案的规模和范围，我们相信其他公司的关系云数据库可以从主动资源分配功能中受益。

papers.title: Property Graph Stream Processing In Action with Seraph.
papers.abstract: Given the growing popularity of graphs, data models like Property Graph and corresponding query languages like Cypher, have become popular in industry and research. However, real-time graph data analysis and management are increasingly important for today's businesses. Still, graph query languages lack the features to handle streaming graph data and their continuous query evaluation. % This demonstration showcases Seraph, a Cypher-based language supporting native continuous querying features. Seraph is formally defined, declarative, and backwards compatible with Cypher. This demonstration gives a comprehensive overview of the language with three use cases and a carefully design Web UI, allowing the execution of continuous queries on the property graphs stream.
papers.abstract_zh: 鉴于图的日益普及，Property Graph 等数据模型和 Cypher 等相应的查询语言已在工业和研究领域变得流行。然而，实时图形数据分析和管理对于当今的企业来说越来越重要。尽管如此，图查询语言仍然缺乏处理流图数据及其连续查询评估的功能。 % 该演示展示了 Seraph，一种基于 Cypher 的语言，支持本机连续查询功能。 Seraph 是正式定义的、声明性的，并且与 Cypher 向后兼容。该演示通过三个用例和精心设计的 Web UI 全面概述了该语言，允许在属性图流上执行连续查询。

papers.title: Querying Graph Databases at Scale.
papers.abstract: The tutorial provides an in-depth overview of recent advances in algorithms and data structures for processing graph database queries. The focus will be on scalable algorithms that have been demonstrated to work over real world knowledge graphs. We will also present detailed performance comparisons of classical and recent algorithms. The tutorial will be divided into four sections. The first section will motivate the use of graph databases for querying knowledge graphs, and will introduce the attendees to graph data models and the query language landscape. The second section will discuss how to efficiently evaluate graph patterns, introducing the worst-case optimal join techniques and comparing them to classical join algorithms. The third section will discuss techniques for efficiently evaluating path queries and for constructing compact representations of potentially exponential sets of paths. In the final section we will introduce recent advances in compressed data structures that ease the high memory requirements of worst-case optimal join algorithms and also provide a template for evaluating path queries in a highly optimised manner.
papers.abstract_zh: 本教程深入概述了用于处理图数据库查询的算法和数据结构的最新进展。重点将放在已被证明可以在现实世界知识图谱上工作的可扩展算法。我们还将展示经典算法和最新算法的详细性能比较。本教程将分为四个部分。第一部分将促进使用图数据库来查询知识图，并向与会者介绍图数据模型和查询语言环境。第二部分将讨论如何有效地评估图模式，介绍最坏情况的最佳连接技术并将其与经典连接算法进行比较。第三部分将讨论有效评估路径查询和构建潜在指数路径集的紧凑表示的技术。在最后一节中，我们将介绍压缩数据结构的最新进展，这些进展可以缓解最坏情况最优连接算法的高内存需求，并提供用于以高度优化的方式评估路径查询的模板。

papers.title: QueryShield: Cryptographically Secure Analytics in the Cloud.
papers.abstract:
We present a demonstration of QueryShield, a service for streamlined, cryptographically secure data analytics in the cloud. With QueryShield, data analysts can advertise analysis descriptions to data owners, who may agree to participate in a computation for profit or for the greater good, provided that their data remain private. QueryShield supports relational and time series analytics with provable data privacy guarantees using secure multi-party computation (MPC). At the same time, it makes MPC accessible to non-expert users by offering a familiar web interface and fully-automated orchestration of cryptographic computations.

We devise three demonstration scenarios for conference attendees: (i) an interactive survey of private employment information to estimate the industry-academia wage gap in the data management community, (ii) a relational analysis that identifies credit score anomalies in sensitive customer data from multiple credit agencies, and (iii) a medical use case that assesses the effectiveness of insulin dose frequency in a patient cohort.
papers.abstract_zh:
我们演示了 QueryShield，这是一项用于在云中进行简化、加密安全数据分析的服务。借助 QueryShield，数据分析师可以向数据所有者宣传分析描述，数据所有者可能同意参与计算以获取利润或更大的利益，前提是他们的数据保持私密。 QueryShield 支持关系和时间序列分析，并使用安全多方计算 (MPC) 提供可证明的数据隐私保证。同时，它通过提供熟悉的 Web 界面和全自动的加密计算编排，使非专家用户也可以访问 MPC。

我们为与会者设计了三种演示场景：（i）对私人就业信息进行交互式调查，以估计数据管理界的行业与学术界工资差距；（ii）进行关系分析，识别来自多个信用机构的敏感客户数据中的信用评分异常；以及（iii）评估患者队列中胰岛素剂量频率有效性的医疗用例。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2023 Paper: 'MorphStream: Adaptive Scheduling for Scalable Transactional Stream Processing on Multicores'.
papers.abstract: We were able to reproduce the findings of the original paper. This includes performance comparisons with other systems, measurements of throughput and latency for different workloads and scheduling strategies, handling of abort decisions, and scalability comparisons. In total, this involves sixteen different figures in the original paper that were reproduced.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们能够重现原始论文的研究结果。这包括与其他系统的性能比较、不同工作负载和调度策略的吞吐量和延迟测量、中止决策的处理以及可扩展性比较。总共，这涉及复制的原始论文中的十六个不同的数字。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'A Unified Approach for Resilience and Causal Responsibility with Integer Linear Programming (ILP) and LP Relaxations'.
papers.abstract: We successfully reproduced the experimental results presented in the original paper. The authors provided clear documentation of the software and hardware requirements, along with well-structured scripts and datasets hosted in an organized GitHub repository. In this reproducibility report, we compare the original results with our reproduced findings, both of which show similar trends, thereby supporting the authors' claims.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们成功地重现了原始论文中提出的实验结果。作者提供了有关软件和硬件要求的清晰文档，以及托管在有组织的 GitHub 存储库中的结构良好的脚本和数据集。在这份再现性报告中，我们将原始结果与再现的结果进行了比较，两者都显示出相似的趋势，从而支持了作者的主张。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'ALP: Adaptive Lossless Floating-Point Compression'.
papers.abstract: This report describes the reproducibility evaluation carried out for the research paper ''ALP: Adaptive Lossless Floating-Point Compression'', authored by Azim Afroozeh, Leonardo Kuffó, and Peter Boncz from the Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica (CWI), located in Amsterdam, Netherlands. The paper, presented at SIGMOD 2024, introduces ALP, a novel adaptive lossless compression algorithm for floating-point datasets. The results of the reproducibility evaluation confirm the core contributions of the original paper, showing that ALP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art techniques in both compression ratio and (de)compression speed.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告介绍了对研究论文“ALP：自适应无损浮点压缩”进行的可重复性评估，该论文由荷兰阿姆斯特丹 Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica (CWI) 的 Azim Afroozeh、Leonardo Kuffó 和 Peter Boncz 撰写。该论文在 SIGMOD 2024 上发表，介绍了 ALP，一种用于浮点数据集的新型自适应无损压缩算法。再现性评估的结果证实了原始论文的核心贡献，表明 ALP 在压缩比和（解）压缩速度方面始终优于最先进的技术。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'CAFE: Towards Compact, Adaptive, and Fast Embedding for Large-scale Recommendation Models'.
papers.abstract: We have been able to reproduce the original paper's findings partially. The authors provided the documentation with clear instructions on reproducing each experiment, from pre-processing to training. While most of the code can be run without any modifications, some scripts require manual changes to select particular datasets. Moreover, some experiments failed with an error or were not reproduced due to hardware limitations. All experiments with avazu and criteo datasets were reproduced. Additionally, HotSketch performance tests were successfully repeated.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们已经能够部分重现原始论文的发现。作者为文档提供了关于重现每个实验（从预处理到训练）的清晰说明。虽然大多数代码无需任何修改即可运行，但某些脚本需要手动更改才能选择特定数据集。此外，一些实验因错误而失败或由于硬件限制而无法重现。再现了 avazu 和 criteo 数据集的所有实验。此外，HotSketch 性能测试也已成功重复。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Can Learned Indexes be Built Efficiently? A Deep Dive into Sampling Trade-offs'.
papers.abstract: In general, most of the main experiments in the paper ''Can Learned Indexes be Built Efficiently? A Deep Dive into Sampling Trade-offs'' can be successfully reproduced.
papers.abstract_zh: 总的来说，论文《Can Learned Indexes be Efficiently》中的大部分主要实验都是这样的。可以成功地重现“深入研究采样权衡”。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Dias: Dynamic Rewriting of Pandas Code'.
papers.abstract: We successfully replicated all of the original paper's findings, including key performance results, as detailed in the experimental section. The authors provided data, scripts, and plotting tools to recreate key figures.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们成功地复制了原始论文的所有发现，包括关键性能结果，如实验部分所述。作者提供了数据、脚本和绘图工具来重新创建关键人物。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'DProvDB: Differentially Private Query Processing with Multi-Analyst Provenance'.
papers.abstract: The paper [1] introduces DProvDB, a fine-grained privacy provenance framework. Our reproducibility evaluation shows that the key findings of the paper have been successfully reproduced, with minor discrepancies from the original figures explained by inherent randomness.
papers.abstract_zh: 论文[1]介绍了DProvDB，一种细粒度的隐私溯源框架。我们的再现性评估表明，论文的主要发现已成功再现，与原始数据存在细微差异，这是由固有的随机性造成的。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'FACET: Robust Counterfactual Explanation Analytics'.
papers.abstract: We are able to faithfully reproduce the original paper's findings as well as the key results reported in its experimental section (i.e., explanation quality, speed, robustness). The authors provided example data, comprehensive scripts, and plotting functions that allowed near-identical reconstruction of all of the paper's figures.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们能够忠实地重现原始论文的发现以及实验部分报告的关键结果（即解释质量、速度、稳健性）。作者提供了示例数据、综合脚本和绘图函数，可以对论文中的所有图形进行近乎相同的重建。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Fast Maximal Quasi-clique Enumeration: A Pruning and Branching Co-Design Approach'.
papers.abstract: The core thesis of the paper has been successfully reproduced. The authors provided a set of scripts that can execute the experiments and generate the individual plots. The resulting figures were near identical to the submitted paper.
papers.abstract_zh: 论文核心论文已成功转载。作者提供了一组可以执行实验并生成单独图的脚本。得出的数据与提交的论文几乎相同。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Faster Algorithms for Fair Max-Min Diversification in Rd'.
papers.abstract: This report evaluates the reproducibility of the paper titled ''Faster Algorithms for Fair Max-Min Diversification in R d ''. The paper's artifacts were available and evaluatable. Reproducibility was straightforward. The paper's core claims were reproduced, with minor variations in a few graphs.
papers.abstract_zh: 本报告评估了题为“R d 中公平最大最小多样化的更快算法”的论文的可重复性。该论文的成果是可用且可评估的。再现性很简单。该论文的核心主张被复制，仅在一些图表中略有变化。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Grafite: Taming Adversarial Queries with Optimal Range Filters'.
papers.abstract: We have successfully reproduced the evaluation results in the paper. We appreciate that the authors have prepared detailed documents and automation scripts for reproducibility. Our reproduced results show similar trends and relationships between the alternatives compared and support the scientific contributions and claims of the original paper.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们成功地重现了论文中的评估结果。我们感谢作者准备了详细的文档和自动化脚本以实现可重复性。我们复制的结果显示了比较的替代方案之间相似的趋势和关系，并支持原始论文的科学贡献和主张。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Hierarchical Cut Labelling - Scaling Up Distance Queries on Road Networks'.
papers.abstract: The core claims from the paper are reproducible. The authors provided a repository with the code needed to run the program, instructions on how to build and run, and links to the datasets to be used in the experiments.
papers.abstract_zh: 论文的核心主张是可重复的。作者提供了一个存储库，其中包含运行程序所需的代码、如何构建和运行的说明以及实验中使用的数据集的链接。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'NOCAP: Near-Optimal Correlation-Aware Partitioning Joins'.
papers.abstract: We have successfully replicated the key performance results of the paper depicted in Figure 8, despite not having access to the same experimental hardware. We thank the authors for providing resources for verifying the experiments and assisting with setup and debugging.
papers.abstract_zh: 尽管无法访问相同的实验硬件，但我们已经成功复制了图 8 中所示论文的关键性能结果。我们感谢作者提供资源来验证实验并协助设置和调试。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'On Efficient Large Sparse Matrix Chain Multiplication'.
papers.abstract: All major findings of the paper were reproduced. Unfortunately, due to privacy concerns, the authors could not share their code on GitHub.
papers.abstract_zh: 论文的所有主要发现均被转载。不幸的是，出于隐私考虑，作者无法在 GitHub 上分享他们的代码。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'On The Reasonable Effectiveness of Relational Diagrams'.
papers.abstract: The paper [1] introduces Relational Diagrams, a visual representation of relational queries, and presents two experimental studies: a textbook analysis and a user study. The authors made all necessary files and scripts available for reproducibility through an Open Science Framework project. During the reproducibility process, a minor error was identified in the analysis of the textbook queries, though it does not affect the main conclusions of the paper. The figures reproduced for the user study are nearly identical to the ones presented in the paper. Overall, all major findings of the paper were successfully reproduced.
papers.abstract_zh: 论文 [1] 介绍了关系图（关系查询的可视化表示），并提出了两项​​实验研究：教科书分析和用户研究。作者通过开放科学框架项目提供了所有必要的文件和脚本，以实现可重复性。在重现性过程中，在对教科书查询的分析中发现了一个小错误，但这并不影响论文的主要结论。用户研究中复制的数据与论文中提供的数据几乎相同。总的来说，论文的所有主要发现都被成功重现。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Optimizing Distributed Protocols with Query Rewrites'.
papers.abstract: The authors provided detailed descriptions for setting up the re- quired system and performing experiments using individual scripts provided. While the experiments required considerable effort and time, the detailed instructions facilitated evaluating the results and generating graphs that reproduced the same patterns claimed by the authors in the paper Optimizing Distributed Protocols with Query Rewrites
papers.abstract_zh: 作者提供了设置所需系统和使用提供的单独脚本进行实验的详细描述。虽然实验需要大量的精力和时间，但详细的说明有助于评估结果并生成图表，这些图表再现了作者在论文《使用查询重写优化分布式协议》中声称的相同模式

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'PECJ: Stream Window Join on Disorder Data Streams with Proactive Error Compensation'.
papers.abstract: This paper proposes a new solution to handle the issues posed by out-of-order arrival of data when computing a stream window join (SWJ). The authors propose PECJ, a system which proactively manages disordered data streams by relying not just on already arrived data but also factoring in late-arriving data when updating the results of the join. The authors present a set of experiments comparing the efficacy of PECJ to state-of-the-art methods on a range of stream oscillation patterns. We find that on the hardware specified we were able to reproduce the results and plots shown in the paper, illustrating the robust nature of these experiments. Furthermore, the authors provided well-written scripts and easy-to-follow instructions to reproduce this material.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新的解决方案来处理计算流窗口连接（SWJ）时数据无序到达所带来的问题。作者提出了 PECJ，这是一种主动管理无序数据流的系统，不仅依赖已到达的数据，而且在更新连接结果时还考虑迟到的数据。作者提出了一组实验，比较了 PECJ 与最先进的方法在一系列水流振荡模式上的功效。我们发现，在指定的硬件上，我们能够重现论文中显示的结果和图表，说明这些实验的稳健性。此外，作者还提供了编写良好的脚本和易于遵循的说明来复制此材料。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'PimPam: Efficient Graph Pattern Matching on Real Processing-in-Memory Hardware'.
papers.abstract: This is a reproducibility report for the paper ''PimPam: Efficient Graph Pattern Matching on Real Processing-in-Memory Hardware'' published in SIGMOD 2024. The paper proposes an efficient graph pattern matching framework that utilizes the real Processing-in-Memory hardware UPMEM. The main evaluation results of the paper are successfully reproduced. They demonstrate the better performance of PimPam over conventional systems.
papers.abstract_zh: 这是 SIGMOD 2024 上发表的论文“PimPam：在真实内存处理硬件上的高效图形模式匹配”的可重复性报告。该论文提出了一种利用真实内存处理硬件 UPMEM 的高效图形模式匹配框架。论文主要评价结果成功复现。他们展示了 PimPam 比传统系统更好的性能。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'PreVision: An Out-of-Core Matrix Computation System with Optimal Buffer Replacement'.
papers.abstract: We report our experience in reproducing the benchmarks from the paper "PreVision: An Out-of-Core Matrix Computation System with Optimal Buffer Replacement"[1]. While we successfully reproduced only a portion of the benchmarks from [1], we consider the current results to be sufficient to validate the core thesis of the paper.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们报告了我们在论文“PreVision: An Out-of-Core Matrix Computation System with Optimal Buffer Replacement”[1] 中重现基准的经验。虽然我们仅成功地重现了[1]中的部分基准，但我们认为当前的结果足以验证本文的核心论文。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Proving Query Equivalence Using Linear Integer Arithmetic'.
papers.abstract: The reproducibility evaluation successfully replicated key functionalities of SQLSolver. During the review process, we found a discrepancy in the verification speed regarding SQLSolver , i.e., SQLSolver 's reproduced verification latency is much higher than the result in the paper. Because after the paper was published, the authors of the paper further added some optimizations to SQLSolver , which aim at enhancing the verification capabilities and can help prove 4 more cases. However, these optimizations affect the speed of verification. After the authors of the paper provided the original version, we can successfully reproduce the main results.
papers.abstract_zh: 再现性评估成功复制了 SQLSolver 的关键功能。在审核过程中，我们发现 SQLSolver 的验证速度存在差异，即 SQLSolver 的重现验证延迟远高于论文中的结果。因为论文发表后，论文作者进一步对 SQLSolver 进行了一些优化，旨在增强验证能力，可以帮助证明另外 4 个案例。然而，这些优化会影响验证速度。在论文作者提供原始版本后，我们可以成功重现主要结果。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Proximity Queries on Point Clouds using Rapid Construction Path Oracle'.
papers.abstract: The reproducibility evaluation successfully replicated key functionalities of Rapid Construction path Oracle, i.e., RC-Oracle. During the review process, apart from the missing gnuplot installation instruction that was initially pointed out by the reviewer (and updated by the author), the instructions were straightforward, and the process was smooth. The authors provided datasets, codes, experiment scripts, plotting scripts and a README file that allowed the reproduction of the main results.
papers.abstract_zh: 再现性评估成功复制了快速构建路径 Oracle（即 RC-Oracle）的关键功能。在审稿过程中，除了审稿人最初指出（并由作者更新）缺少gnuplot安装说明外，说明很简单，过程也很顺利。作者提供了数据集、代码、实验脚本、绘图脚本和自述文件，可以重现主要结果。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Query Refinement for Diverse Top-k Selection'.
papers.abstract: The experimental evaluation of the paper has been successfully reproduced. The reproduced plots are similar to those in the paper, supporting the claims made by the authors. The provided scripts and instructions simplify the reproducibility process, but running all experiments takes multiple days.
papers.abstract_zh: 论文的实验评估已成功复现。复制的情节与论文中的情节相似，支持作者的主张。提供的脚本和说明简化了重现过程，但运行所有实验需要几天时间。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'StarfishDB: A Query Execution Engine for Relational Probabilistic Programming'.
papers.abstract: The core thesis of this paper could easily be reproduced in the original environment. The authors provided an almost fully automated set of scripts that automatically execute the experiments. The experimental data was automatically collected and plotted. The resulting figures have some minor deviations from the originals, but the overall trends remain the same, and hence they can be considered a good fit.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文的核心论文可以很容易地在原始环境中复制。作者提供了一组几乎完全自动化的脚本来自动执行实验。自动收集并绘制实验数据。所得数据与原始数据有一些微小偏差，但总体趋势保持不变，因此可以认为它们是很好的拟合。

papers.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2024 Paper: 'Temporal JSON Keyword Search'.
papers.abstract: This is the reproducibility report for the paper ''Temporal JSON Keyword Search'' by Dyreson et al. The code the authors provided is available on GitHub https://github.com/cdyreson/temporalHierarchicalKeywordSearch. We tested the code's correctness, data availability, and the reproducibility of the results. In particular, we aim to replicate the two key findings of the evaluation: the cost of the temporal functionality and the additional overhead of sequenced search compared to the consequences version. Generally, the results are consistent with the original paper. Overall, the reproduced results align closely with those reported in the paper, demonstrating that the study is reproducible.
papers.abstract_zh: 这是 Dyreson 等人的论文“Temporal JSON Keyword Search”的重现性报告。作者提供的代码可在 GitHub https://github.com/cdyreson/temporalHierarchicalKeywordSearch 上找到。我们测试了代码的正确性、数据可用性和结果的可重复性。特别是，我们的目标是复制评估的两个关键发现：时间功能的成本以及与结果版本相比的排序搜索的额外开销。总体来说，结果与原论文一致。总体而言，重现的结果与论文中报告的结果非常一致，表明该研究是可重现的。

papers.title: Responsible Model Selection with Virny and VirnyView.
papers.abstract: In this demonstration, we present a comprehensive software library for model auditing and responsible model selection, called Virny, along with an interactive tool called VirnyView. Our library is modular and extensible, it implements a rich set of performance and fairness metrics, including novel metrics that quantify and compare model stability and uncertainty, and enables performance analysis based on multiple sensitive attributes, and their intersections. The Virny library and the VirnyView tool are available at https://github.com/DataResponsibly/Virny and https://r-ai.co/VirnyView.
papers.abstract_zh: 在本次演示中，我们展示了一个名为 Virny 的用于模型审核和负责任模型选择的综合软件库，以及一个名为 VirnyView 的交互式工具。我们的库是模块化和可扩展的，它实现了一组丰富的性能和公平性指标，包括量化和比较模型稳定性和不确定性的新颖指标，并支持基于多个敏感属性及其交集的性能分析。 Virny 库和 VirnyView 工具可在 https://github.com/DataResponsbilly/Virny 和 https://r-ai.co/VirnyView 上获取。

papers.title: Responsible Sharing of Spatiotemporal Data.
papers.abstract: There is a growing need for responsible spatiotemporal data sharing in our daily lives. Applications such as connected vehicles and mobile advertising are currently undergoing a significant digital shift, demanding new standards for privacy-aware solutions and the integration of machine learning technologies. In this tutorial, we present the concepts and challenges encountered when maximizing the utility of spatiotemporal data while enforcing rigorous privacy and security measures. We review modern data sharing mechanisms that provide stakeholders with the power to establish precise terms for the usage and sharing of their data, secured by a robust data infrastructure. We will explore how such sharing mechanisms interplay with complex privacy stipulations and advanced spatiotemporal analytics. Attendees will leave with a comprehensive understanding of how to navigate the delicate balance of spatiotemporal data usage, paving the way for innovation in privacy and compliance methodologies across various industries.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们的日常生活中对负责任的时空数据共享的需求日益增长。联网车辆和移动广告等应用目前正在经历重大的数字化转变，需要隐私意识解决方案和机器学习技术集成的新标准。在本教程中，我们介绍了在执行严格的隐私和安全措施的同时最大化时空数据的效用时遇到的概念和挑战。我们回顾了现代数据共享机制，这些机制为利益相关者提供了建立精确数据使用和共享条款的权力，并由强大的数据基础设施保障。我们将探讨这种共享机制如何与复杂的隐私规定和先进的时空分析相互作用。与会者将全面了解如何驾驭时空数据使用的微妙平衡，为各行业隐私和合规方法的创新铺平道路。

papers.title: RobOpt: A Tool for Robust Workload Optimization Based on Uncertainty-Aware Machine Learning.
papers.abstract: Relational database management systems (RDBMSs) employ query optimizers to search for execution plans deemed optimal for specific queries. Classical optimizers rely on inaccurate parameter estimates and assumptions that may not hold true in real-world scenarios. Consequently, suboptimal execution plans may be chosen, leading to poor query execution performance. Recent proposals of learned query optimizers that leverage Machine Learning suffer also from the selection of suboptimal plans. In order to fill this gap, we have created Robust Workload Optimization (RobOpt), a prototype tool that facilitates robust execution of a query workload in RDBMSs. It implements a novel technique that takes workload logs as input, generates training samples, and trains a risk-aware learned cost model. It optimizes risk-aware plan selection strategies to achieve a desired level of runtime performance and robustness. In addition, it analyzes a workload according to its training samples and determines an optimal plan selection strategy either at the workload or query level. Ultimately, it enables the robust execution of any workload by determining an optimal plan selection strategy per query. RobOpt can work on top of any RDBMS.
papers.abstract_zh: 关系数据库管理系统 (RDBMS) 使用查询优化器来搜索对于特定查询而言最佳的执行计划。经典优化器依赖于不准确的参数估计和假设，这些估计和假设在现实场景中可能不成立。因此，可能会选择次优的执行计划，从而导致查询执行性能不佳。最近提出的利用机器学习的学习查询优化器也受到次优计划选择的影响。为了填补这一空白，我们创建了鲁棒工作负载优化 (RobOpt)，这是一个原型工具，可促进 RDBMS 中查询工作负载的鲁棒执行。它实现了一种新技术，将工作负载日志作为输入，生成训练样本，并训练具有风险意识的学习成本模型。它优化风险意识计划选择策略，以实现所需的运行时性能和稳健性水平。此外，它还根据训练样本分析工作负载，并在工作负载或查询级别确定最佳计划选择策略。最终，它通过确定每个查询的最佳计划选择策略来实现任何工作负载的稳健执行。 RobOpt 可以在任何 RDBMS 之上工作。

papers.title: Rock: Cleaning Data by Embedding ML in Logic Rules.
papers.abstract: We introduce Rock, a system for cleaning relational data. Rock implements a framework that unifies machine learning (ML) and logic deduction by embedding ML classifiers in rules as predicates. In a unified process, it identifies tuples that refer to the same real-world entity, catches semantic inconsistencies among the entities, deduces the timeliness of the attribute values of the entities, and imputes missing values by possibly extracting data from knowledge graphs. That is, Rock conducts entity resolution, conflict resolution, incomplete information imputation and timeliness deduction in the same process, makes use of their interactions and improves the overall quality of the data. Moreover, Rock supports methods, batch and incremental, for discovering rules from real-life data, detecting errors with the learned rules, accumulating ground truth, and fixing the errors, such that the corrections are logical consequences of the rules and ground truth. We present the design and implementation of Rock. We evaluate the scalability and accuracy of Rock, and share lessons learned from a variety of real-life applications.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们介绍Rock，一个用于清理关系数据的系统。 Rock 通过将 ML 分类器作为谓词嵌入规则中，实现了一个统一机器学习 (ML) 和逻辑演绎的框架。在统一的过程中，它识别引用同一现实世界实体的元组，捕获实体之间的语义不一致，推断实体属性值的时效性，并通过可能从知识图谱中提取数据来估算缺失值。即Rock在同一过程中进行实体解析、冲突解析、不完全信息插补和时效性推导，利用它们的相互作用，提高数据的整体质量。此外，Rock 支持批量和增量方法，用于从现实数据中发现规则，用学习的规则检测错误，积累基本事实，并修复错误，以便更正是规则和基本事实的逻辑结果。我们介绍 Rock 的设计和实现。我们评估 Rock 的可扩展性和准确性，并分享从各种实际应用程序中吸取的经验教训。

papers.title: Sawmill: From Logs to Causal Diagnosis of Large Systems.
papers.abstract: Causal analysis is an essential lens for understanding complex system dynamics in domains as varied as medicine, economics and law. Computer systems are often similarly complex, but much of the information about them is only available in long, messy, semi-structured log files. This demo presents Sawmill, an open-source system that makes it possible to extract causal conclusions from log files. Sawmill employs methods drawn from the areas of data transformation, cleaning, and extraction in order to transform logs into a representation amenable to causal analysis. It gives log-derived variables human-understandable names and distills the information present in a log file around a user's chosen causal units (e.g. users or machines), generating appropriate aggregated variables for each causal unit. It then leverages original algorithms to efficiently use this representation for the novel process of Exploration-based Causal Discovery - the task of constructing a sufficient causal model of the system from available data. Users can engage with this process via an interactive interface, ultimately making causal inference possible using off-the-shelf tools. SIGMOD'24 participants will be able to use Sawmill to efficiently answer causal questions about logs. We will guide attendees through the process of quantifying the impact of parameter tuning on query latency using real-world PostgreSQL server logs, before letting them test Sawmill on additional logs with known causal effects but varying difficulty. A companion video for this submission is available online.
papers.abstract_zh: 因果分析是理解医学、经济学和法律等不同领域的复杂系统动力学的重要视角。计算机系统通常也同样复杂，但有关它们的大部分信息只能在长而杂乱的半结构化日志文件中获得。该演示展示了 Sawmill，这是一个开源系统，可以从日志文件中提取因果结论。 Sawmill 采用数据转换、清理和提取领域的方法，将日志转换为适合因果分析的表示形式。它为日志派生变量提供人类可理解的名称，并围绕用户选择的因果单元（例如用户或机器）提取日志文件中存在的信息，为每个因果单元生成适当的聚合变量。然后，它利用原始算法有效地将这种表示形式用于基于探索的因果发现的新过程 - 从可用数据构建系统的足够因果模型的任务。用户可以通过交互式界面参与此过程，最终使使用现成工具进行因果推理成为可能。 SIGMOD'24 参与者将能够使用 Sawmill 有效回答有关日志的因果问题。我们将指导与会者使用真实的 PostgreSQL 服务器日志量化参数调整对查询延迟的影响，然后让他们在具有已知因果影响但难度不同的其他日志上测试 Sawmill。本次提交的配套视频可在线观看。

papers.title: Second Workshop on Simplicity in Management of Data (SiMoD).
papers.abstract: At a first glance, data systems today are complex, with various components, tuning knobs and delicate design decisions. However, at their core - as researchers and practitioners have been observing at least anecdotally - are simple ideas that work well in practice. Simple ideas are easier to understand, less prone to bugs and more likely to be implemented by real products. Keeping individual design points simple also helps reduce system complexity and improve maintainability. However, this does not mean ignoring the necessary complexity or tricky corner cases. Meanwhile, it also often takes a tremendous amount of experience to propose such ideas. SiMoD promotes simple but non-trivial and effective ideas, by gathering researchers and practitioners to share their perspectives, new ideas, and experience on building practical data systems.
papers.abstract_zh: 乍一看，当今的数据系统非常复杂，具有各种组件、调节旋钮和微妙的设计决策。然而，正如研究人员和实践者至少在轶事上观察到的那样，其核心是在实践中运作良好的简单想法。简单的想法更容易理解，不易出现错误，并且更有可能被实际产品实现。保持各个设计点简单也有助于降低系统复杂性并提高可维护性。然而，这并不意味着忽略必要的复杂性或棘手的极端情况。同时，提出这样的想法往往也需要大量的经验。 SiMoD 通过聚集研究人员和实践者分享他们在构建实用数据系统方面的观点、新想法和经验，提倡简单但不平凡且有效的想法。

papers.title: Seventh International Workshop on Exploiting Artificial Intelligence Techniques for Data Management (aiDM).
papers.abstract: Recent advances in AI techniques, as well as enabling hardware and infrastructure, have led to the integration of AI across wide-ranging domains and tasks. In particular, AI has been used to handle various types of data (including numerical, textual and image data) and has been adopted in large-scale distributed systems. From a data management perspective, this calls for the harnessing of state-of-the-art AI solutions for data management tasks and systems. aiDM is a full-day workshop that offers a stage for innovative interdisciplinary research that studies the interaction between AI and data management and develops new AI technologies for data-related tasks. This year, aiDM'24 particularly focuses on the transparent exploitation of AI techniques (e.g., using Generative AI frameworks) for data management for enterprise class workloads.
papers.abstract_zh: 人工智能技术以及支持硬件和基础设施的最新进展导致人工智能在广泛的领域和任务中的集成。特别是，人工智能已被用于处理各种类型的数据（包括数字、文本和图像数据），并已在大规模分布式系统中得到采用。从数据管理的角度来看，这需要利用最先进的人工智能解决方案来执行数据管理任务和系统。 aiDM 是一个全天的研讨会，为创新的跨学科研究提供了一个舞台，研究人工智能和数据管理之间的相互作用，并为数据相关任务开发新的人工智能技术。今年，aiDM'24 特别关注人工智能技术的透明利用（例如，使用生成式人工智能框架）进行企业级工作负载的数据管理。

papers.title: ShiftScope: Adapting Visualization Recommendations to Users' Dynamic Data Focus.
papers.abstract: Visualization Recommendation Systems help users discover important insights during data exploration. These systems should understand users' exploration behaviors and goals to suggest relevant visualizations. However, users' mental models constantly evolve as they learn more about their data or as their personal or organizational goals change, leading to shifts in their data focus. Current systems do not adapt to these changes; therefore, they may inevitably suggest irrelevant visualizations over time. Thus, we introduce ShiftScope, an interactive system that recommends personalized visualizations while adapting to users' conceptualization of data. ShiftScope utilizes a dual-agent reinforcement learning framework, where one agent adapts to evolution in data focus and collaborates with the other agent to recommend the best visualizations to satisfy users' current and future exploration needs.
papers.abstract_zh: 可视化推荐系统帮助用户在数据探索过程中发现重要的见解。这些系统应该了解用户的探索行为和目标，以建议相关的可视化。然而，随着用户对数据的了解越来越多，或者随着个人或组织目标的变化，用户的心理模型会不断发展，从而导致他们的数据关注点发生变化。当前的系统无法适应这些变化；因此，随着时间的推移，他们可能不可避免地会提出不相关的可视化。因此，我们引入了 ShiftScope，这是一个交互式系统，可以推荐个性化可视化，同时适应用户对数据的概念化。 ShiftScope 采用双代理强化学习框架，其中一个代理适应数据焦点的演变，并与另一个代理协作推荐最佳可视化，以满足用户当前和未来的探索需求。

papers.title: SIERRA: A Counterfactual Thinking-based Visual Interface for Property Graph Query Construction.
papers.abstract: Attractive visual query interfaces (VQIs) have great potential to democratize the usage of property graph databases as they facilitate user-friendly query formulation without demanding the need to learn a property graph query language e.g.Cypher. Existing VQIs, however, do not embrace HCI principles and psychology theories to inform their design and as a result may limit their potential due to usability andaesthetics challenges. In this demonstration, we present a novelcounterfactual thinking -based (i.e. theory-informed) VQI called SIERRA for property graph query construction to address this limitation. It realizes a novel visual abstraction calledlabeled composite graph (LCG) and avisual shape definition language to create and maintain an LCG during query construction guided by theories and principles from HCI, visualization and psychology.
papers.abstract_zh: 有吸引力的可视化查询界面（VQI）在使属性图数据库的使用民主化方面具有巨大的潜力，因为它们有助于用户友好的查询制定，而无需学习属性图查询语言（例如 Cypher）。然而，现有的 VQI 并未采用人机交互原理和心理学理论来指导其设计，因此可能会因可用性和美观方面的挑战而限制其潜力。在本次演示中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于反事实思维（即基于理论）的 VQI（称为 SIERRA），用于构建属性图查询，以解决此限制。它实现了一种称为标记复合图（LCG）的新颖视觉抽象和视觉形状定义语言，以在人机交互、可视化和心理学的理论和原则指导下在查询构造期间创建和维护 LCG。

papers.title: SIMDified Data Processing - Foundations, Abstraction, and Advanced Techniques.
papers.abstract: Query execution techniques in database systems are constantly adapting to novel hardware features in order to improve query performance, in particular for analytical queries. In the last decade, the Single Instruction Multiple Data (SIMD) paradigm was established as a state-of-the-art approach to increase the single-query performance by parallelizing in-core. Such SIMD capabilities are constantly increasing in modern processors by more comprehensive SIMD instruction set extensions. Therefore, this tutorial will provide an up-to-date overview about SIMDified query processing concepts. Conceptually, this tutorial is divided into three parts. In the first part, we lay the foundations by describing what SIMD is, what it was introduced for, and how it is traditionally used to accelerate query processing. The subsequent two parts focus on novel developments in this area. While the second part of this tutorial is dedicated to an abstraction layer to handle the increasing heterogeneity in the SIMD hardware landscape, the third part provides a survey of advanced techniques to address upcoming challenges such as wider and flexibly-sized SIMD registers.
papers.abstract_zh: 数据库系统中的查询执行技术不断适应新的硬件功能，以提高查询性能，特别是分析查询。在过去的十年中，单指令多数据 (SIMD) 范例被建立为一种最先进的方法，通过内核并行化来提高单查询性能。通过更全面的 SIMD 指令集扩展，现代处理器中的这种 SIMD 功能不断增强。因此，本教程将提供有关 SIMDified 查询处理概念的最新概述。从概念上讲，本教程分为三个部分。在第一部分中，我们通过描述 SIMD 是什么、引入它的用途以及传统上如何使用它来加速查询处理来奠定基础。接下来的两部分重点介绍该领域的新发展。虽然本教程的第二部分致力于抽象层来处理 SIMD 硬件环境中日益增长的异构性，但第三部分提供了对先进技术的调查，以应对即将到来的挑战，例如更宽和灵活大小的 SIMD 寄存器。

papers.title: Similarity Joins of Sparse Features.
papers.abstract: Identifying all pairs of records from two datasets whose similarity exceeds a given threshold is crucial for data cleaning and clustering. Our work on similarity-joins is motivated by detecting fraud and abuse. We focus on similarity-joins of sparse features, where records represent sparse sets, multisets, or vectors. Most state-of-the-art techniques are distributed versions of sequential algorithms. This is the reason they fail to scale when the alphabet is large, the records are large, or some elements are shared by numerous records. In this paper, we propose FastScalableSparseJoiner (FSSJ) that introduces quasi-prefix filtering, a novel flavor of prefix filtering [7] that exploits the skew in element popularity to avoid processing the most-popular elements without broadcasting the sorted elements to all executors. FastScalableSparseJoiner effectively prunes candidate pairs, and efficiently distributes the computation of partial results for load-balancing across executors. FSSJ can be adopted in any shared-nothing architecture. Based on our evaluation on synthetic and real datasets using a Spark-based implementation, FSSJ is very competitive on small datasets, and is the only algorithm that can join industry-scale datasets even with limited resources.
papers.abstract_zh: 识别两个数据集中相似度超过给定阈值的所有记录对对于数据清理和聚类至关重要。我们对相似​​连接的工作是通过检测欺诈和滥用来推动的。我们关注稀疏特征的相似连接，其中记录表示稀疏集、多重集或向量。大多数最先进的技术都是顺序算法的分布式版本。这就是当字母表很大、记录很大或某些元素被大量记录共享时它们无法扩展的原因。在本文中，我们提出了 FastScalableSparseJoiner (FSSJ)，它引入了准前缀过滤，这是一种新颖的前缀过滤 [7]，它利用元素流行度的偏差来避免处理最流行的元素，而不将排序的元素广播到所有执行器。 FastScalableSparseJoiner 有效地修剪候选对，并有效地分配部分结果的计算，以在执行器之间实现负载平衡。 FSSJ 可以在任何无共享架构中采用。根据我们使用基于 Spark 的实现对合成数据集和真实数据集的评估，FSSJ 在小型数据集上非常有竞争力，并且是唯一可以在资源有限的情况下加入行业规模数据集的算法。

papers.title: SmartNICs in the Cloud: The Why, What and How of In-network Processing for Data-Intensive Applications.
papers.abstract: In modern datacenters and clouds, Resource Disaggregation has been adopted as a way of offering scalability and efficient resource utilization for large-scale applications. Provisioning CPU, memory, and storage resources independently for distributed data-intensive applications is a great enabler but it also brings challenges, especially in the form of networking and processing overhead. To reduce this overhead and to make disaggregation-related tasks significantly more efficient, cloud providers are offloading these to the network, i.e., to Smart Network Interface Cards (SmartNICs) and Smart Switches. Beyond this specific use-case, the presence of such programmable hardware in commercial clouds creates future opportunities for offloading application-level operations, e.g., parts of SQL queries or ML pipelines. To map out this exciting space, in this tutorial we take a detailed look at SmartNICs, explaining how they work, giving examples of what they are good for, and highlighting how they can best be utilized to make future data-intensive and distributed systems more efficient.
papers.abstract_zh: 在现代数据中心和云中，资源分解已被用作为大规模应用程序提供可扩展性和高效资源利用的一种方式。为分布式数据密集型应用程序独立配置 CPU、内存和存储资源是一个很好的推动因素，但它也带来了挑战，特别是在网络和处理开销方面。为了减少这种开销并显着提高与分解相关的任务的效率，云提供商正在将这些任务卸载到网络，即智能网络接口卡 (SmartNIC) 和智能交换机。除了这个特定的用例之外，商业云中此类可编程硬件的存在为卸载应用程序级操作（例如部分 SQL 查询或 ML 管道）创造了未来的机会。为了描绘这个令人兴奋的空间，在本教程中，我们详细介绍了 SmartNIC，解释了它们的工作原理，举例说明了它们的用途，并强调了如何最好地利用它们来提高未来数据密集型和分布式系统的效率。

papers.title: Stage: Query Execution Time Prediction in Amazon Redshift.
papers.abstract:
Query performance (e.g., execution time) prediction is a critical component of modern DBMSes. As a pioneering cloud data warehouse, Amazon Redshift relies on an accurate execution time prediction for many downstream tasks, ranging from high-level optimizations, such as automatically creating materialized views, to low-level tasks on the critical path of query execution, such as admission, scheduling, and execution resource control. Unfortunately, many existing execution time prediction techniques, including those used in Redshift, suffer from cold start issues, inaccurate estimation, and are not robust against workload/data changes.

In this paper, we propose a novel hierarchical execution time predictor: the Stage predictor. The Stage predictor is designed to leverage the unique characteristics and challenges faced by Redshift. The Stage predictor consists of three model states: an execution time cache, a lightweight local model optimized for a specific DB instance with uncertainty measurement, and a complex global model that is transferable across all instances in Redshift. We design a systematic approach to use these models that best leverages optimality (cache), instance-optimization (local model), and transferable knowledge about Redshift (global model). Experimentally, we show that the Stage predictor makes more accurate and robust predictions while maintaining a practical inference latency and memory overhead. Overall, the Stage predictor can improve the average query execution latency by 20% on these instances compared to the prior query performance predictor in Redshift.
papers.abstract_zh:
查询性能（例如执行时间）预测是现代 DBMS 的关键组成部分。作为开创性的云数据仓库，Amazon Redshift 依赖于许多下游任务的准确执行时间预测，从高级优化（例如自动创建物化视图）到查询执行关键路径上的低级任务（例如准入、调度和执行资源控制）。不幸的是，许多现有的执行时间预测技术（包括 Redshift 中使用的技术）都存在冷启动问题、估计不准确，并且对于工作负载/数据变化的鲁棒性不强。

在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的分层执行时间预测器：阶段预测器。 Stage 预测器旨在利用 Redshift 的独特特征和面临的挑战。 Stage 预测器由三个模型状态组成：执行时间缓存、针对具有不确定性测量的特定数据库实例进行优化的轻量级本地模型，以及可在 Redshift 中的所有实例之间传输的复杂全局模型。我们设计了一种系统方法来使用这些模型，最好地利用最优性（缓存）、实例优化（本地模型）和有关 Redshift 的可转移知识（全局模型）。通过实验，我们表明阶段预测器可以做出更准确、更稳健的预测，同时保持实际的推理延迟和内存开销。总体而言，与 Redshift 中之前的查询性能预测器相比，Stage 预测器可以将这些实例上的平均查询执行延迟提高 20%。

papers.title: Tenth International Workshop on Testing Database Systems (DBTest).
papers.abstract: With the ever-increasing amount of data stored and processed and ever-evolving hardware technology, there is not only an ongoing need for testing database management systems but also data-intensive systems in general. Reviving the previous success of nine previous workshops, the goal of the Workshop on Testing Database Systems (DBTest) 2024 is to bring researchers and practitioners from academia and industry together to discuss key problems and ideas related to testing database systems and applications. The long-term objective of our efforts is to reduce the cost and time required to test and tune data management and processing products so that users and vendors can spend more time and energy on actual innovations.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着存储和处理的数据量不断增加以及硬件技术的不断发展，不仅需要持续测试数据库管理系统，而且还需要测试一般的数据密集型系统。 2024 年数据库系统测试研讨会 (DBTest) 的目标是让学术界和工业界的研究人员和从业者齐聚一堂，讨论与测试数据库系统和应用程序相关的关键问题和想法，延续之前九场研讨会的成功经验。我们努力的长期目标是减少测试和调整数据管理和处理产品所需的成本和时间，以便用户和供应商可以将更多的时间和精力用于实际创新。

papers.title: The Future of Graph Analytics.
papers.abstract:
In the last two decades, we have been witnessing high demand for graph-based technologies in industry. On the research side, several recent advances have been made about large-scale graph processing, graph analytical systems and graph databases. The landscape of graph query languages is currently evolving with the definition of new standards, and the need for domain-specific languages to express graph algorithmic and analytical primitives will continue and increase in the next future.

In this SIGMOD panel, we will discuss the impact of the above changes on the future of graph analytics. Is there a demand for more expressive languages and libraries for analyzing relationships in a graph? Are new hybrid OLTP/OLAP architectures required with improved performance and scalability? What are the graph analytical workloads and benchmarks that users expect on real-world graph applications? What will be the impact of graph ML on graph analytical systems? How to adapt these systems to the dynamic changes that are ubiquitous for graph-shaped data? These and other questions will be addressed in the panel.
papers.abstract_zh:
在过去的二十年中，我们目睹了工业界对基于图的技术的高需求。在研究方面，大规模图处理、图分析系统和图数据库方面最近取得了一些进展。目前，图查询语言的格局正在随着新标准的定义而不断发展，并且对特定于领域的语言来表达图算法和分析原语的需求将在未来继续并增加。

在本次 SIGMOD 小组中，我们将讨论上述变化对图分析未来的影响。是否需要更具表现力的语言和库来分析图表中的关系？是否需要新的混合 OLTP/OLAP 架构来提高性能和可扩展性？用户在现实世界的图形应用程序中期望的图形分析工作负载和基准是什么？图机器学习对图分析系统有何影响？如何使这些系统适应图形数据普遍存在的动态变化？这些问题和其他问题将在小组讨论中得到解决。

papers.title: The Game Of Recourse: Simulating Algorithmic Recourse over Time to Improve Its Reliability and Fairness.
papers.abstract: Algorithmic recourse, or providing recommendations to individuals who receive an unfavorable outcome from an algorithmic system on how they can take action and change that outcome, is an important tool for giving individuals agency against algorithmic decision systems. Unfortunately, research on algorithmic recourse faces a fundamental challenge: there are no publicly available datasets on algorithmic recourse. In this work, we begin to explore a solution to this challenge by creating an agent-based simulation called The Game of Recourse (an homage to Conway's Game of Life) to synthesize realistic algorithmic recourse data. We designed The Game of Recourse with a focus on reliability and fairness, two areas of critical importance in socio-technical systems. You can access the application at https://game-of-recourse.streamlit.app.
papers.abstract_zh: 算法追索权，或者向从算法系统收到不利结果的个人提供如何采取行动并改变结果的建议，是赋予个人针对算法决策系统的代理权的重要工具。不幸的是，算法追索的研究面临着一个根本性的挑战：没有关于算法追索的公开数据集。在这项工作中，我们开始探索这一挑战的解决方案，通过创建名为“追索游戏”（向康威的生命游戏致敬）的基于代理的模拟来合成现实的算法追索数据。我们设计追索游戏的重点是可靠性和公平性，这是社会技术系统中至关重要的两个领域。您可以通过 https://game-of-recourse.streamlit.app 访问该应用程序。

papers.title: The Hopsworks Feature Store for Machine Learning.
papers.abstract:
Data management is the most challenging aspect of building Machine Learning (ML) systems. ML systems can read large volumes of historical data when training models, but inference workloads are more varied, depending on whether it is a batch or online ML system. The feature store for ML has recently emerged as a single data platform for managing ML data throughout the ML lifecycle, from feature engineering to model training to inference.

In this paper, we present the Hopsworks feature store for machine learning as a highly available platform for managing feature data with API support for columnar, row-oriented, and similarity search query workloads. We introduce and address challenges solved by the feature stores related to feature reuse, how to organize data transformations, and how to ensure correct and consistent data between feature engineering, model training, and model inference. We present the engineering challenges in building high-performance query services for a feature store and show how Hopsworks outperforms existing cloud feature stores for training and online inference query workloads.
papers.abstract_zh:
数据管理是构建机器学习 (ML) 系统中最具挑战性的方面。机器学习系统在训练模型时可以读取大量历史数据，但推理工作负载更加多样化，具体取决于它是批处理还是在线机器学习系统。 ML 的特征存储最近已成为单一数据平台，用于在整个 ML 生命周期（从特征工程到模型训练再到推理）中管理 ML 数据。

在本文中，我们将用于机器学习的 Hopsworks 特征存储作为一个高度可用的平台，用于管理特征数据，并为列式、面向行和相似性搜索查询工作负载提供 API 支持。我们介绍并解决了特征存储解决的与特征重用相关的挑战，如何组织数据转换，以及如何确保特征工程、模型训练和模型推理之间数据的正确性和一致性。我们提出了为特征存储构建高性能查询服务的工程挑战，并展示了 Hopsworks 如何在训练和在线推理查询工作负载方面优于现有的云特征存储。

papers.title: The Journey to a Knowledgeable Assistant with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
papers.abstract:
For decades, multiple communities (Database, Information Retrieval, Natural Language Processing, Data Mining, AI) have pursued the mission of providing the right information at the right time. Efforts span web search, data integration, knowledge graphs, question answering. Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in comprehending and generating human language, revolutionizing techniques in every front. However, their inherent limitations such as factual inaccuracies and hallucinations make LLMs less suitable for creating knowledgeable and trustworthy assistants.

This talk describes our journey in building a knowledgeable AI assistant by harnessing LLM techniques. We start with our findings from a comprehensive set of experiments to assess LLM reliability in answering factual questions and analyze performance variations across different knowledge types. Next, we describe our federated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that integrates external information from both the web and knowledge graphs for trustworthy text generation on real-time topics like stocks and sports, as well as on torso-to-tail entities like local restaurants. Additionally, we brief our explorations on extending our techniques towards multi-modal, contextualized, and personalized Q&A. We will share our techniques, our findings, and the path forward, high- lighting how we are leveraging and advancing the decades of work in this area.
papers.abstract_zh:
几十年来，多个社区（数据库、信息检索、自然语言处理、数据挖掘、人工智能）一直追求在正确的时间提供正确的信息的使命。努力涵盖网络搜索、数据集成、知识图谱、问题解答。大型语言模型 (LLM) 的最新进展展示了在理解和生成人类语言方面的卓越能力，彻底改变了各个领域的技术。然而，其固有的局限性，例如事实不准确和幻觉，使得法学硕士不太适合培养知识渊博、值得信赖的助手。

本次演讲描述了我们利用法学硕士技术构建知识渊博的人工智能助手的历程。我们从一系列全面的实验中得出结论，评估法学硕士在回答事实问题方面的可靠性，并分析不同知识类型的表现差异。接下来，我们描述我们的联合检索增强生成（RAG）系统，该系统集成了来自网络和知识图的外部信息，以生成关于股票和体育等实时主题以及当地餐馆等从躯干到尾部的实体的可信文本。此外，我们还简要介绍了将我们的技术扩展到多模式、情境化和个性化问答的探索。我们将分享我们的技术、我们的发现和前进的道路，重点介绍我们如何利用和推进该领域数十年的工作。

papers.title: The Limitations of Data, Machine Learning and Us.
papers.abstract: Machine learning (ML), particularly deep learning, is being used everywhere. However, not always is applied well or has ethical and/or scientific issues. In this keynote we first do a deep dive in the limitations of supervised ML and data, its key input. We cover small data, datification, bias, and evaluating success instead of harm, among other limitations. The second part is about ourselves using ML, including different types of social limitations and human incompetence such as cognitive biases, pseudoscience, or unethical applications. These limitations have harmful consequences such as discrimination, misinformation, and mental health issues, to mention just a few. In the final part we discuss regulation on the use of AI and responsible principles that can mitigate the problems outlined above.
papers.abstract_zh: 机器学习（ML），特别是深度学习，正在被广泛使用。然而，并不总是得到很好的应用，或者存在伦理和/或科学问题。在本次主题演讲中，我们首先深入探讨监督机器学习及其关键输入数据的局限性。我们涵盖小数据、数据化、偏见、评估成功而不是伤害等限制。第二部分是关于我们自己如何使用机器学习，包括不同类型的社会限制和人类无能，例如认知偏见、伪科学或不道德的应用。这些限制会产生有害后果，例如歧视、错误信息和心理健康问题等等。在最后一部分中，我们讨论了人工智能使用的监管以及可以缓解上述问题的负责任原则。

papers.title: Third International Workshop on Data Systems Education (DataEd'24).
papers.abstract: Interest in data systems education is increasing, especially with the rise in demand for well-trained and re-trained data scientists. The database and the computing education research communities have complementary perspectives and experiences to share with each other. The DataEd workshop is organized as a dedicated venue for these communities to come together to share findings, cross-pollinate perspectives and methods, and shed light on opportunities for mutual progress in data systems education. In the DataEd 2024 workshop, we will present and discuss data management systems education experiences and research via keynote, paper presentations, and panel discussions.
papers.abstract_zh: 人们对数据系统教育的兴趣与日俱增，尤其是随着对训练有素和再培训的数据科学家的需求不断增加。数据库和计算教育研究界有互补的观点和经验可以相互分享。 DataEd 研讨会是为这些社区提供一个专门的场所，让他们聚集在一起分享研究成果、交叉传播观点和方法，并揭示数据系统教育方面共同进步的机会。在 DataEd 2024 研讨会中，我们将通过主题演讲、论文演示和小组讨论来展示和讨论数据管理系统教育经验和研究。

papers.title: TimeCloth: Fast Point-in-Time Database Recovery in The Cloud.
papers.abstract:
In recent years, we have noted the frequent occurrence of user-triggered database recoveries in the cloud. In contrast to traditional failure-triggered recovery scenarios, they come with unique consumer-centric challenges. Unfortunately, current solutions prove inefficient, falling short either in meeting our customers' requirements or due to their close integration with the native recovery support of the underlying database engines.

In this work, we present TimeCloth, a generic cloud-native recovery mechanism that achieves sublinear recovery time while meeting the specific needs of our customers. It comprises a recovery module optimized for fine-grained point-in-time recoveries and an import module enabling nearly instantaneous access to remote tables. The recovery module performs fast log filtering, parallelizes replay of non-conflicting log records and coalesce log records to reduce the volume of replay work. The import module implements a transparent FUSE-based lazy loading mechanism as well as a smart prefetcher to achieve good access performance for remote tables. Collectively, they significantly accelerate user-triggered recoveries in the cloud. TimeCloth is launched in production at Alibaba Cloud for about 15 months. We have witnesses a reduction in RTO among our customers by 44% on average and sometimes up to 92%.
papers.abstract_zh:
近年来，我们注意到云中用户触发的数据库恢复频繁发生。与传统的故障触发恢复场景相比，它们面临着以消费者为中心的独特挑战。不幸的是，当前的解决方案被证明效率低下，要么无法满足客户的需求，要么由于它们与底层数据库引擎的本机恢复支持的紧密集成而无法实现。

在这项工作中，我们提出了 TimeCloth，这是一种通用的云原生恢复机制，可以实现亚线性恢复时间，同时满足客户的特定需求。它包括一个针对细粒度时间点恢复进行优化的恢复模块和一个支持几乎即时访问远程表的导入模块。恢复模块执行快速日志过滤，并行重放非冲突日志记录并合并日志记录以减少重放工作量。导入模块实现了基于FUSE的透明延迟加载机制以及智能预取器，以实现对远程表的良好访问性能。总的来说，它们显着加速了云中用户触发的恢复。 TimeCloth 在阿里云上投入生产约 15 个月。我们见证了客户的 RTO 平均减少了 44%，有时甚至高达 92%。

papers.title: Unified Query Optimization in the Fabric Data Warehouse.
papers.abstract: Over a decade ago, Microsoft introduced Parallel Data Warehouse (PDW), a massively parallel processing system to manage and query large amounts of data. Its optimizer was built by reusing SQL Server's infrastructure with minimal changes, which was an effective approach to bring cost-based query optimization quickly to PDW. Over time, learnings from production as well as architectural changes in the product (such as moving from an appliance form factor to the cloud, separation of compute and storage, and serverless components) required evolving the query optimizer in Fabric DW, the latest offering from Microsoft in the cloud data warehouse space. In this paper we describe the changes to the optimization process in Fabric DW, compare them to the earlier architecture used in PDW, and validate our new approach.
papers.abstract_zh: 十多年前，微软推出了并行数据仓库（PDW），这是一个用于管理和查询大量数据的大规模并行处理系统。其优化器是通过重用 SQL Server 的基础架构而构建的，只需进行最少的更改，这是将基于成本的查询优化快速引入 PDW 的有效方法。随着时间的推移，从生产中学到的知识以及产品的架构变化（例如从设备外形尺寸转向云、计算和存储分离以及无服务器组件）需要改进 Fabric DW 中的查询优化器，Fabric DW 是 Microsoft 在云数据仓库领域的最新产品。在本文中，我们描述了 Fabric DW 中优化过程的变化，将它们与 PDW 中使用的早期架构进行比较，并验证我们的新方法。

papers.title: UniTS: A Universal Time Series Analysis Framework Powered by Self-Supervised Representation Learning.
papers.abstract: Machine learning has emerged as a powerful tool for time series analysis.
